PREFACE

[The so called "minor prophets" constitute some of the most neglected books of the Bible in the modern Church Age, and there are no critical or exegetical commentaries on these prophets that are faithful to the Authorized text. ]{.class_s321}[The Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_s322}[makes (or suggests) about two changes per verse in the minor prophets, as they follow the apostate leadership of the treacherous ]{.class_s321}[RV ]{.class_s322}[(1881--1884) Committee. Bullinger suggests about 200 substitutions, and of course, the work by the major expositors---Marti, Guthe, Horton, Nowack, Hoffmann, Harper, Orelli, Rothstein, Cheyne, Delitzsch, Keil, Ewald, Schultz, and Clarke---amounts to little more than fourteen volumes of destructive criticism aimed at replacing the ]{.class_s321}[Holy Bible]{.class_s322}[ with theological hot air.]{.class_s321}

[The devotional commentaries (Matthew Henry, Greene, Feinberg, Ironside, et al.) have some merit to them since the "milk" which they dish out for us at least is not curdled; however, a ]{.class_s321}[devotional exposition ]{.class_s322}[of a Bible book is a long way from what is needed to nourish those who desire the ]{.class_s321}["fullness of the stature of Christ."]{.class_s323}

[We adopt the position that where the Bible speaks, Christian scholarship can shut its mouth and learn; and we are not in the least intimidated by the "findings of science," ancient or modern. [Throughout our discussion of the "minor prophets" we shall take the position that where the infallible authority of Almighty God speaks (the ]{.class_s321}[Authorized Version of 1611]{.class_s322}[) contrary to the findings of Conservative and "Fundamental" scholars, they are to be "sacked" without any consideration whatsoever. \[See ]{.class_s321}[The Christian's Handbook of Manuscript Evidence, ]{.class_s322}[1970.\] The ]{.class_s321}[Authorized Version]{.class_s322}[ has already proved itself on a thousand battlefields in a thousand ways: It is bulletproof. [The ]{.class_s321}["prophets" ]{.class_s323}[in a Hebrew Old Testament ]{.class_s321}[("Nahbim"]{.class_s322}[) are called the "former" and the "latter" prophets. The "former" include the books of Joshua, Judges, 1 and 2 Samuel, and 1 and 2 Kings. The "latter" include Isaiah through Malachi. We call Hosea through Malachi the "minor prophets" only because of the shortness of their recorded utterances. They are in no way ]{.class_s321}[inferior ]{.class_s322}[to Daniel, Jeremiah, Ezekiel, or Isaiah.]{.class_s321}

[For the word ]{.class_s321}["prophet" ]{.class_s322}[we refer the reader to the comments on Genesis 20:7 found in the ]{.class_s321}[Bible Believer's Commentary on Genesis. ]{.class_s322}[Moses and David are Prophet-Priest King types, foreshadowing Christ. Samuel is only a Prophet Priest, and Saul a dubious Prophet King. Joshua is a Prophet King but certainly not a Priest. The prophets were men raised up in times of spiritual []{#c3F.xhtml_page_9}declension and apostasy so that they could take God's side against a nation when God had a controversy with that nation. They were prophets in the truest sense, and the nonsense about prophets being primarily "forth tellers" is just more of the same tripe you get from men to whom God has shown nothing. \[When the seminary professor finally "arrives," he has already been exposed to six to ten years of destructive criticism which has succeeded in killing the authority of the word of God in his own life. In this situation he can never really get any refreshing (something "new") from the old Book; so, he spends the rest of his life altering it so that it appears "new" to his students.) "Forth teller" for "foreteller," when speaking of the prophets, is about as relevant as saying "a penitent" lives in a "penny tent." False prophets who do not have ]{.class_s321}["the testimony of Jesus" ]{.class_s323}[(Rev. 19:10) cannot prophesy the truth (1 Kings 22:23), so they have to "forth tell." As we have said before, by this standard Adolf Hitler, FDR, Ho Chi Minh, Jerry Lee Lewis, John Lennon, and Tiny Tim are ]{.class_s321}["also among the prophets" ]{.class_s323}[(1 Sam. 19:24).]{.class_s321}

[There are ]{.class_s321}[seven ]{.class_s322}[periods of prophecy in the Bible:]{.class_s321}

1\. Adam to Abraham (note that Enoch is called a prophet).

[2. Abraham to Moses (note that men prophesy in ]{.class_s321}["the camp," ]{.class_s323}[Num. 11:29--30).]{.class_s321}

3\. Moses to Hosea (1500--689 B.C.).

4\. Hosea to Malachi (689 B.C.--389 B.C.).

5\. John the Baptist to Acts 1 (John and Christ are both prophets).

[6. Acts 1 to the Rapture (the Holy Spirit showing us ]{.class_s321}["things to come," ]{.class_s323}[John 16:13).]{.class_s321}

7\. The 144,000 witnesses with Moses and Elijah in the Tribulation. There are no prophets in the Millennium (Zech. 13:2) nor is there any prophecy (Heb. 8:11). Prophets in the Millennium are to be executed (Zech. 13:3).

[Among the MINOR PROPHETS who are ]{.class_s321}[dated ]{.class_s322}[in the Bible are Hosea, Amos, Micah, Zephaniah, Haggai, and Zechariah; the minor prophets who are ]{.class_s321}[undated ]{.class_s322}[are Joel, Obadiah, Jonah, Nahum, Habakkuk, and Malachi. Hosea is a contemporary of Isaiah and Micah. Zephaniah and Habakkuk are []{#c3F.xhtml_page_10}contemporaries of Jeremiah, and Daniel overlaps Joel and Obadiah. Haggai and Zechariah are contemporary, and Malachi sits off by himself.]{.class_s321}

According to Bullinger, the sixteen prophetical books fall into four divisions. The first group would be six prophets (Jonah, Amos, Hosea, Isaiah, Micah, and Nahum); the second group would be seven prophets (Jeremiah, Habakkuk, Zephaniah, Daniel, Joel, Ezekiel, and Obadiah); the third group would be two prophets (Haggai and Zechariah), and then follows Malachi, sitting all by himself. These four groups would cover a period of about 316 years. In this commentary we will be concerned only with the minor prophets which, of course, exclude Daniel, Ezekiel, Isaiah, and Jeremiah.

[A good way to divide up the minor prophets is to note that there are ]{.class_s321}[twelve]{.class_s322}[ of them making up one book in the Hebrew canon. These can best be divided into a three-six three sequence: Hosea, Joel and Amos in the first sequence; Obadiah, Jonah, Micah, Nahum, Habakkuk, and Zephaniah in the second sequence; and Haggai, Zechariah, and Malachi in the third sequence. In memorizing the minor prophets the best sequence is as follows: Hosea, Joel, Amos, Obadiah-break-Jonah-break-Micah, Nahum-break-Habakkuk and Zephaniah-break-Haggai and Zechariah-break-Malachi.]{.class_s321}

[The Hebrew twelve volume unit of the minor prophets is divided into twenty one ]{.class_s321}[sedarim ]{.class_s322}[(sections for public reading) which shows that, originally, the twelve prophets were read as a single book.]{.class_s321}

[In our study of the minor prophets we shall encounter an undue amount of trouble in expositing certain passages---especially in Zechariah. So, let it be stated at the beginning that the author does not (nor has he ever done so) profess to understand all of the Bible. Where we encounter these passages, we shall simply say "I DON'T KNOW." No attempt will be made on our part to juggle the Hebrew around, run to the ]{.class_s321}[Septuagint, ]{.class_s322}[fake out to the Coptic, feint to the Syriac, or put some "body English" on the verse to make it line up with what Larkin, Pember, and Scofield already told us. This is of the utmost importance when one remembers that the faculty members of 98 percent of the Christian schools in America are controlled by cultists who are merely rehashing material given to []{#c3F.xhtml_page_11}them before 1929 and using Hebrew and Greek to kid the student into thinking that this is HOW he can learn the Bible. Kenneth Wuest only uses Greek to prove Premillennial doctrine after Dwight L. Moody stated the truths ]{.class_s321}[fifty years before Wuest got out of college. ]{.class_s322}[Robertson only used the Greek to prove eternal security]{.class_s321}[ 200 years after Whitefield knew it.]{.class_s322}

So, where we have trouble with interpreting a verse we are:

1\. Not going to add words to it so that it will make sense.

2\. Not going to take words from it so that it will make sense.

3\. Not going to run to some Greek or Hebrew text to correct the English simply because we are too stupid to understand the English.

4\. Not going to complain about "the condition of the text" or "the corruptness" of the text to cover up our own stupidity.

5\. And, above all, we are not going to take any man's word for what the verse means or says where that man doesn't have enough respect for the Bible to keep his dirty, unbelieving hands off of the Authorized Text.

[Since God tells us to take the PROPHETS for an ]{.class_s321}["example of suffering affliction" ]{.class_s323}[(James 5:10) and since our Saviour was a Prophet "like unto Moses" (Deut. 18:15) and was introduced by the greatest prophet ]{.class_s321}["born of women" ]{.class_s323}[(Matt. 11:11) and since the proof of Divine authorship lies primarily in the mathematical structure of fulfilled prophecy (Isa. 42:9), we will certainly not be wasting our time studying the minor prophets.]{.class_s321}

[Each of the minor prophets, then, is a genuine ]{.class_s321}[prophet; ]{.class_s322}[he is not a rabble rousing Communist like Michael Luther King Jr. He is not a generalizing Roman Catholic psychic like Jean Dixon, and he is not a passive spiritist like Edgar Cayce. The minor prophets are ]{.class_s321}[prophets; ]{.class_s322}[they foretell the future. Concealed within the various contemporary messages to the nation of Israel and Judah, one will find that:]{.class_s321}

[1. ]{.class_s321}[Hosea ]{.class_s322}[lays an emphasis on Israel's repentance and restoration at the Second Advent.]{.class_s321}

[2. ]{.class_s321}[Joel ]{.class_s322}[lays the emphasis on the battle of Armageddon at the Second Advent.]{.class_s321}

[3. ]{.class_s321}[Amos ]{.class_s322}[gives the route of the Second Advent and the nature of judgments as they fall on other cities besides Jerusalem.]{.class_s321}

[4. ]{.class_s321}[Obadiah ]{.class_s322}[points out that Edom will be the location for the lake of fire in the Millennium.]{.class_s321}

[5. ]{.class_s321}[Jonah ]{.class_s322}[stands as one of the greatest types in the Bible of a Tribulation Jew called to witness to the Gentiles (see Rev. 7 and comments).]{.class_s321}

[6. ]{.class_s321}[Micah ]{.class_s322}[compares the First Advent with the Second Advent.]{.class_s321}

[7. ]{.class_s321}[Nahum ]{.class_s322}[deals with Nineveh as a type of Babylon and shows the conditions that will prevail at the Second Advent.]{.class_s321}

[8. ]{.class_s321}[Habakkuk ]{.class_s322}[lays the emphasis on the route of the Second Advent.]{.class_s321}

[9. ]{.class_s321}[Zephaniah ]{.class_s322}[lays the emphasis on the negative nature of the Second Advent.]{.class_s321}

[10. ]{.class_s321}[Haggai ]{.class_s322}[typifies the building of the temple that will take place in the next twenty years.]{.class_s321}

[11. ]{.class_s321}[Zechariah ]{.class_s322}[compares the two Advents and gives the details of events after Armageddon.]{.class_s321}

[12. ]{.class_s321}[Malachi ]{.class_s322}[gives the final warnings that precede the Advent.]{.class_s321}

Any commentary, that fails to mention these prophesies, is a lying witness.

Since it would be impossible to write an exegetical and devotional commentary on all twelve of these prophets in less than 1,400 pages, we have divided up the series into two volumes: Volume One will contain Hosea through Nahum, Volume Two will complete the series with Habakkuk through Malachi.

INTRODUCTION

TO HOSEA

[The Book of Hosea has 14 chapters, 197 verses, and 5,175 words. The word itself (Hosea) means "God is salvation" or "Save O God!" In Numbers 13:8 and 16 this name is given to "Joshua," which, of course, any student knows is the Old Testament name for Jesus (see Acts 7:45 and Heb. 4:8 where ]{.class_s321}[The International Version ]{.class_s322}[and the ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[have corrupted all four families of Greek texts and mistranslated the word). Literally, we have "Jeh is Salvation," the abbreviated form being used scores of times in the Old Testament (Heb.: ]{.class_s321}[Yod, Quamets, He]{.class_s322}[).]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s322}[The name ]{.class_s321}["Hosea" ]{.class_s323}[is also found in three other places: 2 Kings 15:30, the last king of the Northern Kingdom; 1 Chronicles 27:20, a chief of the tribe of Ephraim; and Nehemiah 10:23, a leader under Nehemiah.]{.class_s321}

Among the many expositors of the book of Hosea, the most well known and widely "recognized" are Mauerer, Hitzig, Delitzsch, Volch, Keil, Cheyne, G. A. Smith, Farrar, Clarke, Horton, A. B. Davidson, Cornill, Guthe, Valeton, Nowack, Hustable, and Marti. All of them were Cultists, and none of them believed the Reformation text for five minutes; and aside from corrections and nauseam, they have nothing to contribute to real Biblical knowledge but the monotonous tautologies of dead orthodox apostasy.

[Hosea prophesies in the days of ]{.class_s321}["Uzziah, Jotham, Ahab and Hezekiah," ]{.class_s323}[which would be about 689 B.C. to 611 B.C. Since this is well over seventy years, ]{.class_s321}[The International Standard Bible Encyclopedia ]{.class_s322}[says that we are not to believe Hosea 1:1 ]{.class_s321}[literally]{.class_s322}[ (off to a flying start, ain't we baby?) because the "accepted chronology" of Kautzsch (Ibid., p. 1425) forbids Hosea to prophesy for fifty-two years. Bob Jones Sr., (1883--1968) prophesied that long and so did Mordecai Ham (1878--1959) and George Mueller (1805--1898). Why Hosea could not have prophesied fifty two years is a greater mystery than what happened to Patty Hearst (1970--1973). If Hosea lived to be 78 years old (689--611), he does begin to prophesy in 663, and he preaches till 611---a period []{#c57.xhtml_page_15}of exactly fifty two years. With Ussiah ascending the throne in 650, Jotham in 647, Ahaz in 632, and Hezekiah in 617, Hosea ministers through the exact "days" given to him by a ]{.class_s321}[King James 1611 Authorized Version.]{.class_s322}

We will give him the same amount of days, and if the Scholars Union doesn't like it, they can go twiddle their Chaldean lexicons.

[As a ]{.class_s321}[prophet, ]{.class_s322}[Hosea follows the standard format for all of the Old Testament prophets:]{.class_s321}

1\. He never preaches revolution as a means of overthrowing the "established" social order.

[2. He is a strong ]{.class_s321}[segregationist ]{.class_s322}[who opposes every form of religious racial integration.]{.class_s321}

[3. He points to the sinful heart of man as the source of mankind's woes---not the ]{.class_s321}[economic system.]{.class_s322}

4\. He is never Utopian and never wastes five minutes preaching permanent "peace on earth" apart from the millennial reign of a Jewish Messiah in Palestine.

5\. All of his messages are based on a Biblical concept of God and God's holiness; he knows nothing about "relative values" or "situation ethics" or "cultural exchanges."

6\. He emphasizes God's WRATH and God's JUDGMENT on individuals and nations who think that God will put up with anything.

7\. He is quite able to predict events accurately that take place 2,000 years after he is dead.

[In short, Hosea (as Jeremiah, Amos, Ezekiel, Obadiah, et al.) is as far removed from the modern preacher of the "Social Gospel," "Kingdom Gospel," and "Soviet Republic" as Tahiti is removed from Iceland. (No prophet in the Old Testament would subscribe to one major tenet of the National Council of Churches where it dealt with ]{.class_s321}[equal rights, socialism, ]{.class_s322}[or ]{.class_s321}[integration]{.class_s322}[.)]{.class_s321}

[The book of Hosea points to the events which precede the fall of Israel and Samaria (which was the capital of the ten northern tribes, who apostatized under Jeroboam). This fall took place in the sixth year of the reign of Hezekiah, and that []{#c57.xhtml_page_16}is prophesied by the statement found in Hosea 13:16. Since Hosea is the first prophet in the Old Testament chronologically (of the prophets who left written books), we are not surprised to find ]{.class_s321}["THE BEGINNING OF THE WORD OF THE LORD" ]{.class_s323}[in Hosea 1:2.]{.class_s321}

[You don't slip us the shaft that easily. You see, some of us who have been around here over half a century know a con man when we see one. You might fool some proud, educated chump who is looking for a way to let folks know how smart he is, but you aren't going to fool us old ex-infantrymen and ex dance band drummers for a minute. We know the shills and the eyeshades when we see them, and we've seen crap games where the man could roll the bones better than you could translate. The ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[is a Christ dishonoring piece of trash that would disgrace the bookshelf of a Roman Catholic (see ]{.class_s321}[Satan's Masterpiece, the New ASV, ]{.class_s322}[1978). The apostate cult members who recommend it are not to be taken seriously by a real Biblebelieving Christian.]{.class_s321}

[So, we will use the ]{.class_s321}[RSV, ASV, NASV, NEB, ]{.class_s322}[and ]{.class_s321}[The "Living" ]{.class_s322}[(saints preserve us!) ]{.class_s321}[Bible ]{.class_s322}[for comic relief in our serious study of the Living Words of the Living God. We shall occasionally refer to these juvenile rag mops to show the reader the difference between the work of the Holy Spirit and the Mickey Mouse type of exposition so common among twentieth-century "scholars." Every []{#c57.xhtml_page_18}Christian should have a good laugh once in awhile, and since our libelous scholars (many of them attended the World Congress of Fundamentalism at BJU) have had many a laugh in their classrooms at the expense of the ]{.class_s321}[AV ]{.class_s322}[(1611), we shall be granted "equal time" and get a few boffs ourselves from their ridiculous scholarship.]{.class_s321}

Here comes Hosea! He comes in the great tradition of his forebearers---Elijah (1 Kings 17--18) and Elisha (2 Kings 2--7)! Here comes one of those heroic preachers of whom Dr. Bob Jones Sr., said: "The prophets are not sugar sticks, they were salty." Prophets dig foxholes (Ezek. 8:8); they run around naked (Isa. 20:2); they lose their wives (Ezek. 24:18); they sit on ash heaps (Job 2:8); they get slapped in the face (1 Kings 22:24); they get mixed up with whale vomit (Jonah 2:10); they get stoned and crucified (Matt. 23:37, 27:20--45); and they get their heads cut off (Mark 6:24).

The prophets are the Rangers, Green Berets, Seabees, Marines, Paratroops, SS troops, and demolition experts of the Old Testament theocracy. They stormed through shot and shell and strafed the enemy till he either had to kill them (Matt. 23:35) or run for his life (1 Kings 22:25). We must believe God's prophets to prosper (2 Chron. 20:20), and certainly we should know a prophet of God (Deut. 18:22) from a prophet of the Devil (Jer. 23:9, 14, 15, 16, 21, 25, 26, 28). Never should we confound men like Cheyne, Robertson, Moulton, Machen, Warfield, Aland, Custer, Wuest, Hort, Zodhiates, and Weniger with men like Mordecai Ham, Burgon, Billy Sunday, Pember, Frank Norris, Clarence Larkin, Dwight L. Moody, and Lee Roberson. Never should we make the mistake of thinking that a Christian who corrects Hosea is a "prophet of God." Hosea (as Daniel, Ezekiel, Jeremiah, and Isaiah) is quite able to correct any pseudo-prophet who spent his life trying to alter the Authority of God Almighty.

Here comes Hosea! He is equipped to fight the good fight of faith and endure hardness as a good soldier of Jesus Christ (2 Tim. 2:3)! Where he speaks, we shall listen, and where Christian scholarship interrupts him (1 Kings 22:24), we shall wave it aside like a carton of rotten eggs.

"[IF THEY SPEAK NOT ACCORDING TO THIS WORD, IT IS []{#c57.xhtml_page_19}BECAUSE THERE IS NO LIGHT IN THEM" ]{.class_s323}[(Isa. 8:20).]{.class_s321}

[]{#c6B.xhtml}

CHAPTER ONE

1:1 The word of the LORD that came unto Hosea, the son of Beeri, in the days of Uzziah, Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah, kings of Judah, and in the days of Jeroboam the son of Joash, king of Israel.

2 The beginning of the word of the LORD by Hosea. And the LORD said to Hosea, Go, take unto thee a wife of whoredoms and children of whoredoms: for the land hath committed great whoredom, departing from the LORD.

3 So he went and took Gomer the daughter of Diblaim; which conceived, and bare him a son.

4 And the LORD said unto him, Call his name Jezreel; for yet a little while, and I will avenge the blood of Jezreel upon the house of Jehu, and will cause to cease the kingdom of the house of Israel.

5 And it shall come to pass at that day, that I will break the bow of Israel in the valley of Jezreel.

We get off to a flying start in the book of Hosea by observing that Hosea has been commanded by God to marry a whore (vs. 2), and if the woman was not a professional prostitute, at least she was a "swinger" or a "hooker" (i.e., fornicator) in the modern vernacular.

[With this roaring "opener" the Scholars Union immediately panics and decides that the book is an allegory (see Alexandria, Egypt: Pantaenus, Origen, Clement, etc.), and it is not to be taken as literal ]{.class_s321}[history]{.class_s322}[. The Alexandrian Cultists who take this position are John Calvin, Jerome, Origen, Keil, Havernick, Hengstenberg, Rosenmuller, Reuss, Eichhorn, and Hitzig. (Observe the "apostolic succession" of the "cult" from Egypt and Africa through the Roman Catholic Church into dead Orthodox Conservatism via German Rationalism.) In []{#c6B.xhtml_page_21}opposition to this school of thought are Kurtz, Delitzsch, Hofmann, Cheyne, Wellhausen, G. A. Smith, and others. But since not one man in either list could grasp two thirds of the Old Testament where it dealt with the Second Advent, we don't have to choose up sides and throw mud pies.]{.class_s321}

Hosea 1:1--5 is literal history, and if verse 2 is not history, what is verse 1? How are verses 4 and 5 fulfilled literally in history (2 Kings 9:14; 10:14; 15:10 and 17) and yet verses 2 and 3 are not?

[The answer given to this by the destructive critics is: There is no record of a major ]{.class_s321}[battle ]{.class_s322}[in the Valley of Jezreel (vs. 5) given in Kings and Chronicles. But this reveals immediately the inability of the critics to expound Bible material, for the reference given reaches out to the Second Advent and the Battle of Armageddon, and (as we have stated above) not one man in a list of ten major writers was able to understand two-thirds of the Old Testament. No Amillennial or Postmillennial expositor, of ANY degree of learning, could ever qualify as a Bible expositor. A ]{.class_s321}[grammarian? ]{.class_s322}[Yes. A ]{.class_s321}[linguist? ]{.class_s322}[Yes. But not a ]{.class_s321}[Biblical ]{.class_s322}[scholar.]{.class_s321}

The fulfillment of verse 5 is plainly found in Hosea 1:11 and 2:22, but no Amillennial expositor (Robert Dick Wilson, A. T. Robertson, G. Machen, B. Warfield, John Calvin, et al.) could possibly expound any of the verses in the sense of Biblical exposition.

[The expression ]{.class_s321}["AT THAT DAY" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 5) is the tip off to ]{.class_s321}["the day of the Lord." ]{.class_s323}[The expression occurs more than 100 times in the prophets. And those of us who take our Bible studies more "seriously" than Dr. A. T. Robertson or Dr. R. D. Wilson know that Israel is REJOICING (2:22) and REGATHERING (1:11) after their ]{.class_s321}["bow"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 5) has been broken.]{.class_s321}

But how could any Hebrew scholar find this if he had already rejected the doctrinal statements of the New Testament relative to it (Rom. 11:25)? He couldn't. And this proves again why we say that no Christian was ever told to respect any man (alive or dead) on God's earth where that man thought he was smart enough to make a liar out of God. "Ignorant" and "conceited" are the terms the Holy Spirit used to describe the Biblical scholarship of Dr. A. T. Robertson and J. Gresham Machen. Their "godliness" was their reputation, and their fame []{#c6B.xhtml_page_22}came from their "stand" against Liberals. Neither had anything to do with their attitude toward the authority of God Almighty.

[So, Hosea has to marry ]{.class_s321}["a wife of whoredoms"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 2). His life, as the life of Ezekiel (Ezek. 12:4--7), is to be an object lesson to his contemporaries. Gomer, the daughter of Diblaim (vs. 3), begats ]{.class_s321}["children of whoredoms"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 2) to prove that ]{.class_s321}["the land hath committed great whoredom, departing from the Lord" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 2). Durnmelow immediately covers up this God given object lesson by saying that Hosea is speaking in the "light of his later experiences" and that when he married his wife she was ]{.class_s321}[innocent, ]{.class_s322}[or that Hosea was ]{.class_s321}["ignorant ]{.class_s322}[of her true character." Nice work.]{.class_s321}

[Go on and call God a liar (like Kimchi, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown who say that verses 2 and 3 didn't really happen---it was just a VISION, p. 766)! ]{.class_s321}[The New Bible Commentary ]{.class_s322}[makes an attempt to show that when the commission was given, the woman had ]{.class_s321}[not yet been unfaithful, ]{.class_s322}[and this may be, but to say that Hosea ]{.class_s321}[did not know her true character]{.class_s322}[ is fantasy: He not only knew what she would do, he knew that the children born of her would be "children of whoredom." All attempts to whitewash the verse (see ]{.class_s321}[The "Living Bible" ]{.class_s322}[on Phil. 1:28 or the ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[on Job 41:1) must fail. Hosea marries a woman who is either a harlot or an adulteress, and she is called a WHORE ]{.class_s321}[before ]{.class_s322}[she is married and an ADULTERESS (3:1) ]{.class_s321}[after ]{.class_s322}[she is married.]{.class_s321}

It is evident that prophets are not "run of the mill" saints.

[But Augustine and ]{.class_s321}[The Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_s322}[will never allow God to mean what He says and say what He means; ]{.class_s321}[so, The Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_s322}[(written by "good men who loved the Lord"---to quote the contemporary bunk) says that Hosea 1 is "AN IMAGINARY NARRATIVE" (p. 1, Vol. 13), while Calvin says that it is a "parable." But when one remembers how ninety nine percent of the Conservatives handled the Book of Jonah (see Preface to the Book of Jonah), we are not surprised to find Calvin lined up with Harry Emerson Fosdick, Eugene Carson Blake, and Bishop Pike where real fidelity to the word of God is an issue.]{.class_s321}

"[So he went and took Gomer" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 3). That is, he went and took []{#c6B.xhtml_page_23}the woman AFTER God told him what kind of a woman she was. ]{.class_s321}["Which conceived, and bare him a son" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 3). The son is immediately called ]{.class_s321}["Jezreel,"]{.class_s323}[ which means "God planteth" or "scattered by God." Here the word is a reference to ]{.class_s321}["THE BLOOD OF JEZREEL" ]{.class_s323}[(i.e., the blood of something that God had sown). This blood will be avenged (vs. 4) because of the murderous Jehu (see 2 Kings 10:31), and Israel will then be scattered (Hosea 8:8).]{.class_s321}

[Now notice the double fulfillment we find in verses 4 and 5 which the revisors and expositors so often stumble over in their works. God has so many cases in the Bible of historical prophetic statements in one verse that it is impossible to understand the passages without the Premillennial system. Observe that Nebuchadnezzar's destruction of Jerusalem (2 Kings 24; 2 Chron. 36) is yet to be fulfilled after 2003 (Jer. 32:36--41). Observe that Babylon's permanent destruction (Jer. 51:62--64) does not take place till after 1977. Observe that Titus' siege of Jerusalem (Luke 21:24) was only a rehearsal of the real one (Rev. 11:1--3). Observe that the expression ]{.class_s321}["the day of the Lord" ]{.class_s323}[and ]{.class_s321}["in that day" ]{.class_s323}[can refer to anywhere from ten to sixty events that take place historically before 400 B.C. (Isa. 1--39), while, at the same time, they can reach out past 1977 and put the events into the United Nation's lap (Zeph. 3:8; Isa. 2, 11).]{.class_s321}

[Now this "overlapping" (see Daniel 9:26--27 specifically) is what confounds the dead orthodox apostate who is always trying to nullify the word of God by limiting it to completed history in the ]{.class_s321}[past. ]{.class_s322}[This penchant for]{.class_s321}[ "making the word of God of none effect"]{.class_s323}[ (Mark 7:31) is so strong that there have been at least 100 "godly, dedicated, scholarly Conservatives, etc.," who relegated Revelation 1 through 19 to PAST history (see ]{.class_s321}[The Bible Believer's Commentary on Revelation, ]{.class_s322}[1970). But what is worse is the fact that the successors to these men are now trying to force you to accept their critical views of the Bible on the grounds of their ]{.class_s321}[reputations ]{.class_s322}[and ]{.class_s321}[character references. ]{.class_s322}[Real fidelity to the word of God, in the ]{.class_s321}[Biblical ]{.class_s322}[sense, is unknown among this class of "godly" scholars.]{.class_s321}

[Verse 4: ]{.class_s321}["Yet a little while" ]{.class_s323}[comes to a very small "while" on God's calendar because in thirty years Israel is kapoot (2 Kings 18:11), but no one can proceed into verse 5 (]{.class_s321}["IN THE VALLEY OF JEZREEL"]{.class_s323}[) without running his finger up the map of Palestine to Megiddo on the southeast end of the valley of Megiddo. This is the "hill of the crowded" (Armageddon) spoken of in Revelation 16:16, and it is the scene of the victory of Christ's armies (Rev. 19:14--21)---not Israel's---over the Son of Perdition (Rev. 19:19). ]{.class_s321}["The day of Jezreel," ]{.class_s323}[then (see 1:11), goes beyond the defeat of Israel by Assyria; it refers to the regathering of Israel on the other side of the Church Age (note 1:10 quoted by the Apostle Paul in Rom. 9:25). ]{.class_s321}["Jezreel" ]{.class_s323}[is, therefore, associated with three things: 1. The captivity in 2 Kings 17 by the King of Assyria with the defeat of Israers armies. 2. The destruction of Israel's power at the approach of 200,000,000 troops from the East (1975--plus). 3. The defeat of the Antichrist and the regathering of Israel in the Millennium.]{.class_s321}

[In chapter one the scene is set for Hosea's ministry. He is to lay the emphasis on a nation that was "married" to Jehovah and "stepped out" on Him. Spiritual idolatry is likened to ]{.class_s321}[adultery ]{.class_s322}[throughout the Old Testament (Num. 25:1--8), and the two are so closely linked in Palestine and Phoenicia (as they were in Rome, Greece, Egypt, and Babylon) that fornication (Rev. 2:20) and sodomy (2 Kings 23:7) were a part of the "spiritual" worship of devils (1 Cor. 10:20). Israel is a wife about to be cast off (Hosea 4:6) and given a bill of divorcement (Deut. 24:1). The ]{.class_s321}["bill of divorcement" ]{.class_s323}[of Matthew 19:7 is---of all things!---AN APOSTATE BIBLE! \[Look at "the Greek," if you think so much of it, and tell us how Kenneth Wuest and Spiros Zodhiates missed that one! (Biblion apostasiou)!\] And as surely as the chaste virgin of 2 Corinthians 11:1--3 was beguiled from the simplicity of Christ into accepting "recognized scholarship" as a god, God has given his "bride" an apostate Bible. This "Bible" was given to the bride of Christ in 1884 by SAVED Englishmen. It was presented to American Christians in 1901, to the Liberals in 1946, and to the fundamental colleges and universities in 1969. Sometimes it goes under the heading of ]{.class_s321}[RV]{.class_s322}[, sometimes ]{.class_s321}[ASV]{.class_s322}[, sometimes ]{.class_s321}[NIV]{.class_s322}[, sometimes something else, but it is the []{#c6B.xhtml_page_25}same book---A BILL OF DIVORCEMENT. It is a ]{.class_s321}[cult textbook ]{.class_s322}[promoted by the Alexandrian Cult.]{.class_s321}

[The book of Hosea, then, is going to deal primarily with the temporary rejection of Israel as a ]{.class_s321}[cast off wife]{.class_s322}[ (2:1, 14) who will later be reconciled to her husband (Isa. 54:5). Any exposition which attempts to make this reconciliation refer only to the ]{.class_s321}[spiritual conversion of individuals ]{.class_s322}[in "the church" without recognizing that it is a ]{.class_s321}[NATIONAL restoration of a NATION ]{.class_s322}[(see any commentary by Prof. Given, Jerome, Albertus Magnus, Lightfoot, Staudlin, Henderson, Archbishop Necome, Dr. Pusey, Reinke, Schegg, Cowles, Knabenbauer, Drake, Bishop Horsley, Neale, Kuinoel, Schroder, Abarbanel, Pocock, Simson, or Wunsche) is an anti Biblical or non Biblical excursion into cloudland.]{.class_s321}

6 And she conceived again, and bare a daughter. And God said unto him, Call her name Loruhamah: for I will no more have mercy upon the house of Israel; but I will utterly take them away.

7 But I will have mercy upon the house of Judah, and will save them by the LORD their God, and will not save them by bow, nor by sword, nor by battle, by horses, nor by horsemen.

[The second child born is ]{.class_s321}["Loruhamah" ]{.class_s323}[("not beloved" or "not having obtained compassion"). The word can mean "she is not pitied," and it is defined in the text with ]{.class_s321}["I will no more have mercy upon the house of Israel" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 6).]{.class_s321}

[These fearful words have never been taken seriously by any Liberal preacher in the World Council or National Council of Churches. "NO MERCY" is an expression that is unheard of and unknown in the writings and sermons of Weatherhead, Poteat, Tillich, Oral Roberts, Niebuhr, Sartre, Jimmy Swaggart, Heidegger, Blake, Spellman, C. W. Burpo, Dahlberg, and the pussyfooting of the []{#c6B.xhtml_page_26}Unitarian and Unity sects. "NO MERCY" is spoken by the One of whom it was said ]{.class_s321}["HIS MERCY ENDURETH FOREVER" ]{.class_s323}[(Psa. 136), and there is no way any sophist can reconcile the passages unless he recognizes that God's mercy only "endures forever" in that it is available if a man meets God's conditions (see Rom. 9:16, where Calvin bloodied his nose) for obtaining it. God's conditions for everlasting mercy in this age are clearly stated in Romans 10:9--10 and Ephesians 2:1--8. God's conditions for mercy on the nation of Israel are clearly stated in Deuteronomy 28:1--14 and Leviticus 26:1--13. The cup is full for Israel when Hosea begins to "shell the corn." Now he is to preach a NEGATIVE message: ]{.class_s321}["I will NO MORE HAVE MERCY upon the house of Israel" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 6). Don't you know he wasn't winning any popularity contests!]{.class_s321}

"[But I will have mercy upon the house of Judah" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 7). Judah, at the time of this offence (about 680--670 B.C.), has not reached the point of apostasy found among the ten northern tribes. Observe that Judah is still ]{.class_s321}["faithful with the saints" ]{.class_s323}[in Hosea 11:12 which is about the time of Hezekiah's ascension to the throne. ]{.class_s321}["Save them by the LORD their God" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 7) is a reference to the divine deliverance given to Hezekiah (2 Kings 19:35) when Sennacherib besieged Jerusalem. \[This account is also a picture of the Second Advent, as Sennacherib is one of the clearest types of the Man of Sin found in the Old Testament (see Isa. 10:5--19).\]]{.class_s321}

8 Now when she had weaned Loruhamah, she conceived, and bare a son.

9 Then said God, Call his name Loammi: for ye are not my people, and I will not be your God.

[The third child is called ]{.class_s321}["Loammi" ]{.class_s323}[(literally "not my people"), which is defined in the verse. Exodus 19:1--7 started Israel off as God's people, but here He disowns them exactly as He did before Moses in Exodus 32:7 (see ]{.class_s321}[The []{#c6B.xhtml_page_27}Bible Believer's Commentary on Exodus, ]{.class_s322}[1976).]{.class_s321}

The condition of the ten northern tribes, then, has been described perfectly. They were God's people; they are no longer. They were pitied; they are no longer. They were sown; they will be scattered.

10 Yet the number of the children of Israel shall be as the sand of the sea, which cannot be measured nor numbered; and it shall come to pass, that in the place where it was said unto them, Ye are not my people, there it shall be said unto them, Ye are the sons of the living God.

11 Then shall the children of Judah and the children of Israel be gathered together, and appoint themselves one head, and they shall come up out of the land: for great shall be the day of Jezreel.

[The narrative does an "about face" of 180 degrees at verse 10. In the face of ]{.class_s321}["ye are not my people, and I will not be your God" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 9) we read, ]{.class_s321}["Ye are the sons of the living God" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 10). From the blackest picture of judgment and wrath, we are turned about to face the brightest picture of love and mercy. Nothing could more clearly illustrate the nature of God than this type of narrative. (See the identical thing in Psa. 89:37--38 and Psa. 22:16, 23.) God is not LOVE without wrath; He is not mercy without judgment; He does not forgive without avenging; in short, the God revealed by the Book of God, written by God, presents a God who cannot be accepted by "modern man" at all. "Modern man" wants a sinner just like himself (John 18:40) who can tolerate anything he can tolerate. Modern man wants a magnetic "field of energy" with no standards, no principles, no ethics, no morals, and (above all) no knowledge of the future. The God of the Bible is a misfit to the modern, educated idiot; so, the modern, educated idiot declares with Nietzsche: "God is dead." Wrong again: You're dead (Eph. 2:1--6).]{.class_s321}

[Verses 10 and 11 will be fulfilled literally at the end of Daniel's Seventieth Week. The ]{.class_s321}["one head" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 11) will be David (Ezek. 34:23), and the gathering ]{.class_s321}["together" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 11) will be around a covenant made with both houses (Heb. 8:10) that will bring them together as one stick (Ezek. 37:16). And, as during the reign of Solomon (1 Kings 4:25), they shall number ]{.class_s321}["as the sand of the sea" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 10) under the reign of their Messiah, the Lord Jesus Christ.]{.class_s321}

[Since the expositors of ]{.class_s321}[The Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_s322}[are Postmillennialists, they immediately tell us that verses 10 and 11 refer to "gospel times" and that the coming ]{.class_s321}["up out of the land"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 11) is a reference to Israel's spiritual conversion to Christ in this age. Wrong again. They were ]{.class_s321}[not ]{.class_s322}[spiritually converted to Christ in the "gospel times" of the New Testament, and they will not be spiritually converted to Christ (Ezek. 36:26) until ]{.class_s321}["AFTER THOSE DAYS" ]{.class_s323}[(Heb. 8:9--12) when they will settle the literal, physical, visible soil of the land of Palestine (Amos 9:15).]{.class_s321}

[Dummelow also makes the "gathering together" of verse 11 the gathering of the whole of Israel in the New Testament church (p. 547). Wrong again. ]{.class_s321}[The whole of Israel has never been in the New Testament church and never will be ]{.class_s322}[(Rom. 11:26), and God the Holy Spirit said that a man is conceited and ignorant (Rom. 11:25) if he believes this.]{.class_s321}

[The trouble that the commentators are having with the verse is due to the fact that Paul appropriates verse 10 for the body of Christ (Rom. 9:26), and no attempts by Stam or Bullinger (see any of their writings) can nullify the fact that verses which deal with the ]{.class_s321}[Jewish Tribulation remnant ]{.class_s322}[are often used by Paul while referring to ]{.class_s321}[saved Gentiles ]{.class_s322}[(see Rom. 9:29). This is what caused Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, Dummelow, and others to stumble at the passage and made them to think that verse 10 and 11 (and 2:23) were past fulfillment. But as we have remarked before (see above), and will remark again, ]{.class_s321}[dual fulfillment]{.class_s322}[ is the common characteristic of Old Testament prophesy where it deals with ]{.class_s321}["the day of the Lord."]{.class_s323}

The funny books alter the passage we have just read to read as follows:

[1. ]{.class_s321}[NASV]{.class_s322}[: "When the Lord first spoke" for]{.class_s321}[ "THE]{.class_s323}[ ]{.class_s321}[BEGINNING OF []{#c6B.xhtml_page_29}THE WORD" ]{.class_s323}[(1:2). Notice this is the way that the Liberals and ]{.class_s321}[The Living Bible ]{.class_s322}[handled Genesis 1:1---"WHEN God began" for ]{.class_s321}["IN THE BEGINNING." ]{.class_s323}[In addition to this, the ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[has quietly torn out a whole phrase from verse 6, ]{.class_s321}["But I will utterly take them away." ]{.class_s323}[And, in keeping with their mania for impressing people with their knowledge of the original languages, the Lockman Foundation has mistranslated ]{.class_s321}["children of Judah and children of Israel"]{.class_s323}[ as "SONS," in spite of the fact that there isn't an orthodox Jewish scholar or Rabbi alive (or dead) who ever translated ]{.class_s321}["benay Yisrael" ]{.class_s322}[(Heb.) in that fashion.]{.class_s321}

[2. The ]{.class_s321}[ASV ]{.class_s322}[(1901) supports the Jehovah's Witnesses by removing the term ]{.class_s321}["LORD"]{.class_s323}[ from the entire passage and then by inserting "Jehovah" (vss. 1, 2, 4, 6, 9) into it. The serious student of the Bible who is conscientious in his studies knows, of course, that the word ]{.class_s321}["JEHOVAH"]{.class_s323}[ is not found in the ]{.class_s321}[ASV ]{.class_s322}[New Testament. This succeeds in severing the]{.class_s321}[ "LORD"]{.class_s323}[ of the Old Testament from the ]{.class_s321}["Lord" ]{.class_s323}[of the New Testament. Judge Rutherford and Pastor Russell couldn't have done it any better. And just to make sure you get the drift, the ]{.class_s321}[ASV ]{.class_s322}[(1901) has told you (in the margin of John 9:38) that Christ was not the "Creator" but a CREATURE. (And you understand that you are supposed to accept that godless piece of newsprint because Wilbur Smith, Culbertson, and other "godly" men were mercenary enough to recommend it!)]{.class_s321}

[3. The Dodd Version (]{.class_s321}[The New English Bible]{.class_s322}[)]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s322}[has raised up its holy hands in shocked horror at the word ]{.class_s321}["WHOREDOM" ]{.class_s323}[and substituted "take a wanton for a wife" (vs. 2) and "a worthless woman" (vs. 3). The word]{.class_s321}[ "Diblaim"]{.class_s322}[ ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 3) has been cut out of the text with Jehudi's penknife, and then Dodd puts the capstone on his private interpretations by reading that God will "NEVER AGAIN FORGIVE" Israel (vs. 6).]{.class_s321}[ This is the Postmillennial position exactly as the Lockman Foundation stated it in The Amplified Version, recommended at times by Theodore Epp. ]{.class_s322}[C. H. Dodd and his board of editors are LIARS. Hebrews 8:11 states that if any man (I don't give a flip how "godly" he is) says that God will never forgive Israel, he is a LIAR. In addition to lying and toning down the word of God, the ]{.class_s321}[NEB ]{.class_s322}[calmly removes verse 7 from []{#c6B.xhtml_page_30}your Bible and throws it out of the text with the note ]{.class_s321}[that it is written "in HEBREW." ]{.class_s322}[(Well, bless my soul, ol' buddy, I thought Hosea was written in Hebrew! You sure could'a fooled me!)]{.class_s321}

[4. The Weigle Version (]{.class_s321}[RSV]{.class_s322}[) repeats the boo boo of the ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[in verse 2 ("When the Lord, etc."), then it alters]{.class_s321}[ "whoredom"]{.class_s323}[ to "harlotry," then it translates ]{.class_s321}["benay" ]{.class_s322}[(Heb.) as "people" instead of ]{.class_s321}["children," ]{.class_s323}[and then it removes the Hebrew names of the children in verses 6 and 8.]{.class_s321}

[5. Our last caricature of the truth is called ]{.class_s321}[The Living Bible ]{.class_s322}[(the Kenneth Taylor Version). It knocks out ]{.class_s321}["THE WORD OF THE LORD" ]{.class_s323}[in verse 1, ]{.class_s321}["THE BEGINNING OF THE WORD OF THE LORD" ]{.class_s323}[in verse 2, and mistranslates about twenty five words in the next eight verses.]{.class_s321}

[But it is just as well; after all, ]{.class_s321}[The Living Bible ]{.class_s322}[is not a Bible at all so it has no business talking about ]{.class_s321}["THE WORD OF THE LORD" ]{.class_s323}[and the ]{.class_s321}["BEGINNING OF THE WORD OF THE LORD." ]{.class_s323}[The Living Bible is ]{.class_s322}[popular because:]{.class_s321}

a\. It is a Roman Catholic Bible (Matt. 16:18 and 1 Pet. 5:13).

b\. It is disloyal to the Reformation (James 5:16; Luke 2:33).

c\. It can get no one under conviction.

d\. It is popular with unsaved people who go right on rejecting Christ while they are reading it year in and year out.

[]{#c85.xhtml}

CHAPTER TWO

2:1 Say ye unto your brethren, Ammi; and to your sisters, Ruhamah.

2 Plead with your mother, plead: for she is not my wife, neither am I her husband: let her therefore put away her whoredoms out of her sight, and her adulteries from between her breasts;

3 Lest I strip her naked, and set her as in the day that she was born, and make her as a wilderness, and set her like a dry land, and slay her with thirst.

4 And I will not have mercy upon her children; for they be the children of whoredoms.

5 For their mother hath played the harlot: she that conceived them hath done shamefully: for she said, I will go after my lovers, that give me my bread and my water, my wool and my flax, mine oil and my drink.

[The last two verses of chapter 1 have been transposed to the first two verses of chapter 2 by the writers of ]{.class_s321}[The Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_s322}[on the grounds that they "fit the context." Having committed this abortion, the commentator tells us that Hosea 2:1 refers to "divine mercy now being received, etc." (Robinson, ]{.class_s321}[The Twelve Minor Prophets, ]{.class_s322}[follows this blind alley to a dead end). This is quite typical of the botched up Charlie Brown type of exegesis the Conservatives get into when they mess with the Authorized Text. There is about as much "mercy received" in verses 2--4 as you could find in the destruction of Sodom and Gomorrah. The thing that misled the destructive critics was the fact that the two names given here (]{.class_s321}["Ammi" ]{.class_s323}[and ]{.class_s321}["Ruhamah"]{.class_s323}[)]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s323}[mean "my people" and "pitied," the exact opposite of Hosea 1:6, 9.]{.class_s321}

[But the contrast is deceptive. Hosea 2:1 is Hosea himself telling the girl to []{#c85.xhtml_page_32}call her brother ]{.class_s321}["AMMI" ]{.class_s323}[("my people") and telling the boy to call his sisters "PITIED." The male members of Israel are addressed corporately as "BRETHREN" and the female members as ]{.class_s321}["SISTERS"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 1). Hosea commands them to call each other by the name they will have when they are restored. ]{.class_s321}[That they have not yet been restored ]{.class_s322}[is perfectly apparent by what follows: They are to address their mother as an unfaithful wife who needs to "straighten up and fly right"---]{.class_s321}["Let her therefore put away her whoredoms out of her sight, and her adulteries from between her breasts" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 2). There is no restored Israel yet in verse 1; and, therefore, nobody has any business at all tearing verses 10 and 11 out of the preceding chapter and sticking them on to chapter 2 to "make sense." "Good sense" for a Bible rejecting Postmillennialist is often nonsense to a Bible believing Christian.]{.class_s321}

"[Plead with your mother, plead" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 2). Here, Gomer is likened to Israel, and the expression ]{.class_s321}["SHE IS NOT MY WIFE" ]{.class_s323}[so clearly interprets Romans 7:14 that no one could mistake the gist of the passage but a Roman Catholic or a self righteous Fundamentalist. The woman who plays the ]{.class_s321}["harlot" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 5) is an ]{.class_s321}["adulteress" ]{.class_s323}[if she is "married" to another man other than her husband (Rom. 7:3), and that is telling anyone (but a Roman Catholic and a self righteous Fundamentalist) that ADULTERY or FORNICATION are grounds for divorce, since divorce has been committed by the offending party. Until God gives Israel a bill of divorcement on grounds of fornication (Isa. 23:37--45), she is an adulteress, exactly as the woman of Romans 7:14 who ]{.class_s321}[has no bill of divorce on any grounds.]{.class_s322}

[The terrific threats of verses 3--5 come to pass literally (see Hosea 4:14). And yet out of verse 3]{.class_s321}[---"Lest I strip her naked, and set her as in the day that she was born" ]{.class_s323}[comes the restoration of Israel (see 2:14--15 and comments). The events of Exodus and Numbers will most assuredly happen again (Ps. 78:1--6) for it is Israel's return to ]{.class_s321}["the days of her youth" ]{.class_s323}[(2:15) that purges out her rebels (Ezek. 36:24) and restores her marriage (2:20). But here the judgments against an adulteress are given (Cf. Ezek. 16 and 23). The "strange children" (vss. 5, 7) are ]{.class_s321}["children of whoredom" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 4) who []{#c85.xhtml_page_33}come from the unlawful integration of parties who have crossed the God appointed "boundaries" (Acts 17:26; Hosea 4:2). What the educators and politicians call "community involvement" and "getting involved" or "activism" or "sensitivity therapy" is often just plain International Socialism with some fornication and adultery thrown in to make it "interesting."]{.class_s321}

"[She that conceived them hath done shamefully" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 5). Israel went after her enemies thinking they were able to help her financially (see 5:13), and sometimes she adopted the religious customs of the heathen if the heathen helped her out (see 7:11). She went after her ]{.class_s321}["lovers"]{.class_s323}[ (cf. Ezek. 16:15--50) because of their material wealth: ]{.class_s321}["bread\... water\... wool\... flax\... oil\... drink" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 5). But since a ]{.class_s321}["man's life consisteth not in the abundance of the things which he possesseth" ]{.class_s323}[(Luke 12:15), Israel made the mistake that America made between 1930 and 1970. She tried to make friends with people who were God's enemies (1 Kings 22:1--4). Israel's prosperity was due to God's blessing (Deut. 28:12--13), exactly as America's prosperity was due to God's blessing, and where God ]{.class_s321}[withdraws ]{.class_s322}[His blessings because of the conduct described in Hosea 4:6, 12, 17; 6:9--11; 7:1, 4, 17, 16; 8:14; 9:7; 10:4, and 13:2, a nation has "had it."]{.class_s321}

[Every time a John Bircher gives the capitalistic system credit for American wealth, he helps Engels and Lenin slit our throats. Any time any International Socialist tells us that by changing the ]{.class_s321}[Constitution ]{.class_s322}[we can prosper, he is setting us up for destruction. No nation that prints "In God We Trust" on its coins is ever going to get by the judgments of Hosea 2:2--5 while preaching "sex revolutions," "gay liberation," "the new morality," and the ]{.class_s321}["NASV."]{.class_s322}[ It doesn't work that way. \[See comments under Prov. 14:34 in ]{.class_s321}[The Bible Believer's Commentary on Proverbs, ]{.class_s322}[1973.\]]{.class_s321}

[Israel has a promise of restoration that is not granted to any other nation (Dan. 7:13, 14; Isa. 59:21; Deut. 12:28; Gen. 13:15; Joel 3:20; Psa. 72:5--17; Jer. 31:33--37; and Deut. 29:29), but she will not be restored until she has been stripped naked (vs. 3) and set in a ]{.class_s321}["dry land" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 3) and slain with thirst (vs. 3). The terrible judgments of Treblinka, Buchenwald, Auschwitz, and Belsen Belsen []{#c85.xhtml_page_34}(1939--1945) were only the opening guns of a massacre that will reduce the Nation of Israel to less than half a million people.]{.class_s321}

6 Therefore, behold, I will hedge up thy way with thorns, and make a wall, that she shall not find her paths.

7 And she shall follow after her lovers, but she shall not overtake them; and she shall seek them, but shall not find them: then shall she say, I will go and return to my first husband; for then was it better with me than now.

8 For she did not know that I gave her corn, and wine, and oil, and multiplied her silver and gold, which they prepared for Baal.

9 Therefore will I return, and take away my corn in the time thereof, and my wine in the season thereof, and will recover my wool and my flax given to cover her nakedness.

10 And now will I discover her lewdness in the sight of her lovers, and none shall deliver her out of mine hand.

11 I will also cause all her mirth to cease, her feast days, her new moons, and her sabbaths, and all her solemn feasts.

12 And I will destroy her vines and her fig trees, whereof she hath said, These are my rewards that my lovers have given me: and I will make them a forest, and the beasts of the field shall eat them.

13 And I will visit upon her the days of Baalim, wherein she burned incense to them, and she decked herself with her earrings and her jewels, and she went after her lovers, and forgat me, saith the LORD.

[The general drift of the passage is clear. Israel has "stepped out" on her husband, and she is "shacking up" (making out, hootching, etc.---the world always has told it "like it wasn't") with her lovers. She is headed for []{#c85.xhtml_page_35}disappointment, dissolution, and bereavement. Eventually she will decide to go back to her FIRST husband, clearly showing (again) that an act of adultery (Rom. 7) creates a "marriage" (Rom. 7) where two husbands are involved (Rom. 7). Baal (vs. 8) is the chief false god mentioned (Judg. 10:6) and readers of ]{.class_s321}[The Two Babylons]{.class_s322}[ (Hislop) will recognize Satan in one of his disguises; whether Baal wore the name of ]{.class_s321}[Bel ]{.class_s322}[(in Babylon) or the name of ]{.class_s321}[Zeus ]{.class_s322}[(in Asia Minor) or ]{.class_s321}[Jove ]{.class_s322}[(in Greece) or ]{.class_s321}[Jupiter ]{.class_s322}[(in Rome) or ]{.class_s321}[Ra ]{.class_s322}[(in Egypt), the reference is always to a male deity represented by the fire in the sun or the fire in lightning. \[See comments under Exod. 32 and Exod. 20 in that Commentary.\]]{.class_s321}

"[I will hedge up thy way with thorns" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 6). What was normally a protective wall (Job 1:10; Isa. 5:5) becomes a narrow path (Num. 22:26) which prevents Israel from going in any direction but straight on into judgment. John 19:8 and Lamentations 3:7 picture the condition exactly. The wall on either side of Israel will be like a briar hedge (2 Sam. 23:7); so, the picture is that of a woman chasing a lover down a primrose lane and being unable to catch up with him; she then finds herself in a hedge maze encompassed with thorns. At this point she says to herself, ]{.class_s321}["I will go and return to my first husband; for then was it better with me than now" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 7).]{.class_s321}

[The gold, silver, iron, wine, and oil that God had given to Israel had been offered up to the Devil (see Acts 7:41 and comments), and instead of honoring the Lord with their ]{.class_s321}["firstfruits" ]{.class_s323}[(see Prov. 3:9 and comments), Israel had partaken of the cup of devils at the Devil's table and had sacrificed to devils (1 Cor. 10:20). The ]{.class_s321}[gold ]{.class_s322}[God gave America she sent overseas to bankrupt her own people. The ]{.class_s321}[silver ]{.class_s322}[God gave America was depleted in three wars she had no business getting into, and the ]{.class_s321}[corn and wheat ]{.class_s322}[went into Seagrams Seven and Miller's High Life.]{.class_s321}

[The ]{.class_s321}["wool and flax" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 9) given to cover the nakedness of Israel (vs. 9) did not cover her nakedness (Ezek. 23:18 and 16:36) anymore than it covered America's nakedness; the nude calendars, the magazine covers, the billboards, the theater ads, the "streakers," and the seminude fashions all broadcast America's spiritual and moral condition---it is that of a pagan jungle.]{.class_s321}

"[I will also cause all her mirth to cease, her feast days" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 11). Notice how the Lord takes aim at the religious observances of the apostate nation, ]{.class_s321}["her new moons, and her sabbaths, and all her solemn feasts" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 11). Of these feasts the LORD said, ]{.class_s321}["it is INIQUITY" ]{.class_s323}[(Isa. 1:13). God said that it "wore Him out" (Isa. 1:14) to accept the religious sacraments of a bunch of Sodomites (Isa. 1:10). ]{.class_s321}["The calling of assemblies" ]{.class_s323}[(Isa. 1:13) by a nation carrying on like the Israelites was nothing short of an abomination (see Prov. 6:16 and comments). Men always get ]{.class_s321}[religious ]{.class_s322}[when they are up to some devilment (1 Kings 21:9 and Matt. 26:2); and the more sacramental and formal they act, the more filthy and wicked they are (Matt. 23:27--28), according to the highest and foremost authority on the subject who ever gave an opinion about the matter (Matt. 23:33). Look out when the ]{.class_s321}["vestments"]{.class_s323}[ come out (2 Kings 10:22), the "sign of the cross" starts cutting the air (Ezek. 8:14), and the "monks" begin to monkey with beads, incense, candles, and "holy wafers"! Somebody is up to criminal activity as sure as you live and breathe; that's the bunch who murdered 2,000,000 Christians during the Dark Ages.]{.class_s321}

"[I will make them a forest, and the beasts of the field shall eat them" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 12). So it comes to pass, and the desolation of Israel is so great following the captivity (2 Kings 18) that LIONS come back on the land and prey on the population.]{.class_s321}

"[And I will visit upon her the days of Baalim"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 13) \[cf. Judg. 2:11, 13\]. Each section of the country worshipped a local Baal (note ]{.class_s321}["devils" ]{.class_s322}[and ]{.class_s321}["Devil" ]{.class_s322}[in the ]{.class_s321}[King James]{.class_s322}[ New Testament, and note the refusal of the modern translators to admit to a multiplicity of "devils," although Judas is said to be ]{.class_s321}["a devil"]{.class_s323}[ \[not the Devil\] in John 6:70--71).]{.class_s321}

[The name ]{.class_s321}[Baal ]{.class_s322}[(meaning "Master" or "Lord") was attached to any number of places. For example, Baalperazim, Baal zepon, Baal peor, Baal-berith, Baal-tamar, or Baal-shalisha, etc. Baal worship consisted of the worship of the sex functions of the animal nature. These "fertility rites" (call it "like it ain't"!) were so common among ALL pagan religions (3000--100 B.C.) that one is surprised to []{#c85.xhtml_page_37}see how any European or American could be deceived into thinking the "Roman Catholic Church was a "Christian" church. All of the ancient "mystery religions," long before the time of Christ, worshipped a mother and child; the child was the husband (or lover) of his mother, and the child was usually killed and went to the "underworld" to come up later. The "mother" was always connected with a queen or an entrance to heaven, and she was always symbolized by some type of the Holy Spirit. The "son" was always connected with some rite that purified from sin or "delivered men." All of the ancient religions had suggestions of the Trinity, and all of them professed to have a king who had a supernatural birth from the gods. That is, some of the "fundamentals" are well known to all pagans and have been for 3,000--4,000 years. The hats that the bishops wear in the Vatican are the official hats of the priests of Dagon (1100--900 B.C.), and they are found in Egypt and Philistia before Romulus and Remus found a she wolf dumb enough to raise them.]{.class_s321}

[Baal ]{.class_s322}[was the male deity; ]{.class_s321}[Ashtoreth ]{.class_s322}[was the female deity; and the "Baalim" were the little Baals who took care of the customers while Baal was occupied with other matters (1 Kings 18:27). Baal worshippers worship at high noon (1 Kings 18:29) on the SUNday, and they bake cakes to the ]{.class_s321}["queen of heaven" ]{.class_s323}[(Jer. 44:17) while they wear long robes (Mark 12:38) and call each other ]{.class_s321}["father" ]{.class_s323}[(Judg. 17:10). Ever met one?]{.class_s321}

Israel (vs. 13) has done what Jerusalem did in Ezekiel 16:33 and 34. She couldn't get enough customers with the pimps out drumming up trade so she got dolled up and went out on the street to do her own hawking (vs. 13).

14 Therefore, behold, I will allure her, and bring her into the wilderness, and speak comfortably unto her.

15 And I will give her her vineyards from thence, and the valley of Achor for a door of hope: and she shall sing there, as in the days of her youth, and as in the day when she came up out of the land of Egypt.

16 And it shall be at that day, saith the LORD, that thou shalt call me Ishi; and shalt call me no more Baali.

17 For I will take away the names of Baalim out of her mouth, and they shall no more be remembered by their name.

18 And in that day will I make a covenant for them with the beasts of the field, and with the fowls of heaven, and with the creeping things of the ground: and I will break the bow and the sword and the battle out of the earth, and will make them to lie down safely.

19 And I will betroth thee unto me for ever; yea, I will betroth thee unto me in righteousness, and in judgment, and in lovingkindness, and in mercies.

20 I will even betroth thee unto me in faithfulness: and thou shalt know the LORD.

21 And it shall come to pass in that day, I will hear, saith the LORD, I will hear the heavens, and they shall hear the earth;

22 And the earth shall hear the corn, and the wine, and the oil; and they shall hear Jezreel.

23 And I will sow her unto me in the earth; and I will have mercy upon her that had not obtained mercy; and I will say to them which were not my people, Thou art my people; and they shall say, Thou art my God.

[Now we come to one of the greatest passages in the entire Bible on the Second Coming of Christ, and because of this we shall save the critical exegesis until the end of our comments on it. (It would be a crying shame at this point to waste our time with the stupid comments of "the majority of recognized whatever they are" or the blubbering perversions of the ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[used by Bob Jones University and Pensacola Christian College.)]{.class_s321}

[1. Israel will be called out into the literal wilderness where she wandered in []{#c85.xhtml_page_39}the book of Numbers: ]{.class_s321}["I will alure her, and bring her into the WILDERNESS" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 14). This ]{.class_s321}["wilderness" ]{.class_s323}[is identified exactly with the identical same word ]{.class_s321}["WILDERNESS"]{.class_s323}[ (Rev. 12:14) in the New Testament so that nobody but an Amillennialist (Calvin, Cheyne, Marti, Berkhof, or Jerome) or a Roman Catholic (Jerome, Augustine, Pope Paul, et al.) could miss the interpretation. Psalm 78 tells us that the wilderness sojourning was a parable; Revelation 12:1--3 with Genesis 37:9 identifies the woman who "flees to the wilderness," and she is no more "the church" or "Mary" than your Uncle Tom's trotline.]{.class_s321}

2\. Israel will go through the events she went through between Exodus 15 and Numbers 23 (vs. 15), and this will "purge out the rebels" (Ezek. 20:38) before she reenters her homeland for the Millennium.

[3. At this time, Israel will REMARRY her husband (vs. 9) permanently and never again be removed from the land of Palestine (Amos 9:15). When this takes place, NATURE will be regenerated (vss. 18, 22) and the animals will get "saved" (Isa. 11:6--9)! (The term ]{.class_s321}["regeneration"]{.class_s323}[ only occurs twice in your Bible: once in relation to your salvation \[Titus 3:5\] and once in regard to the regeneration of the EARTH \[Matt. 19:28\] when Jesus Christ returns \[Rom. 8:21--22\].)]{.class_s321}

[4. At this time, there will be peace for 1,000 years (vs. 8), and nations will not ]{.class_s321}["learn war" ]{.class_s323}[(Isa. 2:4) anymore. Since there is no ]{.class_s321}["peace on earth" ]{.class_s323}[until there is ]{.class_s321}["GLORY TO GOD IN THE HIGHEST," ]{.class_s323}[this world is predestinated and doomed to continual bloodshed until Revelation 20:1, and nothing you or your religious or political constituents are doing (or will do) will ever change that forecast far enough to keep you out of the grave. "Bigger and better wars" is the forecast for the next thirty years. You will certainly have them; and nothing science, education, religion, philosophy, or economic systems are going to do (or have done) will alter that ]{.class_s321}[fact ]{.class_s322}[far enough to make a comment on it. Not even changing Luke 2:14 to "with whom He is pleased" (see the atrocious ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[recommended by Pensacola Christian College) will successfully cover up the truth: no glory to God, no peace on earth. Like, it, leaven it, love it, learn it, []{#c85.xhtml_page_40}lampoon it, leave it, or lament it, it still is solid history written 1,900 years ahead of time. No glory to God, no peace on earth. \[See exhaustive treatment in ]{.class_s321}[The Sure Word of Prophecy]{.class_s322}[.\]]{.class_s321}

"[Thou art my people; and they shall say, Thou art my God" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 23). The double application (Rom. 9:25) successfully unsaddles 95 percent of the commentators, and they say lamely that the promises of verses 14--23 are fulfilled completely "in the Christian church" (]{.class_s321}[New Bible Commentary,]{.class_s322}[ p. 707). The certainly are ]{.class_s321}[not, ]{.class_s322}[and the ]{.class_s321}[reputation]{.class_s322}[ of the scholar has no more bearing on his ]{.class_s321}[ability to interpret the verse ]{.class_s322}[than if he had never learned to read or write.]{.class_s321}

"[The valley of Achor"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 15) is said to ]{.class_s321}[be "for a door of hope," ]{.class_s323}[for the valley was known to everyone of Hebrew extraction from any one of the twelve tribes; it was fixed indelibly on their memory by the incident of Joshua 7:25--26. The Lord's reason for pulling it out of the hat here (when discussing the coming Millennium) is obvious, yet not one major commentator from Origen to ]{.class_s321}[The Wycliffe Bible Commentary ]{.class_s322}[could even guess in the right direction. \[If you will read the commentators (Clarke, Lange, Henry, Bullinger, Marti, Cheyne, Budde, Duhm, Pfieffer, Williams, Jamieson, Fausset, Brown, Ellicott, Durnmelow, and J. J. Given), you will find everything under God's sun from "that which was bad would become that which was good" to "only through trouble could Israel come back to fellowship."\]]{.class_s321}

[How did the commentators forget that JOSHUA (Josh. 7:25) and Hosea are the same name and that JOSHUA is a picture of JESUS entering the land at the Second Advent (Acts 7:45)? Since Joshua had attacked a cursed city, so Jesus will attack one (Rev. 17--18); and since the Tribulation lasts seven years, Joshua circled his city SEVEN days (Josh. 6). It is after that marvelous victory that Achan "troubles Israel," so the ]{.class_s321}["VALLEY OF ACHOR" ]{.class_s323}[in Hosea is mentioned to warn that nation that when they see the events of Joshua 6 and 7 shaping up again, they had better keep their hands off the Antichrist's property (Josh. 7:1, 13).]{.class_s321}

[Isaiah 54:5 and Jeremiah 3:12--14 speak of the times of Hosea 2:17--18. ]{.class_s321}["That thou shalt call me ISHI" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 16) means that Israel will refer to God []{#c85.xhtml_page_41}as a husband (Isa. 54:5), for the expression means "MY MAN," as the Germans have used the expression for ten centuries. "]{.class_s321}[And shalt call me no more BAALI" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 16) would mean that they would no longer call Him by the name of the pagan devil "Baal," because as ]{.class_s321}["FRIENDS"]{.class_s323}[ (John 15:15), they would no longer call Him "master" (Baal); He would be ]{.class_s321}[more ]{.class_s322}[than a master to them (Isa. 54:5).]{.class_s321}

The promised rain of verse 21 is found in 2 Samuel 23:4; Joel 2:23; 1 Kings 18:45; and Psalm 68:9, and it will occur just before the Feast of Tabernacles, dating the Second Advent. \[You read it right; never mind the Conservative and Fundamental scholars. They haven't been studying the Bible for seventy years; they've only been messing around with "reliable translations" to reinforce what any Christian knew in 1910.\]

[And now before proceeding to chapter 3 we must waste a little ink and paper (may God forgive us!) to examine the funny books as they seek to replace the despised ]{.class_s321}[King James 1611 Authorized Holy Bible.]{.class_s322}

[1. ]{.class_s321}[The Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_s322}[writes four pages of comments on the passage with sixty lines to the page and never once lets the truth get through. The writer has written over 3,000 words on the passage without even one hint that any of it will be fulfilled ]{.class_s321}[literally.]{.class_s322}

2\. Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown tell us that the wilderness of verse 14 is not literal (p. 768), the wilderness sojourn is not literal (p. 768), and that Jerome's hilarious theory that "bringing into the wilderness" means "a deliverance from her enemies" is the truth. It is if you're an atheist.

[3. The clumsy (and hypocritical) ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[(any issue) has "festal assemblies" in verse 11 for the clearer ]{.class_s321}["solemn feasts"]{.class_s323}[;]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s323}[and after all the stink about the ]{.class_s321}[AV ]{.class_s322}[not translating "gender, number and tense" correctly, the ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[translators (the Lockman Foundation) simply ignore the SINGULAR number of ]{.class_s321}[every Hebrew word in the passage. ]{.class_s322}[You will also notice that in their eagerness to get in good with the religious liberals, the ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[has broken the meter of Hosea into poetry in chapter 2 so that you would not take it to be ]{.class_s321}[literal prophecy. ]{.class_s322}[Observe further that both the ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[and the ]{.class_s321}[RSV ]{.class_s322}[of the []{#c85.xhtml_page_42}National Council of Churches have included Hosea 2:14 in the ]{.class_s321}[poetic part of the discourse ]{.class_s322}[so that you would be inclined to believe that Hosea 2:14 has nothing to do with Hosea's ]{.class_s321}[real wife. ]{.class_s322}[Typical ecumenical sabotage carried on by "Fundamentalists" who belong to the Alexandrian Cult.]{.class_s321}

[4. The Dodd Version (]{.class_s321}[The New English Bible]{.class_s322}[)]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s322}[busts the meter up to match the ]{.class_s321}[RSV ]{.class_s322}[and ]{.class_s321}[NASV]{.class_s322}[, and then it calls God a liar to His face in verse 2 by saying "is she NOT my wife and I her husband." And then, to reinforce the private interpretation of the commentator before his time, Dodd has written that "the Valley of Trouble" (vs. 15) will be turned into the "gate of Hope." And then just to make sure that he violates Proverbs 30:6 (with the Lockman Foundation), the translator has added eight words to verse 17 that are found in no Hebrew manuscripts known to man.]{.class_s321}

[5. ]{.class_s321}[The "Living" Bible ]{.class_s322}[(Kenneth Taylor version) adds about fifty words to the text, changes 150 words, and then adopts Dodd's nineteenth century theory about verse 15. [6. Between Budde, Duhm, Durnmelow, Guthe, Nowack, and Marti we are to make forty five changes in the text, and then we are not to believe it ]{.class_s321}[literally. ]{.class_s322}[The ]{.class_s321}[ASV ]{.class_s322}[follows the ]{.class_s321}[RSV ]{.class_s322}[text on verse 15 ("answer" for ]{.class_s321}["sing"]{.class_s323}[) and verse 22 ("answer" for ]{.class_s321}["hear"]{.class_s323}[), but this is to be expected as the ]{.class_s321}[RSV ]{.class_s322}[is foreshadowed in the ]{.class_s321}[ASV ]{.class_s322}[(1901), since the translators of both versions rejected the Protestant text of the Reformation.]{.class_s321}

[Now we hate to keep harping on these matters, and (believe it or not) we have never relished the task of "nailing" these birds down and showing their scholarship for what it is (jello), but most readers have no idea about the extent of the damage that has been done to the body of Christ---and to young ministers in particular---by these egotistical "Fundamentalists" who continually mislead people into thinking that because a man is a "godly saint" (who believes in the "verbal inspiration of the originals") he could not possibly be as crooked as a dog's hind leg ]{.class_s321}[when it comes to matters of AUTHORITY. ]{.class_s322}[Well, he can be, and most of them are; so, we shall not fail to point out their blatant infidelity []{#c85.xhtml_page_43}wherever it occurs.]{.class_s321}

CHAPTER THREE

3:1 Then said the LORD unto me, Go yet, love a woman beloved of her friend, yet an adulteress, according to the love of the LORD toward the children of Israel, who look to other gods, and love flagons of wine.

2 So I bought her to me for fifteen pieces of silver, and for an homer of barley, and an half homer of barley:

3 And I said unto her, Thou shalt abide for me many days; thou shalt not play the harlot, and thou shalt not be for another man: so will I also be for thee.

4 For the children of Israel shall abide many days without a king, and without a prince, and without a sacrifice, and without an image, and without an ephod, and without teraphim:

5 Afterward shall the children of Israel return, and seek the LORD their God, and David their king; and shall fear the LORD and his goodness in the latter days

[Our scholarly friends are in trouble again. Their prudish morality which allows them to call God a liar (and blaspheme the greatest Book on earth) does not allow them to believe that the "woman beloved of her friend" (3:1) could be a ]{.class_s321}[different ]{.class_s322}[woman than the woman of Hosea 1:3. [J. B. Hindley and G. Campbell Morgan (with Dummelow, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown) adopt the ageold tradition that Gomer had somehow gotten sold into slavery and Hosea was buying her back. Campbell Morgan and anyone like him.]{.class_s321}

[Gomer has gone with the wind. Since the end of this type of exposition is mischievous madness we shall simply discard 100 percent of the Conservative scholars at this point and give our undivided attention to the words of the Holy Spirit.

"[Go yet, love a woman beloved of her friend, yet an adulteress" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 1). Here the LORD presents himself as a FRIEND of the adulteress woman. Note that this is ]{.class_s321}["according to THE LOVE OF THE LORD toward the children of Israel" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 1). [Hosea's second wife (see Deut. 24:1--6 for God's court procedure and legal jurisprudence) does not picture Israel as being put aside in 1 Kings 17--18 until the restoration under Nehemiah and Ezra;]{.class_s321}[ it pictures the displacing of Israel in Acts 7, 18, and 28 until the beginning of Daniel's Seventieth Week. ]{.class_s322}[The two women illustrate ]{.class_s321}[two ]{.class_s322}["puttings aside," and nothing but the obstinate fury of the Fundamental and Conservative scholars could have led them to butcher the text the way they did. The price of Hosea's concubine (see Judges 19:1--2 for the correct interpretation) is found in Exodus 21:32 and 2 Kings 7:1--18; thirty shekels is a fair approximation (cf. Isa. 20:1--3 and Num. 23:9).

[Now when the Alexandrian Cult perverted the text and made a disobedient wife and husband out of Hosea and his purchased wife (see above), they did it by the famous HPD method (higgledy piggledy derangement); they applied ]{.class_s321}["THOU SHALT ABIDE FOR ME MANY DAYS" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 3) to ]{.class_s321}["THE CHILDREN OF ISRAEL SHALL ABIDE MANY DAYS" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 4). Israel is NOT a faithful concubine in this age, nor was she between 1 Kings 17 and Malachi, nor is she waiting for anyone, nor did she abide for her husband. To the contrary, she has been for ]{.class_s321}["another man" ]{.class_s323}[(in the sense of spiritual adultery) for more than twenty five centuries. The statement was that ]{.class_s321}[she SHOULD obey her []{#cA5.xhtml_page_47}husband]{.class_s322}[ (vs. 3) and stay by him and not step out on him---]{.class_s321}[which she did---]{.class_s322}[exactly as Gomer did (read Deut. 28:64--66). [The ]{.class_s321}["afterward" ]{.class_s323}[of verse 5 matches the ]{.class_s321}["AFTER THOSE DAYS" ]{.class_s323}[of Hebrews 8:10--12, which see. Aren't we poor stupid Christians fortunate to have a God inspired, God preserved, God honored Bible by which we can correct the foremost []{#cA5.xhtml_page_48}Biblical authorities of our age? Or any age for that matter?

"[For the children of Israel shall abide many days without" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 4).]{.class_s321}

[1. A KING. God took His seal of approval off of the house of Judah when He impeached Jeconiah (Jer. 22:24). With the sceptre "not departing from Judah" (Gen. 49:10) till ]{.class_s321}["Shiloh come"]{.class_s323}[ (Gen. 49:10), there is no way on God's earth Israel can have a king until one shows up who is virgin born (Jer. 22:30)! [2. A PRINCE. You see, David IS the prince in the Millennium (Ezek. 44:3) under the King (Ezek. 48:35). With neither prince nor king for 2,575 years, the term ]{.class_s321}["many days" ]{.class_s323}[is a masterpiece of understatement. [3. A SACRIFICE. And so it comes to pass. Every orthodox Jew has to break his own God given law monthly, or at least yearly. Anyone who ever read Leviticus and Deuteronomy knows that this is an impossibility.
