This is the Bible recommended by Billy Graham and the Southern Baptists. It is no more a "bible" than Uncle Sam's Form 1040.

"[Set the trumpet to thy mouth" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 1) as in 5:8. The ]{.class_s321}[eagle]{.class_s322}[ is coming (Jer. 4:13; Hab. 1:6--8). He comes on horses the first time, but he literally FLIES the second time (Rev. 12:4)! ]{.class_s321}["Against the house of the Lord" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 1). Then, the prophecy reaches beyond the captivity of Israel to the judgments which are fulfilled against Judah in Jeremiah 52.]{.class_s321}

"[Israel shall cry unto me, My God, we know thee"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 2). This plainly puts the reference forward to a "second time" (see above) because Israel does NOT know God until Hebrews 8:8--12 at the Second Advent. The word ]{.class_s321}["Israel"]{.class_s323}[ has been removed from the verse by the apostate LXX and from many of the Hebrew manuscripts used by Kennicott and DeRossi to correct the Massoretic text of Jacob Ben Chayim. Since Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown cannot ]{.class_s321}[believe ]{.class_s322}[the text, they ]{.class_s321}[spiritualize ]{.class_s322}[it (with Origen and Augustine) and say that it applies to individual Israelites in the body of Christ---which, of course, it does NOT.]{.class_s321}

Verse 3 is self explanatory (cf. 6:6 and 7:16 with comments).

"[They have set up kings, but NOT BY ME\...I knew it not" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 4). The sarcasm is evident and the use of the expression ]{.class_s321}["I knew it not,"]{.class_s323}[ in view of Hosea 7:2, shows that the expression is to be taken exactly like Amos 3:2. Deuteronomy 7:6 shows that when God "knew" the nation it was in the sense of close, personal and intimate fellowship \[cf. Matt. 7:23 for ]{.class_s321}["I NEVER KNEW YOU"]{.class_s323}[ and Galatians 4:9 for ]{.class_s321}["are KNOWN OF GOD"]{.class_s323}[\]. God knows everything (1 Chron. 28:9) and everybody (Psa. 139; Matt. 12:36), but ]{.class_s321}["I KNEW IT NOT" ]{.class_s323}[implies, "You did it without consulting Me" (5:4 and 7:7). Zimri, Omri, Shallum, Menahem, and Pekah (1 Kings 17; 2 Kings 17) were man-chosen kings who obtained the throne by literal assassinations and political ambushes. The scepter was not to depart from JUDAH (Gen. 49); therefore, the entire line of []{#cMW.xhtml_page_107}Israelish kings from Ahab to Pekah was really spurious (Deut. 17:15). ]{.class_s321}["THEY have set up kings" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 4).]{.class_s321}

Verse 4 is self explanatory. God will cut off any nation that worships idols (Exod. 20:1--4), and He promises retribution to their great grandchildren (Exod. 20:5) \[cf. 2 Kings 15:13 and 1 Kings 13\]. The main reference is to the two golden calves of Jeroboam; so, we continue to read:

5 Thy calf, O Samaria, hath cast thee off; mine anger is kindled against them: how long will it be ere they attain to innocency?

6 For from Israel was it also: the workman made it; therefore it is not God: but the calf of Samaria shall be broken in pieces.

7 For they have sown the wind, and they shall reap the whirlwind: it hath no stalk: the bud shall yield no meal: if so be it yield, the strangers shall swallow it up.

8 Israel is swallowed up: now shall they be among the Gentiles as a vessel wherein is no pleasure.

9 For they are gone up to Assyria, a wild ass alone by himself: Ephraim hath hired lovers.

10 Yea, though they have hired among the nations, now will I gather them, and they shall sorrow a little for the burden of the king of princes.

11 Because Ephraim hath made many altars to sin, altars shall be unto him to sin.

12 I have written to him the great things of my law, but they were counted as a strange thing.

13 They sacrifice flesh for the sacrifices of mine offerings, and eat it; but the LORD accepteth them not; now will he remember their iniquity, and visit their sins: they shall return to Egypt.

14 For Israel hath forgotten his Maker, and buildeth temples; and Judah hath multiplied fenced cities: but I will send a fire upon his cities, and it shall devour the palaces thereof.

[The ]{.class_s321}["calf of Samaria" ]{.class_s323}[is in Bethel (1 Kings 12:28--29), and it casts off the nation of Israel in the sense that Israel leaned on it and trusted that it would help them (Isa. 31:1; 1 Sam. 4:3). The golden calf simply gives them the "brush off." The first time it showed up (see Exod. 32 and comments), it caused the death of 3,000 Israelites; this time it finishes off more than ten times that number. "[For they have sown the wind, and they shall reap the whirlwind" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 7). However, the final reference is to the literal whirlwind of Ezekiel 1:4 at the Second Advent (see Job 38:1 and comments) mentioned in Isaiah 40:24, 66:15; Nahum []{#cMW.xhtml_page_110}1:3; and Jeremiah 23:19. Jeremiah calls Israel a ]{.class_s321}["wild ass"]{.class_s323}[ in Jeremiah 2:24, and you will notice the context is Israel's running to Egypt and Assyria to find "lovers" (Jer. 2:14, 18). The ]{.class_s321}["wild ass" ]{.class_s323}[is a picture of the unregenerate sinner (see comments on Exod. 13:13 and Matt. 21:5--8). Instead of finding]{.class_s321}[ "lovers,"]{.class_s323}[ Ephraim will wind up alone on the mountain ranges with no friends at all (vs. 9). ]{.class_s321}["Yea, though they have hired among the nations, now will I gather them" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 10), in spite of hired mercenaries to do their fighting and alliances with the enemies of Judah to preserve their own nation (see Isa. 7:1 6 and 2 Kings 16:5--15). ]{.class_s321}["And they shall sorrow a little for the burden of the king of princes" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 10).]{.class_s321}

1\. Israel is going to be sorry for trying to hire friends (vs. 10). (She will go through what America has gone through since World War II). Christians don't hire "friends" from people who have no use for God or the word of God.

[3. ]{.class_s321}["The burden of the king of princes" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 10) can be the tax revenue placed on Israel by Assyria (Isa. 10:5--13), but there is no way you can stop the interpretation there when the expression ]{.class_s321}["KING OF PRINCES" ]{.class_s323}[shows up. []{#cMW.xhtml_page_112}3:19; Isa. 43:5; etc.)]{.class_s321}

Robertson, Machen, Warfield, Wuest, Zodhiates, Nowack, Keil, Delitzsch, Kuiper, Marti, Salmonn, Calvin, Berkhof, Thierne, Dabney, Rendall, Hodge, Jerome, Augustine, Ewald, Cheyne, Olson, Linton, Wedge, Muntz, Coder, Haggai, and the majority of destructive critics---Conservative or Evangelical.

[The Liberals said that the Law taught immorality (Num. 31:17) and cruelty (Num. 25:4). English, McLain, Wilbur Smith, Culbertson) covered up for the Devil by calling Satan a "CROCODILE" (see Job 41:1 and comments in that Commentary) and then called the Lord's army (Joel 2:11) the Devil's army, proceeding to alter the ]{.class_s321}[AV ]{.class_s322}[text in 500 places while publishing it as the "Authorized Version."]{.class_s321}

"[Good, godly, dedicated" something or others.]{.class_s321}

Remind us not to fool with them.

"[They sacrifice flesh for sacrifices of mine offerings\...but the LORD accepteth them not" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 13). Isaiah 1:11--13 and Proverbs 1:26 explain the text along with Jeremiah 14:10--11. They go through the routine of the sacrament, but they are as detached from the spirit of it as is a Catholic priest thinking about a game of golf in the middle of a high mass. ]{.class_s321}["Visit their sins" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 13) is the way of saying that God is going to call on a man (or a nation) to pay back something He owes them (cf. Isa. 10:3; Jer. 50:27; and Micah 7:4).]{.class_s321}

"[They shall return to Egypt" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 13). Then all of the dispersed Israelites do NOT go into Assyria and Syria; some of them go to Egypt before the dispersion of Jeremiah 42--43 (see Hosea 9:6 and Deut. 28:68).]{.class_s321}

"[For Israel hath forgotten his Maker, AND BUILDETH TEMPLES"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 14). Wow! You can lose God in a building program! When Peter gets confused (Luke 9:33), all he can think of is "putting on some additions to the plant" (Matt. 17:4). Imagine forgetting God while you are engaged in building a "house of God" that is supposedly dedicated to HIM! But it can happen, you know. I mean, what with the trustees unwilling to borrow the extra \$8,000 for the parking lot and two of their wives arguing over the color of the carpet and three deacons fighting against two other deacons about the cost of the steeple and the painters and plumbers showing up drunk and the electrician held up three weeks because of the "code" and two men suing you for falling off a scaffolding and the contractor leaving town with the money paid in advance for "siding," it can get "kinda thick," if you know what we mean!]{.class_s321}

[Israel, like Judah, was building little "temples" all around the country (Jer. 7:4) when God had given a pattern for ONE temple (1 Chron. 28:12). Calling the hutches]{.class_s321}[ "temples" ]{.class_s323}[(Jer. 7:4) or "houses of God" didn't make them that way (see Amos 7:13 and comments). When God writes "Ichabod" over a church building, it is no longer God's house, but ]{.class_s321}["A DEN OF THIEVES"]{.class_s323}[ (Matt. 21:13). When Stephen held his revival meeting in the Sanhedrin (Acts 7:48), he drove home Hosea 8:14 with such vim, vigor, and vitality that the congregation []{#cMW.xhtml_page_114}voted to beat his brains out (Acts 7:54).]{.class_s321}

[The remainder of verse 14 is clear. Judah and Israel are both included in judgment. It is literal fire (2 Chron. 36:19, Jer. 39:8) which burns the palaces and the ]{.class_s321}["temples."]{.class_s323}

We shall not take a lot of time with the "parade of the wooden soldiers" (Rev. Cimino's name for the new "versions") as we have seen enough now, in eight chapters of Hosea, to know what we are dealing with---money changers. Bruce or Briggs, for after all, linguistic ability with "grammar" is NOT "Biblical scholarship" in anyone's dictionary except the dictionary of a dead orthodox apostate.

[2. Rosenmuller says "the calf cast THEM off"(vs. 5). The LXX has "cast off thy calf." Keil and Wunsche have "thy calf disgusts." So, we had better be careful not to take "Christian scholarship" seriously. If we actually considered these opinions---without partiality, you understand!---we would have this novel reading for a "bible":

"[I, he, them, it hath stunk a spurned calf that is disgusting, which hath cast, or was cast, or is casting thee, them, or it, off, that I or he rejected."]{.class_s321}

CHAPTER NINE

9:1 Rejoice not, O Israel, for joy, as other people: for thou hast gone a whoring from thy God, thou hast loved a reward upon every cornfloor.

2 The floor and the winepress shall not feed them, and the new wine shall fail in her.

3 They shall not dwell in the LORD'S land; but Ephraim shall return to Egypt, and they shall eat unclean things in Assyria.

4 They shall not offer wine offerings to the LORD, neither shall they be pleasing unto him: their sacrifices shall be unto them as the bread of mourners; all that eat thereof shall be polluted: for their bread for their soul shall not come into the house of the LORD.

5 What will ye do in the solemn day, and in the day of the feast of the LORD?

6 For, lo, they are gone because of destruction: Egypt shall gather them up, Memphis shall bury them: the pleasant places for their silver, nettles shall possess them: thorns shall be in their tabernacles.

7 The days of visitation are come, the days of recompence are come; Israel shall know it: the prophet is a fool, the spiritual man is mad, for the multitude of thine iniquity, and the great hatred.

8 The watchman of Ephraim was with my God: but the prophet is a snare of a fowler in all his ways, and hatred in the house of his God.

[The first verse explains the so called "mistake" in Isaiah 9:3 which is also clarified in the 1611 text at Isaiah 10:22 and 26:15. The "increased nation" (see []{#cRF.xhtml_page_116}Isa. 9:3 and 26:15) is increased BEFORE the middle of Daniel's seventieth week; so thus, when it is ]{.class_s321}["multiplied" ]{.class_s323}[(Isa. 9:3), it has no cause to "JOY" (see correct Reformation Hebrew text in the ]{.class_s321}[King James Bible]{.class_s322}[, Isa. 9:3) until the Messiah lands on the ground (see Isa. 26:16, 17, 18 and 19). Careful readers of the Bible, who have better sense than to take "bibles" like the ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[seriously, observe that the tense of Isaiah 9:3 is a ]{.class_s321}[present perfect ]{.class_s322}[(completed action) ]{.class_s321}[tense ]{.class_s322}[to the colon and a ]{.class_s321}[present tense]{.class_s322}[ after the colon. Since the Westcott and Hort Cult have not regarded the colons in a ]{.class_s321}[King James]{.class_s322}[ anyway, you need not expect an intelligent comment from any of them. The ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[reads exactly as every liberal version (]{.class_s321}[NEB, RSV,]{.class_s322}[ etc.) in the world reads: it removes the word "NOT" from the correct text in Isaiah 9:3. (The "Cult" takes in Fundamentalists along with infidels.)]{.class_s321}

Verses 1--2 are self explanatory. Verse 3 is clear as it stands. The three places listed are "the Lord's land" (Palestine), Assyria, and Egypt. (Note in Isa. 19:24--25, the Millennial promise concerning these three areas.)

"[Their sacrifices shall be unto them as the bread of mourners\...their bread for their soul shall not come into the house of the LORD" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 4). The ]{.class_s321}["bread of mourners" ]{.class_s323}[was polluted bread (see text) if it was eaten in the house of the dead man, and Israel is deader than a hammer at this point. None of this bread could be offered in ]{.class_s321}["the house of the LORD" ]{.class_s323}[(see text) because it was polluted (Mal. 1:7) and would not be accepted. The word ]{.class_s321}["soul"]{.class_s323}[ here is used in the sense of Leviticus 22:11 (see comments under Genesis 2:7 in that Commentary.)]{.class_s321}

"[What will ye do" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 5). Bullinger, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, and the Wycliffe commentator "pass" on the verse and let their partners do the bidding. ]{.class_s321}["The feast of the Lord" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 5) ]{.class_s321}[is "chag" ]{.class_s322}[(Hebrew), in distinction from ]{.class_s321}[lechern, moag, moed, chagag ]{.class_s322}[and ]{.class_s321}[moadah; ]{.class_s322}[and it indicates a festival where dancing goes on. Since this is not the nature of the Passover Feast (although that is said to be "a feast unto the LORD" and a "solemn day"), we are shut up to the Feast of ]{.class_s321}[Pentecost ]{.class_s322}[(firstfruits, Lev. 23:17) or the Feast of ]{.class_s321}[Tabernacles ]{.class_s322}[(Lev. 23:34); but there is not much to choose between these for []{#cRF.xhtml_page_117}the Feast of Tabernacles is accompanied by the Second Advent (Matt. 17:4) and by the blowing of trumpets mentioned in Hosea 8:1. Further, the ]{.class_s321}["solemn DAY" ]{.class_s323}[is the Day of Atonement (Lev. 23:27) which also occurs in the same month. How will the Jew observe the Feast of Tabernacles? He won't; at least he cannot according to his own law. At this time his males are to appear in JERUSALEM (Deut. 16:16). Since Jeroboam had set up the calves, the males in ]{.class_s321}[Israel ]{.class_s322}[had not even been going down to Jerusalem ]{.class_s321}[before ]{.class_s322}[the captivity.]{.class_s321}

[Verse 6 is clear as it stands. The dead bodies are gathered in Africa and buried in Africa near the corrupt school of Alexandria, where the high priest of the Alexandrian Cult (Origen) corrupted the corrupt manuscripts recommended by A. T. Robertson, William Culbertson, and J. G. Machen. In this corrupt hellhole, the corrupt papyrus was found alongside the corrupt uncial manuscripts which God left there when He called ]{.class_s321}["his children" ]{.class_s323}[(Exod. 4:23) and His SON (Matt. 2:15) out of Africa.]{.class_s321}

"[The prophet is a fool, the spiritual man is mad, for the multitude of thine iniquity, and the great hatred" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 7). The great hatred which the rulers had for the word of God and the ]{.class_s321}["multitude" ]{.class_s323}[of their iniquities (vs. 7) caused them to hate God's prophets (see Matt. 23:34) and to vilify them as madmen (Matt. 23:31). "[The watchman of Ephraim WAS with my God: BUT"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 8). The watchmen, like Ezekiel (Ezek. 33:2--4), were []{#cRF.xhtml_page_118}faithful men who warned about coming judgment as faithfully as any German Shepherd heralded the approach of trespassers (Ezek. 32:7. These faithful watchdogs had degenerated into ]{.class_s321}[D.D.'s ]{.class_s322}[(Isa. 56:10, "dumb dogs") and they could no longer ]{.class_s321}[bark. ]{.class_s322}[They had become the ecumenical socialists of the "one world" movement to get along with the Devil (see Jer. 38:4). They were, therefore, engaged in "civil rights" struggles to give "rights" and "liberation" to immoral queers and religious freaks. They (as the Benjamites) were ready to fight and kill to defend their right to loaf, complain, live in sin, and violate the moral precepts of God.]{.class_s321}

9 They have deeply corrupted themselves, as in the days of Gibeah: therefore he will remember their iniquity, he will visit their sins.

10 I found Israel like grapes in the wilderness; I saw your fathers as the firstripe in the fig tree at her first time: but they went to Baalpeor, and separated themselves unto that shame; and their abominations were according as they loved.

11 As for Ephraim, their glory shall fly away like a bird, from the birth, and from the womb, and from the conception.

12 Though they bring up their children, yet will I bereave them, that there shall not be a man left: yea, woe also to them when I depart from them!

13 Ephraim, as I saw Tyrus, is planted in a pleasant place: but Ephraim shall bring forth his children to the murderer.

14 Give them, O LORD: what wilt thou give? give them a miscarrying womb and dry breasts.

15 All their wickedness is in Gilgal: for there I hated them: for the wickedness of their doings I will drive them out of mine house, I will love them no more: all their princes are []{#cRF.xhtml_page_119}revolters.

16 Ephraim is smitten, their root is dried up, they shall bear no fruit: yea, though they bring forth, yet will I slay even the beloved fruit of their womb.

17 My God will cast them away, because they did not hearken unto him: and they shall be wanderers among the nations.

[God reviews the Old Testament history of Exodus through Joshua. Israel ate grapes in the ]{.class_s321}[wilderness ]{.class_s322}[(Deut. 32:14) despite the opinion of 100 percent of the you know whats. The "first ripe fig tree" is also a symbol of the nation of Israel (vs. 10). Baalpeor is a reference to the religious immorality of Psalm 106:28 and Numbers 25:3. Since profaneness came to Israel and Judah from the ]{.class_s321}[prophets ]{.class_s322}[(Jer. 23:9--12), we may surmise that Baal worship never ceased entirely from Israel. As long as Hamitic women (1 Kings 3:1) intermarried with the Hebrews (Neh. 13:26) the immoral rites of incest and beastiality stayed with the nation (Lev. 20:13--16). Deuteronomy 32:10 pictures God's delight when He found a nation that would refresh Him and bear fruit for Him. The sour grapes (Hos. 4:11) and rotten figs (Jer. 24) show up later.]{.class_s321}

[The glory of Ephraim (vs. 11) will ]{.class_s321}["fly away like a bird, from the BIRTH, and from the WOMB, and from the CONCEPTION" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 11). That is, the glory will depart (1 Sam. 4:22), and it is leaving Israel first from childbearing (birth), then from pregnancy (the womb), then from conception (text). The order for the Israelites is no more births, then only stillbirths or abortions, then ]{.class_s321}[sterility. ]{.class_s322}[This is also the order for any church (Liberal or Conservative) that produces leaves instead of fruit (Matt. 21:19; Rev. 3:17). "[All their wickedness is in Gilgal: for there I hated them"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 15). God didn't just hate their ]{.class_s321}[deeds: ]{.class_s322}[He hated THEM (see John 3:36). [The altars are all over Gilgal (12:11), the very place where the Passover and circumcision were observed by Israel on their first entrance into the Promised Land; the place where the Angel of the Lord appeared to Joshua, as the captain of the LORD's host (Josh. 5:10, 13). But the reference in verse 15 is primarily a reference to 1 Samuel 11:15 and to 1 Samuel 13:8, 12. It was at Gilgal that the first king "of the people, by the people and for the people" was chosen; he was chosen because Israel wanted to conform to the military structures of the heathen (1 Sam. 8:5, 20). Furthermore, Gilgal is the place where the "king" (see Amos 5:5) ]{.class_s321}[intruded into the priest's office. ]{.class_s322}[And it was at Gilgal (1 Sam. 15:12--21) that Saul committed the sin which cost him his life, after losing the crown to David. (Amos 5:5 pronounces the same judgments on this place.)]{.class_s321}

Israel is a dead fig tree and an ]{.class_s321}["EMPTY vine"]{.class_s323}[ (10:1) that ]{.class_s321}["bringeth forth fruit UNTO HIMSELF" ]{.class_s323}[(10:1). Verse 17 is fulfilled literally from 1 Kings 17 to Ezra (and Nehemiah), and, then, is fulfilled again from A.D. 70 to A.D. 1948.]{.class_s321}

10:1 Israel is an empty vine, he bringeth forth fruit unto himself: according to the multitude of his fruit he hath increased the altars; according to the goodness of his land they have made goodly images.

2 Their heart is divided; now shall they be found faulty: he shall break down their altars, he shall spoil their images.

3 For now they shall say, We have no king, because we feared not the LORD; what then should a king do to us?

4 They have spoken words, swearing falsely in making a covenant: thus judgment springeth up as hemlock in the furrows of the field.

5 The inhabitants of Samaria shall fear because of the calves of Bethaven: for the people thereof shall mourn over it, and the priests thereof that rejoiced on it, for the glory thereof, because it is departed from it.

6 It shall be also carried unto Assyria for a present to king Jareb: Ephraim shall receive shame, and Israel shall be ashamed of his own counsel.

7 As for Samaria, her king is cut off as the foam upon the water.

8 The high places also of Aven, the sin of Israel, shall be destroyed: the thorn and the thistle shall come up on their altars; and they shall say to the mountains, Cover us; and to the hills, Fall on us.

9 O Israel, thou hast sinned from the days of Gibeah: there they stood: the battle in Gibeah against the children of iniquity did not overtake them.

10 It is in my desire that I should chastise them; and the people shall be gathered against them, when they shall bind themselves in their two furrows.

11 And Ephraim is as an heifer that is taught, and loveth to tread out the corn; but I passed over upon her fair neck: I will make Ephraim to ride; Judah shall plow, and Jacob shall break his clods.

12 Sow to yourselves in righteousness, reap in mercy; break up your fallow ground: for it is time to seek the LORD, []{#cTC.xhtml_page_131}till he come and rain righteousness upon you.

13 Ye have plowed wickedness, ye have reaped iniquity; ye have eaten the fruit of lies: because thou didst trust in thy way, in the multitude of thy mighty men.

14 Therefore shall a tumult arise among thy people, and all thy fortresses shall be spoiled, as Shalman spoiled Betharbel in the day of battle: the mother was dashed in pieces upon her children.

15 So shall Bethel do unto you because of your great wickedness: in a morning shall the king of Israel utterly be cut off.

"[Gibeah" ]{.class_s323}[is mentioned twice in verse 9 (see Judg. 19). ]{.class_s321}["There they stood" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 9) and successfully whipped ten tribes two times in a row (Judg. 20); furthermore, ]{.class_s321}["the battle in Gibeah against the children of iniquity did not overtake them" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 9)---the children of iniquity: ]{.class_s321}[they ]{.class_s322}[never were completely eliminated (see Judg. 1:21). "[When they shall bind themselves in their two furrows" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. []{#cTC.xhtml_page_132}10). [Ephraim ]{.class_s321}["is as an heifer THAT IS TAUGHT" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 11). "[But I passed over upon her fair neck" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 11). "[As Shalman spoiled Beth arbel in the day of battle" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 14). "[The mother was dashed in pieces upon her children"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 14) (cf. 13:16 and Psa. 137:9). The tragedy is quite common in ancient and modern warfare, although many more women are killed in modern warfare than in ancient warfare. Ancient warfare (with an occasional exception of characters like Genghis Khan and Tamerlane) was largely concerned with defeating an ]{.class_s321}[enemy's army ]{.class_s322}[in the field. The Assyrians were noted for killing men, women, and children if a city didn't surrender, and they had a reputation for "staking male prisoners out." But this kind of ghastly treatment is as old as human nature. In World War I they cut out their noses with bayonets and stuffed sawdust in the holes; they also collected ears off of the wounded. In World War II they knocked gold teeth out of mouths of living men with the butt end of a rifle, castrated prisoners, and drove empty shell cases through their kneecaps. "Man" (to quote the contemporary bullshooters) is "the measure of all things," which means (in Biblical language), if you want to see how noble an ]{.class_s321}[animal ]{.class_s322}[is, compare him to man (Job 35:11). The vast majority of animals come out pretty good by comparison (Rom. 1:21--25).]{.class_s321}

[The barbarities of Buchenwald, Treblinka, and Auschwitz are almost too horrible to describe, but when one remembers that the commandants of all three camps were Roman Catholics under a Roman Catholic "feuhrer," whose Gestapo chief (Heinrich Himmler) was a Roman Catholic, we are not too surprised. 11:1 When Israel was a child, then I loved him, and called my son out of Egypt.

3 I taught Ephraim also to go, taking them by their arms; but they knew not that I healed them.

4 I drew them with cords of a man, with bands of love: and I was to them as they that take off the yoke on their jaws, and I laid meat unto them.

God is going from one "plan" to another (see Exod. 32:14) as His Holiness (an essential attribute of God) conflicts with His Compassion (an essential attribute of God). Superficial theologians like Teilard, Aquinas, Tillich, Sartre, Niebhur, and Michael King Jr., can never get this conflict straight. On the one hand, the God of the Bible is dismissed on the grounds that if He is as weak and as indecisive as His creature man, then He is not really God; and on the other hand, some suppose that the writers have invented Him and given Him human attributes because that is all they could comprehend. CHAPTER TWELVE

12:1 Ephraim feedeth on wind, and followeth after the east wind: he daily increaseth lies and desolation; and they do make a covenant with the Assyrians, and oil is carried into Egypt.

2 The LORD hath also a controversy with Judah, and will punish Jacob according to his ways; according to his doings will he recompense him.

[Verse 2 is what threw the Lockman Foundation. They figured that the written prophecies had to be in order ]{.class_s321}[chronologically]{.class_s322}[---although where they got this harebrained idea from, I have no idea---and, then, they figured that if anything followed something by ]{.class_s321}[two verses ]{.class_s322}[(11:12 followed by 12:2) that it had to be the same ]{.class_s321}[time ]{.class_s322}[context. Now that last blooper is bigger than the first one. A ]{.class_s321}[comma ]{.class_s322}[separates 2,000 years is Isaiah 61:2; a ]{.class_s321}[colon ]{.class_s322}[separates 2,000 years in Genesis 49:11, and ]{.class_s321}[one verse ]{.class_s322}[separates 2,000 years in Isaiah 40:2--3. You would presume that if a scholar thought he was smart enough to revise the Holy Bible and correct God, he would have enough sense to remember what he read in the Bible, now, wouldn't you?]{.class_s321}

"[Ephraim feedeth on wind" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 1). Well, wind and ashes (Psa. 102:9) are a pretty rough diet. This is the "hot air" to which we refer many times in discussing Conservative scholarship. You see, this is ]{.class_s321}["the EAST WIND" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 1) of Jonah 4:8 and Exodus 10:13. It brings sunstroke and locust typical companions for the "newer translations." Most of the verse is apparent. Israel is trading with the Hamites by sending olive oil down to Egypt (vs. 1).]{.class_s321}

"[The LORD hath also a controversy with Judah" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 2). Hosea's prophecies (as we said in the Preface) cover a period of more than fifty years. In these fifty years (Jeroboam II to Hezekiah), Judah climbs on and off the bandwagon several times. Notice that under Uzziah (vs. 2) it could be said; under []{#cYN.xhtml_page_154}Ahaz (2 Chron. 28) it could again be said. Conversely, under Hezekiah (2 Kings 18), Hosea 11:12 would apply, and it would also apply to Jotham in 2 Kings 15. We see, then, that Hosea's prophecies can alternate depending upon the condition Judah is in at the time he preaches. But you would think that all of this would be self evident to anyone who read Kings and Chronicles. Did the Lockman Foundation read Kings and Chronicles? If they didn't, how did they translate the Bible text in them? If they DID, how did they miss the obvious transactions which anyone could see at a glance?]{.class_s321}

[He ]{.class_s321}["will punish Jacob according to his ways" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 2). Then this could have been prophesied to Uzziah (2 Chron. 26) or to Ahaz (2 Kings 16) or to any of their subjects. And you will observe that it is FORE telling, not "FORTHtelling," as the perennial superstition mistranslates the word.]{.class_s321}

3 He took his brother by the heel in the womb, and by his strength he had power with God:

4 Yea, he had power over the angel, and prevailed: he wept, and made supplication unto him: he found him in Bethel, and there he spake with us;

5 Even the LORD God of hosts; the LORD is his memorial.

6 Therefore turn thou to thy God: keep mercy and judgment, and wait on thy God continually.

[The references deal with Jacob as ]{.class_s321}[representing ]{.class_s322}[Judah. Jacob did take ]{.class_s321}["his brother by the heel," ]{.class_s323}[and he did have ]{.class_s321}["power with God" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 3). \[See comments under Gen. 32:24 in that Commentary.\] He did prevail in prayer (vs. 4), and he did make ]{.class_s321}["supplication unto him"]{.class_s323}[ (v. 4). The Angel found Jacob in Bethel (v. 4),]{.class_s321}[ "and there he spake with US"]{.class_s323}[!]{.class_s321}

"[Even the LORD God of hosts; the LORD is his memorial" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 5).]{.class_s321}

[Now observe that the ]{.class_s321}["he"]{.class_s323}[ in all three passages is JACOB---not ]{.class_s321}[God:]{.class_s322}[ ]{.class_s321}["He took his brother," "He had power with God," "He had power over the angel,"]{.class_s323}[ ]{.class_s321}["He wept," "He found him in Bethel," ]{.class_s323}[and ]{.class_s321}["There HE spake with US."]{.class_s323}[ ]{.class_s321}[Notice that not one single time does the masculine pronoun ever refer to God. ]{.class_s322}[It is JACOB ]{.class_s321}[everytime. ]{.class_s322}[But if it is Jacob, then the ]{.class_s321}["US"]{.class_s323}[ (in vs. 4) is a direct reference to the ]{.class_s321}[Godhead ]{.class_s322}[(see Gen. 1:26 and comments).]{.class_s321}

[Since all of the new Bibles---especially the ]{.class_s321}[NASV]{.class_s322}[, recommended by the faculty at Lynchburg---are Arian in their attacks on the Deity of Christ, the ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[has capitalized the word "He" in verse 4 three times. This succeeds in making the "He" God and the ]{.class_s321}["us"]{.class_s323}[---nobody! The ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[has written "And there He spoke with us"---Capital "H" on the "He" but no capital on the]{.class_s321}[ "us"]{.class_s323}[ of the Godhead. Exactly. And that is what you can expect from any twentieth century "qualified authority" on Bible versions; tripe. In order to rid themselves of the reference to the Deity of the Godhead, the Lockman Foundation has written a fairy story which says that Jacob sought the angel's favor ("He wept and sought His favor"), making the Angel Deity, and then Jacob found "Him" (the angel) at Bethel, and then THE ANGEL spoke with "us"---small "u"---NOBODY. Who is "us" if God isn't included? ]{.class_s321}[Jacob was ALONE with the angel, remember?]{.class_s322}

[Now this is so characteristic of "Fundamental" scholarship these days that it needs to be documented. [Now to prevent "the madness of the prophet" (2 Peter 2:16, ]{.class_s321}[NASV]{.class_s322}[), the Holy Spirit has given three names for the Godhead immediately following verse 4, and He has done it without changing the fact that the "he" of verses 3 and 4 was Jacob. "[Therefore turn thou to THY God" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 6). Notice the ]{.class_s321}[four ]{.class_s322}[main characters in the drama: Jacob, the Angel of the Lord, the Godhead, and Judah. The God of Judah should be the God of Jacob (vs. 2) who appeared to Jacob in Bethel (see Gen. 28:10 and comments in that Commentary), and this God is a TRINITY (LORD, GOD OF HOSTS, LORD). \[Cf. Isaiah 26:4 (LORD, LORD, JEHOVAH) and Deuteronomy 6:4 (LORD, GOD, LORD). 7 He is a merchant, the balances of deceit are in his hand: he loveth to oppress.

8 And Ephraim said, Yet I am become rich, I have found me out substance: in all my labours they shall find none iniquity in me that were sin.

9 And I that am the LORD thy God from the land of Egypt will yet make thee to dwell in tabernacles, as in the days of the solemn feast.

10 I have also spoken by the prophets, and I have multiplied visions, and used similitudes, by the ministry of the prophets.

11 Is there iniquity in Gilead? surely they are vanity: they sacrifice bullocks in Gilgal; yea, their altars are as heaps in the furrows of the fields.

12 And Jacob fled into the country of Syria, and Israel served for a wife, and for a wife he kept sheep.

13 And by a prophet the LORD brought Israel out of Egypt, and by a prophet was he preserved.

14 Ephraim provoked him to anger most bitterly: therefore shall he leave his blood upon him, and his reproach shall his Lord return unto him.

[Jacob has turned from a sheepherder (see verse 12) into a merchant (see Gen. 4:12 and comments in that Commentary). The ]{.class_s321}[King James Bible ]{.class_s322}[has translated the Hebrew (]{.class_s321}["kenaan"]{.class_s322}[)]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s322}[correctly, and the new Bibles have to follow suit, even though they would like to strut their stuff and transliterate the word as "Canaan."]{.class_s321}

"[AND EPHRAIM said, Yet I am become rich, I have found me out substance" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 8). Then Ephraim, as well as Judah, is bragging about "ill gotten gains" from placing their hands on the weighing scales (see vs. 7). A "deceitful balance" (see Prov. 16:11 and comments) is one where the weights used to counterbalance the goods are LIGHTER than they should be. ]{.class_s321}["In all my labours they shall find none iniquity in me that were sin" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 8). Ephraim has covered his track so that nobody can get a rebate from him if they go over the "books." Like the scrupulous Pharisees (see John 18:28), they handled their business in a business like fashion, being careful to obey all []{#cYN.xhtml_page_158}the rules and regulations they had to obey, in case they were going to be checked up on. Like Job (see Job 31 in ]{.class_s321}[The Bible Believer's Commentary on Job, ]{.class_s322}[1978), they justified their works, although Job was in better condition than they were because his works were better and were done with a better motive (see Job 31 and comments in that Commentary).]{.class_s321}

[At verse 9, the prophet interrupts the discourse abruptly with an entirely new theme, and (violating all the laws of continuity and "outlining") he reminds them that God was their LORD ]{.class_s321}["from the land of Egypt" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 9).]{.class_s321}

"[Will YET make thee to dwell in tabernacles, as in the days of the solemn feast" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 9). \[Cf. 9:5 and comments.\] This is partially fulfilled the first time in Ezra 3:4, but that sad affair, observed by only a small remnant, is not the real fulfillment of Hosea 12:9. [1. ]{.class_s321}[The Sun ]{.class_s322}[(Psa. 19; Mal. 4), typifying the Messiah come to reign.]{.class_s321}

[2. ]{.class_s321}[The Moon ]{.class_s322}[(Song of Sol. 6:10), typifying the Body of Christ in this age witnessing until "morning" (Matt. 13:43).]{.class_s321}

[3. ]{.class_s321}[The Night Watches ]{.class_s322}[(Isa. 21:11--12), illustrating the four divisions of 4 B.C.--A.D. 2000 (see Mark 13:35).]{.class_s321}

[4. ]{.class_s321}[The Stars ]{.class_s322}[(Dan. 12:3), pointing out witnesses for Christ in this age and in the Tribulation.]{.class_s321}

"[Is there iniquity in Gilead\...bullocks in Gilgal\...furrows of the fields" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 11). The bullocks sacrificed in Gilgal and Gilead are ]{.class_s321}["vanity"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 11) because God does not regard them or accept them (Isa. 1:11).]{.class_s321}

[Misreading the tense (as usual), ]{.class_s321}[The Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_s322}[tells us that the ]{.class_s321}["heaps"]{.class_s323}[ in verse 11 are destroyed altars, but no one has destroyed anything ]{.class_s321}[yet ]{.class_s322}[in Hosea's ministry: all is future. "[And Jacob fled into the country of Syria" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 12). Moses got a Gentile bride while he was looking after a flock (see Exod. 2:21 and comments), and so the second Moses (Deut. 18:18) gets a Gentile bride (Eph. 5) while tending His Father's flock (Zech. 11:11--13).]{.class_s321}

The Lord has two things in mind when He recites the history of Jacob to the backsliding Israelites:

1\. God brought Jacob back from his backslidden condition (see Gen. 31:13 and comments) to the very area where Israel was about to go into captivity, and when He brought him back, He brought him back to BETHEL---the place []{#cYN.xhtml_page_161}where he first met the Angel of the Lord (see vs. 4 and comments).

And this explains why the humanistic "do-gooders," Communist race mixers, and anemic welfare socialists like to classify their leaders as "prophets." The documented facts that appear in the written accounts by the prophets show a "credibility gap" (twentieth century clichè) between a Bible prophet and a modern prophet that is so great that only people who believe what they read in the newspapers could pretend to span it.

CHAPTER THIRTEEN

13:1 When Ephraim spake trembling, he exalted himself in Israel; but when he offended in Baal, he died.

2 And now they sin more and more, and have made them molten images of their silver, and idols according to their own understanding, all of it the work of the craftsmen: they say of them, Let the men that sacrifice kiss the calves.

3 Therefore they shall be as the morning cloud, and as the early dew that passeth away, as the chaff that is driven with the whirlwind out of the floor, and as the smoke out of the chimney.

4 Yet I am the LORD thy God from the land of Egypt, and thou shalt know no god but me: for there is no saviour beside me.

[The meaning is clear in the ]{.class_s321}[AV ]{.class_s322}[text in spite of the stupid bungling one will find in the ]{.class_s321}[NIV]{.class_s322}[, ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[and similar apostate "bibles." When ]{.class_s321}["Ephraim"]{.class_s323}[---here]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s323}[used for the twelve tribes of Jacob (see the context, Hosea 12:13--14)---was small, he trembled when he spoke: this is true literally of ]{.class_s321}[Jacob ]{.class_s322}[in the preceding context (12:4) and true literally of ]{.class_s321}[Jeroboam ]{.class_s322}[who had to speak against Solomon (1 Kings 11:28, 40) and later was "exalted in Israel" (1 Kings 12:25, 11:31). Observe that ]{.class_s321}["Israel" ]{.class_s323}[and ]{.class_s321}["Baal," ]{.class_s323}[as used in this passage, are both figurative and are used in the sense of "in regard to his relationship with." When Ephraim (the ten or the twelve tribes) feared God, he spoke carefully and trembled when he had to pass judgment (Deut. 17:12, 13; 24:17; Exod. 20:20; Deut. 4:10, 5:29, 17:19). You will notice that in the last reference the command is to a KING, exactly ]{.class_s321}[like Solomon and Jeroboam. You ]{.class_s322}[will notice further that it has to do with a king in a judicial position where he can pass sentence, exactly like ]{.class_s321}[Solomon ]{.class_s322}[and ]{.class_s321}[Jeroboam ]{.class_s322}[(1 Kings 13:4). You will notice, further, that the []{#c118.xhtml_page_168}trouble is that his "HEART" is in danger of being "LIFTED UP" (Deut. 17:20). That is, he would ]{.class_s321}[exalt himself. ]{.class_s322}[God is the one who exalts a king (1 Kings 4:29). When Solomon spoke trembling (1 Kings 3:17), he ]{.class_s321}[exalted himself the RIGHT way; ]{.class_s322}[when he offended in Baal (1 Kings 11:4--7), ]{.class_s321}[he DIED. ]{.class_s322}[When Jeroboam was a servant (1 Kings 11:26), he was careful what he said and how he said it and ]{.class_s321}[he exalted himself the right way ]{.class_s322}[(1 Kings 11:31), then when he offended in Baal (1 Kings 12:28--32), ]{.class_s321}[HE DIED.]{.class_s322}

The commentary by Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown tells us (p. 780) "RATHER\..." and then gives us the reading that they would "rather" have if they had their "druthers" (a disyllabic, first ingressive, hithpolael, undrugged, irresistible, Latvian, pluperfect flabblebabble). We take it for granted that where Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown correct the Holy Bible, we would "rather" just ignore them.

[The grossly corrupt ]{.class_s321}[NASV]{.class_s322}[, recommended by Falwell's school in Lynchburg, Tennessee Temple, and Pensacola Christian College, has paraphrased the verse exactly like Taylor's ]{.class_s321}[Living Bible. ]{.class_s322}[In spite of the ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[paraphrase, Bob Jones and the "WORLD CONGRESS OF FUNDAMENTALISM" took a "stand" against Taylor's paraphrase (1978) but didn't dare open their yap about the paraphrase of the ]{.class_s321}[NASV]{.class_s322}[. Typical. Yellow dog silence is quite common in some "Fundamentalist" circles where the deciding factor is enrollment, attendance or a bond payment. The ]{.class_s321}["Beth" ]{.class_s322}[(Hebrew "in") has been translated "IN" the first time (IN ISRAEL) but "through" the second time (THROUGH BAAL). 5 I did know thee in the wilderness, in the land of great drought.

6 According to their pasture, so were they filled; they were filled, and their heart was exalted; therefore have they forgotten me.

"[According to their pasture so were they filled; they were filled, and their heart was exalted; therefore have they forgotten me" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 6). 9 O Israel, thou hast destroyed thyself; but in me is thine help.

10 I will be thy king: where is any other that may save thee in all thy cities? and thy judges of whom thou saidst, Give me a king and princes?

11 I gave thee a king in mine anger, and took him away in my wrath.

12 The iniquity of Ephraim is bound up; his sin is hid.

13 The sorrows of a travailing woman shall come upon him: he is an unwise son; for he should not stay long in the place of the breaking forth of children.

14 I will ransom them from the power of the grave; I will redeem them from death: O death, I will be thy plagues; O []{#c118.xhtml_page_174}grave, I will be thy destruction: repentance shall be hid from mine eyes.

[Self destruction (vs. 9) is one of the common operations of life; and most of the problems we have came from our "many inventions" to get around problems. Sometimes it is a matter as basic as not being prepared ]{.class_s321}[militarily, ]{.class_s322}[sometimes it is a matter of politics that entangle the nation with a weak ]{.class_s321}[ally, ]{.class_s322}[sometimes it is something as small as a wine bottle, and sometimes it is as large as a Maignot Line. Spain did not drop into the rank of a fifteenth rate world power because of the British Navy.]{.class_s321}

[Now the basic rule adopted by the Athenian translators (Acts 17:21) of the Alexandrian Cult (A.D. 100--300) and the "modern" cultists (1880--1980) is this: if it is ]{.class_s321}[possible ]{.class_s322}[to alter the Authorized Text ]{.class_s321}[by any means, ]{.class_s322}[DO IT. [The second clause is self evident. Israel calls up her judges and asks them to provide a king for her. But all exposition breaks down at this point, for after Hoshea's imprisonment (2 Kings 17) they were NOT asking for a king---remember Hosea 10:3? God gave them a king in his ANGER (vs. 11); but this king, without a doubt, is SAUL, who was taken away in WRATH (vs. 11). And if the hearkening back to Saul seems strange, let the reader []{#c118.xhtml_page_178}never forget that ]{.class_s321}[Saul ]{.class_s322}[was Israel's first king (before David), and he was chosen by the people who said ]{.class_s321}["GIVE ME A KING"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 10). The ]{.class_s321}[AV ]{.class_s322}[reading, therefore, is the only reading that makes sense, and all the nonsense about the "king" of verse 11 being Shallum, or Hoshea, is pish posh.]{.class_s321}

Verse 1 is clear without any "helps." The commandment is to "return" (see Deut. 30:4--5), and they will return between A.D. 1948 and 2000 (see 6:1--2).

"[Take away all iniquity and receive us graciously" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 2). 4 I will heal their backsliding, I will love them freely: for mine anger is turned away from him.

[]{#c158.xhtml}

INTRODUCTION TO

JOEL

Joel has three chapters, seventy three verses, and 2,034 words. A full one-third of the book deals with the coming of the Lord's Army (see Rev. 19:10--17 and comments) at the Battle of Armageddon, with the subsequent judgment of the nations.

Joel prophesies from 488 to 477 B.C. according to Bullinger. Dummelow agrees with Knabenbauer's and Scholz's and Merx's post exilic date. Evidence for Joel prophesying in the reign of Joash (837--801 B.C.) is given by some of the commentators. Various reasons are given for date setting but since all of these come from internal evidence---no date is given in the book---a lot of it depends upon how the reader interprets the passage. Knabenbauer, Scholtz, Merx, and Dummelow, for example, have based a post exilic date on the theory that the word "GRECIANS" (3:6) could not have popped up till the time of Alexander the Great or at least the time of Artaxerxes. We know what to make of this kind of "Bible study": pretzels.

[Arguments for the late date of 598--488 B.C. are limited to the supposition that since the destruction of Jerusalem and Judah is mentioned (with their restoration---see 3:1), that the destruction is imminent. But this too breathes of "desperation." Bullinger (who accepts the late date) moves it up another 100 years to 488--477 because in Bullinger's system (see Appendix 86, ]{.class_s321}[Companion Bible,]{.class_s322}[ p. 120--124) Jerusalem is destroyed at 498 B.C., not 598 B.C.]{.class_s321}

Perhaps the most remarkable thing about the Book is the fact that Joel never mentions any king in Jerusalem. If Jehoida the priest were in charge (2 Chron. 24), this would explain the omission and it would also help to explain the reference to the priests in the book (1:13, 2:17). Joel also avoids any reference to Israel; this leads most of the scholars to believe that Israel has gone into captivity a good while ago. But this reasoning has holes in it, for if Israel had gone into []{#c158.xhtml_page_191}captivity, Sennacherib would be threatening Judah (see the reign of Hezekiah) or Nebuchadnezzar would be threatening Judah (see the reign of Zedekiah). Neither Syrians, Assyrians, nor Chaldeans are mentioned in the Book of Joel. When all the evidence is in (including several dead ends on Shishak's invasion---see 1 Kings 14:25), it would appear that 885--865 is a good period in which to look for Joel's prophecies to Judah. (On Bullinger's calendar this would be 785 to 765.)

[Regardless of date setting, it is apparent that Joel is preaching to the Southern tribes---Judah and Benjamin---heading up at Jerusalem. The most unique thing about the book is not that it ]{.class_s321}[is undated; ]{.class_s322}[by far the most unique thing about the book is the use to which chapter 2 has been put by Fundamentalists, Pentecostals, Liberals, and Conservatives. When one considers the appalling foolishness that is carried on by the Scofield Board of Editors, the Lockman Foundation, the thirty five major commentators, and every version on the market in regard to chapter 2, it is hard to keep from laughing out loud.]{.class_s321}

[The pitiful Dummelow says that Joel 2 is about literal ]{.class_s321}[locusts ]{.class_s322}[and a locust plague. Bullinger and Scofield are able to see that chapter 2 is about a literal ]{.class_s321}[army ]{.class_s322}[(symbolized by locusts), but they call it the Devil's army ("the northern army") whereas the Holy Ghost called it the "LORD's ARMY" (see 2:4--11 and comments). ]{.class_s321}[The Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_s322}[says that a plague of locusts was the occasion for writing the book and proceeds to write three pages of small print on locusts. Hengstenberg refers to the "locusts" as the ]{.class_s321}[spiritual enemies ]{.class_s322}[of Israel. Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown have the "locusts" of chapter 2 only injuring vegetation (read 2:8 and exposit that one, Mac!). Williams has the Antichrist leading the Lord's army (William's commentary, p. 645). ]{.class_s321}[The New Bible Commentary ]{.class_s322}[starts with locusts, runs to metaphors, climbs over Syrian troops, decides it is further out than Syria, and settles for SOMETHING natural as long as it is not "supernatural"! Dr. Deere (]{.class_s321}[The Wycliffe Bible Commentary]{.class_s322}[) has]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s322}[chapter 2 down as a "POETIC STRAIN of matchless elegance," entirely confounding the symbol with the thing symbolized. He winds up with locusts ]{.class_s321}[]{#c158.xhtml_page_192}[looking ]{.class_s322}[like an army and ]{.class_s321}[acting ]{.class_s322}[like an army, although they are not. In cases like this we are constrained to say with that great graduate of Ash Heap University, Dr. Job: ]{.class_s321}["But as for you all\...I cannot find one wise man among you" ]{.class_s323}[(Job 17:10).]{.class_s321}

Well, the word JOEL means "Jehovah is GOD." There are thirteen other Joels in Scripture, but this one prophesies either in the reign of Joash or perhaps Uzziah (800--760 B.C.).

[Joel stays on a negative kick through most of the book. With seventy three verses in the book, only twenty of them could be called "positive." Scholars make a grave error in assuming that all of Joel's prophecies have to have reference to ]{.class_s321}[contemporary ]{.class_s322}[historical events. One will observe that David speaks of having his hands and feet ]{.class_s321}[pierced]{.class_s322}[ when they never were pierced a day in his life (see Psa. 22:16), and who that read Isaiah 53 would be a big enough fool to think that some man from Judah went through Isaiah 53:1--10 so Isaiah could record it in the past tense? When Joel sights in his opening guns (1:2), he is already glancingforward with the prophetic eye of an inspired eagle and has picked up the last three years of Daniel's Seventieth Week; all attempts to run verses like 18--20 back into 880--440 B.C. are ridiculous. Although Nebuchadnezzar can fulfill as a type (see Hosea 8:1 and comments), he certainly does not stop rain (vss. 18--20), nor does the DAY OF THE LORD (vs. 15) take place at his attack in 598 B.C. There is no way such passages as Hosea 2:2, 10, 11, 28, 32 and 3:1, 2, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 20 and 21 can be explained by messing around with Pusey, Eiselen, Ellison, Deere, Robinson, Henry, Marti, Nowack, Scholz, Merx, Meier, Ho1zhausen, Bauer, Schurman, Baumgarten, Svanborg, Wunsche, Credner, Leusden, Pocock, and the majority of Bible rejecting butchers.]{.class_s321}

We shall approach Joel with an open heart, an open mind, and our "ears on" (Matt. 13:9). Where Joel speaks in the Authorized Text, we shall believe him, and if believing him will label us as a leper outside the Conservative scholars "camp," then we shall graciously accept that label and continue to listen to Joel instead of the scholars.

Enter, the prophet Joel!

[]{#c15T.xhtml}

CHAPTER ONE

1:1 The word of the LORD that came to Joel the son of Pethuel.

2 Hear this, ye old men, and give ear, all ye inhabitants of the land. Hath this been in your days, or even in the days of your fathers?

3 Tell ye your children of it, and let your children tell their children, and their children another generation.

4 That which the palmerworm hath left hath the locust eaten; and that which the locust hath left hath the cankerworm eaten; and that which the cankerworm hath left hath the caterpiller eaten.

5 Awake, ye drunkards, and weep; and howl, all ye drinkers of wine, because of the new wine; for it is cut off from your mouth.

6 For a nation is come up upon my land, strong, and without number, whose teeth are the teeth of a lion, and he hath the cheek teeth of a great lion.

7 He hath laid my vine waste, and barked my fig tree: he hath made it clean bare, and cast it away; the branches thereof are made white.

[The word ]{.class_s321}["Joel" ]{.class_s323}[means "Jehovah is God." Joel is the son of ]{.class_s321}["Pethuel" ]{.class_s323}[("the sincerity of God" or "godly simplicity"). Joel starts off with predictions of the future. No events that would match verses 4, 11, 12, 17, 18, 19, and 20 took place at the coming of Nebuchadnezzar nor did they take place during the times of Jehoida or Joash.]{.class_s321}

"[Hath this been in your days, or even in the days of your fathers?" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 2). The implied answer is "no." The conditions that Joel []{#c15T.xhtml_page_194}describes are so terrible that no one listening to him then had seen the conditions, nor had anyone preceding the time of his prophecy. ]{.class_s321}["A nation is come up upon my land, strong, and without number" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 6) might have historical application to Nebuchadnezzar and the Babylonians (see Jer. 49:19 and 50:17), but no plague of insects preceded the invasion of Nebuchadnezzar (see Jer.]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s323}[52 and 2 Kings 25); and if the references ]{.class_s321}[were ]{.class_s322}[to the invasion (see Bullinger's chronology), there would be no reason for verses 2 and 3, for when Shalmaneser and Sennacherib cleaned out the Ten Northern Tribes (see Hos. 13:16, 11:10, and 2:12), famine, crop failure, and enemy troops were all involved. Joel is plainly looking beyond A.D. 1970.]{.class_s321}

"[Tell ye your children of it\...and their children" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 3). The reader must compare the passage with the great seventy two verse "parable" found in the Psalms (Psa. 78:4). There we are told that the historical events from Exodus 3 to 1 Samuel are an informal rehearsal for events that will come on stage in full dress in the late twentieth century (see Rev. 11--12 and comments).]{.class_s321}

"[The palmerworm\... locust\... cankerworm... caterpiller" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 4). These four animals are mentioned again in Joel 2:25, and there they are called ]{.class_s321}["my great ARMY."]{.class_s323}

[Now it is this twist of the wrist that causes 95 percent of the expositors to assume that the]{.class_s321}[ "army"]{.class_s323}[ of Chapter 2:1--11 is a ]{.class_s321}[figurative description]{.class_s322}[ of locusts and five percent of the commentators (the Premillennialists) to assume that although it is a literal army, it must be literal locusts (see 2:11) because Joel 2:25 said that the locusts were ]{.class_s321}["MY great army."]{.class_s323}[ \[We shall comment on this more when we get to chapter 2.)]{.class_s321}

[The palmerworm (Heb. ]{.class_s321}["gazam"]{.class_s322}[) means a gnawer or a biter. The locust (Heb. ]{.class_s321}["arbeh"]{.class_s322}[)]{.class_s321}[ is ]{.class_s322}[the ordinary word used for the locusts of Moses' plagues (see Exod. 10 and comments). The cankerworm (Heb. ]{.class_s321}["yeleq"]{.class_s322}[)]{.class_s321}[ is ]{.class_s322}[mentioned three times in Nahum; the word means "a licker." The caterpillar (Heb. ]{.class_s321}["chasil"]{.class_s322}[) is a "devourer," an animal that cuts off the blossom or bud from the stem. Raschi gives all four as a species of locusts while Jerome, Cyril, and Theodoret make them refer to Assyrians, Babylonians, or Chaldeans; or Medes, Persians, and []{#c15T.xhtml_page_195}Macedonians; or Shalmaneser, Antiochus, and Sennacherib; or time to color the pretty color book, kiddies! See the pretty pictures?]{.class_s321}

[Since not one commentator (]{.class_s321}[including either Scofield Board of Editors]{.class_s322}[) bothers to consult the ]{.class_s321}[Scriptures ]{.class_s322}[for an interpretation, we are not surprised to find Revelation 9:2--3 sluffed off like it wasn't in the book. The Scofield Board of Editors, at least, had the ]{.class_s321}[time ]{.class_s322}[of the prophecies of Joel 1--2 right because they knew the prophecies had to do with the "end time" (see footnote on p. 930), but since the]{.class_s321}[ "locusts"]{.class_s323}[ of Revelation 9 were not ]{.class_s321}[literal locusts ]{.class_s322}[that ate grass (see Rev. 9:4), the connections were broken. [Verse 4 is a remarkable sermon outline on what happened to the Body of Christ after the Alexandrian Cult took control of it (1880--1884) through the leading Conservative scholars (Machen, Green, Schaff, Robertson, Keil, Delitzsch, Moulton, Wuest, et al.) while they were "fighting modernism" and "taking stands for the faith" as the "outstanding defenders of the faith," etc. It is also true of Church History generally: What impurity ]{.class_s321}[left ]{.class_s322}[(A.D. 200--500) after it got through with the church, ]{.class_s321}[apostasy ]{.class_s322}[got (500--800); what apostasy finished []{#c15T.xhtml_page_196}off (800--1000), the ]{.class_s321}[pope ]{.class_s322}[picked up; and what he corrupted (1000--1500) into a ruin was finished off by ]{.class_s321}[infidelity ]{.class_s322}[(1500--1900). 8 Lament like a virgin girded with sackcloth for the husband of her youth.

9 The meat offering and the drink offering is cut off from the house of the LORD; the priests, the LORD'S ministers, mourn.

10 The field is wasted, the land mourneth; for the corn is wasted: the new wine is dried up, the oil languisheth.

11 Be ye ashamed, O ye husbandmen; howl, O ye vinedressers, for the wheat and for the barley; because the harvest of the field is perished.

12 The vine is dried up, and the fig tree languisheth; the pomegranate tree, the palm tree also, and the apple tree, even all the trees of the field, are withered: because joy is withered away from the sons of men.

13 Gird yourselves, and lament, ye priests: howl, ye ministers of the altar: come, lie all night in sackcloth, ye ministers of my God: for the meat offering and the drink offering is withholden from the house of your God[]{#c15T.xhtml_page_197}.

The reader should pay careful attention to the supernatural placement of the paragraph markings in the Book (see comments on Acts 20:36). "[Lament like a VIRGIN"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 8). Now we know Israel is pictured as an adulteress wife (see Hosea) who will later be reconciled to her husband. But here the Tribulation remnant is likened to a VIRGIN (see Jer. 31:4 and 2 Kings 19:21). How, then, can this be?]{.class_s321}

Observe that the BRIDE of Christ is a "chaste virgin" (2 Cor. 11:1--3), yet she is a married WIFE (Eph. 5:32) at the same time. Thus, in the Tribulation, the saved remnant is called the ]{.class_s321}["VIRGIN\...DAUGHTER OF ISRAEL"]{.class_s323}[ (Isa. 23:12, 37:22, 62:5) while the restored nation (Hos. 1--2) is pictured as a reconciled WIFE (Isa. 54:5).]{.class_s321}

[The picture, in verse 8, is a young woman who is engaged to be married and her fiance' has been killed. ]{.class_s321}["The meat offering and the drink offering is CUT OFF from the house of the LORD" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 9). The ]{.class_s321}["priests" ]{.class_s323}[and ]{.class_s321}["ministers" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 9) are mourning because their daily bread came from the meat and drink offerings brought to the Temple (see Lev. 2; 23; Exod. 29; Num. 15). With prices up, crops down, no rain, locust plagues, and destroyed fig trees and vines (vs. 7), the priests are going to have to howl with the drunkards (vs. 5) and the husbandmen (vs. 11).]{.class_s321}

"[The field is wasted\...the corn is wasted the oil languisheth" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 10). To quote Van Gorder (]{.class_s321}[Ill Fares the Land]{.class_s322}[), a []{#c15T.xhtml_page_198}"famine" is on the way. In America's case it was ]{.class_s321}[planned by the government; ]{.class_s322}[in Israel's case it was brought about by sin. Eitherway, America will not escape judgment, for America has sin, ]{.class_s321}[plus ]{.class_s322}[a planned famine. With meat prices going from 98 cents a pound to a \$1.60 a pound in two years, the farmer was given 26--35 cents a pound for the meat on the hoof when he delivered it. With the professional liars of the news media (the greatest professional liars in the world) saying, "Last month the price index went up only 1.15% as compared to 2.58% last year at this same time, etc.," any fool knows that the prices on gasoline, candy, milk, bread, fruit juice, meat, cheese, and vegetables went up 100 in three years, and chocolate went up five hundred percent in ten years---while BEER and WHISKEY remained stable.]{.class_s321}

[Thirty percent price increases in a year is 2.50% increase ]{.class_s321}[every month, ]{.class_s322}[running three years straight (1971--1973). There have been months (in 1975) when the price of a jar of mayonnaise or a roll of toilet paper or a jar of jam went up thirty percent ]{.class_s321}[in one month. ]{.class_s322}[The housewives know what the "wholesale index" is. It is an index that increases retail prices thirty to fifty percent every ten months.]{.class_s321}

The corn is gone (vs. 10) because of liquor; the new wine is gone because it went into fermented hootch on the grocery store counter (vs. 11); the oil (vs. 10) dried up because America threw away 100,000,000 barrels of it in two "police actions" that weren't worth two hours of Class "C" time on daylight television.

[The barley (vs. 11) went into Millers, Busch Bavarian, Schlitz, Jax, and Wiedemanns, and the ]{.class_s321}["harvest of the rield"]{.class_s323}[---especially the wheat (vs. 11)---went to nations who have taken a public stand against God, Christ, the Bible, and everything that gave Americans their homes, cars, pools, hospitals, garages, offices, churches, parks, highways, banks, farms, and cities. (See Rev. 6:6 and comments in that Commentary.) Leviticus 26:20 is a forecast of verse 12. The men who took care of the cattle and the vineyards (vs. 11) could not feed their flocks anymore than the farmer could feed them in 1975, although I doubt if they shot them (as the farmers did in 1975) or sent them to the Roman Catholic Church in Honduras (1974). Observe the word ]{.class_s321}["HOWL" ]{.class_s323}[(vss. 5, 11) exactly as []{#c15T.xhtml_page_199}it appeared in Hosea 7:14 (Heb. ]{.class_s321}["yalal"]{.class_s322}[), and notice how the ridiculous ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[has translated it as "wail" and yet as "howl" in Isaiah 52:5. \[That is some more of that "consistent" translating you hear these wolves howl about.\] Students of Greek and Hebrew know that the word for "wailing" in either Testament means "to smite the breast" (see Rev. 1:7). The]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s323}[word ]{.class_s321}["yalal" ]{.class_s322}[has been mistranslated. as "wail" in about twenty places in the ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[to match the liberal ]{.class_s321}[RSV ]{.class_s322}[of the NCCC---see Jer. 48:20, 31, 39, etc.]{.class_s321}

[One must never forget that when the Conservatives went liberal (1880--1920), they adopted the text of the ]{.class_s321}[RSV before it was published. ]{.class_s322}[Likewise, when the Fundamentalists went Liberal (1940--1980), they too adopted the ]{.class_s321}[RSV ]{.class_s322}[of ]{.class_s321}[the National Council. ]{.class_s322}[Knowing that the average Christian would not check the readings in about 3,000 places, Bob Jones III, and the Fundamentalists promoted both the ]{.class_s321}[ASV ]{.class_s322}[(1901) and the ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[(1969) as "reliable" translations, whereas both of these bibles were ]{.class_s321}[Cult publications from Alexandria, Egypt ]{.class_s322}[(A.D. 330--350).]{.class_s321}

[When the pomegranate tree, the apple tree, and the fig tree (vs. 12) become barren, much of Israel's natural medicine disappears along with the corn bread (vs. 10) and the rye bread (vs. 11). 14 Sanctify ye a fast, call a solemn assembly, gather the []{#c15T.xhtml_page_200}elders and all the inhabitants of the land into the house of the LORD your God, and cry unto the LORD,

15 Alas for the day! for the day of the LORD is at hand, and as a destruction from the Almighty shall it come.

16 Is not the meat cut off before our eyes, yea, joy and gladness from the house of our God.

17 The seed is rotten under their clods, the garners are laid desolate, the barns are broken down; for the corn is withered.

18 How do the beasts groan! the herds of cattle are perplexed, because they have no pasture; yea, the flocks of sheep are made desolate.

19 O LORD, to thee will I cry: for the fire hath devoured the pastures of the wilderness, and the flame hath burned all the trees of the field.

20 The beasts of the field cry also unto thee: for the rivers of waters are dried up, and the fire hath devoured the pastures of the wilderness.

[The ]{.class_s321}["solemn assembly" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 14) could be any of the three major feasts (Deut. 16:16), but we have learned from Hosea 9:5 and 12:9 that the reference is connected with the Feast of Tabernacles which heralds ]{.class_s321}["THE DAY OF THE LORD" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 15).]{.class_s321}

Verse 14 shows that when a Christian or a nation (or a church) is about to pass through famine (vs. 19), starvation (vs. 18), or destruction (vs. 20), the proper meeting to call for is a PRAYER meeting (see 2:15--16). Superbowl games, NHL games, Mardi Gras, fiestas, Watergate investigations, CIA investigations, United Nations' assemblies and Hall of Fame nominations are out of the question.

"[Alas for the day! for the DAY OF THE LORD is at hand" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 15). [The Liberals have sought to circumnavigate the expression by altering it to "A DAY of the Lord, etc.," in four dozen places in the Old Testament. [Notice how the ]{.class_s321}[RV ]{.class_s322}[of 1884 and the ]{.class_s321}[ASV ]{.class_s322}[of 1901 lead the apostates in the subtle stratagem which rids the believer of the Bible's authority. Since the average Christian has not spent a great deal of time with the German Rationalists of the nineteenth century, he is unaware of the horseplay. Those of us familiar, however, with the Astruc Briggs Keil-Graf Bauer Ewald method of "exegesis" know that "POETIC IMAGERY" was the favorite expression in the nineteenth century of those who applied Darwin's theory to Bible exegesis. This explains the ]{.class_s321}[NASV's]{.class_s322}[ use of the form throughout the prophets. ]{.class_s321}["Evil communications corrupt good manners" ]{.class_s323}[(1 Cor. 15:33).]{.class_s321}

"[The day of the Lord," ]{.class_s323}[among Post  and Amillennialists who believe that it is future, becomes the ]{.class_s321}["DAY"]{.class_s323}[ mentioned in 2 Peter 3:10. This]{.class_s321}[ "day"]{.class_s323}[ is matched with 2 Peter 2:9 and then run head on into 1 Thessalonians 5:2 where the whole mismatched mish mash is molded to Matthew 25:31 and the Judgment Seat of Christ (2 Cor. 5:10). If the reader doubts this statement, let him obtain a copy of the ]{.class_s321}[Thompson Chain Reference Bible ]{.class_s322}[and look up the references on "the Last Judgment." Again, if he doubts the tendencies of Conservative scholars (even of Premillennialists) to approve of this imbroglio, let him observe how the Judgment Seat of CHRIST (Rom. 14:10) has been converted to the Judgment Seat of GOD in the "bibles" recommended by the ]{.class_s321}[Sword ]{.class_s322}[of ]{.class_s321}[the Lord, ]{.class_s322}[Midwestern, and Bob Jones University.]{.class_s321}

[The trouble with the ]{.class_s321}["Day of the Lord" ]{.class_s323}[(note how it has been altered in the ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[in 2 Thess. 2:1--2) is that it seems to cover too many periods of time. Since ]{.class_s321}["one day is with the Lord as a thousand years" ]{.class_s323}[(2 Pet. []{#c15T.xhtml_page_202}3:8), the ]{.class_s321}["Day"]{.class_s323}[ can refer to anything from the Second Advent (see 1 Thess. 5:2) to the White Throne Judgment (see 2 Pet. 2:9). Furthermore, there is an overlapping of the Day of the LORD into Daniel's Seventieth Week, which is apparent by such passages as Isaiah 13:9, Jeremiah 46:10, 21, Ezekiel 30, and Amos 5. And, beyond this (may the saints preserve the superstitious scholars!), the expression can also refer to any historical incident that typifies the Second Advent (see Ezekiel 30:3,10--11). The expression constantly used by the prophets is ]{.class_s321}["IN THAT day"]{.class_s323}[ and this expression will be found tied to the invasions of Sennacherib and Nebuchadnezzar, the Second Advent of Christ, the Judgments of the Tribulation, the Battle of Armageddon, the millennial reign of Christ, the conversion of Israel, and the First Advent.]{.class_s321}

[In Joel 2, the majority of the commentators (Williams and Scofield excepted) take the term ]{.class_s321}["AT HAND"]{.class_s323}[ to refer to a literal locust plague which is about to come then (when Joel was alive), although they allow that it is a "figure" to "presage" the White Throne Judgment.]{.class_s321}

Nothing could be more ridiculous.

[Everything which follows (chapter 2) pictures the Second Advent of the armies of Revelation 19:11--16, and everything in the context that is said to be ]{.class_s321}["AT HAND"]{.class_s323}[ is ]{.class_s321}["AT HAND,"]{.class_s323}[ exactly as Revelation 22 will be ]{.class_s321}["at hand" ]{.class_s323}[in the Tribulation and exactly as Matthew 3:2 and Mark 1:15 were]{.class_s321}[ "at hand"]{.class_s323}[ in the First Advent IF the Jews had received the risen Messiah (see Acts 7 and comments in that Commentary). Anything ]{.class_s321}["at hand" ]{.class_s323}[can be postponed and still be ]{.class_s321}["at hand" ]{.class_s323}[when the time for its fulfillment arrives the SECOND TIME: see Matthew 17:11--12 and John 1:21 and compare these with Malachi 4:5 and Luke 1:17. Comparing the English with the English (1611) always produces a more intelligent Biblical exegesis than comparing the Hebrew and the Coptic with Syriac and Greek.]{.class_s321}

[What follows (vss. 16--20) is a description of the last weeks and months of Daniers Seventieth Week, following the "FLAME THROWER" of chapter 2:1---]{.class_s321}["the FIRE hath devoured\...and the FLAME hath burned\...and the FIRE hath devoured" ]{.class_s323}[(vss. 19--20). \[Ezek. 20:46--49 []{#c15T.xhtml_page_203}and Isa. 24:3--6.\]]{.class_s321}

[Palestine, at this time, has undergone 3½ years of drought (see Rev. 11:6 and comments), and in addition to this, the earth has moved in toward the sun, and the sun has scorched (Rev. 16:9) ]{.class_s321}[men ]{.class_s322}[as well as land. The land referred to (vs. 14) is the land of Palestine (see 1 Kings 17:7 and 18:45). The land of Palestine (proper) is in the condition of Egypt during the ministry of Moses (Exod. 6--10). The seed rots in the field (vs. 17), the barns and grain elevators are broken down (vs. 17), the cows and horses are groaning and dying with no green pasture (see Rev. 8:7), and the rivers in Palestine have dried up (vs. 20), as well as the Euphrates (see Rev. 16:12).]{.class_s321}

[Williams (a Premillennial Bullingerite) has told us that the FIRE and FLAME of verses 19 and 20 are simply "figures" of locusts. I would like to see a "locust" ]{.class_s321}[burn a tree ]{.class_s322}[(vs. 19) unless it is a locust from Revelation 9:2--8.]{.class_s321}

[The mythological ]{.class_s321}[Septuagint]{.class_s322}[---not one copy written before the New Testament was completed---has "joy and gladness" removed from Judah's "mouth" (1:5), not their liquor. In verse 15, the Day of the Lord only brings "trouble on trouble," and seven words have been added to verse 18.]{.class_s321}

[The preposterous ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[(called "reliable" at Pensacola and Lynchburg) quietly removes the palmerworm, the caterpillar and the cankerworm from verse 4; the archaic Latin, Roman Catholic word "LIBATION" has been substituted for ]{.class_s321}["drink offering" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 9); the seed is not ]{.class_s321}["rotten" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 17) but it "shrivels," while the grain is "dried up" (vs. 17) instead of ]{.class_s321}["withered."]{.class_s323}

[The liberal ]{.class_s321}[RSV]{.class_s322}[ (1952) is the source of the reading above (vs. 17) in its twin sister translation (the ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[of Bob Jones University), and it also has turned the ]{.class_s321}["great lion" ]{.class_s323}[of verse 6 into a "lioness": ditto the ]{.class_s321}[NIV]{.class_s322}[. In keeping with the ]{.class_s321}[NASV]{.class_s322}[, the ]{.class_s321}[NRSV ]{.class_s322}[(same "bible") has eliminated three animals from verse 4.]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s322}[Locusts of a feather destroy crops together.]{.class_s321}

[In ]{.class_s321}[The "Living" Bible ]{.class_s322}[(Kenneth Taylor's private commentary), we find ]{.class_s321}["The day of the LORD" ]{.class_s323}[has been knocked out of the chapter (vs. 15), and to make sure that we have to accept the LOCUSTS in chapter ]{.class_s321}[2 ]{.class_s322}[as insects, the word "LOCUST" has been substituted for]{.class_s321}[ "A NATION"]{.class_s323}[ in verse 6. This is what []{#c15T.xhtml_page_204}is known as "private interpretation," and only the Roman Catholic Knights of Columbus (see ]{.class_s321}[Rome: The Great Private Interpreter,]{.class_s322}[ p. 169) could compete with Taylor's ]{.class_s321}["Living" Bible ]{.class_s322}[in these matters. But Taylor is so set on getting rid of the Lord's army in chapter 2 and replacing it with insects that he has inserted the word "TINY" in JOEL 2:4---]{.class_s321}["tiny horses"!]{.class_s322}[---so help me Tom Paine! There is no fire nor is there any]{.class_s321}[ "flame"]{.class_s323}[ (vss. 19--20) in Taylor's private paraphrase, and ]{.class_s321}["the house of your God"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 13) has quietly been removed from the chapter just as if Taylor were writing a religious novel instead of translating a "bible."]{.class_s321}

[Our kindergarten schoolmates (Orelli, Nowack, Marti) are still with us, and they suggest that ]{.class_s321}["old men" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 2) should be "hay horse," that ]{.class_s321}["withered" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 12) should be "ashamed," etc., and that ]{.class_s321}["for the wheat and for the barley" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 11) shouldn't be in the Bible, and ]{.class_s321}["pomegranate"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 12) shouldn't be in the Bible, and that ]{.class_s321}["and the apple tree"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 12) shouldn't be in the Bible, and that ]{.class_s321}["old men"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 2) shouldn't be in the Bible, and that ]{.class_s321}["tell ye your children"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 3) shouldn't be in the Bible, etc., etc. Neither should Nowack, Marti, and Orelli be "in the Bible." They ought to be "in" Mickey Spillane, Norman Mailer, James Baldwin, James Jones, ]{.class_s321}[Esquire, Playgirl, Lampoon, ]{.class_s322}[and ]{.class_s321}[The Anarchists' Cookbook.]{.class_s322}

[Dummelow insists that all of the insects in verse 4 are locusts (see ]{.class_s321}[NASV]{.class_s322}[), that ]{.class_s321}["the new wine"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 5) should be "sweet wine," that the seed "shrivels" (vs. 17---see ]{.class_s321}[NASV]{.class_s322}[), and that the ]{.class_s321}["day of the Lord" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 15) was only "A POPULAR THOUGHT" at the time when Jehovah "gave victory unto Israel."]{.class_s321}

Cole (]{.class_s321}[The New Bible Commentary]{.class_s322}[), Ellicott and the ]{.class_s321}[NEB? ]{.class_s322}[Why plow through 400 acres of slime and hemlock to find the hidden treasures of the Bible?
