CHAPTER TWO

2:1 Blow ye the trumpet in Zion, and sound an alarm in my holy mountain: let all the inhabitants of the land tremble: for the day of the LORD cometh, for it is nigh at hand;

2 A day of darkness and of gloominess, a day of clouds and of thick darkness, as the morning spread upon the mountains: a great people and a strong; there hath not been ever the like, neither shall be any more after it, even to the years of many generations.

3 A fire devoureth before them; and behind them a flame burneth: the land is as the garden of Eden before them, and behind them a desolate wilderness; yea, and nothing shall escape them.

4 The appearance of them is as the appearance of horses; and as horsemen, so shall they run.

5 Like the noise of chariots on the tops of mountains shall they leap, like the noise of a flame of fire that devoureth the stubble, as a strong people set in battle array.

6 Before their face the people shall be much pained: all faces shall gather blackness.

7 They shall run like mighty men; they shall climb the wall like men of war; and they shall march every one on his ways, and they shall not break their ranks:

8 Neither shall one thrust another; they shall walk every one in his path: and when they fall upon the sword, they shall not be wounded.

9 They shall run to and fro in the city; they shall run upon the wall, they shall climb up upon the houses; they shall enter in at the windows like a thief.

10 The earth shall quake before them; the heavens shall tremble: the sun and the moon shall be dark, and the stars []{#c181.xhtml_page_207}shall withdraw their shining:

11 And the LORD shall utter his voice before his army: for his camp is very great: for he is strong that executeth his word: for the day of the LORD is great and very terrible; and who can abide it?

[Now the fur hits the fan. The reader has seen how the scholars struggled mightily with the Holy Spirit in chapter 1 in trying to overthrow His words, but now they resist Him with a near superhuman force as He musters and commands the army of Revelation 19. Williams and Scofield put the ]{.class_s321}[Antichrist ]{.class_s322}[as general over the ]{.class_s321}[Lords army, ]{.class_s322}[Dummelow and Taylor are still messing with four kinds of ]{.class_s321}[locusts, ]{.class_s322}[and ]{.class_s321}[The Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_s322}[(J. J. Given) cinches the case by telling us that if you look closely at the head of a locust you will find that it is "like that of a ]{.class_s321}[horse]{.class_s322}[."]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s322}[(This little exegetical nugget came from comparing the German ]{.class_s321}["Heupferde" ]{.class_s322}[with a hay horse! There is nothing like "Biblical" scholarship for the truth, is there?)]{.class_s321}

Setting aside the Scofield editors, with the National Council of Churches, Williams, Dummelow, the popes, Cole, Given, the cardinals, Pfeiffer, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, Taylor, and the Atheists, let us see what we can find in the infallible text of 1611.

"[Blow ye the trumpet in Zion" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 1). When did God Almighty ever tell the watchman to blow a trumpet (see Num. 10) ]{.class_s321}[when locusts showed up?]{.class_s322}

"[Sound an ALARM in my holy mountain" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 1). Alarm? For ]{.class_s321}[locusts]{.class_s322}[? In Numbers 10:5--6, I read about sounding alarms, but God never wasted one of them on ]{.class_s321}[locusts]{.class_s322}[.]{.class_s321}

"[For the DAY of the LORD cometh" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 1). It didn't "come" in the days of Nebuchadnezzar or Jehoida or Joash or Shalmaneser or Alexander the Great.]{.class_s321}

"[A day of darkness\...gloominess\...clouds...thick darkness, []{#c181.xhtml_page_208}AS THE MORNING spread upon the mountains" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 2). The ]{.class_s321}["MORNING"]{.class_s323}[? Ever read Malachi 4:14? Ever read Matthew 13:43? Ever read Mark 13:35? Ever read Psalm 19:4, 5? Ever read Song of Solomon 6:10? Ever read 2 Samuel 23:4? ]{.class_s321}[Ever read Exodus 19:16?]{.class_s322}[ How did 100 percent of the commentators---including the Premillennial Fundamentalists---miss that last reference?]{.class_s321}

"[A great PEOPLE\...there hath not been EVER THE LIKE, neither SHALL BE any more after it, even to the years of many generations" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 2). You say the ]{.class_s321}["ants are a people" ]{.class_s323}[(Prov. 30:25), therefore, the locusts can be a]{.class_s321}[ "people."]{.class_s323}[ Then what is this deal about ]{.class_s321}["there hath not been EVER THE LIKE"]{.class_s323}[?]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s323}[There certainly was ]{.class_s321}["the like" ]{.class_s323}[in the days of Moses and Pharaoh (see Exod. 10:13)!]{.class_s321}

"[A fire devoureth before them; and behind them a flame burneth" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 3). Locusts? Fire is what you kindle to get rid of locusts. ]{.class_s321}["The land is as the garden of Eden before them, and behind them a desolate wilderness" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 3). Then if this is a real supernatural army (which it is), its destructive route covers more territory than the burnt out (see 1:18--20) land of Palestine (see Amos 1--3).]{.class_s321}

"[The appearance of them is as the appearance of HORSES" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 4). Then how did all the commentators miss the horses of]{.class_s321}[ Revelation 19:11--17?]{.class_s322}[ Scofield found the reference (see footnote p. 930), but had to split a sentence into two paragraphs (2:10--11) to get the ]{.class_s321}["horses"]{.class_s323}[ of verse 4 separated from the horses of Revelation 19:11--16 which he had just mentioned!]{.class_s321}

"[And as horsemen, so shall they run" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 4). How did 100 percent of the commentators miss 2 Kings 2:11, Song of Solomon 6:12 13, and Habakkuk 3:8? What are our "qualified authorities" doing? How is it that time after time (see the Commentaries on Job, Exodus, Acts, and Genesis) these Conservatives who brag about being "loyal to the word" do not even ]{.class_s321}[consult ]{.class_s322}[the word to exposit the word? You call that "unquestioned LOYALTY"?]{.class_s321}

"[Like the noise of chariots on the tops of mountains" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. []{#c181.xhtml_page_209}5). Exactly; these chariots are seen in 2 Kings 6:17; they are ridden in the Song of Solomon, and Elijah went back to glory in one of them (2 Kings 2:11).]{.class_s321}

Locusts, your foot!

"[As a strong people set in BATTLE array" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 5). How could anyone who believed the Bible have missed the cross references to Zechariah 14:1--3 and Isaiah 31. The armies of God, led by the LORD, ]{.class_s321}[do]{.class_s322}[ ]{.class_s321}[come down and fight, ]{.class_s322}[and they do come down to battle on ]{.class_s321}[horses, ]{.class_s322}[and they make a noise like a ]{.class_s321}["flame of fire" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 5) because they are riding chariots and ]{.class_s321}[horses of FIRE ]{.class_s322}[(see 2 Kings 2:11).]{.class_s321}

Locusts, your achin' back!

"[All faces shall gather blackness" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 6) because the flesh is rotting away in front of an atomic blast that peels off the skin (see Zech. 14:12 and comments). ]{.class_s321}["They shall run like mighty men\...they shall march every one on his ways, and they shall not break their ranks" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 7). Observe that these troops match David's troops (1 Chron. 12:22), where David is a type of the Angel of the LORD (1 Sam. 25:28; 2 Sam. 14:17); and they march as Joshua's troops (Josh. 6:5), ]{.class_s321}[straight forward]{.class_s322}[ to attack an accursed city (Rev. 17; Josh. 6:18). ]{.class_s321}["They shall walk every one in his path" ]{.class_s323}[(v. 8).]{.class_s321}

[You never saw a "locust" walk a straight line ]{.class_s321}["in his path" ]{.class_s323}[a day in your life.]{.class_s321}

("Locusts," your lexicon!)

"[And when they FALL UPON THE SWORD, they shall not be wounded" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 8). Isn't that a rather stupid thing to say about a "locust"? Didn't Isaiah 33:4 explain the passage? Ever see a locust wounded by falling on a sword? Or did you ever ]{.class_s321}[hear ]{.class_s322}[of one "failing on a sword" for that matter? \[Yes, child, we know the "scholarly" alibis: the prophet was only speaking ]{.class_s321}[figuratively]{.class_s322}[, he was only using poetic imagery in the "bold imagination" of his "prophetic insight," he uses "broad strokes" to paint the bold picture of the apocalyptic\..."; sure, son, we've been to school too.\]]{.class_s321}

"[They shall enter in at the windows LIKE A THIEF" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 9). []{#c181.xhtml_page_210}And a "qualified authority" could read ]{.class_s321}[that ]{.class_s322}[without thinking of Matthew 24:43 and Revelation 16:15 and 1 Thessalonians 5:2? Well how could any "recognized scholar" who took his Bible study "seriously" miss ANY ]{.class_s321}[of the three major ]{.class_s322}[New Testament references? Was any reference a reference to ]{.class_s321}[locusts ]{.class_s322}[in Joel's day? Was any reference a reference to the Antichrist attacking Jerusalem from the North? Then how do you explain these educated monkeys talking about the ]{.class_s321}[NIV ]{.class_s322}[and the ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[being Bibles for "SERIOUS Bible students" who take their studies "CONSCIENTIOUSLY"? Why those poor self deceived baboons can't even find ]{.class_s321}[the Biblical truth]{.class_s322}[ ]{.class_s321}[in three one syllable words in any Bible---]{.class_s322}["LIKE A THIEF."]{.class_s323}

"[The earth shall quake\...the sun and the moon shall be dark, and the stars shall withdraw their shining" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 10). No man who ever read Acts, Revelation, or Isaiah could miss the context. It is the Second Advent of Jesus Christ with the events that precede the Battle of Armageddon (see Rev. 19; Acts 2; Isa. 9; and Joel 2:30--31).]{.class_s321}

"[AND THE LORD SHALL UTTER HIS VOICE BEFORE HIS ARMY"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 11). There, that tied the rag on the bush! There went the bottom out of the oil pan, there went the universal joint, and there went the transmission all over the pavement. You see, the Fundamentalists cannot believe verses 1--11]{.class_s321}[ literally ]{.class_s322}[if they are a reference to saved Christians ]{.class_s321}[in this age ]{.class_s322}[who will make up the Lord's army at Armageddon. So, ]{.class_s321}[if you cannot believe the Bible, you invent a Bible! ]{.class_s322}[Scofield promptly erases the ]{.class_s321}[King James]{.class_s322}[ paragraph mark and makes a paragraph out of HALF A SENTENCE: ]{.class_s321}[verse 11 is ONE SENTENCE.]{.class_s322}

[Now this well illustrates, again, the lengths to which a born again Fundamentalist in the Cult (see Hosea 12:4 and comments) will go in an effort to rid the body of Christ of the hated ]{.class_s321}[Authorized Bible]{.class_s322}[ of 1611. We are not saying they are "Liberals"; we are saying, however, that where the ]{.class_s321}[authority of God Almighty ]{.class_s322}[is at stake, anyone of them would ]{.class_s321}[make a liar out of Him just as quickly as look at Him ]{.class_s322}[if that authority were connected with a ]{.class_s321}[King James 1611 Authorized Version.]{.class_s322}

[Scofield says (footnote, p. 931) that the invading army is described in verse 10 and then "at verse 11" Jehovah's army appears. ]{.class_s321}[Wrong again. ]{.class_s322}[The heading of chapter 2 in the ]{.class_s321}[Scofield Reference Bible]{.class_s322}[ says "the invading host from the north." ]{.class_s321}[Wrong again. ]{.class_s322}[Anytime you have to erase a paragraph mark in an ]{.class_s321}[AV ]{.class_s322}[and then ]{.class_s321}[refuse to give seven cross-references ]{.class_s322}[and then ]{.class_s321}[split a sentence into two paragraphs ]{.class_s322}[you have left the realm of ]{.class_s321}[Biblical studies ]{.class_s322}[and have entered the realm of Disneyland animation. He gives one cross reference at verse 3, which had nothing to do with the Advent, and one cross reference at verse 11. ]{.class_s321}[You left out seven verses, doc!]{.class_s322}[ I wonder what "serious" Bible studying you were doing when you skipped those seven verses? You were probably trying to exposit Genesis 1:4--6 in the light of Paley, Kepler, and Einstein. \[See ]{.class_s321}[The Bible Believer's Commentary on Genesis, ]{.class_s322}[Gen. 1:3--10.\]]{.class_s321}

If you don't believe it, you will when you get in the saddle and ride through the blood (Psa. 68:23) of God's enemies (Hab. 3:15). In the meantime "go by the Book," and that isn't any reference to any Bible published since 1880.]{.class_s321}

[And let no one mistake our intentions. We all know that C. I. Scofield (a converted lawyer) was one of the finest Christian men who ever lived. More than that, he was a real man before his conversion and a brave and trustworthy man in public life. Even more than that, God used him to open Central America to missionaries, and the reference Bible which he wrote is still the best reference Bible available today. But all of this we will set aside in one second if he (or any man ]{.class_s321}[better ]{.class_s322}[than him) undertakes to correct God Almighty. The most Satanic death trap the Devil ever set for a young minister is the destructive Biblical work of saved sinners who are truly born again and "Christian" in their deportment.]{.class_s321}

The material in Joel 2:1--11 is describing the glorified bodies of saved people who have become like Christ (1 John 3:1--3; Phil. 3:21; Rom. 8:29) and []{#c181.xhtml_page_212}who are returning to earth with Him in a "mopping up" operation.

Having grasped this truth, let us enroll in the Christian Kiddy Corral and watch the children playing with their tops and jacks.

[1. Kenneth Taylor removes ]{.class_s321}["THE DAY OF THE LORD" ]{.class_s323}[from verse 1 so that no one will ever find the time of the prophecy. 12 Therefore also now, saith the LORD, turn ye even to me with all your heart, and with fasting, and with weeping, and with mourning:

13 And rend your heart, and not your garments, and turn unto the LORD your God: for he is gracious and merciful, slow to anger, and of great kindness, and repenteth him of the evil.

14 Who knoweth if he will return and repent, and leave a blessing behind him; even a meat offering and a drink offering unto the LORD your God?

[Twice the LORD commands Judah to ]{.class_s321}["turn" ]{.class_s323}[back to Him (vss. 12--13). The repentance is to come from the HEART (]{.class_s321}["with all your heart" ]{.class_s323}[and ]{.class_s321}["rend your heart"]{.class_s323}[),]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s323}[and the mourning and fasting that accompanies it (vs. []{#c181.xhtml_page_214}13) is not to be an ]{.class_s321}[outward show ]{.class_s322}[(see Matt. 6:16--18), but the real thing. Since the sacrifices of God are a broken and contrite heart (Psa. 51:17), the Lord wants the ]{.class_s321}[inside ]{.class_s322}[plowed (see Hosea 10:12), not the outside: ]{.class_s321}["rend your HEART." ]{.class_s323}[The fasting commanded (vs. 12) is not to be of the nature of Zechariah 7:5--6. There is a great deal of difference between setting up regulations on eating and drinking (see Rom. 14:1--10), so that you can brag about your will power (see Col. 2:23 and comments), and fasting because you are sorry for your sins (see 1 Kings 21:27).]{.class_s321}

"[For he is gracious and merciful, slow to anger, and of great kindness, and repenteth him of the evil" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 13). Joel knows what Moses knew (Exod. 34:7) and what Manasseh (2 Chron. 33:19) and Jonah found out (Jonah 2--3). The ]{.class_s321}["LORD is not willing that any should perish, but that all should come to repentance" ]{.class_s323}[(2 Pet. 3:9; Ezek. 18:32), and in the midst of wrath (Hab. 3:2), He remembers mercy (see Hosea 11:8--9 and comments).]{.class_s321}

"[A blessing behind him; even a meat offering and a drink offering unto the LORD your God?" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 14). These offerings were withheld in Joel 1:13 because of crop failure. The idea, therefore, is that perhaps God will change His mind about judgment and ]{.class_s321}[leave ]{.class_s322}[some crops so that the temple offerings may be resumed. The expression ]{.class_s321}["IF HE WILL RETURN" ]{.class_s323}[is remarkably placed in view of the fact that there is an unknown interval of time (Matt. 24:48) between the Post Tribulation rapture (see Rev. 14:16) and the Advent (see Rev. 19:11). A mid Tribulation appearance of the Lord to Israel (or at least an appearance six months before the Feast of Tabernacles) is one of the prophetic doctrines in the Bible which 99 percent of the Premillennial scholars overlooked (see Ezek. 1:1--6, 10:1--6; Acts 9:5--7; Dan. 10:4--9, etc.). (You'll miss a lot when you major in showing off your scholarship.)]{.class_s321}

15 Blow the trumpet in Zion, sanctify a fast, call a solemn assembly:

16 Gather the people, sanctify the congregation, assemble the elders, gather the children, and those that suck the breasts: let the bridegroom go forth of his chamber, and the bride out of her closet.

17 Let the priests, the ministers of the LORD, weep between the porch and the altar, and let them say, Spare thy people, O LORD, and give not thine heritage to reproach, that the heathen should rule over them: wherefore should they say among the people, Where is their God?

"[Blow the trumpet in Zion" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 15). \[Cf. 2:1 and Hosea 12:9.\]]{.class_s321}

Now follows seven types of the New Testament church, and although the body of Christ is not revealed to the prophets, the seven terms that follow can all be found in the New Testament as they apply to the Pauline Christians.

[1. ]{.class_s321}["GATHER the people" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 16). The New Testament church is a people called out into a "gathering"---an ]{.class_s321}[ecclesia.]{.class_s322}

[2. ]{.class_s321}["Sanctify the CONGREGATION" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 16). The local church is a congregation, an assembly of people who have "congregated" for a purpose.]{.class_s321}

[3. ]{.class_s321}["Assemble the ELDERS"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 16). This is the New Testament name given to the ordained overseers of a flock (see 1 Peter 5:1; Acts 20:17; and 1 Tim. 5:17).]{.class_s321}

[4. ]{.class_s321}["Gather the CHILDREN" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 16). And so, the Christians are sons or ]{.class_s321}["children of God" ]{.class_s323}[and are called by that name (see Rom. 8:16 and Eph. 5:1).]{.class_s321}

[5. ]{.class_s321}["And the BRIDE out of her closet" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 16). This is the New Testament name (John 3:29) for ]{.class_s321}["the Lamb's wife" ]{.class_s323}[(Rev. 21:9).]{.class_s321}

[6. ]{.class_s321}["Let the PRIESTS"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 17). Every child of God in the New Testament is a priest (1 Pet. 2:9), chosen to offer ]{.class_s321}["spiritual sacrifices" ]{.class_s323}[(1 Pet. 2:5).]{.class_s321}

[7. ]{.class_s321}["The MINISTERS of the LORD" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 17) is the designation for []{#c181.xhtml_page_216}Timothy, Apollos, Titus, Cephas, and Paul (see 1 Cor. 4:1 and 3:5).]{.class_s321}

[There can be no doubt that everything found in the passage will have a spiritual application to the Body of Christ preceding the coming of "Nebuchadnezzar" (Rev. 13). Although we are in an Old Testament setting, there is nothing out of place about the church praying: "Spare thy people O LORD, and give not thine heritage to reproach that Russia, China, and the United Nations should rule over them!" (vs. 17). ]{.class_s321}["Where is their God?" ]{.class_s323}[is exactly what the Chinese Communists taunted Christians with after their imprisonment by "the people." The torturers demanded instant miracles and answers to prayers from the prisoners or else they were mocked exactly as the Pharisees mocked Christ on the cross (Matt. 27:40--41).]{.class_s321}

Occasionally this procedure backfired. One missionary (China, 1950) was demanded an answer to prayer and he prayed that his tormentor would be blinded. The man went bat blind in less than an hour. Subsequently, he was converted and killed by his own companions who had helped him torture the Christians. They killed him by driving two chopsticks up through his nose.

[In the annals of the Great Mother Whore we have an interesting account (]{.class_s321}[Foxe's Book of Martyrs, ]{.class_s322}[edited by Forbush) where a young martyr was burned at the stake; and just before the flames and smoke choked him into silence, he laughed at the Roman Catholic priests and said, "Behold, you papists, you're always looking for a miracle! Look at me! I feel no pain, this flame to me is as sweet as a bed of roses!"]{.class_s321}

[The reference to the ]{.class_s321}["HEATHEN" ]{.class_s323}[ruling over Judah (vs. 17) should settle the matter about the]{.class_s321}[ "army"]{.class_s323}[ of verses 1-11. It could not possibly be a heathen army, for it is THE LORD'S ARMY (vs. 11), ]{.class_s321}[if you believe what God wrote the way He wrote it. ]{.class_s322}[The heathen, here, is a reference to the ]{.class_s321}["nation" ]{.class_s323}[of Joel 1:6, and that nation will match what we are about to read.]{.class_s321}

18 Then will the LORD be jealous for his land, and pity his people.

19 Yea, the LORD will answer and say unto his people, Behold, I will send you corn, and wine, and oil, and ye shall be satisfied therewith: and I will no more make you a reproach among the heathen:

20 But I will remove far off from you the northern army, and will drive him into a land barren and desolate, with his face toward the east sea, and his hinder part toward the utmost sea, and his stink shall come up, and his ill savour shall come up, because he hath done great things.

21 Fear not, O land; be glad and rejoice: for the LORD will do great things.

22 Be not afraid, ye beasts of the field: for the pastures of the wilderness do spring, for the tree beareth her fruit, the fig tree and the vine do yield their strength.

23 Be glad then, ye children of Zion, and rejoice in the LORD your God: for he hath given you the former rain moderately, and he will cause to come down for you the rain, the former rain, and the latter rain in the first month.

24 And the floors shall be full of wheat, and the fats shall overflow with wine and oil.

25 And I will restore to you the years that the locust hath eaten, the cankerworm, and the caterpiller, and the palmerworm, my great army which I sent among you.

26 And ye shall eat in plenty, and be satisfied, and praise the name of the LORD your God, that hath dealt wondrously with you: and my people shall never be ashamed.

27 And ye shall know that I am in the midst of Israel, and that I am the LORD your God, and none else: and my people shall never be ashamed.

[Now we find out how Scofield, Williams, Durnmelow, Dodd, Fausset, Brown, Given, Pfeiffer, Cole (]{.class_s321}[The New Bible Commentary]{.class_s322}[), and the majority []{#c181.xhtml_page_218}of the recognized eggheads got so fouled up in their exposition of Joel 2:1--11.]{.class_s321}

"[I will remove far off from you THE NORTHERN ARMY" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 20).]{.class_s321}

[Since this army is the army of the ]{.class_s321}[Antichrist ]{.class_s322}[in Ezekiel 39, Scofield and E. S. English called the Lord's army the ]{.class_s321}[Antichrist's army ]{.class_s322}[in Joel 2:1--11. No one on either board could believe that verses 3--9 were descriptions of Christians. Such is the folly of Bible-rejecting Fundamentalism where it rejects the supernatural and espouses the Alexandrian Cult. Again, notice by ]{.class_s321}[joining verse 25 with verse 20]{.class_s322}[ you can talk yourself into believing that the "Lord's army" was LOCUSTS. As a matter of fact, you can talk yourself into anything if you just reject enough of the ]{.class_s321}[AV ]{.class_s322}[Bible to silence your conscience. One paragraph mark was placed at 2:12, another at 2:18, and another at 2:21 so that no one could confuse the three separate "armies" ]{.class_s321}[unless he thought he was smarter than the Holy Spirit.]{.class_s322}

[No, don't give us this gas about the ]{.class_s321}[King James]{.class_s322}[ translators. They could not possibly have known about this fine a distinction. They were "led," and they were led straighter and truer than any missile fired from a launcher. Observe that the work of the locust, caterpillar, cankerworm, and palmerworm (not the gnawlocust, bite locust, chew locust and snap locusts; see any of the comic strips) took place through ]{.class_s321}[a period of YEARS]{.class_s322}[ ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 25): ]{.class_s321}["I will restore to you THE YEARS that the\...." ]{.class_s323}[The work of the Lord's army in 2:1--11 takes place in ]{.class_s321}[less than ]{.class_s322}[24 ]{.class_s321}[hours ]{.class_s322}[(vss. 1--2, 11), and ]{.class_s321}[neither ]{.class_s322}[army (locusts or the Lord's army) was the ]{.class_s321}["NORTHERN ARMY" ]{.class_s323}[of verse 20. The army of verse 20 is killed and buried (see Ezek. 39). You didn't even WOUND any ]{.class_s321}["locust"]{.class_s323}[ in Joel 2:1--11.]{.class_s321}

How say ye then that they are the same?

Things different are not equal.

("Locusts," your fan belt!)

"[Then will the Lord be jealous for his land" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 18). \[See Deut. 32:43.\] This is the literal restoration of the land and the people mentioned so many times in the Scripture (Isa. 60:18,21; Jer. 24:6,8; Mal. 3:12; Ezek. 39:16, []{#c181.xhtml_page_219}etc.).]{.class_s321}

Verse 19 is self explanatory.

"[The northern army, and will drive him into a land barren and desolate, with his face toward the east sea, and his hinder part toward the utmost sea" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 20). The fate of this army is that their rotting corpses will STINK (vs. 20) because they consist of United Nations mounted troops whose bodies will rot in Palestine (Ezek. 39:4,11--16). They are NOT the "Lord's army," and they are no more "locusts" than your Aunt Sally's housecat. Jeremiah 1:14--15 is a foreshadowing of this attack, although Nebuchadnezzar escaped the fate of Joel 2:20, which the Antichrist will not (see Rev. 19:18--21).]{.class_s321}

Verses 21 and 22 are self explanatory.

"[He hath given you THE FORMER RAIN moderately, and he will cause to come down for you the ]{.class_s323}[rain, ]{.class_s321}[THE FORMER RAIN, and the LATTER RAIN in the first month" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 23). We have quoted most of the verse because of the modern emphasis being put on the passage by the Hobbledy Gobbledy Unbala \
Gumbala-Full Gospel-Charismatic Shandai Handi \
Hastalamumba Fellowship. These wretched Bible-rejecting "Fundamentalists" have claimed that the Pentecost of ]{.class_s321}[Acts 2 ]{.class_s322}[was the ]{.class_s321}["FORMER RAIN" ]{.class_s323}[(using Joel 2:28 for justification) and that the Azusa Street Mission movement (1910--1920) that went through Topeka, Kansas, (1920--1935) became ]{.class_s321}["THE LATTER RAIN" ]{.class_s323}[(1940--1980) under Dave Wilkerson, Pat Boone, Bill Gothard, Kathryn Kuhlman, Jimmy Swaggart, and the "Pentecostals."]{.class_s321}

[To obtain this torturous doctrine, the entire second half of Joel 2 has been ]{.class_s321}[allegorized. ]{.class_s322}[Observe that the Biblical "former and latter rain" do ]{.class_s321}[not ]{.class_s322}[come down 1,900 years apart; as a matter of fact, they do not come down ]{.class_s321}[seven months ]{.class_s322}[apart (as normally). They come down ]{.class_s321}["IN THE FIRST MONTH" ]{.class_s323}[together;]{.class_s322}[ twice as much rain as normally (see 1 Kings 18:45). Observe further that the Biblical reference has nothing to do with the "poured out spirit" of verse 28; the reference has to do with the land of Palestine getting WET after a drought []{#c181.xhtml_page_220}so that it will grow crops (vss. 22, 24). Observe further that the LORD is not in the church or connected with the church, nor is any Christian in the context; instead, the Lord is ]{.class_s321}["IN THE MIDST OF ISRAEL."]{.class_s323}

[But we cannot really understand why the Charismatics ("Pentecostals" of thirty years ago, "Holiness" of fifty years ago, "Holy Rollers" of seventy years ago) got so screwed up in their theology until we look at the last paragraph of Joel 2. And notice, again, the remarkable placement of the paragraph mark at verse 28 so that no fool would make the mistake of thinking that ]{.class_s321}[verse 23 ]{.class_s322}[was a reference to it. But since no Fundamental scholar from Origen to Thieme accepted the paragraph markings as they stand, 400,000 Bible-rejecting Christians can build a non denominational denomination on the teaching that ]{.class_s321}[verse 23 ]{.class_s322}[is a reference ]{.class_s321}[to verse 28]{.class_s322}[. It is not. One verse is literal rain falling on dirt and the other is the Spirit being poured upon ALL flesh---not just ]{.class_s321}[Israel ]{.class_s322}[(see Acts 2:17 and comments). Behold how great a matter a little fire (unbelief) kindleth!]{.class_s321}

Verse 24 is self explanatory. The double dose of rain (vs. 23) produces a bumper crop, and the years that the "locusts" ate (vs. 25) are restored.

"[And ye shall eat in plenty, and be satisfied, and praise the name of the LORD your God\...and my people shall never be ashamed" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 26). Notice that]{.class_s321}[ "my people"]{.class_s323}[ here (as in Psa. 14:4, 28:9, 78:52, 78:62; Jer. 12:14, 30:22; Isa. 65:19, 63:8; Amos 8:2; Micah 6:5, etc.) are NEVER the church or the "Pentecostals" or "Christians" or "Charismatic fellowships" or any other ungodly, perverted misapplication of Truth. These are ]{.class_s321}[Israelites]{.class_s322}[, in the land of Israel, eating crops that were grown as a result of a plentiful rain of literal WATER (vs. 23). To abort the passage, then, and claim that the "former and the latter rain" are Pentecost A.D. 33 and "Pentecost" 1900--1980 is senseless poppycock. Some of these ridiculous buffoons say, "Pentecost is not a ]{.class_s321}[denomination; ]{.class_s322}[it is an ]{.class_s321}[experience!" ]{.class_s322}[Well, it is "an experience" to a natural-born, Bible rejecting ]{.class_s321}[liar. ]{.class_s322}[Pentecost, in the Bible, is ]{.class_s321}[a Jewish feast ]{.class_s322}[(Lev. 23:16) where no one in 6,000 years of recorded history "experienced" anything but twelve Jewish Apostles (Acts 1:26) ]{.class_s321}[on ONE Pentecost ]{.class_s322}[(Acts []{#c181.xhtml_page_221}2:1--3). Of the thirty Pentecosts that take place in the book of the Acts (A.D. 33--61), ]{.class_s321}[twenty nine of them are "blanks" for every Christian in Europe, Asia Minor, Africa, and Palestine. ]{.class_s322}["Pentecost" is not an "experience" unless you are a FAKER. Anybody can fake an "experience" (see 1 Kings 13:18).]{.class_s321}

"[And YE shall know that I am in the MIDST OF ISRAEL, and that I am the LORD your God\...my people shall never be ashamed" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 27). Notice the exact fitting of the verse with Ezekiel 39:28. There isn't a "Pentecostal Christian" anywhere in the paragraph, and what follows was NOT fulfilled at the Pentecost of Acts 2 (see comments under Acts 2 in that Commentary).]{.class_s321}

28 And it shall come to pass afterward, that I will pour out my spirit upon all flesh; and your sons and your daughters shall prophesy, your old men shall dream dreams, your young men shall see visions:

29 And also upon the servants and upon the handmaids in those days will I pour out my spirit.

30 And I will shew wonders in the heavens and in the earth, blood, and fire, and pillars of smoke.

31 The sun shall be turned into darkness, and the moon into blood, before the great and the terrible day of the LORD come.

32 And it shall come to pass, that whosoever shall call on the name of the LORD shall be delivered: for in mount Zion and in Jerusalem shall be deliverance, as the LORD hath said, and in the remnant whom the LORD shall call.

Now this is the place where the homicide was committed. Since Simon Peter quotes the passage in Acts 2, it is assumed by all of the []{#c181.xhtml_page_222}Hobbledy-gobbledy Hastala-mumba gumba Fellowship that Acts 2:16 was the FULFILLMENT of Joel. And before we attempt to expound the passage let us give God and His word the honor due to them as the infallible authorities in the matter. Without bothering to find out what the text "teaches," let us first find out what it SAYS. (This is something that the Hobbledy gobbledy \
shaddai illashada bubbedy glubbedy Charismatic never bothers to do, so we shall do it for him.)

[1. The Spirit was NOT poured out ]{.class_s321}["UPON ALL FLESH" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 28) at ]{.class_s321}[Pentecost, ]{.class_s322}[nor has the Spirit ever been "POURED OUT ON ALL FLESH" one time in the history of the Christian church (see Col. 2:13 and comments).]{.class_s321}

[2. Joel says nothing at all about ]{.class_s321}["TONGUES" ]{.class_s323}[("unknown" or "other") anywhere within thirty verses of anything in the passage.]{.class_s321}

[3. None of the ]{.class_s321}["WONDERS"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 30) mentioned happened at Pentecost nor have they ever happened one time in 1,940 years ]{.class_s321}[since.]{.class_s322}

[4. The ]{.class_s321}[deliverance ]{.class_s322}[in Joel is not at ]{.class_s321}[Calvary ]{.class_s322}[or ]{.class_s321}[Golgotha ]{.class_s322}[(vs. 32); it is at the place of ]{.class_s321}["the breaking forth of children" ]{.class_s323}[(see Hos. 13:13 and comments): Jerusalem on Mt. Zion.]{.class_s321}

In view of these direct, plain, clear, truthful statements by the Holy Ghost Himself, what could be more blasphemous or God dishonoring than the Christ-insulting, private interpretations which teach that Pentecost was the fulfillment of Joel 2:28--32?

You say, "What then does Acts 2:16--17 mean?"

[That's your trouble. You are always trying to find out what the Bible ]{.class_s321}["MEANS" ]{.class_s322}[before you are willing to ]{.class_s321}[believe what it SA YS.]{.class_s322}

[If you will not accept what it ]{.class_s321}[says, ]{.class_s322}[who gives a flip what you ]{.class_s321}[think ]{.class_s322}[it ]{.class_s321}["MEANS"! ]{.class_s322}[You can make it ]{.class_s321}["mean" ]{.class_s322}[anything as long as you disregard what it ]{.class_s321}[says.]{.class_s322}

[Notice, for example, the gullible faculty at Bob Jones University teaching their students that 2 Pet. 3:4--6 is a reference to Noah's flood because they want to present the Biblical ]{.class_s321}[doctrine ]{.class_s322}[of the Gap (Gen. 1:2) as a mere "theory." Typical. They didn't believe what 2 Peter 3:4--6 SAID to start with; they only []{#c181.xhtml_page_223}wanted to make it ]{.class_s321}["teach" ]{.class_s322}[something or ]{.class_s321}["mean" ]{.class_s322}[something.]{.class_s321}

[Now, to get our bearings by dead reckoning (instead of a dime store compass made in Japan) let us see what ]{.class_s321}[God ]{.class_s322}[said about the time of Joel's prophecy. Note: ]{.class_s321}["IN THOSE DAYS will I pour out my spirit" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 29). This matches ]{.class_s321}["IN THOSE DAYS\...when I shall bring again the captivity of Judah and Jerusalem" ]{.class_s323}[(3:1). Observe that the context, in either case, is the judgment of nations (3:1--2) ]{.class_s321}[following the Second Advent ]{.class_s322}[(2:30--31). \[Cf. Zech. 12:10; Isa. 54:13; and Ezek. 39:29.\] Note: ]{.class_s321}["AFTERWARD, that I will pour out" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 28) which matches ]{.class_s321}["AFTER THOSE DAYS" ]{.class_s323}[(Heb. 8:10), and these match ]{.class_s321}["IN THE LAST DAYS, saith God" ]{.class_s323}[(Acts 2:17).]{.class_s321}

[Everywhere we turn we are confronted with the Biblical spectre which spooked 99 percent of the commentators out of sound Bible exegesis. The spectre is the Second Advent looming immediately in Acts 1--7 and then ]{.class_s321}[postponed ]{.class_s322}[to the end of the Church Age. This even caused the superstitious faculties at Dallas, Wheaton, Princeton, Columbia, Fort Worth, BIOLA, Hyles-Anderson, and Pensacola Christian College to think that the ]{.class_s321}["LAST DAYS"]{.class_s323}[ ]{.class_s321}[began with the First Advent ]{.class_s322}[(since the expression is found in Acts 2 and Heb. 1:2) and continued since then; and, therefore, there is nothing significant about the expression in 2 Timothy 3:1.]{.class_s321}

[The truth is that when Simon Peter says ]{.class_s321}["THIS IS THAT" ]{.class_s323}[(Acts 2:16), he is careful not to say "THIS IS THE ]{.class_s321}[FULFILLMENT" ]{.class_s322}[so what Joel said (see Acts 2:17--20, citing Joel) is going to come to pass in the ]{.class_s321}[immediate future. ]{.class_s322}[That immediate future was ]{.class_s321}[displaced ]{.class_s322}[at Acts 7 to 1970 plus. And that is not a "postponement theory"; that is a Biblical fact supported in either Testament with more verses than you can find to support the Bodily Resurrection or the Virgin Birth. The only thing "theoretical" about the Biblical Postponement Doctrine is the salvation of those who call it a "theory." If it is not true, three-fourths of the Old Testament is a lie, and you can no more understand the book of Hebrews than you could a Chinese cookbook. \[Readers who think this is an overstatement may study any work on "Hebrews" in the ]{.class_s321}[Biblical View Point]{.class_s322}[ (BJU), or by Zodhiates []{#c181.xhtml_page_224}(a Greek speaking Greek), and he will quickly discover that where this Biblical doctrine (above) is ignored, the exposition becomes the garbled nonsense of a grammatical contortionist. You'd better be careful about calling any sound Bible doctrine from a ]{.class_s321}[King James Bible]{.class_s322}[ a "theory" simply because you know of some "godly qualified authority" who didn't ]{.class_s321}[believe ]{.class_s322}[it.\]]{.class_s321}

[Now observe how the Holy Spirit is poured out on the House of Israel]{.class_s321}[ "IN THOSE DAYS"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 29) which overlaps the Tribulation. This is apparent because the signs of verses 30 and 31 happen in the last six months of Daniel's Seventieth Week (see Rev. 16 and comments). The ]{.class_s321}["ALL FLESH"]{.class_s323}[ of verse 28 is neatly separated from ]{.class_s321}["your sons and YOUR daughters" ]{.class_s323}[in the same verse by a semicolon. If you ignore this semicolon (as 100 of the Hebrew scholars do), you will get Jews ]{.class_s321}[PROPHESYING in the Millennium, ]{.class_s322}[and this is ]{.class_s321}[HERESY according to Zechariah 13:1--5,]{.class_s322}[ which see.]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s322}[We, therefore, should be very careful in observing not only the paragraph markings in the ]{.class_s321}[AV ]{.class_s322}[but also the ]{.class_s321}[punctuation. ]{.class_s322}[(Yes, we know what the Cult will think of that statement, but they won't laugh half as hard at us as we've been laughing at them for twenty eight years!) The ]{.class_s321}["servants\...and handmaids" ]{.class_s323}[of verse 29 (see Num. 11:29) are ]{.class_s321}[Jewish ]{.class_s322}[servants and handmaids.]{.class_s321}

[Observe further, the small "s" on ]{.class_s321}["spirit" ]{.class_s323}[(vss. 28--29) is changed to a capital "S" in Acts 2. This remarkable, undesigned "coincidence" shows the deeper Bible student (deeper than those who ignore the ]{.class_s321}[AV ]{.class_s322}[text) that the operations of the Holy Spirit in the Tribulation will be different than His "operation" (see Col. 2:13 and comments) in the Church Age. \[Notice the use of the word in Rev. 11:11 and 14:13.\]]{.class_s321}

"[And I will shew wonders in the heavens and in the earth, blood, and fire, and pillars of smoke" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 30). \[See Luke 21:11.\] The phenomenon that occurs in verse 31, which will affect the sun and the moon (see Rev. 16:8, 6:12, and Mal. 3:10), will be due to the gravitational and orbital disturbances described in Isaiah 24:4--7, 19--20. Nothing like it happened at ANY Pentecost, anywhere in ]{.class_s321}[either ]{.class_s322}[Testament, and the stupid, irreverent, blasphemy of the Charismatics of today, who insist on interpreting the Scriptures in the light []{#c181.xhtml_page_225}of their emotional upheavals, is as irreligious as anything you will find outside of Atheism. The very idea of teaching new converts that Acts 2:17 20 was a FULFILLMENT of Joel 2:29--30 simply because your nervous system got out of whack during an emotional strain and you lost control of your vocal chords! My, my, my, my, my! What "loyalty to the word of God" and what a "leading of the Holy Spirit"!]{.class_s321}

"[The great and the terrible DAY OF THE LORD" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 31) has always been the Second Advent in any context where the events described have never yet taken place. ]{.class_s321}["Whosoever shall call on the name of the LORD shall be delivered: for IN MOUNT ZION and in JERUSALEM shall be deliverance" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 32); not at Calvary, which is ]{.class_s321}["WITHOUT THE CAMP" ]{.class_s323}[(Heb. 13:13), and not at Golgotha, which is ]{.class_s321}["WITHOUT THE GATE"]{.class_s323}[ (Heb. 13:12). Any attempt therefore to ]{.class_s321}[spiritualize ]{.class_s322}[Joel 2 and make it fit the Gospel, or the events of Acts 2, is the attempt of a demon led Christian critic who is superimposing his private interpretations over the authority of the Holy Ghost. The called ]{.class_s321}["remnant" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 32) is one tenth of a nation (Isa. 6:13), and there isn't one "Christian" or "Gentile' in the entire lot.]{.class_s321}

[Observe: ]{.class_s321}["And it shall come to pass, that HE THAT IS LEFT IN ZION, and he that remaineth IN JERUSALEM, shall be called holy, even every one that is written among the living IN JERUSALEM: When the LORD shall have washed away the filth\...and shall have purged the blood of JERUSALEM from the midst thereof" ]{.class_s323}[(Isa. 4:3--4). There is no "gospel of the grace of God" for Gentile masses within ten chapters of Joel 2 in any direction. A man who bases his Christian theology, therefore, on Acts 2 is under the spell of a Bible denying demon. These spirits are called ]{.class_s321}["seducing spirits" ]{.class_s323}[in 1 Timothy 4:1--3, and they do not teach Bible doctrines; they teach ]{.class_s321}["doctrines of DEVILS" ]{.class_s323}[(1 Tim. 4:14). And whether these devils control Christians, Modernists, Liberals, Fundamentalists, or Conservatives, their perversions of the word of God are just as harmful and destructive as those by the worst Atheist or Papist who ever lived.]{.class_s321}

[]{#c1BX.xhtml}

CHAPTER THREE

3:1 For, behold, in those days, and in that time, when I shall bring again the captivity of Judah and Jerusalem,

2 I will also gather all nations, and will bring them down into the valley of Jehoshaphat, and will plead with them there for my people and for my heritage Israel, whom they have scattered among the nations, and parted my land.

3 And they have cast lots for my people; and have given a boy for an harlot, and sold a girl for wine, that they might drink.

4 Yea, and what have ye to do with me, O Tyre, and Zidon, and all the coasts of Palestine? will ye render me a recompence? and if ye recompense me, swiftly and speedily will I return your recompence upon your own head;

5 Because ye have taken my silver and my gold, and have carried into your temples my goodly pleasant things:

6 The children also of Judah and the children of Jerusalem have ye sold unto the Grecians, that ye might remove them far from their border.

7 Behold, I will raise them out of the place whither ye have sold them, and will return your recompence upon your own head:

8 And I will sell your sons and your daughters into the hand of the children of Judah, and they shall sell them to the Sabeans, to a people far off: for the LORD hath spoken it.

Joel is still "on target." His subject is the Second Advent and the events which attend it.

Having observed this, let us return for a brief moment to the Alexandrian Cult (known as "qualified authorities" whose "unquestioned loyalty to the word of []{#c1BX.xhtml_page_227}God" demands our "respect" and "recognition"):

[1. The corrupt ]{.class_s321}[RSV ]{.class_s322}[of 1952: It quietly changes the ]{.class_s321}[tense ]{.class_s322}[of verse 18 to the PAST tense so that the prophecy will have no ]{.class_s321}[future ]{.class_s322}[application: "HAD pity." The ]{.class_s321}[same thing is ]{.class_s322}[done again in verse 21: "HAS done great things," and the ]{.class_s321}[same thing ]{.class_s322}[is done again in verse 23: "HE HAS poured down for you. The archaic word "PORTENT" has been put into verse 30 to replace the up to date ]{.class_s321}[King James]{.class_s322}[ word ]{.class_s321}["WONDERS."]{.class_s323}

[2. The corrupt ]{.class_s321}[NASV]{.class_s322}[, promoted by Afman, Yaeger, and Custer, follows its sister (see above) since the Fundamentalists who made this translation (the Lockman Foundation) wanted to get as close to the ]{.class_s321}[RSV ]{.class_s322}[as they could without being caught. You will find the past tense (as above) in verses 21 and 23. It also erases the caterpillar, the palmerworm, and the cankerworm from verse 25. In order to line up with the liberal ]{.class_s321}[RSV, ]{.class_s322}[the ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[has put verses 18--29 into poetic form.]{.class_s321}

[3. The word ]{.class_s321}["east" ]{.class_s323}[has been removed from verse 20. The ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[(see any faculty member at BJU) has followed this reading exactly, and it is also found in the sister of the ]{.class_s321}[NASV]{.class_s322}[---the ]{.class_s321}[RSV of the National Council of Churches. ]{.class_s322}[The Hebrew text does not even suggest "as before." Every Hebrew text known to man says ]{.class_s321}["Barishon"--- ]{.class_s322}["IN THE FIRST." ]{.class_s323}[The reading of the LXX and its apostate children (]{.class_s321}[ASV, NASV, RSV, NRSV]{.class_s322}[) is a deliberate mistranslation.]{.class_s321}

Vultures of a feather pick corpses together.

[To make sure that everyone confounds Joel 2 with ]{.class_s321}[Pentecost, ]{.class_s322}[Taylor has privately decided that there is NO DELIVERANCE ]{.class_s321}["IN JERUSALEM" ]{.class_s323}[or Zion (see vs. 32), although the Holy Spirit just said that ]{.class_s321}["IN MOUNT ZION AND IN JERUSALEM shall be DELIVERANCE." ]{.class_s323}[Since neither Taylor, nor the Lockman Foundation (]{.class_s321}[NASV]{.class_s322}[), nor the NCCC (]{.class_s321}[NRSV]{.class_s322}[) believed what they read in the Bible for one minute, all three of them have ]{.class_s321}[erased the sentence ]{.class_s322}[or ]{.class_s321}[transposed it ]{.class_s322}[so that no deliverance is in either place.]{.class_s321}

Rice (and those like him) actually think that if a Christian "takes a stand against Modernism" or "Neo evangelicalism," that makes him a "defender of the faith" and "loyal to the word." [And what shall we say of Nowack, Marti, Pusey, Cheyne, Orelli, Gesenius, Hitzig, Ewald, Wunsche, Driver, Davidson, and other destructive critics who succeeded in getting their works read by Christians because they professed to believe in "the fundamentals"? Verse 1 is self explanatory (see Job 42:10 and comments in that Commentary).

"[I will also gather all nations" ]{.class_s323}[(see Zech. 14:14 and Zeph. 3:8). ]{.class_s321}["And will bring them down into the valley of Jehoshaphat" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 2). This is called the valley of ]{.class_s321}["Berechah"]{.class_s323}[ (2 Chron. 20:21), but it will have to be "imagery" (see above) for those Bible rejecting Conservatives who have already made a slaughterhouse of the last chapter. The valley itself is between Jerusalem and the Mt. of Olives (Kedron). The Satanic ]{.class_s321}[Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_s322}[immediately says: "It matters little whether we understand the valley of Jehoshaphat in the ]{.class_s321}[literal ]{.class_s322}[sense\...." so you will not take Zechariah 14:1--12 literally. It matters a great deal. It matters so much that when we Bible believing people approach ]{.class_s321}[any ]{.class_s322}[work by ]{.class_s321}[any ]{.class_s322}[Conservative scholar, we would do well to make up our minds that it "matters little what any Conservative scholar thinks about ]{.class_s321}[anything ]{.class_s322}[where it treats such matters as 'little'." The word ]{.class_s321}["Jehoshaphat" ]{.class_s323}[means "Jehovah hath judged," and the reference is clearly to Matthew 25:31, which see. Dummelow, siding with ]{.class_s321}[The Pulpit Commentary, ]{.class_s322}[tells us that it is an "IDEAL valley." Yes, child, it certainly is; it is "ideal" for the Lord Jesus judging the nations after He lands on the Mt. of Olives.]{.class_s321}

"[And they have cast lots for my people\...that they might drink" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 3). The criteria of this judgment, therefore, is how the Gentiles have treated the Jew (see Matt. 25:32--42). No amount of shuffling around in historical notes, ancient translations, manuscript evidence, scholarly conjectures, or "alternate readings" can erase the fact that Joel 3:2, 3 and 12 refers to Matthew 25. ]{.class_s321}[The Pulpit Commentary's]{.class_s322}[ pitiful and plaintive misrendering of the verses (see especially Joel 3:12) should not be taken seriously by any Christian, []{#c1BX.xhtml_page_230}regardless as to which scholar was deceived by the reputation of which commentator.]{.class_s321}

"[Whom they have scattered among the NATIONS" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 2). Notice the greater reference to the dispersion of A.D. 701--948---]{.class_s321}["nations" ]{.class_s323}[(plural) and not to the Babylonian captivity which followed Joel's prophecy. ]{.class_s321}["And parted MY LAND" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 2). Politicians engaged in trying to placate Rockefeller and the Chase Manhattan Bank (1973--1980) should take heed to the word of God. Palestine is God's land according to the author of the Bible who is the creator of the universe and the owner of every foot of dirt on the planet (Acts 7:5). ]{.class_s321}["MY LAND"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 2) settles once and forever any argument about "private ownership" or "communal own ership" or "]{.class_s321}[community ]{.class_s322}[property." Palestine is not ]{.class_s321}[community property]{.class_s322}[ to be divided up by the United Nations (see Dan. 11:39). It is God's land, and since He holds the mortgage, He can give the deed to whomsoever He will. It was "deeded" (with mineral rights) to the twelve sons of Jacob (see Gen. 12:1--3, 13:14--18, 22:17, and 15:18).]{.class_s321}

"[And they have cast lots" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 3). The Jews were sold into slavery, and their masters shot craps to see who would get which Jews. Instead of paying cash to the Greek and Roman "nuns" who played "vestal virgin" for Jupiter and Apollo, the fornicators paid their cathouse bill (see Gen. 38:15--16 and comments) with a boy slave. When they ran out of rot gut (see Prov. 20:1 and comments), they swapped a girl slave for a bottle.]{.class_s321}

"[Yea, and what have ye to do with me, O Tyre, and Zidon]{.class_s323}[" (vs. 4). Since two cannot walk together except they be agreed (see Amos 3:3 and comment), the ]{.class_s321}["coasts of Palestine" ]{.class_s323}[have no business inheriting one inch of the coasts of Palestine; consequently, when the flamethrower passes over (see Joel 2:3), it will rake out Damascus, Tyre, and Zidon before it ever moves up to Jordan from Mt. Sinai (see Hab. 3:3 and comments). ]{.class_s321}["Will ye render me a recompence\...speedily will I return your recompence upon your own head"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 4); so, Alexander the Great sold 13,000 people of Tyre into slavery, and Artaxerxes sold 40,000 of the inhabitants of Sidon into slavery. ]{.class_s321}["And I will sell your sons and daughters" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 8). You don't mess with []{#c1BX.xhtml_page_231}the Jew (Mal. 3:6 and comments) and get away with it (see Gen. 4:12 and comments).]{.class_s321}

"[MY silver and MY gold\...MY goodly pleasant things" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 5). God never fails to take credit for any material prosperity anyone has (see Rom. 11:36 and Acts 14:17). Not once does the Lord God Almighty blush about taking credit for any riches a nation acquires (Dan. 5:23; Ezek. 16:15--20; Hosea 2:8). Not once does either Testament hint that America's prosperity is due to its living standards, its humanism, its educational institutions, its scientific investigations, its foreign policies, its social security set up, its welfare programs, its form of government, or its intelligence. That is, there is a "credibility gap" between any form of ]{.class_s321}[secular education ]{.class_s322}[and a ]{.class_s321}[Biblical education ]{.class_s322}[that no man can breach with the trans Atlantic cable. God says that the silver and the gold and the good crops which Israel enjoyed were HIS (vs. 5), as well as the people of Israel themselves. ]{.class_s321}["The children also of Judah and the children of Jerusalem have ye sold unto the Grecians" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 6).]{.class_s321}

[Notice that the past tense here has nothing to do with anything. Greece, at this time, was not even a nation to be reckoned with. Speaking in the past tense (as Isaiah in Isa. 53), Joel is speaking of three slaveries; the ]{.class_s321}[first ]{.class_s322}[was under Nebuchadnezzar following 590--585 B.C., and the ]{.class_s321}[second ]{.class_s322}[under Titus (A.D. 70) and the ]{.class_s321}[third ]{.class_s322}[under the Son of Perdition (Rev. 11:1--2) in the next twenty five years.]{.class_s321}

The sons and daughters of the Philistines and Phoenicians (vs. 4) will eventually be bought by Jews (vs. 8), and they will swap them off to the Arabians in South Arabia (vs. 8---Sabeans).

9 Proclaim ye this among the Gentiles; Prepare war, wake up the mighty men, let all the men of war draw near; let them come up:

10 Beat your plowshares into swords, and your pruninghooks into spears: let the weak say, I am strong.

11 Assemble yourselves, and come, all ye heathen, and gather yourselves together round about: thither cause thy mighty ones to come down, O LORD.

12 Let the heathen be wakened, and come up to the valley of Jehoshaphat: for there will I sit to judge all the heathen round about.

13 Put ye in the sickle, for the harvest is ripe: come, get you down; for the press is full, the fats overflow; for their wickedness is great.

14 Multitudes, multitudes in the valley of decision: for the day of the LORD is near in the valley of decision.

15 The sun and the moon shall be darkened, and the stars shall withdraw their shining.

16 The LORD also shall roar out of Zion, and utter his voice from Jerusalem; and the heavens and the earth shall shake: but the LORD will be the hope of his people, and the strength of the children of Israel.

17 So shall ye know that I am the LORD your God dwelling in Zion, my holy mountain: then shall Jerusalem be holy, and there shall no strangers pass through her any more.

There can be no mistaking of the passage. It is not the Last Judgment (see any work by any Postmillennial or Amillennial commentator). It is not the "forces of Satan" opposing the "Kingdom of God" (see any work by any Postmillennial or Amillennial commentator), and it certainly is not "the final victory of the church, ideally presented in a poetic setting" (see any of the above named). The events deal with the Battle of Armageddon and the Judgment of the Nations which follows.

"[Prepare war\...Beat your plowshares into swords, and your pruninghooks into spears" ]{.class_s323}[(vss. 9--10). We often see the reversed version (Isa. 2:4) quoted. As a matter of fact, it is printed on the side of the United []{#c1BX.xhtml_page_233}Nations building in New York---there's one for you! Thirty four wars in thirty years by a group which chose Isaiah 2:4 in a ]{.class_s321}[King James Bible ]{.class_s322}[for a "motto"! Ain't that a peacherino!]{.class_s321}

"[Let the weak say, I am strong" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 10). Notice the divine sarcasm, and compare it with this one: ]{.class_s321}["STAND, STAND, shall they cry; but none shall look back" ]{.class_s323}[(Nah. 2:8). The Lord's sense of humor is very ironic (see Mal. 3:15 and comments). The Lord says, "All right all of you 100-pound weaklings, kid yourself. Talk yourself into it. Convince yourself you're Charles Atlas or Paul Anderson. Think positively. Tell yourself 'I'M ALL RIGHT! I'M STRONG!' Then when you get into the battle holler, 'Stand, stand, don't give way, hold the line boys, stand!'Oh, yes---and one other thing---the people you'll be telling to 'stand' will be running so hard in the opposite direction they won't look over their shoulder to see who's doing the yelling." ]{.class_s321}["BUT NONE SHALL LOOK BACK" ]{.class_s323}[(Nah. 2:8).]{.class_s321}

"[Assemble yourselves, and come, all ye heathen, and gather yourselves together round about.... "]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 11). (Cf. Zeph. 3:8 and Matt. 13:30.)]{.class_s321}

[The hypersensitive Lockman Foundation (]{.class_s321}[NASV]{.class_s322}[), as the NCCC (]{.class_s321}[NRSV]{.class_s322}[), has a sneaking suspicion that the ]{.class_s321}["HEATHEN"]{.class_s323}[ referred to are Europeans, Americans, Africans, and Asiatics---which they ]{.class_s321}[are---]{.class_s322}[so, the word has been toned down nicely to "nations." One will find many of these "better" readings (to quote Aland and Metzger) in the ]{.class_s321}[NASV]{.class_s322}[, as it seeks to smooth over the stubborn text for "ears polite" and "snugly keep damnation out of sight."]{.class_s321}

"[Thither cause THY MIGHTY ONES TO COME DOWN, O LORD" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 11). ]{.class_s321}[There it is! ]{.class_s322}[There is that "Lord's Army" of Joel 2! There are those two military divisions of Judges 5 and Song of Solomon 6:13 typified by Genesis 32:2. ]{.class_s321}[There it is!]{.class_s322}

We have come to the Biblical clincher on Joel 2:1--11 that unseats the Scofield Board of Editors, the Lockman Foundation, the NCCC, the RCCC, the nineteenth and twentieth century "versions," plus 100 percent of the "recognized" scholars of ANY profession.

[The Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_s322}[(Dr. Given) almost has a heart attack and runs like a jet propelled jackrabbit back to Jerome and the Catholic ]{.class_s321}[Vulgate ]{.class_s322}[to turn the Lord's ]{.class_s321}["mighty ones"]{.class_s323}[ into the enemies of Israel (Vol. 13, p. 50). The corrupt ]{.class_s321}[RSV ]{.class_s322}[(1952) senses too much aerial action in the passage; so, they retranslate it as "bring down thy warriors, O Lord" (goodness to Betsy how proper!), and the ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[follows the Liberals as closely as it dares ("Bring down\...thy mighty ones).]{.class_s321}

[From the standpoint of Armageddon, the two ]{.class_s321}["multitudes" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 14) are a NORTHERN army (see Ezek. 38--39) and the ]{.class_s321}["kings of the EAST" ]{.class_s323}[(see Rev. 16:12). In respect to the ]{.class_s321}["valley of decision"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 14), they could turn out to be SHEEP and GOATS (see Matt. 25:31--45 and comments).]{.class_s321}

"[THE LORD ALSO SHALL\...UTTER HIS VOICE"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 16). Now this is the same Lord (2:11) who utters the same ]{.class_s321}[voice ]{.class_s322}[(2:11) before the same ]{.class_s321}[army ]{.class_s322}[(2:11), and it is ]{.class_s321}[His army ]{.class_s322}[(2:11), for it is composed of HIS MIGHTY ONES (3:11) who come down on the armies in the ]{.class_s321}["valley of decision"]{.class_s323}[ (3:14). The ]{.class_s321}[context ]{.class_s322}[of both passages is the same; neither has anything to do with an Antichrist's "northern army" called "the Lord's army," and neither has anything to do with some insects called ]{.class_s321}["my great army"]{.class_s323}[ in Joel 2:25. The insect army was ]{.class_s321}["SENT"']{.class_s323}[ (2:25); it certainly was not led down from heaven (Rev. 19:11--16) by a white horse rider (Rev. 19:11) who fights in the valley of]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s322}[Megiddo!]{.class_s321}

("Locusts," your pedal pushers!)

"[The lion of the tribe of Judah" ]{.class_s323}[(Rev. 5:5) is doing the roaring in verse 16 (see Jer. 25:30; Amos 1:2; Hosea 11:10; Job 37:4). He roars]{.class_s321}[ "out of ZION"]{.class_s323}[ (see 2:32) and ]{.class_s321}["FROM JERUSALEM"]{.class_s323}[ (see 2:32), in spite of the fact that the Bibler-ejecting apostates who translated the ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[altered Joel 2:32 to match the]{.class_s321}[ NEB ]{.class_s322}[and the ]{.class_s321}[NRSV ]{.class_s322}[of the NCCC.]{.class_s321}

[Go on check it, you truth-evading, truth ducking, professional ]{.class_s321}[liar. ]{.class_s322}[Go on! Holler about "uncharitable language," you dirty, two faced ]{.class_s321}[reprobate! ]{.class_s322}[Go on! Scream about "wild statements" and "fanatical nuts," just because you, yourself, are too rotten to stand for the truth or even check it out. Go on, check it out, if you []{#c1BX.xhtml_page_235}have the spiritual guts.]{.class_s321}

[The army of Joel 2:1--12 is the Body of Christ, plus the Old Testament Saints---"the chariots of Israel and the horseman thereof"---exactly as foretold by Enoch (Jude 14) ]{.class_s321}[IN NO BOOK THAT HE EVER WROTE.]{.class_s322}

[R. A. Cole says the meaning of the verse is "not clear" but perhaps it means that God is leading the heathen hordes into a trap. Wrong again. There isn't a "heathen" in the army that God causes to ]{.class_s321}["COME DOWN"]{.class_s323}[ (Rev. 19:11--16). They are clad in FINE WHITE LINEN (Rev. 19:8--10). With verse 13 right in front of their snoots (]{.class_s321}["PUT YE IN THE SICKLE"]{.class_s323}[), the commentators can no more place Revelation 14:14--16 rightly than dance on their left ear. Kimchi and Aben Ezra find the cross reference, but in the ]{.class_s321}[Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s322}[ (where they are quoted) Dr. Given places all such references in Revelation 20---]{.class_s321}[the last judgment of the unsaved dead]{.class_s322}[. Joel 3:11 and 13 has nothing to do with Revelation 20:11--15; it has to do with Revelation 14:14 and Revelation 19:11--16, which see.]{.class_s321}

"[For there will I sit to judge all the heathen round about" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 12). Again, the allusion is to the valley of Kedron at Jerusalem, for the Lord is on the Davidic Throne at Jerusalem (Matt. 25:31--45) when this judgment takes place (Matt. 19:28). Four localities for judgment and destruction, then, must be demarked if we are going to understand the Second Advent. One initial attack comes down the Palestinian coast from Damascus, via Tyre and Zidon. One attack proceeds from Mt. Sinai up the King's Highway across Jordan at Gilgal. One attack comes from the Mt. of Olives and goes westward over the valley of Jehoshaphat to Jerusalem, and a third attack comes down the valley of Megiddo to Jordan, via Jezreel.]{.class_s321}

"[For the press is full," ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 13). \[See Rev. 14:14--16 and comments and compare with Isaiah 63:1--8.\] Note the identical order in Revelation 14:15, 19 and also Isaiah 2:4. ]{.class_s321}["The fats overflow"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 13) \[see 2:24\]. The modern English word for ]{.class_s321}["fats"]{.class_s323}[ is "vats" (notice the German "v" pronounced "f'). The blood of the grape (Deut. 32:32--35) is squeezed out of the winepress to the horses bridles (Rev. 14:17--20) as the Lord Jesus Christ stomps (Isa. 63:1--8) []{#c1BX.xhtml_page_236}200,000,000 troops to death (see Psa. 68:23). INTRODUCTION

TO AMOS

The only "Amos" that most Americans know is "Amos and Andy," and when the violently racist NAACP banned it from radio and TV on the grounds of "freedom of speech," many Americans even lost track of that Amos.

Amos means "to burden," and anyone who reads the first two chapters of Amos sees the "burden" (cf. Jer. 23:36, 38) which Amos puts on his listeners.

[Amos is a shepherd from a small town in Judah named]{.class_s321}[ "Tekoah"]{.class_s323}[ (1:1). It is twelve miles from Jerusalem and about six miles southeast of Bethlehem. Although Amos is from ]{.class_s321}[Judah, ]{.class_s322}[he is called to prophesy against the ten northern tribes currently led by Jeroboam, II (vs. 1:1). Amos is a contemporary of ]{.class_s321}[Hosea, ]{.class_s322}[and he quotes his predecessor Joel in Amos 1:2. The book of Amos is quoted two times in the Book of Acts (Acts 7:42--43, 15:16--17).]{.class_s321}

[Bullinger limits Amos' prophesying to only ]{.class_s321}[three years ]{.class_s322}[(689--687 B.C.), whereas most commentators give it a stretch of at least thirty years; and, of course, their dating systems differ due to Bullinger's interpretation of "the fourth year of Jehoiakim" (Append. 86). A standard chronology would make Amos a prophet from 782--763 or 782--755 B.C. Since absolute dates cannot be established, we shall take 783--753 as a fairly good bracket for a ministry that occurred in ]{.class_s321}["the days of Uzziah king of Judah and in the days of Jeroboam" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 1).]{.class_s321}

The main critics of the book are J. Bright, Y. Kauffmann and C. Kuhl---who say that Amos never claimed to be a prophet (see 7:14 15); C. F. Whitley and J. Lindblom think Amos was trying to do away with all the God given sacrifices; G. P. Hyatt and J. W. Robinson think that Amos could not have known as much about God as appears in the book; and R. S. Cripps and G. E. Wright, who, etc., etc.

[Since "there are ]{.class_s321}[some ]{.class_s322}[who think," etc.---this is the gimmick used by John R. Rice, R. A. Torrey, Wilbur Smith, Philip Schaff, and 100 percent of the []{#c1EK.xhtml_page_243}Bible-rejecting Fundamentalists---that a certain thing "may or may not be," there is always enough leeway allowed by the "qualified authorities" to kick God Almighty out of His Book anytime they feel like it. Anyone can find a "good man" who doesn't ]{.class_s321}[think ]{.class_s322}[the Book means what it says. SO WHAT?]{.class_s321}

The main objection to the Book of Amos quite naturally centers around 9:8--15, since that passage deals with the literal restoration (permanently) of the nation of Israel. Every Postmillennial and Amillennial scholar from Augustine (A.D. 400) to A. T. Robertson (A.D. 1940) finds something wrong with the wording in the passage since he subjectively rejected it before he began to try to "find out what it taught." You can never learn what it teaches till you believe what it SAYS.

[Since Amos is a prophet, he FOREtells the future exactly as any prophet does (see Gen. 20:7 and comments). Amos is a prophet to whom God reveals things (Amos 3:7), and he is no more afraid of the "face of man" (Amos 7:16--17) than the face of a wounded butterfly. There are ten mighty verses in the Book of Amos that have a shattering effect on the modern Bible rejecting "Fundamentalist," such as the translators of the ]{.class_s321}[NASV]{.class_s322}[; these neatly spaced time bombs are Amos 3:3, Amos 3:7, Amos 4:3, Amos 5:8, Amos 5:25, 26, Amos 6:12, Amos 8:11, and Amos 9:11--12.]{.class_s321}

[The extremely and grossly corrupt ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[(being promoted by many Christian colleges and universities) has to place the entire ninth chapter in "poetic form" to match the ]{.class_s321}[RSV ]{.class_s322}[and ]{.class_s321}[NRSV of the National Council of Christian Churches. ]{.class_s322}[And this is understandable, for Postmillennialists and Amillennialists have their prestige, incomes, and social images intertwined with the Lockman Foundation, and they cannot afford to take a STRONG position on Premillennialism. CHAPTER ONE

1:1 The words of Amos, who was among the herdmen of Tekoa, which he saw concerning Israel in the days of Uzziah king of Judah, and in the days of Jeroboam the son of Joash king of Israel, two years before the earthquake.

2 And he said, The LORD will roar from Zion, and utter his voice from Jerusalem; and the habitations of the shepherds shall mourn, and the top of Carmel shall wither.

3 Thus saith the LORD; For three transgressions of Damascus, and for four, I will not turn away the punishment thereof; because they have threshed Gilead with threshing instruments of iron:

4 But I will send a fire into the house of Hazael, which shall devour the palaces of Benhadad.

5 will break also the bar of Damascus, and cut off the inhabitant from the plain of Aven, and him that holdeth the sceptre from the house of Eden: and the people of Syria shall go into captivity unto Kir, saith the LORD.

Furthermore, preceding the coming of the Lord's army (see Joel 2:1--10), ]{.class_s321}["all the kingdoms of the world, WHICH ARE UPON THE FACE OF THE EARTH" ]{.class_s323}[(Jer. 25:26) will get a taste of Isaiah 28:21, 22 and 29 as the UFO makes a ]{.class_s321}["SHADOW OF DEATH" ]{.class_s323}[under it (Isa. 9:2; Psa. 34:19; Jer. 13:16; and Amos 5:8).]{.class_s321}

Amos is telling us that before Christ lands on the Mount of Olives (Zech. 14:4), He has some "mopping up" to do. One route will take Him from Sinai up through Edom, Moab, and Ammon into Judah, and the other route will take Him from Damascus down through Tyre and Sidon into Israel and Philistia. "Armageddon," therefore, is not as easy as it looks. The battle covers everything from Mt. Carmel and Megiddo (see Zech. 12:11) to Mt. Sinai and Bozrah (Hab. 3:1--14), and this explains the "space" of Revelation 14:20, which see.

10 But I will send a fire on the wall of Tyrus, which shall devour the palaces thereof.

Punishment that led to obliteration has been ]{.class_s321}["turned away"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 3) where the transgression was not persecution of God's people (see Jonah 3:1--10), but the thing that all of the following have in common is the killing, selling, or threatening of the Children of Israel.]{.class_s321}

CHAPTER TWO

2:1 Thus saith the LORD; For three transgressions of Moab, and for four, I will not turn away the punishment thereof; because he burned the bones of the king of Edom into lime:

2 But I will send a fire upon Moab, and it shall devour the palaces of Kerioth: and Moab shall die with tumult, with shouting, and with the sound of the trumpet:

3 And I will cut off the judge from the midst thereof, and will slay all the princes thereof with him, saith the LORD.

4 Thus saith the LORD; For three transgressions of Judah, and for four, I will not turn away the punishment thereof; because they have despised the law of the LORD, and have not kept his commandments, and their lies caused them to err, after the which their fathers have walked:

5 But I will send a fire upon Judah, and it shall devour the palaces of Jerusalem.

6 Thus saith the LORD; For three transgressions of Israel, and for four, I will not turn away the punishment thereof; because they sold the righteous for silver, and the poor for a pair of shoes;

7 That pant after the dust of the earth on the head of the poor, and turn aside the way of the meek: and a man and his father will go in unto the same maid, to profane my holy name:

8 And they lay themselves down upon clothes laid to pledge by every altar, and they drink the wine of the condemned in the house of their god.

We have printed three paragraphs running, as they conclude with God's judgments on Israel itself, plainly classifying Israel with the heathen mentioned in []{#c1HN.xhtml_page_257}the previous chapter (see Amos 9:7).

[Verse 1 confirms a similar reference in 2 Kings 3:27 where the King of Moab offers his son up for a burnt offering. Later, he probably burns one of his enemies to powder (see 2 Kings 3:8) because the King of Edom was an ally of Israel at this time. Thus, the Lord determines not only judgment on Edom for persecuting the Jews (1:11), but judgment on Moab for attacking Edom when they were lined up ]{.class_s321}[with the Jews. ]{.class_s322}["He that keepeth Israel" ]{.class_s323}[(Psa. 121:4) does not slumber or sleep! ]{.class_s321}["Kirioth: and Moab shall die with tumult, with SHOUTING, and with THE SOUND OF THE TRUMPET" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 2). Notice the reference to the home of the Son of Perdition (Kerioth) stated in Jeremiah 48:24, 41 (Iscariot means "a man of Kerioth"). Judas was a ]{.class_s321}[half breed ]{.class_s322}[Moabite Jew from Syria (see Larkin's exposition on ]{.class_s321}["the King of the North," ]{.class_s323}[Dan. 11:13), and he is the only disciple who came from "outside the land." ]{.class_s321}["The JUDGE"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 3) is a direct reference to a MAN---not the land of Moab---and the ]{.class_s321}["princes thereof with HIM" ]{.class_s323}[is the same kind of a reference (see Isa. 10:5). Amos never loses contact with Daniers Seventieth Week, although Mauro, Allis, Machen, Robertson, Davis, Strong, Calvin, Berkhof, and Dabney never picked it up on the CB long enough to get a 10 20 on it.]{.class_s321}

[And now the chickens come home to roost: ]{.class_s321}["For three transgressions of JUDAH"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 4). They subtracted from the original manuscripts (Jer. 36:23) while professing to believe in "Godbreathed" something-or-the others (Jer. 23:16). ]{.class_s321}["Because they have DESPISED the law of the LORD" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 4). \[See comments under Prov. 13:13 in that Commentary.\] They said that the table of the Lord was ]{.class_s321}["contemptible" ]{.class_s323}[(see Mal. 1:7 and comments). They treated the living words of the living God (Deut. 8:3) exactly as they treated the manna (Num. 21:5). They forsook the living word, the living water (Jer. 2:13), the living bread (John 6:57), and the living Bible (A.D. 1611), and invented lying words (Jer. 23:32), stinking cisterns (2 Pet. 2:17), moldy bread (Matt. 13:33), and "bibles" that were no more "living" than a wrecked Edsel.]{.class_s321}

[The second flock of chickens take the roost: ]{.class_s321}["For three transgressions of ISRAEL" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 6). Although God was "known" in the palaces of Judah (Psa. 48:3), God sends a fire upon Judah (2:5), and Israel will not escape. Israel's sins are enumerated, and you can be sure that when Amos named them, he called them out on the streets of Bethaven with one hand on the golden calf and one hand raised straight up in the air.]{.class_s321}

"[They sold the righteous for silver, and the poor for a pair of shoes" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 6). They bribed the judges (Deut. 16:19) with silver so that the righteous man lost his case in court; they picked up a thrown off shoe as a title deed (see Ruth 4:7, Psa. 60:8) and drove the poor man off his property. Amos lays it on thick---just like the Lord did in Matthew 23:14 and Paul did in Titus 1:12. Amos says, "You're so hungry for real estate that when you see a poor man go down the street with dirt on his head (vs. 7) your tongue hangs out and you 'pant' to get the deed to his dandruff." Senile and anemic commentators who cannot stand this ironic sarcasm have decided the verse meant that "the poor you ran off are mourning with dust on their heads and their oppressors can't wait to oppress them." More jelly rolls with whipped cream.]{.class_s321}

[Dummelow says "they begrudge the very dust," and Bullinger surrenders to the Devil long enough to alter the verse to read "they crush the head of the poor ones in the dust." There are various other ways to get around Biblical truth that God is often SARCASTIC (see Amos 4:4 and Mal. 3:15) on occasions, but rejection of Biblical doctrine---especially where it deals with the ]{.class_s321}[nature of the One who revealed Himsetf in the Bible---]{.class_s322}[is quite the style these days. Harnack has somebody smiting poor people, Bewer has somebody getting pulverized, and Nowack (always good for a laugh) has somebody getting trodden under foot, while George Smith thinks they were "trampled" instead of "pulverized." Since ]{.class_s321}[nothing ]{.class_s322}[in the verse indicated ]{.class_s321}[anything of the kind, ]{.class_s322}[directly or indirectly, it might do well to send Marti, Nowack, Smith, and Bewer back to a theological seminary where they won't interfere with serious Bible study.]{.class_s321}

[The Lord is being ]{.class_s321}[sarcastic ]{.class_s322}[in Amos 2:7, and if you took what He said []{#c1HN.xhtml_page_259}and drew it out as a cartoon, it would look like something from ]{.class_s321}[Mad Magazine, ]{.class_s322}[not ]{.class_s321}[The Biblical Viewpoint.]{.class_s322}

The rest of verse 7 is self explanatory (see Lev. 18:21, 20:3).

"[And they lay themselves down upon clothes laid to pledge" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 8). \[See Deut. 24:12 and Exod. 22:26 where these clothes are to be returned to the poor man to sleep in.\] ]{.class_s321}["By EVERY ALTAR" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 8) indicates the one true altar at Jerusalem had been forsaken (see 1 Kings 13:1--6 for the time of this).]{.class_s321}

"[They drink the wine of the condemned in the house of their god" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 8).
