There is nothing "accidental" or "unintentional" about 20,000 errors in The International Version or the ]{.class_s321}[NASV]{.class_s322}[.]{.class_s321}

9 Yet destroyed I the Amorite before them, whose height was like the height of the cedars, and he was strong as the oaks; yet I destroyed his fruit from above, and his roots from beneath.

10 Also I brought you up from the land of Egypt, and led you forty years through the wilderness, to possess the land of the Amorite.

11 And I raised up of your sons for prophets, and of your young men for Nazarites. Is it not even thus, O ye children of Israel? saith the LORD.

12 But ye gave the Nazarites wine to drink; and commanded the prophets, saying, Prophesy not.

13 Behold, I am pressed under you, as a cart is pressed that is full of sheaves.

14 Therefore the flight shall perish from the swift, and the strong shall not strengthen his force, neither shall the mighty deliver himself:

15 Neither shall he stand that handleth the bow; and he that is swift of foot shall not deliver himself: neither shall he that rideth the horse deliver himself.

16 And he that is courageous among the mighty shall flee away naked in that day, saith the LORD.

[Observe the immediate reference to the "giants" (vs. 9), following the blood []{#c1HN.xhtml_page_261}offerings (Deut. 32:32--34) to devils (Psa. 16:4). Notice, also, that along with the blood offerings are ]{.class_s321}["oaks" ]{.class_s323}[(Hosea 4:13), rabdomancy (Hos. 4:12) and ]{.class_s321}["WHORES" ]{.class_s323}[(Hos. 4:12). This is the Universal Brotherhood-of-Man-Fatherhood-of-God-United Nations-SovietCatholic-One World Religion of 1990--2000 (see Rev. 13, 17 and comments). The giants will return. (As a matter of fact, at this writing several "sightings" may have been made, but quite naturally they have been attributed to hallucinations or psychopathic inventions.)]{.class_s321}

"[And he was strong AS THE OAKS" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 9). \[See above and Genesis 35:4.\]]{.class_s321}

"[His fruit from above, and his roots from beneath" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 9). Satan and his ]{.class_s321}["princes"]{.class_s323}[ (Isa. 10:10) are likened to TREES (see Ezek. 31:3). The ]{.class_s321}["green bay tree" ]{.class_s323}[(Psa. 37:35) pictures the Antichrist as the]{.class_s321}[ "cedar"]{.class_s323}[ pictures Satan (Ezek. 31:9). \[See comments on Gen. 2:9 in that Commentary.)]{.class_s321}

Verse 10 is self explanatory. \[See Num. 21:21--25; Josh. 24:8, 7:7; and Deut. 1:20.\]

"[And I raised up of your sons for prophets, and of your young men for Nazarites" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 11). It is "so" (see text); Samuel, Samson, and John the Baptist were all separated according to Numbers 6:1--10. The Nazarite was a radical demonstration to Israel of "separation" from the world, and he was a constant reminder wherever he went that Israel was not to "be like other people" (Exod. 19:5 and comments). Whereas the average Jew let his hair grow down his neck to ]{.class_s321}[the collar ]{.class_s322}[(and had a beard---see Lev. 19:27), the Nazarite had hair down the middle of his back like a woman (Num. 6:1--10). Absalom counterfeited the Nazarites' consecration (see 2 Sam. 14:26) as the Antichrist will counterfeit Christ's holiness (Rev. 13).]{.class_s321}

"[But ye gave the Nazarites wine to drink; and commanded the prophets, saying, Prophesy not" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 12). A perfect example is found right in Amos 7:12--13. The land "could not bear" Jeremiah's prophesies (Jer. 38:4) any more than the cities of Asia Minor could stand Paul's preaching (Acts 13:50). The real truth, the whole truth, and "nothing but the truth" has always been an inflaming commodity, and even when it is going to be rationed, there is []{#c1HN.xhtml_page_262}no rush to stock up on it. ]{.class_s321}[There never has been a time in the history of mankind where the TR UTH about races, religions, life after death, evolution, morals, or AUTHORITY was welcomed by a majority--SAVED OR LOST.]{.class_s322}[ \[I have seen born-again, "soulwinning," Christian celebrities get more torn up over a ]{.class_s321}[three minute statement on the Authority of the AV (1611) ]{.class_s322}[than an unsaved drunkard was over a forty five minute message on total abstinence. And you'd better believe it, friend, I've seen it.\]]{.class_s321}

They got their Nazarites drunk and shut the mouths of their prophets, and when they did it, they cut their national and spiritual throats just as neatly as a man would slice his gullet with a barber's razor.

"[Behold, I am pressed under you" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 13). None of the commentators even checked Isaiah 1:13, 24, which is the exact counterpart. Instead, they began to run to Hiphils, Niphals, Hophals, compensatory heightenings, and compound shewas like there was no authority on this earth but a Hebrew lexicon compiled by an unsaved Jew.]{.class_s321}

[The ]{.class_s321}[ASV ]{.class_s322}[says, "I will press you in your place," the ]{.class_s321}[RSV ]{.class_s322}[says, "I will press you down," the ]{.class_s321}[NEB ]{.class_s322}[says, "I groan under the burden," ]{.class_s321}[The "Living" Bible ]{.class_s322}[says, "I will make you groan," others say, "I will depress your place," "I will make it narrow," etc., etc.]{.class_s321}

[Since the ]{.class_s321}[King James]{.class_s322}[ text is far superior to the most brilliant, "dedicated, godly, recognized, qualified authorities" who ever lived (in ]{.class_s321}[any ]{.class_s322}[century), we shall accept ]{.class_s321}["I AM PRESSED UNDER YOU"]{.class_s323}[ as the infallible truth and dismiss the conjectures of Esau's brethren as "pottage." The Lord is tired of carrying the load, and that is the matter, plain and simple. He has been carrying Israel as a mother eagle (Deut. 32:11) and as a nursing father for 800 years, and He now feels like a cart that is nearly broken down from carrying too heavy a load. Again, the Divine sarcasm is so evident that not one "scholar" out of ten can stand for the Lord speaking in such a manner; so, His speech has been knocked out of His mouth and the verse altered to fit the great new "scientific Fundamental scholarship."]{.class_s321}

[Verse 14 is self explanatory: Swift, strong, or mighty, the Israelites are going to be overtaken, overcome, and taken captive (2 Kings 17). ]{.class_s321}["Neither shall he stand that handleth the bow\...neither shall he that rideth the horse deliver himself"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 15). And these judgments prophesied on Israel reach out beyond 720 B.C. to ]{.class_s321}["THAT DAY" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 16) when ALL of the mighty, swift, and strong flee naked (Zech. 14:12).]{.class_s321}

[]{#c1K5.xhtml}

CHAPTER THREE

3:1 Hear this word that the LORD hath spoken against you, O children of Israel, against the whole family which I brought up from the land of Egypt, saying,

2 You only have I known of all the families of the earth: therefore I will punish you for all your iniquities.

3 Can two walk together, except they be agreed?

4 Will a lion roar in the forest, when he hath no prey? will a young lion cry out of his den, if he have taken nothing?

5 Can a bird fall in a snare upon the earth, where no gin is for him? shall one take up a snare from the earth, and have taken nothing at all?

6 Shall a trumpet be blown in the city, and the people not be afraid? shall there be evil in a city, and the LORD hath not done it?

7 Surely the Lord GOD will do nothing, but he revealeth his secret unto his servants the prophets.

8 The lion hath roared, who will not fear? the Lord GOD hath spoken, who can but prophesy?

[Verse 1 is self explanatory (cf. Hos. 12:9 and 13:4). ]{.class_s321}["You only have I known of all the families of the earth" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 2). Now notice how this matches Matthew 7:23 where, in both cases, knowledge is present in regard to the other ]{.class_s321}["families" ]{.class_s323}[and the unsaved professors, but, in both cases, intimate, personal acquaintanceship is denied. God knows ]{.class_s321}[everything]{.class_s322}[ (1 Chron. 28:9) and ]{.class_s321}[everybody ]{.class_s322}[(Matt. 10:30), but He will deny knowledge of ]{.class_s321}[anyone ]{.class_s322}[(or any nation) where that person (or nation) does not stand in an intimate relation of ]{.class_s321}[fellowship ]{.class_s322}[with Him. Christ KNOWS His sheep (John 10:14), but He does not KNOW the "workers of iniquity" (Matt. 7:23); yet ]{.class_s321}["ALL THINGS are naked []{#c1K5.xhtml_page_265}and opened unto the eyes of him with whom we have to do" ]{.class_s323}[(Heb. 4:12--13). In the same way, no man can "SEE GOD" without ]{.class_s321}[holiness ]{.class_s322}[(Heb. 12:14), but ]{.class_s321}[every ]{.class_s322}[human being will surely SEE God (Phil. 2:11), ]{.class_s321}[holy or not ]{.class_s322}[(Rev. 20:11--15).]{.class_s321}

[The former case refers to the split rapture at the end of the Tribulation (see Heb. 12:14), but the verses have been changed in the ]{.class_s321}[NIV ]{.class_s322}[and the ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[so that saved preachers could not locate either doctrine. One must never forget that ]{.class_s321}[rejection of SOUND DOCTRINE is the mark of modern, Conservative scholarship ]{.class_s322}[(2 Tim. 4:1--6).]{.class_s321}

[For further use of the word ]{.class_s321}["know" ]{.class_s323}[in the Bible, consult Psalm 1:6, 144:3; Exodus 19:5; and Deuteronomy 4:20.]{.class_s321}

"[CAN TWO WALK TOGETHER, EXCEPT THEY BE AGREED"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 3)?]{.class_s321}

Ah, there is one of those verses like Proverbs 13:13, 18:1; Romans 1:25; or Colossians 2:8! There is one of those thornbushes that crops up in the word of God like a hedge of bayonets. Now watch the "good, godly, dedicated, qualified authorities" go to pieces on that one.

Here they come---the textbooks of the Alexandrian Cult.

[1. ]{.class_s321}[NASV:]{.class_s322}[ "unless they have made an APPOINTMENT?" Sure. A lot of folks ]{.class_s321}[walk together ]{.class_s322}[that haven't made an "appointment" before they met or ]{.class_s321}[after ]{.class_s322}[they started. The reading is absolutely ridiculous, and it would be an absurdity even if it wasn't put in a "bible."]{.class_s321}

[2. The grossly corrupt ]{.class_s321}[RSV:]{.class_s322}[ "unless they have made an APPOINTMENT?" You ]{.class_s321}[see, the RSV beat the NASV to the punch,]{.class_s322}[ so the "Conservative" translators of the ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[(recommended by the Congress of Fundamentalists) simply copied out the reading of the Liberal ]{.class_s321}[RSV]{.class_s322}[, written fifteen years before them.]{.class_s321}

[You see, the ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[and the ]{.class_s321}[RSV ]{.class_s322}[can]{.class_s321}[ "WALK TOGETHER"]{.class_s323}[ because they are ]{.class_s321}["AGREED"]{.class_s323}[ (see text). They "have an appointment" for ]{.class_s321}[apostasy.]{.class_s322}

[3. ]{.class_s321}[The "Living" Bible: ]{.class_s322}["How can we walk together\....?" Easy. Ignore the []{#c1K5.xhtml_page_266}Bible and you'll get along fine.]{.class_s321}

[4. ]{.class_s321}[The New English Bible: ]{.class_s322}["unless they HAVE agreed." Sure. A lot of folks walk together that didn't agree on anything ]{.class_s321}[when they started ]{.class_s322}[but get along as fine as seven in a bed ]{.class_s321}[after ]{.class_s322}[they all agree that the ]{.class_s321}[AV ]{.class_s322}[is to be gotten rid of.]{.class_s321}

[No Fundamentalist or Conservative AGREED with the NCCC and the Liberals when they ]{.class_s321}[began ]{.class_s322}[their walk; they only agreed to travel together AFTER they accepted the destructive criticisms of Schaff, A. T. Robertson, and Hort.]{.class_s321}

[5. The ]{.class_s321}[LXX:]{.class_s322}[ "if they do not know one another?" Of course, it happens thirty thousand times a day everywhere in the world. A lot of people walk together who don't ]{.class_s321}[know ]{.class_s322}[each other, and where they get to know each other, they often quit "walking together." The reading is nonsense.]{.class_s321}

[Now the reason for all of this fantastic static and "jamming the ten meter band" is the fact that verse 3 is as strong a statement against integration and ecumenicism as you will find in the Bible outside of 2 Corinthians 6:18. And while BJU may profess "secondary separation," the cultists in their Bible department are just as apostate as Union Theological Seminary ]{.class_s321}[when it comes to going along with the worlds "bibles" and the Liberals' "bible." ]{.class_s322}[The ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[and the ]{.class_s321}[RSV ]{.class_s322}[in the New Testament ]{.class_s321}[are the same Greek text. ]{.class_s322}[And while Tennessee Temple may blow hot air about "a stand against modernism," the Bibles they recommend (the ]{.class_s321}[ASV ]{.class_s322}[and the ]{.class_s321}[NASV]{.class_s322}[) are Liberal trash and are as rotten to the core as the worst Bible ever published by the National Council of Churches. Thus the ]{.class_s321}[ASV ]{.class_s322}[has placed the ]{.class_s321}[RSV ]{.class_s322}[reading in its ]{.class_s321}[margin ]{.class_s322}[(1901) and then moved the whole camel into the tent in 1969 in the ]{.class_s321}[NASV]{.class_s322}[. Typical. Typical apostasy.]{.class_s321}

[But ]{.class_s321}["CAN TWO WALK TOGETHER, except they be AGREED" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 3)? ]{.class_s321}[No, they cannot.]{.class_s322}

[God cannot ]{.class_s321}[walk ]{.class_s322}[with a nation if that nation is going to have fellowship with "gods" at the Devil's table (see 2:8). God cannot "walk" with a child of the Devil (John 8:40--44) who is trying to justify himself with filthy rags (Isa. 64:6). And God cannot "walk" (in the sense of "fellowship") with a Christian (Rom. 8:10--13) []{#c1K5.xhtml_page_267}who walks after the flesh (2 Cor. 7:1) instead of after the Spirit (Gal. 5:22). Every question that God asks in this passage (two in verse 4, two in verse 5, and two in verse 6) demands a response of "NO."]{.class_s321}

[Can a saved woman and an unsaved man build a home together (2 Cor. 6--14)? No. Can a Caucasian retain his music and his morals walking with an African? No. Can a Bible-believing Christian walk together with a Bible perverting "Fundamentalist"? No. He can ]{.class_s321}[love ]{.class_s322}[him, he can ]{.class_s321}[pray ]{.class_s322}[for him, and he can ]{.class_s321}[assist him ]{.class_s322}[when he is trying to win people to Christ, but a "walk" is out of the question. ]{.class_s321}["We walk in the LIGHT, as He is in the light" ]{.class_s323}[(1 John 1:7); His word is ]{.class_s321}["LIGHT"]{.class_s323}[ (Psa. 119:105), and in it is NO DARKNESS (Psa. 12:6). So, if our dearly beloved, "dedicated, godly, GOOD men" are going to turn their back on it---any part of it, any time, any place, any where---then we are going to walk ]{.class_s321}[alone, ]{.class_s322}[for two CANNOT walk together ]{.class_s321}["except they be AGREED."]{.class_s323}

It's like a man said, "We broke up over religious convictions. She worshipped money, and I didn't have any." Another "two walking together" went by a field where a yoke of two mules were pulling a plow. The feminine of the species pointed to the mules and said: "You see how harmoniously they get along together. They're not arguing." To which the male of the species answered, "Well, we could too if we only had one TONGUE between us."

[The lesson for the Bible believer is obvious. You will never cross anyone walking WITH them, so if you are walking with the ]{.class_s321}[Devil, ]{.class_s322}[you need not expect any trouble from ]{.class_s321}[him. ]{.class_s322}[In regard to the Bible believing Christian uniting with the Bible destroying Cultists like A. T. Robertson, Bruce Metzger, Aland, Nestle, Wuest, and Thieme, it may be said flatly that you can tie a bobcat and a bulldog together to get "union," but you sure won't have any "UNITY." They are not the same word.]{.class_s321}

"[Will a lion roar in the forest\...if he have taken nothing" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 4)? No, he won't. He will roar to scare the prey before attacking it (vs. 4), and he will sound the victory call if he takes it (vs. 4), but lions don't roar to hear themselves roar. ]{.class_s321}["Can a bird\...shall one take up" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 5). No, a bird can't be caught in a net trap if it isn't set for him, and a fowler will not take up the net []{#c1K5.xhtml_page_268}snare from the ground until he has snared something in it; he will let it lie there till something gets caught in it. ]{.class_s321}["Shall a trumpet be blown\...and the people not be afraid"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 6)? Not unless it is some fool practical joker; and, even then, if he blows it, it will make everyone's hair raise on their scalp like it would at the sound of an air raid warning. Will Smokey ("the bear in the grass") show "lit candles" and not bring you down to double nickels? ]{.class_s321}["Shall there be evil in a city, and the LORD hath not done it"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 6)? No. The LORD is the eventual author of ]{.class_s321}["good and evil" ]{.class_s323}[(Job 2:10) in that He ]{.class_s321}[allows ]{.class_s322}[evil to overtake sinners (Lam. 3:38), and He knows ]{.class_s321}[when ]{.class_s322}[it is coming, and He refuses to]{.class_s321}[ stop it]{.class_s322}[ (Jer. 26:13, 19). No amount of molding and chiseling or carving and shaping a respectable "god" has ever altered the terrible facts of life. "The God who IS" allows murder, rape, misunderstandings, muggings, death, starvation, torture, extortion, mutilation, disease, bankruptcy, disability, war, sin, and natural disasters (Luke 13:13). If you are a Christ rejecting fool, there are only two things you can do with it: You either have to become an ]{.class_s321}[atheist ]{.class_s322}[on the grounds that there couldn't be any God who would allow those things, or you have to become a Liberal and pretend that everything ]{.class_s321}[negative is ]{.class_s322}[due to man's IGNORANCE, and therefore, ]{.class_s321}[education ]{.class_s322}[and ]{.class_s321}[science ]{.class_s322}[will iron out everything listed above. To believe this latter "theology," you have to be about half crazy, for neither "science" nor "education" (nor both combined) have ever solved ]{.class_s321}[one problem ]{.class_s322}[listed above for even a two year stretch since Cain knocked Abel's brains out.]{.class_s321}

"[Surely the LORD God will do NOTHING, but he revealeth his secret unto his servants the prophets" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 7). This places Paul in the ]{.class_s321}[prophet's ]{.class_s322}[category (Eph. 3:1--6). The Lord Jesus Christ, obviously, is ]{.class_s321}[a prophet]{.class_s322}[ (Acts 7:37; John 9:17). The counterfeits (see Gen. 20:7 and comments) who mistake race wars, strike riots, social upheavels, lowering of moral standards, and mongrelizing of the masses for "prophetic insights" and "prophetic dreams" cannot compete with the real prophet who has an inside track (1 Kings 14:5) since the chances of his prophesies coming true are 100 out of 100 (1 Kings 13:2).]{.class_s321}

[Contrary to popular belief (we are here making reference to the standard theologies taught in any Christian university), the Lord reveals a great deal (1 Cor. 2:11--12) to a Bible believer (Deut. 29:29) that is purposely kept hidden (Luke 10:21) from a Fundamentalist "nut" who thinks he is smart enough (Isa. 28:9) to "interpret" Scripture (Luke 24:45) in the light of Greek and Hebrew etymology (Dan. 2:28). If there is one thing the Bible is clear on, it is clear on the fact that ]{.class_s321}[linguistic training ]{.class_s322}[never equipped any saved sinner to interpret ]{.class_s321}[anything.]{.class_s322}

[All the sons of the prophets knew the ]{.class_s321}[day ]{.class_s322}[for Elijah's rapture. (Think about that awhile!) Paul and Peter knew within a matter of WEEKS their execution days. Christ knew His within one hour. Nothing could prevent the Holy Spirit from revealing the ]{.class_s321}[moment ]{.class_s322}[of the Rapture to 400,000 Christians at the same time; but it would be just like the Lord to reveal it about thirty minutes before it took place. The Lord often plays practical jokes (Gen. 17:17--19) on people (Gen. 19:25).]{.class_s321}

"[The Lion hath roared, who will not fear" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 8)? Nobody, unless they don't have any sense to start with (see Prov. 1:7 and comments). ]{.class_s321}["The Lord God hath spoken, who can but prophesy" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 8)? In Numbers 24:1--4 they had to and Saul had to (1 Sam. 19:24); when God speaks it BURNS (Luke 24:32), and you can't keep your mouth shut (2 Cor. 4:13).]{.class_s321}

9 Publish in the palaces at Ashdod, and in the palaces in the land of Egypt, and say, Assemble yourselves upon the mountains of Samaria, and behold the great tumults in the midst thereof, and the oppressed in the midst thereof.

10 For they know not to do right, saith the LORD, who store up violence and robbery in their palaces.

11 Therefore thus saith the Lord GOD; An adversary there shall be even round about the land; and he shall bring down thy strength from thee, and thy palaces shall be spoiled.

12 Thus saith the LORD; As the shepherd taketh out of the mouth of the lion two legs, or a piece of an ear; so shall []{#c1K5.xhtml_page_270}the children of Israel be taken out that dwell in Samaria in the corner of a bed, and in Damascus in a couch.

13 Hear ye, and testify in the house of Jacob, saith the Lord GOD, the God of hosts,

14 That in the day that I shall visit the transgressions of Israel upon him I will also visit the altars of Bethel: and the horns of the altar shall be cut off, and fall to the ground.

15 And I will smite the winter house with the summer house; and the houses of ivory shall perish, and the great houses shall have an end, saith the LORD.

[Ashdod and Egypt are summoned to witness the oppression and violence that is going on in Israel. The idea is that not even the ]{.class_s321}[heathen ]{.class_s322}[are any worse; and, then again, if God is about to judge Israel for these things, the heathen had better take heed or ]{.class_s321}[they will be standing in line for the same payoff ]{.class_s322}[(see Isa. 10: 12).]{.class_s321}

[The word ]{.class_s321}["ASHDOD" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 9) is a special warning connected with the "bastard at Ashdod" (see Amos 1:8 and Zech. 9:6), so the word has swiftly been removed from the ]{.class_s321}[RSV]{.class_s322}[, who got their permission to remove it from the post-Christian "LXX," written 200 years after the completion of the New Testament.]{.class_s321}

[Egypt and Ashdod are invited to]{.class_s321}[ "assemble"]{.class_s323}[ themselves on the mountains of Samaria for a ringside seat on the ]{.class_s321}["great tumults in the midst" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 9) of Israel. They ]{.class_s321}["store up violence and robbery in their palaces" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 10). \[See Prov. 10:2.\] The goods gotten by violence and robbery will only ]{.class_s321}["cry out"]{.class_s323}[ against the wall and the timber (see Hab. 2:11) until a man's household will look like the Kennedy family following their bootleg operations during World War II.]{.class_s321}

Verse 11 is self explanatory. The adversary, in the immediate case, is the Assyrian of 2 Kings 17; in the distant future it is the Man of Sin.

"[As the shepherd taketh out of the mouth of the lion two []{#c1K5.xhtml_page_271}legs, or a piece of an ear; so shall the children of Israel" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 12). Like a brand plucked from the burning (Zech. 3:2), all that will remain of Israel is a few pieces that didn't get chewed up (see Isa. 6:13). The ones that are not "taken out" (vs. 12) of Samaria and Damascus get murdered on their "beds" and "couches" (vs. 12). They are ]{.class_s321}["at ease in Zion" ]{.class_s323}[(see 6:1) lying upon beds and couches of ivory (6:4); so they will be murdered like Ishbosheth was murdered (2 Sam. 4:4--8).]{.class_s321}

Verse 13 is self explanatory.

"[I will also visit the altars of Beth el" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 14) where Jeroboam set up the golden calf. ]{.class_s321}["And the horns of the altar shall be cut off, and fall to the ground" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 14), plainly showing that Amos is speaking of the Second Advent. Jeroboam's original altar already was cut off; it had fallen to the ground back in 1 Kings 13 before Amos began to preach. And although it was probably rebuilt, it was completely demolished under Josiah in 2 Kings 23:15. And ]{.class_s321}["in the day" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 14) cannot be during the reign of Josiah, for Israel had already been in captivity ]{.class_s321}[before ]{.class_s322}[Josiah found Jeroboam's altar. The ]{.class_s321}["altars of Bether, ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 14) are yet to show up, and the golden calves will be there when they show up.]{.class_s321}

Verse 15 is clear (see Psa. 45:8; Jer. 36:22; and 1 Kings 22:39).

We have already examined the "wily weavers" at work on Amos 3:3, and we shall be as brief as possible in listing their accumulated errors throughout the rest of the chapter.

[1. The phony LXX---which was nowhere on this earth before the time of Origen (185--254)---has written "THE ASSYRIANS" for the word ]{.class_s321}["Ashdod" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 9), in spite of the fact that the Hebrew word is ]{.class_s321}["Ashdod,"]{.class_s323}[ exactly as it is found in every other place in the Old Testament. "And SHE knew not what things" has been substituted for ]{.class_s321}["they know not to do right" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 10). Verse 11 has been knocked out of orbit into "O Tyre thy land shall be made desolate...."---which occurs in no Hebrew manuscripts; and "Hear O YE PRIESTS" has been stuck into verse 13. (And imagine some textual critic telling you that "the ]{.class_s321}[shorter]{.class_s322}[ reading is preferable" when the hypocrite substitutes ]{.class_s321}[]{#c1K5.xhtml_page_272}[Vaticanus ]{.class_s322}[and ]{.class_s321}[Sinaiti]{.class_s322}[cus for the Receptus in the New Testament. Knowing that ]{.class_s321}[both of these corrupt corruptions ]{.class_s322}[have ADDED so many words in the Old Testament that you couldn't collect them in an eighteen wheeler!)]{.class_s321}

[2. The ]{.class_s321}[RSV ]{.class_s322}[(1952) alters the Masoretic text inverse 5 to read, "Does a snare spring up." Then it has adopted the archaic LXX reading "Assyrian" (from A.D. 330) after the Body of Christ rejected the reading. And no one is hauled off from their bed or couch (vs. 12); to the contrary, Israel is to be "rescued with the corner of a couch." (Let's see you try that!)]{.class_s321}

4:1 Hear this word, ye kine of Bashan, that are in the mountain of Samaria, which oppress the poor, which crush the needy, which say to their masters, Bring, and let us drink.

2 The Lord GOD hath sworn by his holiness, that, lo, the days shall come upon you, that he will take you away with hooks, and your posterity with fishhooks.

3 And ye shall go out at the breaches, every cow at that which is before her; and ye shall cast them into the palace, saith the LORD.

6 And I also have given you cleanness of teeth in all your cities, and want of bread in all your places: yet have ye not returned unto me, saith the LORD.

7 And also I have withholden the rain from you, when there were yet three months to the harvest: and I caused it to rain upon one city, and caused it not to rain upon another city: one piece was rained upon, and the piece whereupon it rained not withered.

8 So two or three cities wandered unto one city, to drink water; but they were not satisfied: yet have ye not returned unto me, saith the LORD.

9 I have smitten you with blasting and mildew: when your gardens and your vineyards and your fig trees and your olive trees increased, the palmerworm devoured them: yet have ye not returned unto me, saith the LORD.

10 I have sent among you the pestilence after the manner of Egypt: your young men have I slain with the sword, and have taken away your horses; and I have made the stink of your camps to come up unto your nostrils: yet have ye not returned unto me, saith the LORD.

11 I have overthrown some of you, as God overthrew Sodom and Gomorrah, and ye were as a firebrand plucked out of the burning: yet have ye not returned unto me, saith the LORD.

12 Therefore thus will I do unto thee, O Israel: and because I will do this unto thee, prepare to meet thy God, O Israel.

13 For, lo, he that formeth the mountains, and createth the wind, and declareth unto man what is his thought, that maketh the morning darkness, and treadeth upon the high places of the earth, The LORD, The God of hosts, is his name.

[This famous passage has been preached on by every major preacher, evangelist, reformer, missionary, or Bible teacher in the history of the church. All is self evident. God reviews His treatment of Israel. After blessing them (1 Kings 4:25) and fulfilling his promises of goodness to them (Josh. 22:1--4), they went into apostasy. Since ]{.class_s321}["the goodness of God" ]{.class_s323}[(Rom. 2:4) failed to bring about a revival, the Lord let them feel His severity (Rom. 11:22).]{.class_s321}

What follows is a list of things that God brought on the nation in an effort to turn them from their wicked ways (Rom. 2:4). The harsh, and at times terrible, treatment described is absolutely in line with God being the author of "evil" (see Amos 3:6), and there is no way to relieve Him of the responsibility of what follows. As supreme Almighty Sovereign of the universe and all creation, God is able to send famine (vs. 6), withhold showers (vs. 7), cut off springs and fountains (vs. 8), send mildew and insects (vs. 9), send plagues and pestilences like the Black Death (vs. 10), allow military defeats (v. 10), cause cities to be burnt to the ground (vs. 11), and pollute the land so badly that you have to wear a gas mask to breath (vs. 10).

[Second Kings 7:13 and 2 Kings 6:25 are good descriptions of some of these things, and the matters described therein were prophesied in detail in Deuteronomy 28:22--23,27,60 and 29:23. These judgments were the natural outcome of "playing church" and maintaining ]{.class_s321}["a form of godliness" ]{.class_s323}[while ]{.class_s321}["denying the power thereor, ]{.class_s323}[(2 Tim. 3:5). They are also a lesson for the ]{.class_s321}[admonition ]{.class_s322}[(1 Cor. 10:11) and ]{.class_s321}[learning ]{.class_s322}[of the body of Christ (Rom. 15:4), and it wouldn't take any knowledge of Hebrew or Greek to "interpret the writing" (Dan. 5:25).]{.class_s321}

[The ]{.class_s321}[AV ]{.class_s322}[brings in the "copy" loud enough to "bend the windows" and []{#c1N4.xhtml_page_282}"break the needle." He who knows the hearts and ]{.class_s321}["declareth unto man what is HIS THOUGHT" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 13) has never bothered to bless the efforts of any man, woman or child who tried to replace the ]{.class_s321}[AV ]{.class_s322}[1611 with the ridiculous, blasphemous, non Biblical "versions" of the eighteenth, nineteenth, and twentieth centuries. ]{.class_s321}[There is no case in the history of the Christian church where ANY revival ever came from or was connected with or was sustained by any English Bible since 1700 but a King James 1611 AV.]{.class_s322}[ With forty five English versions trying to outsell each other---there were sixty published between 1880--1978---God, the Holy Spirit, has never used the man recommending, selling, buying, using, teaching or preaching them long enough for him to say the Lord's Prayer.]{.class_s321}

The fact that pagans like to daydream and speculate on their goodness and the goodness of their "fellow man" has no bearing on history, past, present or future. The Lord is in charge (1 Chron. 28:9). He knows what you are thinking, what you intend, and what your motive is for taking the course that you take. Man has never "buffaloed" God one time since Adam died spiritually in order to "stay hitched" (1 Tim. 2:14).

"[For, lo, he that formeth the mountains, and createth the wind, and declareth unto man what is his thought" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 13). C. to 4 B. C. would have THOUGHT of putting the word "Christ" into the Old Testament. ]{.class_s322}[That is the work of a reprobate in A.D. 200--500 trying to rewrite the Old Testament so that you would think that the LXX was a PRE CHRISTIAN production. 10 They hate him that rebuketh in the gate, and they abhor him that speaketh uprightly.

11 Forasmuch therefore as your treading is upon the poor, and ye take from him burdens of wheat: ye have built houses of hewn stone, but ye shall not dwell in them; ye have planted pleasant vineyards, but ye shall not drink wine of them.

12 For I know your manifold transgressions and your mighty sins: they afflict the just, they take a bribe, and they turn aside the poor in the gate from their right.

13 Therefore the prudent shall keep silence in that time; for it is an evil time.

14 Seek good, and not evil, that ye may live: and so the LORD, the God of hosts, shall be with you, as ye have spoken.

15 Hate the evil, and love the good, and establish judgment in the gate: it may be that the LORD God of hosts will be gracious unto the remnant of Joseph.

16 Therefore the LORD, the God of hosts, the Lord, saith thus; Wailing shall be in all streets; and they shall say in all the highways, Alas! alas! and they shall call the husbandman to mourning, and such as are skilful of lamentation to wailing.

17 And in all vineyards shall be wailing: for I will pass through thee, saith the LORD.

18 Woe unto you that desire the day of the LORD! to what end is it for you? the day of the LORD is darkness, and not light.

19 As if a man did flee from a lion, and a bear met him; or went into the house, and leaned his hand on the wall, and a serpent bit him.

20 Shall not the day of the LORD be darkness, and not light? even very dark, and no brightness in it?

CHAPTER SIX

6:1 Woe to them that are at ease in Zion, and trust in the mountain of Samaria, which are named chief of the nations, to whom the house of Israel came!

2 Pass ye unto Calneh, and see; and from thence go ye to Hamath the great: then go down to Gath of the Philistines: be they better than these kingdoms? or their border greater than your border?

3 Ye that put far away the evil day, and cause the seat of violence to come near;

4 That lie upon beds of ivory, and stretch themselves upon their couches, and eat the lambs out of the flock, and the calves out of the midst of the stall;

5 That chant to the sound of the viol, and invent to themselves instruments of musick, like David;

6 That drink wine in bowls, and anoint themselves with the chief ointments: but they are not grieved for the affliction of Joseph.

The passage is aimed at the leisure class (twenty-hour week: see Ezek. 16:49) whose "high standard of living" enables them to live "the gracious life."

"[Woe to them that are at EASE in Zion" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 1). Like David on the housetop (2 Sam. 11:1), they are picking the wrong time to enjoy "gracious living." The refrain of Israel's captivity is taken up again at the paragraph mark in Chapter 6 (vs. 7).]{.class_s321}

"[And trust in the mountain of Samaria" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 1) shows that those at ease in Mt. Zion (Judah) were trusting the fortifications north of them (Mt. Ephraim) to keep back the Assyrian flood when it came in. But the Assyrians came clear up to Mt. Zion (2 Kings 19). The ]{.class_s321}["house of Israel"]{.class_s323}[ is given in verse 1 as coming to the ]{.class_s321}["chief of the nations" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 1) for help, and we are asked in verse 2 ]{.class_s321}["be they better than THESE KINGDOMS"---]{.class_s323}[the Philistines and the Syrians? "[Ye that put far away the evil day" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 3). This is the day of Amos 5:17 and 18. Those ]{.class_s321}["at ease in Zion" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 1) are postponing trouble indefinitely (see Ezek. 11:3). They take the positive outlook of modern America; "Our people have always surmounted obstacles. The human spirit is undefeatable! We are only limited by our belief in ourselves. We shall overcome, etc., etc., etc." So they were murdered and killed in the streets like dogs; their women were raped and the children were sold into slavery (Lam. 1--4). 7 Therefore now shall they go captive with the first that go captive, and the banquet of them that stretched themselves shall be removed.

8 The Lord GOD hath sworn by himself, saith the LORD the God of hosts, I abhor the excellency of Jacob, and hate his palaces: therefore will I deliver up the city with all that is therein.

9 And it shall come to pass, if there remain ten men in one house, that they shall die.

10 And a man's uncle shall take him up, and he that burneth him, to bring out the bones out of the house, and shall say unto him that is by the sides of the house, Is there yet any with thee? and he shall say, No. Then shall he say, Hold thy tongue: for we may not make mention of the name of the LORD.

F. Bruce, L. S. Chafer, Dr. Custer, A. T. Robertson, J. G. Machen, Fred Afman, Kenneth Wuest, and all faculties of all Conservative seminaries from 1910--1960) and the ]{.class_s321}[RSV ]{.class_s322}[of 1952 (recommended by Bishop Pike, Dean Weigle, Ralph Sockman, Eugene Carson Blake, Poteat, and all Liberal preachers in the NCCC from 1946--1980). Cow birds of a feather steal nests together.]{.class_s321}

[By reducing the ]{.class_s321}[Nile River ]{.class_s322}[(see 1 Kings 8:65) to the "brook of the Arabah," we are again reminded of the scholar's tendencies (any of any profession) to "water down" the word of God (]{.class_s321}[or dry it up]{.class_s322}[) to the category of tells, wadis, steles, Yawehs, asherahs, and "ostraca." Big stuff.]{.class_s321}

[You can't con an honest man; so, all of the con men in the business appeal to the depraved desires of the ]{.class_s321}[old nature ]{.class_s322}[when they want to destroy the faith of a young minister. They appeal to his desire to conquer and control the word of God, and this nature is as real in men like A. T. Robertson, R. A. Torry, J. G. Machen, Benjamin Warfield, and Robert Sumner as it is in men like Voltaire, Rousseau, Tom Paine, Celsus, or the worst Liberal who ever lived. ]{.class_s321}[The OLD NATURE is never converted ]{.class_s322}[(Rom. 6:6,16), and that is one of the first fundamental truths of New Testament doctrine (1 John 1:8). The absurd conceit of the faculty members at Christian schools in supposing that "separation" or "dedication" eradicates the envy and pride that resides in the ]{.class_s321}[old nature ]{.class_s322}[as it seeks to dictate over the ]{.class_s321}[authority of the Bible is ]{.class_s322}[about as rotten a piece of pagan foolishness as ever fell out of the garbage can of natural humus.]{.class_s321}

"[The river of the wilderness" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 14) indicates that Judah, along with Israel (see remarks under 6:1), is going to "get it" in the ankles and the toes as well as "in the neck."]{.class_s321}

[]{#c1VA.xhtml}

CHAPTER SEVEN

7:1 Thus hath the Lord GOD shewed unto me; and, behold, he formed grasshoppers in the beginning of the shooting up of the latter growth; and, lo, it was the latter growth after the king's mowings.

2 And it came to pass, that when they had made an end of eating the grass of the land, then I said, O Lord GOD, forgive, I beseech thee: by whom shall Jacob arise? for he is small.

3 The LORD repented for this: It shall not be, saith the LORD.

4 Thus hath the Lord GOD shewed unto me: and, behold, the Lord GOD called to contend by fire, and it devoured the great deep, and did eat up a part.

5 Then said I, O Lord GOD, cease, I beseech thee: by whom shall Jacob arise? for he is small.

6 The LORD repented for this: This also shall not be, saith the Lord GOD.

7 Thus he shewed me: and, behold, the Lord stood upon a wall made by a plumbline, with a plumbline in his hand.

8 And the LORD said unto me, Amos, what seest thou? And I said, A plumbline. Then said the Lord, Behold, I will set a plumbline in the midst of my people Israel: I will not again pass by them any more:

9 And the high places of Isaac shall be desolate, and the sanctuaries of Israel shall be laid waste; and I will rise against the house of Jeroboam with the sword.

[Three sermon illustrations are now given to Amos. The first is that of grasshoppers eating up the land (vs. 2), the second is fire devouring ]{.class_s321}["the great deep" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 4), and the third is a plumbline alongside a wall (vs. 7). As []{#c1VA.xhtml_page_310}the illustrations which God gave Moses (see Exod. 4 and comments) are enlightening to the believing heart and mind, so these illustrations point out great truths to Amos.]{.class_s321}

[Before expounding these truths, the Bible believer should notice the peculiar panic that seizes 100 percent of the commentators when they read]{.class_s321}[ "AND IT DEVOURED THE GREAT DEEP." ]{.class_s323}[Readers of ]{.class_s321}[The Bible Believer's Commentary on Genesis ]{.class_s322}[can foretell what will take place where the Bible makes any reference to water ]{.class_s321}[above ]{.class_s322}[the heavens (see Isa. 27:1--3 and Psa. 148:4), for this is the location of the greatest destructive critic the Bible ever contended with (see Job 41:1 and comments). Consequently, when we read about "fire devouring the great deep," the entire body of Conservative, Liberal, Catholic, Atheist, and Fundamental scholars begin to "rake the leaves" and "shake the trees."]{.class_s321}

[1. ]{.class_s321}[It wasn't ]{.class_s322}[apart of the]{.class_s321}[ "great deep,"]{.class_s323}[ it was the ]{.class_s321}[land of ISRAEL! ]{.class_s322}[(]{.class_s321}[ASV, NASV]{.class_s322}[, Dummelow, and ]{.class_s321}[The "Living" Bible, ]{.class_s322}[with ]{.class_s321}[The Wycliffe Bible Commentary, ]{.class_s322}[and the Hyperdispensationalist Bullinger.)]{.class_s321}

[2. It ]{.class_s321}[wasn't ]{.class_s322}[the ]{.class_s321}["great deep," ]{.class_s323}[it was the ]{.class_s321}[springs and rivers ]{.class_s322}[in Israel (]{.class_s321}[The "Living" Bible, The Wycliffe Bible Commentary, ]{.class_s322}[and Williams).]{.class_s321}

[3. It ]{.class_s321}[wasn't ]{.class_s322}[the ]{.class_s321}["great deep,"]{.class_s323}[ it was the ]{.class_s321}[remnant ]{.class_s322}[of Israel (Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown).]{.class_s321}

[4. It ]{.class_s321}[wasn't ]{.class_s322}[the ]{.class_s321}["great deep," ]{.class_s323}[it was "the allotted portion" (Pusey) or "the farmland" (Hitzig) or "the tilled land" (Marti) or\...?]{.class_s321}

[But it WAS the ]{.class_s321}["great deep,"]{.class_s323}[ and of all the stumble bums who stumbled at the passage (1 Pet. 2:8), the only man who left it stand---you won't believe it when we tell you! ---was the rank English Liberal C. H. Dodd (]{.class_s321}[NEB]{.class_s322}[). Dodd understood the ]{.class_s321}["deep"]{.class_s323}[ to be the "great abyss" of Genesis 1:2 and the ]{.class_s321}["part" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 4) to be a reference to part of that abyss which was in the original creation; and that is the sense of the verse. Of course Dodd never did have 2 Peter 3:5--6 placed right with Genesis 1:2, nor did he believe Genesis 1 to be literal history; but with all of his infidelity, he didn't make as bad a mess of the verse as Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown did, ]{.class_s321}[and they were premillennial []{#c1VA.xhtml_page_311}Conservatives! ]{.class_s322}[It's a strange world, isn't it?]{.class_s321}

[So, while Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown have fire reaching the "deep" at the roots of the plants in Israel(!) and are misleading the reader into thinking that Revelation 17:15 is a cross reference to the ]{.class_s321}["GREAT DEEP" ]{.class_s323}[of Amos 7:4, we shall return to the original English and see if we can straighten out 300 years of professional lying by the Cult of "recognized scholarship."]{.class_s321}

[1. The]{.class_s321}[ "grasshoppers"]{.class_s323}[ (not "locusts" as in the corrupt ]{.class_s321}[ASV, ASV, RSV,]{.class_s322}[ and ]{.class_s321}[NRSV]{.class_s322}[) ]{.class_s321}[could ]{.class_s322}[have eaten up all of the land till Israel had nothing to eat, and although the Lord ]{.class_s321}["repented for this"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 3), it will take place in thefuture (see Rev. 6:6 and especially Psa. 72:6, which deals with the Second Advent). One hundred percent of the scholars missed the prophetic cross references, including the premillennial Fundamentalist Charles Feinberg (Talbot Theological Seminary). The idea that the grasshoppers should be "locusts"---see any fraudulent "Bible" published since 1800---is based on the excruciating idea that if the word (Hebrew---]{.class_s321}[gob]{.class_s322}[) ]{.class_s321}[could ]{.class_s322}[be "locusts," it is. Wrong again. It can be "grasshopper," so it is. (Two sides to everything, you know; be broad minded.)]{.class_s321}

[The ]{.class_s321}["IT SHALL NOT BE"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 3) only indicates that God will not completely starve Israel out while Amos is alive. The ]{.class_s321}["latter growth"]{.class_s323}[ is the last crop that anyone can eat after the King has taken the "lion's share" out (]{.class_s321}["AFTER the king's mowings,"]{.class_s323}[ vs. 1). Observe 1 Samuel 8:10--18 and 1 Kings 4:22--23 to see how much is left after the ]{.class_s321}["king's mowings."]{.class_s323}

[Twice, Amos asks: ]{.class_s321}["BY WHOM SHALL JACOB ARISE?"]{.class_s323}[ (vss. 2, 5), but the third time (vs. 8), when the LORD says: ]{.class_s321}["What seest thou?"]{.class_s323}[ the answer is given. No one will cause Jacob to]{.class_s321}[ "arise"]{.class_s323}[ when the events of 2 Kings 17 take place.]{.class_s321}

[2. The]{.class_s321}[ "fire" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 4) will not devour the deep when Amos is preaching, but that event will certainly take place in Isaiah 66:22 and Revelation 20:11--13, which see. There is no more]{.class_s321}[ "SEA" ]{.class_s323}[in Revelation 21:1--2 because the GREAT DEEPS are gone (2 Pet. 3:5--12). When the LORD says, ]{.class_s321}["This ALSO shall not be" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 6), again, the reference is to 783--722 B.C.---not A.D. 3000.]{.class_s321}

[3. The plumbline prophesy is one that will come to pass on the spot: ]{.class_s321}["I will NOT AGAIN pass by them anymore" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 8). No longer will God "passover" Israel when it comes to bringing the judgments upon them which they have earned; judgment is coming (vs. 9). Observe that the "summer fruit vision" of Amos 8:1--2 matches the plumbline vision. Two illustrations, therefore, of future events (the Tribulation and the New Heavens) are found in 7:2 and 7:4, while two illustrations of the ]{.class_s321}[immediate future ]{.class_s322}[(the slaughter and captivity of Israel) are found in 7:8--9 and 8:13. Observe also that 8:3 is a reference to Judah as well as Israel (see Hosea 12:2 and comments).]{.class_s321}

A plumb bob is still used by all masons and carpenters. The idea is simple. You cannot build anything straight vertically unless you begin at the TOP. Throwing a plumb bob up in the air with a line attached to it and then trying to build even with the line would be like trying to get to Heaven by taking the sacraments and keeping the Golden Rule. All religions begin at ground level and work up (Buddhism, Taoism, Confucianism, Hinduism, Mohammedanism, Catholicism, Judaism, and "Christianity"). Biblical salvation is the only "religion" in the world that comes down from the TOP (see Exod. 24:10 and comments). All others stand in the mud and crack skulls while throwing plumblines into the air.

"[Behold, I WILL SET A PLUMBLINE in the midst of my people Israel" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 8). Salvation begins with the throne of God and works DOWN (see Exod. 24:10 and comments), and if you could reverse that procedure and work up (John 10:1), you would still be thrown out of Heaven by bodily force (Matt. 22:13) for being a THIEF and a ROBBER (John 10:1). ]{.class_s321}[Don't try it.]{.class_s322}

[The greatest ]{.class_s321}["plumbline" ]{.class_s323}[that God ever placed in the midst of Israel was a sinless Man (John 15:20--24). And when that Plumbline showed up (Mal. 3:3), it settled forever the question about man's "goodness" (John 15:22). To build even with ]{.class_s321}[that plumbline you ]{.class_s322}[would have to be SINLESS (Heb. 4:15).]{.class_s321}

Verse 9 is self explanatory.

Amos has no sooner delivered the burden of his visions to the populace at []{#c1VA.xhtml_page_313}Bethel than up shows the chief high priest (vs. 10) of the golden calves.

10 Then Amaziah the priest of Bethel sent to Jeroboam king of Israel, saying, Amos hath conspired against thee in the midst of the house of Israel: the land is not able to bear all his words.

11 For thus Amos saith, Jeroboam shall die by the sword, and Israel shall surely be led away captive out of their own land.

12 Also Amaziah said unto Amos, O thou seer, go, flee thee away into the land of Judah, and there eat bread, and prophesy there:

13 But prophesy not again any more at Bethel: for it is the king's chapel, and it is the king's court.

[The passage is a classic. According to the ]{.class_s321}["priest,"]{.class_s323}[ Amos is not a God called preacher; he ]{.class_s321}["conspired against thee" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 10). Amos does not love "the people" (see Acts 21:28 and comments); ]{.class_s321}["the land is not able to bear all his words" ]{.class_s323}[(see Jer. 38:4). Amos is ]{.class_s321}[negative]{.class_s322}[ (vs.]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s322}[11), therefore, he should be defrocked (vs. 13) and put out of the ministry (vs. 12), for there is no room in the ministry for a negative, critical attitude. "In these difficult and trying times, how much more should we strive to have faith in our fellow man, and believe in the ultimate goodness of human nature, whose spirit has conquered so many times in the past, and we trust, in spite of all the complexities of modern life, will yet surmount the seemingly insurmountable obstacle, as it dares to dream the impossible dream and scheme the impossible scheme and cream the impossible cream, and etc., etc." \[See any issue of the ]{.class_s321}[Christian Science Sentinel, ]{.class_s322}[Boston, Mass.\]]{.class_s321}

This has been the song of Screwtape and Wormwood (C. S. Lewis) since Jabal, Jubal, and Tubal Cain tried to bring in the kingdom (see Gen. 4:20--22 []{#c1VA.xhtml_page_314}and comments). (For further information consult the Communist NEA in your nearest federal school system.)

[The real trouble is that ]{.class_s321}["the land is not able to BEAR ALL HIS WORDS" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 10). As Professor Pinkevitch of the USSR said (back in the 1950s), "The world is getting too small for that book; either ]{.class_s321}[that book ]{.class_s322}[must go, or the world must go." ("Good bye cruel world!") Heaven and earth shall pass away but ]{.class_s321}["ALL HIS WORDS" ]{.class_s323}[(Amos 7:10) will NOT pass away (Matt. 24:35). Consequently, those of us who preach and teach ]{.class_s321}["ALL HIS WORDS" ]{.class_s323}[(see Acts 20:27 and comments) are not concerned with what ANYONE thinks of us, for we were called into an off scouring ministry (1 Cor. 4:13) of rudeness (2 Cor. 11:6) and contempt (2 Cor. 10:10); so, what 100 percent of the Atheists or 100 percent of the Conservatives or 100 percent of the Liberals or 100 percent of the United Nations or 100 percent of the Catholics or 100 percent of the Fundamental scholars think of us is of no more interest to us ---where the Authority of His words are at stake---than two fire ants in a field of corn.]{.class_s321}

[The ]{.class_s321}["WORDS" ]{.class_s323}[(not the "Word" which is the modern counterfeit sign of the apostate Fundamentalist when he means "THE MESSAGE") of Amos are ]{.class_s321}[negative: ]{.class_s322}["shall die\...shall surely be led away captive" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 11). Amos is a hell raising, trouble making "nut." He is warned that if he doesn't go home that he will not be able to get three "squares" a day anymore. ]{.class_s321}["THERE eat bread, and prophesy THERE" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 12). The threat is that if you stay here, you will run out of bread, and since your motive for preaching is to feed your ]{.class_s321}[belly ]{.class_s322}[(Rom. 16:18), you'd better leave before your "god" shrinks up.]{.class_s321}

[But you could no more back down a prophet like Amos than you could Micaiah, John the Baptist, or Jeremiah. These gorilla like prophets with eyes like an eagle and hearts like a lion never back down, not even before a KING (1 Kings 22; Mark 6; Jer. 38:15). A ]{.class_s321}["priest of Bethel" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 10) sent out by the South Wide Fellowship or the National Council of Churches to stop a man like Amos is about as funny as sending out a Chihuahua on the street to kill a German Shepherd. If Amos has to eat ]{.class_s321}["bread of affliction" ]{.class_s323}[like Micaiah did []{#c1VA.xhtml_page_315}(1 Kings 22:27), he will do it. A piece of black bread in prison with the Lord is more palatable to Amos than a piece of chocolate cake with the priest of Bethel in ]{.class_s321}["the king's chapel" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 13).]{.class_s321}

[They will try to starve you out, preacher. I have had them cut off my income and then claim I was causing a default in the bond program because of my street preaching; "it hurt the income of the church." On an Easter Sunday morning, in 1968, with 500 people in the building I have seen a \$25.00 offering. The ]{.class_s321}[goats ]{.class_s322}[trying to get rid of the preacher wouldn't put in the plate so the bond program was in danger of defaulting: you see, ]{.class_s321}[the sheep ]{.class_s322}[wouldn't put in to make up the deficit because they were paying the bills that the goats contracted. The case finally went to the circuit court and the judge kicked all the ]{.class_s321}[goats ]{.class_s322}[out and the bond program was paid off with money to spare. I didn't miss one meal during the whole operation. You feed the ]{.class_s321}[sheep ]{.class_s322}[and they will feed you. Let the goats butt each other's heads off.]{.class_s321}

[Bud Robinson said that if the religious big shots did starve him out, finally, he would spend his last dime for a box of ]{.class_s321}[baking soda ]{.class_s322}[and a cup of ]{.class_s321}[vinegar, ]{.class_s322}[and then he would eat and drink that and swell up so bad before he kicked off that people would think he had died of overeating!]{.class_s321}

[In the early days of my ministry, before I had learned how to be ]{.class_s321}[tactful ]{.class_s322}[and ]{.class_s321}[diplomatic---]{.class_s322}[I have mellowed with the years!---an Associational Missionary (SBC) told me that if I didn't quit preaching the way I was doing it, I would have to be cutting down saplings to get a place to preach. (That's how they built brush arbors back in those days.) I went down to the hardware store and bought me a fifteen pound double bladed axe.]{.class_s321}

"[But prophesy not again anymore at BETH EL" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 13). Why not? Wasn't BETHEL the "house of God"? \[See Gen. 35:7 and comments.\] Why kick a God called prophet out of "the house of god" because he was telling the people what God wanted them to hear?]{.class_s321}

See how it's done? Twenty six centuries hasn't changed human nature.

[You take for granted that God would never tell anyone what Amos was telling them ]{.class_s322}[(see vs. 11), and that way you disqualify as a []{#c1VA.xhtml_page_316}"qualified authority" (see any of the HEW Communist literature) any minister ]{.class_s321}[who speaks AGAINST anything you don't like. ]{.class_s322}[Simple, isn't it? Just pretend that God would not send people to Hell (which He will), that God would not kill babies and women (which He ]{.class_s321}[does]{.class_s322}[),]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s322}[that God will not starve out a nation which uses images as "an aid to worship (which He will), that God does not approve of capital punishment (which He ]{.class_s321}[does]{.class_s322}[), that God would put a race in servitude (which He ]{.class_s321}[did]{.class_s322}[), that God would not speak negatively of human nature (when He ]{.class_s321}[has]{.class_s322}[),]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s322}[and that God will not judge you for your bad temper, impatience, fornication, masturbation, lesbianism, premarital relations, lying, laziness, swearing, foul speaking, adultery, and envy (when He will). Then you can get rid of every Bible-believing preacher in this country with a clear conscience. Convince yourself that God has nothing NEGATIVE to offer and you have opened the way for International Socialism, where a select group of Fascists (Communism, Fascism, Catholicism, and the Welfare State are the same system) will do ]{.class_s321}[anything negative they wish ]{.class_s322}[to do to make you conform to an anti Biblical system.]{.class_s321}

There isn't one great man of God in either Testament who doesn't speak NEGATIVELY of the world, the world system, human nature, organized religions, and the future of International Socialism (see Zeph. 3:8; Rev. 13:1--8; Matt. 13:40, and comments).

"[For it is THE KING'S CHAPEL, and it is the KING'S COURT" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 13). Then, the ]{.class_s321}[reason ]{.class_s322}[for getting rid of Amos had nothing to do with his calling, his God, his ministry, the truth of his message, or his dedication. The simple truth was that Amos' message was not ]{.class_s321}[politically expedient. ]{.class_s322}[And this explains why Harold Okenga, Billy Graham, and Duke McCall (Louisville Theological Seminary) refused to quote Acts 17:26 in its entirety when they were on a nationwide broadcast discussing segregation (1950). It wasn't ]{.class_s321}[politically expedient; ]{.class_s322}[so, every man (including the Liberals and Modernists) cut Acts 17:26 at the ]{.class_s321}[comma. ]{.class_s322}[By 1940 the FCC had become ]{.class_s321}["the king's chapel, and\...the king's court"]{.class_s323}[; so, never again was a ministry like Mordecai Ham's or Billy Sunday's ever heard on it, coast to coast.]{.class_s321}

There are "houses of God" up and down this country that ceased to be "houses of God" as far back as 1890. There are hundreds of them that ceased to be "houses of God" after 1933, and by 1960 you could eliminate 4,000 "Bethels" from the list on the grounds of:

1\. Preachers in the pulpit who publicly denied the Virgin Birth and the Bodily Resurrection.

2\. Preachers in the pulpit who publicly supported lesbianism, integration, acid rock, and homosexual marriages.

3\. Preachers in the pulpit who never believed in a literal Heaven or literal Hell one day in their life.

4\. Preachers in the pulpit dedicated to raising funds for the overthrow of foreign governments.

5\. Preachers in the pulpit who were no more called to preach than C. W. Burpo or Michael Luther King Jr., or Jimmy Swaggart. \[See remarks in the preface to Hosea.\]

14 Then answered Amos, and said to Amaziah, I was no prophet, neither was I a prophet's son; but I was an herdman, and a gatherer of sycomore fruit:

15 And the LORD took me as I followed the flock, and the LORD said unto me, Go, prophesy unto my people Israel.

16 Now therefore hear thou the word of the LORD: Thou sayest, Prophesy not against Israel, and drop not thy word against the house of Isaac.

17 Therefore thus saith the LORD; Thy wife shall be an harlot in the city, and thy sons and thy daughters shall fall by the sword, and thy land shall be divided by line; and thou shalt die in a polluted land: and Israel shall surely go into captivity forth of his land.

[Amos racks him out. His answer is rude, aggressive, brutal, and without []{#c1VA.xhtml_page_318}one shred of any "love for man" or a thread of "trying to win people to Christ by a sweet spirit." Amos' retort is a blatant ]{.class_s321}[threat, ]{.class_s322}[and it matches Paul's commandment given to Titus in Titus 1:13.]{.class_s321}

"[I was no prophet" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 14); so, don't blame me. If you're finding fault with my ministry, go blame God Almighty, for ]{.class_s321}["THE LORD TOOK ME as I followed the flock"]{.class_s323}[ (v. 15).]{.class_s321}[ "Neither was I a prophet's son" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 14); so, don't give me this gas about a "daddy-called" or a "momma-called" preacher. Amos didn't graduate from the "schools" of 2 Kings 2:3 and 4:1. He was neither "recognized" nor accredited, nor was he "authorized" or "qualified" or any other lying, perverse, carnal, political adjective invented by God-defying sinners who were trying to cast off the cords of their Creator (Psa. 2:3).]{.class_s321}

"[But I was an HERDMAN, and a gatherer of sycomore fruit" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 14); so, don't pick any fight with me; go pick it with the One who changed my occupation: ]{.class_s321}["THE LORD SAID UNTO ME, Go, prophesy unto my people Israel" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 15). Amos absolves himself of all guilt; God called him, God put him in the ministry, God told him what to say, God told him how to say it, and if Amaziah didn't like it, he could ]{.class_s321}[go BURY HIS 0WN SONS ]{.class_s322}[(vs. 17)! How's that for a "sweet spirit"?]{.class_s321}

[And make no mistake about it, God's prophets are not ]{.class_s321}[sugar sticks. ]{.class_s322}[They are the SALT of the earth. They burn when they land on an open sore, and Israel was an open sore (see Isa. 1:3--12). Placing M. L. King Jr., or Kahlil Gibran or Edgar Cayce into the ]{.class_s321}[prophet's ]{.class_s322}[ranks, when they contain men like Micaiah and Amos (and don't forget Neh. 13:25) is like placing Howard Cosell and Johnny Carson in the starting lineup of the Pittsburg Steelers.]{.class_s321}

"[Now therefore hear thou the word of the LORD" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 16). Amos is not content with ]{.class_s321}[justifying ]{.class_s322}[his calling (vss. 14--15). He never forgets his ]{.class_s321}[duties ]{.class_s322}[in that calling. He is to deliver the NEGATIVE message (see 1 Sam. 3:11--14) no matter who thinks what, who says what, or who does what. He delivers: ]{.class_s321}["THY WIFE shall be an harlot\...THY SONS and thy daughters shall fall by the sword, and THY LAND shall be divided by line; and THOU SHALT DIE in a polluted land" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. []{#c1VA.xhtml_page_319}17).]{.class_s321}

[Is that ]{.class_s321}[personal ]{.class_s322}[enough?]{.class_s321}

Do you want it anymore "pointed" than that?

[You see, Amos is not one of these nice FM Christians (1950--1980) who comes on with, "If we take Christ as our Saviour, then we can truthfully say that God is OUR father and WE\...." No, with Amos it is ]{.class_s321}["THY WIFE\...THY SONS\...THY LAND\...AND THOU" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 17). There is no toning down of the message, there is no excursion into Dale Carnegie land to "win friends and influence people," there is no "I'm all right, you're all right" to lie your way through, and there isn't one trace of "the sweet spirit of Christ" anywhere in the delivery. It is unsheeted Hell on wheels. Amos puts "both feet on the floor" and "knocks the slack out." Amos prophesies ]{.class_s321}[the death ]{.class_s322}[of the man talking to him, the death of his ]{.class_s321}[children ]{.class_s322}[and the prostitution of his wife; and if that weren't enough vilification and "defamation," he adds ]{.class_s321}["AND ISRAEL SHALL SURELY go into captivity forth of his land" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 17)---which was the original message.]{.class_s321}

You couldn't move Amos with a fleet of LeTourneau's bulldozers.

[His rude "overstatements" and "unkind and ungracious remarks," leveled at the king's priest (vs. 10), were about as ]{.class_s321}[charitable ]{.class_s322}[as a meat cleaver between the eyes. Amos was called to do a dirty job, and he did it, and if there were any "clean" brethren around (sitting high and dry on the grounds of their great "defense of the faith"), they probably looked at him like a litter of Chihuahuas would look at a wounded bull mastiff returning after a fight with 200 pack rats in an alley.]{.class_s321}

[The corrections made on the text by the]{.class_s321}[ ASV, NASV, RSV, NRSV,]{.class_s322}[ ]{.class_s321}[The "Living" Bible, ]{.class_s322}[and the ]{.class_s321}[NEB ]{.class_s322}[are not worth recording. Neither are the comments by Durnmelow, Feinberg, Motyer, Jamieson, Nowack, Harnack, Fausset, Marti, Brown, Clarke, Hitzig, Henry, or Schultz. No ]{.class_s321}[commentator applied the passage to one contemporary historical reality of his day or time, and there is no reason why he should, ]{.class_s322}[not a man in the list could walk in Amos' sandals long enough to climb a sycamore tree and pick its fruit; and it DID have a greenish yellow "fig" which was NOT a "mulberry" (in case []{#c1VA.xhtml_page_320}you wasted some time with "scientific research" of the Westcott and Hort Cult and their devoted followers, 1800--1900).]{.class_s321}

CHAPTER EIGHT

8:1 Thus hath the Lord GOD shewed unto me: and behold a basket of summer fruit.

2 And he said, Amos, what seest thou? And I said, A basket of summer fruit. Then said the LORD unto me, The end is come upon my people of Israel; I will not again pass by them any more.

3 And the songs of the temple shall be howlings in that day, saith the Lord GOD: there shall be many dead bodies in every place; they shall cast them forth with silence.

[All is evident. The ]{.class_s321}["summer fruit" ]{.class_s323}[matches Jeremiah 8:20, which see. ]{.class_s321}["THE END" ]{.class_s323}[of verse 2 matches 7:8. Note: ]{.class_s321}["I WILL NOT AGAIN pass by them anymore" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 2). The only difference is that Amos 8:2 reaches beyond the judgments placed on the Northern Tribes, for we read, ]{.class_s321}["And the songs of THE TEMPLE shall be howlings in that day" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 3). Verse 3 in the latter section (]{.class_s321}["they shall cast them forth with silence"]{.class_s323}[) is an additional comment on the activities we have already described under 6:10.]{.class_s321}

4 Hear this, O ye that swallow up the needy, even to make the poor of the land to fail,

5 Saying, When will the new moon be gone, that we may sell corn? and the sabbath, that we may set forth wheat, making the ephah small, and the shekel great, and falsifying the balances by deceit?

6 That we may buy the poor for silver, and the needy for a pair of shoes; yea, and sell the refuse of the wheat?

7 The LORD hath sworn by the excellency of Jacob, Surely I will never forget any of their works.

8 Shall not the land tremble for this, and every one mourn that dwelleth therein? and it shall rise up wholly as a flood; and it shall be cast out and drowned, as by the flood of Egypt.

9 And it shall come to pass in that day, saith the Lord GOD, that I will cause the sun to go down at noon, and I will darken the earth in the clear day:

10 And I will turn your feasts into mourning, and all your songs into lamentation; and I will bring up sackcloth upon all loins, and baldness upon every head; and I will make it as the mourning of an only son, and the end thereof as a bitter day.

[The passage deals with God's judgment on commercialism and the abuses of the Capitalistic system. All is evident and needs very little exposition. The poor of the land fail (vs. 4) and die because they have no money or land left (vs. 6). The big businessmen do not regard the Sabbath (vs. 5) because their only thoughts are on "how to make a buck" (vs. 5). Furthermore, they will change the weights on the scale to gyp the customer (vs. 5). \[See Prov. 20:23 and comments.\] They reduce their brethren to such a state of poverty (Lev. 25:39) that they can buy him as a slave for money, or even for a ]{.class_s321}[pair of shoes ]{.class_s322}[to cover his bare feet (vs. 6). ]{.class_s321}["The REFUSE of the wheat" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 6) is what was "refused"; that is, the leftover wheat that was about half chaff and stubble.]{.class_s321}

"[Right wingers" in America, who have a great deal to say about the Constitution and the Bill of Rights, should never forget that America's greatness did NOT come from its adopting the ]{.class_s321}[capitalistic system. ]{.class_s322}[That is an error which one will find repeated over and over again in the right wing literature. America's financial and material prosperity came from the fact that ]{.class_s321}[from the start she publicly recognized GOD and privately read (and believed) His Book. ]{.class_s322}[When ]{.class_s321}[those things ]{.class_s322}[disappeared from America's national life (1945--1970), she ceased to be great and became the laughing stock of Europe and Asia. No amount of "free enterprise" and "restraint of government controls," etc., ever will restore to her the dignity and might which []{#c1XH.xhtml_page_323}she lost. Commerce, per se, is as great an enemy of Biblical Christianity as its fellow "C's"---Catholicism, Communism, and Charismaticism. Commerce, per se, has no respect for the Bible, no respect for the Sabbath, no respect for the value of a soul, no respect for street preaching, no respect for the oppressed or the unfortunate, and it never has had any respect for God. Jesus Christ was sold for money, and the love of money is ]{.class_s321}["THE ROOT"]{.class_s323}[ (1 Tim. 6:10) (not "a root" as you find it in the corrupt ]{.class_s321}[NASV]{.class_s322}[) of all evil.]{.class_s321}

[In this age, the love of money will be found to be the motive behind the ]{.class_s321}[writings ]{.class_s322}[of Karl Marx and Mao Tse, exactly as it will be found behind the ]{.class_s321}[operations ]{.class_s322}[of Rockefeller and the Mafia. "Communism" (as a system) came from a bum who loved money that he couldn't get his hands on. That love is ]{.class_s321}["THE ROOT of all evil" ]{.class_s323}[in the present world.]{.class_s321}

[It is ]{.class_s321}[love of money ]{.class_s322}[that produced the ]{.class_s321}[ASV, RV, NASV, RSV, NRSV, NEB, TEV,]{.class_s322}[ ]{.class_s321}[Good News for Modern Man, ]{.class_s322}[and every other degraded comic book on the "bible" market. It is the ]{.class_s321}[love of money ]{.class_s322}[that produced the modern educational system's SIECUS, with its "values clarification." It also produced the whole mongrel-animalized-mass media mess.]{.class_s321}

In the present world system, the thoughts pictured in Amos 8:5--6 are behind the great juggernaut COMMERCE. And many a Christian parent has sacrificed his daughter or son to the system as faithfully as any Catholic parent ever turned a son or daughter over to the black-robed Baalite hoods of the cloisters.

Amos has his "toes on the bumper," and he goes right on down the "loose boardwalk" blowing the "doors" off.

"[The Lord hath sworn by the excellency of Jacob" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 7). In Amos 6:8 the reference is to Psalm 47:4, and ]{.class_s321}["palaces"]{.class_s323}[ match those of Psalm 48:3. When God swears by the ]{.class_s321}["excellency of Jacob,"]{.class_s323}[ He is swearing by His choice of Mt. Zion on Jerusalem (1 Kings 9:3), not by any golden calf temple in Bethel. Notice further that the prophesy has to cover Judah because only the "God" of DAN is mentioned (vs. 14) in regard to the northern tribes.]{.class_s321}[ "The manner of Beer sheba"]{.class_s323}[ (see 8:14) shows that JUDAH is included in []{#c1XH.xhtml_page_324}judgments on the ]{.class_s321}["EXCELLENCY OF JACOB"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 7).]{.class_s321}

"[Shall not the land tremble for this, and every one mourn that dwelleth therein?" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 8). Twenty-two words in the verse are repeated in Amos 9:5, and both passages are references to the Second Advent. The inclusion in the prophesy of ]{.class_s321}["I will cause the sun to go down at NOON, and I will darken the earth in the clear day" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 9) cannot be confined to an ]{.class_s321}[eclipse ]{.class_s322}[(see ]{.class_s321}[The Wycliffe Bible Commentary]{.class_s322}[), nor could it possibly be merely an "emblem" of coming trouble (Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown).]{.class_s321}

Motyer lines the eclipse up with an earthquake in 763 B.C., but then he calls the sun going down at noon (vs. 9) "IMAGERY." Durnmelow says that the eclipse must have "impressed his IMAGINATION" powerfully. Sure, sonny, just like Rev. 6:12.

[Now observe how all attempts to make ]{.class_s321}["THAT DAY" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 9) refer to the history of the past end in failure. You see, eclipse or not, earthquake or not, 763 ]{.class_s321}[B.C. was NOT the date of Israel's captivity; ]{.class_s322}[to the contrary, the captivity of the land did not take place until thirty eight to forty years ]{.class_s321}[after ]{.class_s322}[that time. This well illustrates how the dead researches of dead archaeologists doing their deadening work with the dead to bring "light" on the LIVING word---often produce nothing but a stinking corpse (see Exod. 15:1--15 and comments). ]{.class_s321}["AS THE MOURNING OF AN ONLY SON" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 10) clearly marks the passage for Zechariah 12:10 (]{.class_s321}["AS ONE MOURNETH FOR HIS ONLY SON"]{.class_s323}[), and the context of both passages is the Second Advent. Furthermore, what follows (Amos 8:11--12) is so clearly a reference to events beyond A.D. 1900 that only a Bible-rejecting Fundamentalist could join hands with a postmillennial Modernist in rejecting the truth of the matter. And although Isaiah 3:24 (JUDAH, ]{.class_s321}[not Israel]{.class_s322}[) and Jeremiah 6:26 (JUDAH, ]{.class_s321}[not Israel]{.class_s322}[)]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s322}[were preludes or "overtures" to the great drama of Daniel's Seventieth Week, neither of them bring Amos 8:7--10 to the great fulfillment of ]{.class_s321}["THAT DAY" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 9).]{.class_s321}

[In the Tribulation sunlight does go "slap out" (Rev. 6:12) exactly as it did down in Egypt (Exod. 10:22--23), and the earth is darkened on a "clear day" []{#c1XH.xhtml_page_325}exactly as was done in Egypt (see Rev. 16:10) under the ministry of Moses. The earthquake ]{.class_s321}[will ]{.class_s322}[come (Rev. 16:18) as prophesied here (vs. 8), and the land ]{.class_s321}[will ]{.class_s322}[be inundated (Dan. 9:26) with a literal flood (Rev. 12:15), exactly as the Nile inundates Egypt (Ezek. 29), while the remnant (Matt. 24:16) run for their lives (Rev. 12:6).]{.class_s321}

"[Sackcloth upon all loins, and baldness upon every head" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 10) is a reference to the customs of Orientals in regard to wearing burlap apparel (see 2 Kings 6:30 and 1 Kings 21:27) and shaving the head when in mourning (Deut. 21:12; Job 1:20).]{.class_s321}

11 Behold, the days come, saith the Lord GOD, that I will send a famine in the land, not a famine of bread, nor a thirst for water, but of hearing the words of the LORD:

12 And they shall wander from sea to sea, and from the north even to the east, they shall run to and fro to seek the word of the LORD, and shall not find it.

13 In that day shall the fair virgins and young men faint for thirst.

14 They that swear by the sin of Samaria, and say, Thy god, O Dan, liveth; and, The manner of Beersheba liveth; even they shall fall, and never rise up again.

Again, we have stepped into the nuclear power plant. These passages, with passages like Proverbs 18:1--2, 8:8--9; Colossians 2:8,2:23; Matthew 23:14--15; Isaiah 19:12; Romans 1:18,21,25; and John 18:36 are the atom smashers of the Bible. These are places so that no man can handle them in commenting or preaching unless he is faithful to the Divine commission to preach the truth, the whole truth, and nothing but the truth.

[Typical of the way the passage is handled is Dummelow's comment: It wasn't really the word of ]{.class_s321}[the Lord ]{.class_s322}[they were seeking; it was the word of ]{.class_s321}[]{#c1XH.xhtml_page_326}["guidance ]{.class_s322}[out of trouble." They were seeking "Spiritual Satisfaction" (]{.class_s321}[The New Bible Commentary]{.class_s322}[).]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s322}[Just one more load of rubbish! It wasn't the word of the LORD they were seeking, it was the word of "Jehovah." (]{.class_s321}[NASV]{.class_s322}[, any edition; same baloney.)]{.class_s321}

But what have we here?

[Only one church in history KEPT God's word (Rev. 3:8). It wasn't the twentieth-century church; ]{.class_s321}[it wasn't the Apostolic church. ]{.class_s322}[Didn't Daniel tell you that in the end time many would run ]{.class_s321}["TO AND FRO" ]{.class_s323}[(Dan. 12:4)? Why, unless they ]{.class_s321}[themselves ]{.class_s322}[were engaged in ridding the world of the ]{.class_s321}["WORDS OF THE LORD" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 11)? Didn't Christ refer to them as ]{.class_s321}["WORDS" ]{.class_s323}[(John 15:7)? \[See extended comments under Prov. 30:6 and Eph. 6:17 in those commentaries.\]]{.class_s321}

[You see, ]{.class_s321}["THE LAND,"]{.class_s323}[ here, goes far beyond the land of Judah or Israel, and the]{.class_s321}[ "FAMINE" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 11) places Isaiah 29:10, Micah 3:7, and Psalm 74:9 in a "sight more" localities than Palestine two and a half millenniums backwards. ]{.class_s321}[Where are the words of the Lord today? ]{.class_s322}[Now there is a ]{.class_s321}[question! ]{.class_s322}[Robert Sumner doesn't have them---"verbal inspiration" or not. Bob Jones III, doesn't have them---"plenary inspiration" or not; and when Dr. Custer writes in ]{.class_s321}[Faith Magazine ]{.class_s322}[(BJU press) that he would DIE to defend the "plenary inspiration of the Bible," he no more means "the Bible" than Malcom X. Custer believes that the ORIGINAL MANUSCRIPTS (]{.class_s321}[which you don't have]{.class_s322}[) would []{#c1XH.xhtml_page_327}be inspired (]{.class_s321}[although He doesn't have them to check them]{.class_s322}[)]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s322}[when they were written (]{.class_s321}[although no one alive has ever seen them]{.class_s322}[). That's what he believes, and when he says "THE BIBLE," he is no more referring to any book he ]{.class_s321}[has ]{.class_s322}[or you ]{.class_s321}[have ]{.class_s322}[or ]{.class_s321}[ANYONE]{.class_s322}[ HAS than he is referring to a three headed toad on Jupiter. If you don't believe that, corner him and get him to show you "the WORDS of the Lord." No Cult member of the Westcott and Hort Cult has access to the ]{.class_s321}["WORDS OF THE LORD" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 11).]{.class_s321}
