[When Bob Jones III, or any combination of Tennessee Temple professors speak of the "God breathed Bible" or the "God breathed Book," they simply mean that by quoting SOME translation (]{.class_s321}[ASV]{.class_s322}[, ]{.class_s321}[NIV, ]{.class_s322}[NASV, ]{.class_s321}[AV, The Amplified Bible, ]{.class_s322}[etc.) they have convinced themselves that the "original manuscripts" were inspired, and ]{.class_s321}[nothing else is. ]{.class_s322}[This man made doctrine is found nowhere in ]{.class_s321}[ANY Bible. ]{.class_s322}[No Bible states that "the original manuscripts" were inspired, nor does ]{.class_s321}[any ]{.class_s322}[Bible teach ]{.class_s321}[that the "original manuscripts only were infallible and without error." ]{.class_s322}[That is a non Biblical fantasy, and when John R. Rice or Archer Weniger dodge around this truth by saying "nowhere in the Bible does it state that any translation is inspired," they are simply "whipping the devil around the stump." ]{.class_s321}[It doesn't say the "originals" were either.]{.class_s322}[ SO WHAT?]{.class_s321}

"[I will send a famine in the land\...of hearing the WORDS of the LORD" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 11). Well, the famine is here. The men who created it were the Conservatives of the ]{.class_s321}[RV ]{.class_s322}[committee (1884) and the ]{.class_s321}[ASV ]{.class_s322}[committee (1901). It is now greatly augmented by the word of "Christian" universities, "Conservative" seminaries and "Bible" institutes. Of the 32,000 changes in the ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[(and ]{.class_s321}[NIV]{.class_s322}[) from the correct English text, over 5,000 of them were recommended by the National Council of Christian Churches (]{.class_s321}[RSV]{.class_s322}[, 1952). You can't find the ]{.class_s321}["WORDS OF THE LORD,"]{.class_s323}[ according to the seminary professors (any of any profession), unless you have access to the great Cultists (Deismann, Robertson, Souter, Milligan, Moulton, Westcott, Nestle, Metzger, Aland, Hort, []{#c1XH.xhtml_page_328}Trench, Davis, Driver, Machen, Keil, Delitzsch, and Gesenius), and then (ah yes!), and THEN, ]{.class_s321}[you still don't have them, according to the men that I just listed!]{.class_s322}

[Such are the ways of death that "seemeth right" to a ]{.class_s321}[ministerial student ]{.class_s322}[(Prov. 14:12).]{.class_s321}

"[They shall wander from SEA TO SEA" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 12): In Israel's case, from Galilee to the Mediterranean and the Sea of Galilee to the Dead Sea. ]{.class_s321}["And from the north even to the east"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 12). In Israel's case, from Tyre and Sidon to Babylon. In YOUR case, from Canada to Mexico and from Maine to Los Angeles. In Europe's case, from Lapland to Gibraltar and from Belfast to Istanbul. In Asia's case, from Siberia to Mecca and from outer Mongolia to Rabaul. In Africa's case, from Cairo to Cape Hope and from the Gold Coast to Abyssinia. ]{.class_s321}["They shall run to and fro to seek the word of the LORD, and SHALL NOT FIND IT" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 12)! Why?]{.class_s321}

[1. The Roman Catholic Church in cooperation with a ]{.class_s321}[Soviet World Court]{.class_s322}[ will forbid the publication and propagation of the ]{.class_s321}[Authorized Version]{.class_s322}[ of 1611.]{.class_s321}

[2. The Fundamental scholars and Conservative Christian ]{.class_s321}[colleges ]{.class_s322}[will have destroyed the faith of every ministerial student concerning the ]{.class_s321}[AV ]{.class_s322}[1611 before he ever graduated.]{.class_s321}

This is the handwriting on the wall (1976--1990), and it is as sure to come to pass as the death of Belshazzar (Dan. 5:30).

[While I write these words, there are Christians in the Bible Belt (there are several "Bible Belts" according to the Atheists and Agnostics) who are having to travel twenty-five to thirty five miles to hear ]{.class_s321}["THE WORDS OF THE LORD" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 11). It is quite possible today for a born-again Christian to attend Sunday School every week of his life and come out, after fifty years, not knowing enough real Bible to lead a man to Christ. I am overstating NOTHING. There are, within twenty miles of me, people who have been saved forty years, yet they cannot quote five verses of Scripture on marriage, divorce, separation, jobs, schools, making money, raising children, sports, politics, foreign affairs, heresy, []{#c1XH.xhtml_page_329}or prophecy. They are getting something in church, but it is not ]{.class_s321}["THE WORDS OF THE LORD" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 11). One session in a real Bible believing, Bible preaching church would be such a shock to the average twentieth-century "Christian" that he would need aspirin and Anacin to recover his composure. ]{.class_s321}["THE WORDS OF THE LORD" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 11) are ]{.class_s321}[pure ]{.class_s322}[(Psa. 12:6), ]{.class_s321}[powerful]{.class_s322}[ (Jer. 23:29), ]{.class_s321}[preserved ]{.class_s322}[(Psa. 12:6, 7), and ]{.class_s321}[permanent ]{.class_s322}[(Psa. 119:89). However, they only work ]{.class_s321}[effectually]{.class_s322}[ (1 Thess. 2:13) in those who take them at face value to be what they are (1 Thess. 2:13). The educated, orthodox teachers of Christ's day (Mark 7:7) got NOTHING from them.]{.class_s321}

"[In that day shall the fair VIRGINS and young men faint for thirst" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 13).]{.class_s321}

[Three applications come readily to mind: the young people of 760--720 B.C. who lived in the closing years of Israel's degeneration, the virgins of Revelation 14 who live in the Tribulation (see Matt. 25:1--4), and the young people of America who are wandering like sick sheep in a mountain blizzard. With the one prime essential need of this generation so evident that no one could miss it]{.class_s321}[---the need ]{.class_s322}[for ]{.class_s321}[an absolute truthful authority. ]{.class_s322}[Ninety eight percent of the men being paid to teach Christian young people miss it just as completely as Noah's generation "missed the boat." The ]{.class_s321}["fair virgins" ]{.class_s323}[and ]{.class_s321}["young men" ]{.class_s323}[faint for thirst while they get a serpent for a fish, a rock for bread, and a scorpion for an egg (Luke 11:11--12).]{.class_s321}

[In desperate need of PURITY, our young people are handed "bibles" that are about as "pure" as the library of Ernest Hemingway. In desperate need of ]{.class_s321}[TRUTH, ]{.class_s322}[our young people are led to believe that Pilate was a Christian scholar when he asked ]{.class_s321}["What IS TRUTH?" ]{.class_s323}[(John 17:17) and then went away without an answer! Christ said, ]{.class_s321}["THY WORD IS TRUTH" ]{.class_s323}[(John 17:17), and if you don't have HIS WORD, you don't have the truth. In desperate need of ]{.class_s321}[POWER, ]{.class_s322}[our young people are fed on religious slop that couldn't nourish a jay bird. In desperate need of a permanent anchor, our young people are led to believe that the vacillating opinions of destructive critics are more authoritative than the Holy Bible that was responsible for their Salvation.]{.class_s321}

[We get letters in here from morning to night. The last one was from some poor wretch in a "bastion of orthodoxy" complaining that the apparent contradictions between 1 and 2 Samuel and 1 and 2 Chronicles were real because some Liberal had printed them in a book. This student thought this problem was "so serious, that Brother Ruckman's 'thesis' on the ]{.class_s321}[AV ]{.class_s322}[could not be defended till they were answered!" While this deluded child ]{.class_s321}[in graduate school ]{.class_s322}[was paying \$6,000 dollars a year tuition for a fieldhouse, underground wiring, Catholic paintings, and a revolving stage (!), our "red neck" students in Pensacola, in a cement block shack, were ironing out the passages every week; and a few of our young men did not even have a ]{.class_s321}[high school education. ]{.class_s322}[Shame, man! ]{.class_s321}[Disgrace! ]{.class_s322}[If your professor doesn't have ]{.class_s321}["THE WORDS OF THE LORD," ]{.class_s323}[what makes you think he deserves ANY salary from ]{.class_s321}[any ]{.class_s322}[Christian, let alone a ]{.class_s321}[Bible believing ]{.class_s322}[Christian?]{.class_s321}

"[They that swear by the sin of Samaria, and say, Thy God, O DAN" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 14). The passage is a reference to Israel's idolatry (see 1 Kings 12:27--30). ]{.class_s321}["And, The manner of Beer sheba liveth"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 14). The reference is to Judah's idolatry.]{.class_s321}

[Bullinger's note (]{.class_s321}[Companion Bible]{.class_s322}[) that Beersheba was one of the two places set up by Jeroboam (1 Kings 12) is about as wild a comment as you ever read. It was Dan and ]{.class_s321}[Bethel ]{.class_s322}[(1 Kings 12), not Dan and ]{.class_s321}[Beersheba. ]{.class_s322}[Beersheba was SOUTH of Judah, and the tribe of Simeon, which was there, had their inheritance within JUDAH (see Joshua 19:2). In 2 Chronicles 34:6 Simeon has moved up north with Manasseh and Ephraim, but the town of Beersheba remains as the standard southern border (1 Kings 4:25) of Judah; Dan is the southern border for Israel.]{.class_s321}

[But Bullinger is not the only one to fall by the wayside. Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown strike the word "Beersheba" clear out of the passage and refuse to even comment on it. Instead, we read that ]{.class_s321}["THE MANNER"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 14) is not to be connected with anything; it was merely "the mode or way of worship." But the verse said ]{.class_s321}["THE MANNER OF BEER SHEBA LIVETH." ]{.class_s323}[One Premillennialist removes a calf from Bethel and puts it down in Beersheba []{#c1XH.xhtml_page_331}(Bullinger), and the other Premillennialist pretends the word isn't in the text: Typical "Fundamentalism" these days.]{.class_s321}

[However, Nowack is "queen for a day" when it comes to expositing the passage. He says that ]{.class_s321}["the manner of" ]{.class_s323}[means "THE DARLING." (How about "sweetie pie"? How about "apple dumpling"? Gee, here is an oldie but goodie! "Tootsie wootsie." How about, "thy puddin'is a Beersheba hag bag"?) Motyer (]{.class_s321}[The New Bible Commentary]{.class_s322}[)]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s322}[pleads ignorance while Kenneth Taylor (]{.class_s321}[The Dead Bible]{.class_s322}[)]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s322}[simply erases the entire phrase and leaves out ]{.class_s321}["the manner" ]{.class_s323}[and ]{.class_s321}["liveth." ]{.class_s323}[Dummelow steps forward to help us out (after changing words in verses 3 and 8) by saying that what Amos probably wrote was, "by the ]{.class_s321}[life ]{.class_s322}[of the deity of Beersheba." But then again, he could have written "by the beaver of doazy doats near a cussed out water hole." (After all if he didn't write ]{.class_s321}[what he wrote, ]{.class_s322}[he could have written ]{.class_s321}[anything]{.class_s322}[.)]{.class_s321}

[Returning to the infallible authority for absolute truth (]{.class_s321}[AV ]{.class_s322}[1611), we find that:]{.class_s321}

[1. Apostate priests from JUDAH had offered incense to false gods at ]{.class_s321}[BEERSHEBA ]{.class_s322}[(see 2 Kings 23:8).]{.class_s321}

[2. It was the Simeonites who dwelt at Beersheba, and of this tribe Jacob said, ]{.class_s321}["CURSED BE THEIR ANGER," ]{.class_s323}[and ]{.class_s321}["O my soul, come not thou into their secret; unto THEIR ASSEMBLY, mine honour, be not thou UNITED" ]{.class_s323}[(Gen. 49:6--7).]{.class_s321}

[The prophesy of Amos, therefore, is aimed at ]{.class_s321}[both ]{.class_s322}[sections of Israel: the house of Israel (north) and the house of Judah (south). Revelation 3:1 is a comment on the expression ]{.class_s321}["the manner\...LIVETH" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 14). The religion was practiced and the false teaching continued, but it had no ]{.class_s321}[life ]{.class_s322}[(see Hosea 8:6 and comments). The people who swore by the golden calf in Dan and the people who bragged about the "unbroken line of succession" for their "way of worship" ]{.class_s321}["SHALL FALL, and never rise up again" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 14). The ridiculous ]{.class_s321}[RSV ]{.class_s322}[has written "those who swear by ASHIMAH of Samaria"; the LXX has altered ]{.class_s321}["the manner" ]{.class_s323}[to]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s323}["thy God"; the ]{.class_s321}[ASV ]{.class_s322}[(1901) has altered ]{.class_s321}["the manner"]{.class_s323}[ to the "way"; and Nowack, Marti, Harnack, Keil, Hitzig, Guthe, and []{#c1XH.xhtml_page_332}Orelli have made enough changes in the chapter to qualify as change agents for Soviet psycho-politics.]{.class_s321}

[]{#c1ZC.xhtml}

CHAPTER NINE

9:1 I saw the Lord standing upon the altar: and he said, Smite the lintel of the door, that the posts may shake: and cut them in the head, all of them; and I will slay the last of them with the sword: he that fleeth of them shall not flee away, and he that escapeth of them shall not be delivered.

2 Though they dig into hell, thence shall mine hand take them; though they climb up to heaven, thence will I bring them down:

3 And though they hide themselves in the top of Carmel, I will search and take them out thence; and though they be hid from my sight in the bottom of the sea, thence will I command the serpent, and he shall bite them:

4 And though they go into captivity before their enemies, thence will I command the sword, and it shall slay them: and I will set mine eyes upon them for evil, and not for good.

5 And the Lord GOD of hosts is he that toucheth the land, and it shall melt, and all that dwell therein shall mourn: and it shall rise up wholly like a flood; and shall be drowned, as by the flood of Egypt.

6 It is he that buildeth his stories in the heaven, and hath founded his troop in the earth; he that calleth for the waters of the sea, and poureth them out upon the face of the earth: The LORD is his name.

7 Are ye not as children of the Ethiopians unto me, O children of Israel? saith the LORD. Have not I brought up Israel out of the land of Egypt? and the Philistines from Caphtor, and the Syrians from Kir?

8 Behold, the eyes of the Lord GOD are upon the sinful kingdom, and I will destroy it from off the face of the earth; saving that I will not utterly destroy the house of Jacob, saith []{#c1ZC.xhtml_page_334}the LORD.

9 For, lo, I will command, and I will sift the house of Israel among all nations, like as corn is sifted in a sieve, yet shall not the least grain fall upon the earth.

10 All the sinners of my people shall die by the sword, which say, The evil shall not overtake nor prevent us.

[Once again the Holy Spirit points out to us the fact that Amos' prophecies rarely end with the historical captivity of Israel in 720 B.C. Although the majority of Conservative scholars (plus Atheists and Deists) would like to have us believe this, there is no way to believe the Bible text as it stands and accept this "historical" theory as these men would have it. Notice the remarkable insertion in verse 9: ]{.class_s321}["and I will sift the house of Israel AMONG ALL NATIONS, like" ]{.class_s323}[and another one in verse 2: ]{.class_s321}["though they CLIMB UP TO HEAVEN."]{.class_s323}

[These passages are not to be taken in the light and frothy manner by which Charles Feinberg handles Nahum 2:4, which see. The disrespect (or downright contempt) which some Christian educators show toward the ]{.class_s321}[Authorized ]{.class_s322}[text at these points was learned from the priests of the Alexandrian Cult who were taught to disrespect the ]{.class_s321}[AV ]{.class_s322}[(1611), and these, in turn, were brainwashed by a pseudo apostolic succession (see 2 Cor. 11:10--14) which extends from the first high priest of the Cult (Origen) to the last Fundamental "Harvey Wallbumper" (fill in your own list; there are 3,000 of them).]{.class_s321}

[Observe further that the case is exactly as we have stated it when we mentioned the ]{.class_s321}[dual application ]{.class_s322}[of Amos' prophecies to Israel and Judah. Verse 7: ]{.class_s321}["ISRAEL out of the land of Egypt?" ]{.class_s323}[Verse 10: ]{.class_s321}["ALL the sinners of MY PEOPLE." ]{.class_s323}[Verse 8: ]{.class_s321}["I will not UTTERLY DESTROY the house of Jacob."]{.class_s323}[ All attempts to confine the prophecies to Israel ]{.class_s321}[alone ]{.class_s322}[(the northern tribes) will fall flat on their exegetical faces. Watching the expositors misapplying verses 1 and 9 in the passage and then []{#c1ZC.xhtml_page_335}watching them hit verse 11 (in the next section) is like watching a bravo unit (Vietnam, 1970) going into an X ambush.]{.class_s321}

"[I saw the Lord standing UPON THE ALTAR" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 1). One hundred percent of the commentators mistake ]{.class_s321}["the altar" ]{.class_s323}[to be the one at ]{.class_s321}[Bethel ]{.class_s322}[which God destroyed many years before Amos was born (1 Kings 13). Only the scribe of the ]{.class_s321}[Septuagint ]{.class_s322}[grasps the truth, but wherever a dead orthodox apostate grabs ONE truth, he immediately alters every verse of Scripture he can get his hands on to make it reinforce the ]{.class_s321}[one truth ]{.class_s322}[he has found (see any exposition of ]{.class_s321}[Hebrews ]{.class_s322}[or ]{.class_s321}[James ]{.class_s322}[by Zodhiates, Thieme, Wuest, or any writer in the ]{.class_s321}[Biblical Viewpoint, ]{.class_s322}[BJU Press); so, the LXX has written "smite the MERCY SEAT' in the verse. Observe further that the smiting (vs. 1, ]{.class_s321}["Smite the LINTEL of the DOOR"]{.class_s323}[) had nothing to do with ]{.class_s321}[any altar; ]{.class_s322}[it has to do with the ]{.class_s321}[temple. ]{.class_s322}[You read NOTHING about any "temple" anywhere in Dan or Bethel in 1 Kings 12--13. The exposition, therefore, given by Durnmelow, followed by Williams (Postmillennialist), ]{.class_s321}[The New Bible Commentary, The Wycliffe Bible Commentary, ]{.class_s322}[Ellicott, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, Nowack, Marti, Orelli, and the next 3,000 expositors, is poppycock. ]{.class_s321}[The reference here is to JUDAH, ]{.class_s322}[and if there is any argument about the date, observe that Micah the Morasthite was talking about the ]{.class_s321}[disintegration of the temple ]{.class_s322}[while the northern tribes ]{.class_s321}[were still in their own land. ]{.class_s322}[Look at 2 Kings 18:9 and compare it with Jeremiah 26:18 and Micah 1:1. Micah is prophesying before the reign of Hezekiah, and the captivity of Israel did not take place until after the ]{.class_s321}["sixth year" ]{.class_s323}[of Hezekiah (2 Kings 18:10). Amos could have prophesied 9:1 AFTER Micah had dumped the burden on the elders of Judah.]{.class_s321}

"[Smite the lintel of the door, that the posts may shake: and cut THEM in the head, all of them" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 1). Williams and Dummelow say we should read with the ]{.class_s321}[RSV ]{.class_s322}[(1884). The grossly corrupt ]{.class_s321}[ASV ]{.class_s322}[(and ]{.class_s321}[NASV]{.class_s322}[) have translated the word ]{.class_s321}["UPON"]{.class_s323}[ (Heb.---]{.class_s321}[al]{.class_s322}[)]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s322}[as "BESIDE," to match the ]{.class_s321}[RSV ]{.class_s322}[and ]{.class_s321}[NRSV ]{.class_s322}[of the NCCC. The idea suggested here by the Fundamentalists and the Liberals is that the Lord could not possibly have been []{#c1ZC.xhtml_page_336}standing ]{.class_s321}[on the altar. ]{.class_s322}[Why He could not, in view of the fact ]{.class_s321}[that He came and went in the fire that was on the altar ]{.class_s322}[(see Judg. 13:20), is a little foggy to the believing mind; but when did a critic of the ]{.class_s321}[AV ]{.class_s322}[ever drive in anything but "ground clouds"?]{.class_s321}

[Not content with changing one word, the ]{.class_s321}[ASV ]{.class_s322}[and ]{.class_s321}[NASV]{.class_s322}[ follow the ]{.class_s321}[RSV ]{.class_s322}[and ]{.class_s321}[NRSV]{.class_s322}[ with "thresholds" for ]{.class_s321}["POSTS"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 1).]{.class_s321}

[As we have stated before, at least 5,000 of the 32,000 corruptions in the ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[of Pensacola Christian are copies of the ]{.class_s321}[National Council of Churches' ]{.class_s322}["bibles." If you don't believe it, ]{.class_s321}[check it out, ]{.class_s322}[but don't slander and blaspheme just because you're lazy or because you're gullible: ]{.class_s321}[Grow up]{.class_s322}[.\]]{.class_s321}

[The idea is that Amos has been commanded to smite the ]{.class_s321}[top of the doors to the temple ]{.class_s322}[so hard that it rattles the columns (1 Kings 7:21). These posts literally have their]{.class_s321}[ "HEADS" ]{.class_s323}[cut off (2 Kings 25:13) since the head is the ]{.class_s321}[chapiter ]{.class_s322}[(see 1 Kings 7:16--19). The reference is directly to Judah and Jerusalem, and only the dogged, man following, man pleasing, Bible-denying traditionalists could possibly apply the verse to 1 Kings 13. ]{.class_s321}[So all of them do.]{.class_s322}

"[And I will slay the last of THEM with the sword" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 1). So, what happened to Solomon's ]{.class_s321}["posts"]{.class_s323}[---not "threshold," as in the ridiculous ]{.class_s321}[NASV]{.class_s322}[---is going to happen also to ]{.class_s321}["MY PEOPLE" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 10). As a matter of fact, DECAPITATION will be the method of execution (Rev. 20:4) during Daniel's Seventieth Week (see Rev. 6:9--12 and comments). The ]{.class_s321}[RSV ]{.class_s322}[reading ("shatter them on the heads of all the people") was first suggested by Philip Schaff, Machen, Warfield, and Dr. Green back in 1901, when all of these gentlemen promoted the vile ]{.class_s321}[ASV]{.class_s322}[.]{.class_s321}

[And as we have stated in other places, the penchant for promoting the ]{.class_s321}[NIV ]{.class_s322}[and the ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[has nothing to do with Biblical scholarship, defense of the faith, fidelity to the word, or "qualified authorities." It has to do with the fact that the old nature in Torrey, Rice, Sunday, Afman, Whitefield, Spurgeon, Ironside, Custer, Neal, Olson, Thieme, Wuest, Zodhiates, Trench, Driver, Davis, Metzger, and Aland was just like the old nature in Cain, Pharaoh, Ahab, Judas, and Pilate. By recommending ]{.class_s321}[two authorities ]{.class_s322}[which ]{.class_s321}[conflict ]{.class_s322}[(]{.class_s321}[AV ]{.class_s322}[and ]{.class_s321}[ASV]{.class_s322}[, or ]{.class_s321}[AV ]{.class_s322}[and ]{.class_s321}[]{#c1ZC.xhtml_page_337}[NASV]{.class_s322}[, or ]{.class_s321}[AV ]{.class_s322}[and ]{.class_s321}[NIV]{.class_s322}[, or etc., etc.), the "godly scholar" is able to make the word of God of none effect and set ]{.class_s321}[scholarship up as the final authority. ]{.class_s322}[This is being done fifty times a day in every Christian university in America.]{.class_s321}

"[Though they dig into hell" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 2) indicates that Hell is DOWN (Matt. 12:40; Jonah 2:1--2), as in D O W N.]{.class_s321}

[Readers of the ]{.class_s321}[New Scofield Bible ]{.class_s322}[would be wise to ignore its apologetic notes like the one found on 2 Corinthians 12:1--2. Those of us who have a compass, anchor, chart, and captain know that there is no such thing as "relative directions" unless they are relative to ]{.class_s321}[the absolute standard--]{.class_s322}[the plumbline that has already been dropped (see Amos 7:7 and comments).\]]{.class_s321}

"[Though they climb UP to heaven" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 2) is showing that neither deep sea diving (Rom. 10:5--9) nor interplanetary space travel (see Deut. 30:12) nor "moon trips" will get anyone out of anything. It is nice for those of superstitious fancy to dream about Baalite monks saving mankind by getting them off the earth before the final atomic holocaust (see A ]{.class_s321}[Canticle for Leibowitz, ]{.class_s322}[Miller) but ]{.class_s321}[Grimm's Fairy Tales ]{.class_s322}[are just as good. If you got to Mars, Jupiter, or Venus (or beyond) you'd burn up anyway (2 Pet. 3:8--14) when the whole furshlugginer mess exploded (Rev. 20:11).]{.class_s321}

[Here, ]{.class_s321}[Carmel is ]{.class_s322}[likened to the Heavens (see Song of Sol. 7:5--6, applying to the bride and bridegroom after the rapture), and ]{.class_s321}[Hell is ]{.class_s322}[likened to ]{.class_s321}["the bottom of the SEA"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 3). The ]{.class_s321}["serpent"]{.class_s323}[ here (vs. 3) is the ]{.class_s321}[Leviathan ]{.class_s322}[of Job 41, and we have commented on that matter at length in the Job Commentary---enough so that no Christian should be as stupid about it as ]{.class_s321}[The New Bible Commentary ]{.class_s322}[which says the serpent was "a ]{.class_s321}[mythological ]{.class_s322}[monster of contemporary paganism."]{.class_s321}

[My, my, what Dr. Motyer is going to learn at the Judgment Seat of Christ---if he ever makes it! That ]{.class_s321}["serpent" ]{.class_s323}[(see Rev. 12:2--6) is about as ]{.class_s321}[mythological ]{.class_s322}[as the laws of thermodynamics. He is no more "contemporary" with Amos than he was in the Garden of Eden or at the White Throne Judgment. That ]{.class_s321}["serpent"]{.class_s323}[ is an]{.class_s321}[ "OLD SERPENT" ]{.class_s323}[(Rev. 12:9), and deceiving Christian scholars like Dr. Motyer, Dr. Yaeger, and Dr. Schultz is his main []{#c1ZC.xhtml_page_338}business in life. (Human nature will deceive the average sinner without too much pressure being applied; the "big" game" is the Christian scholar who was given the lifetime job of getting rid of the ]{.class_s321}[King James Bible]{.class_s322}[.)]{.class_s321}

"[And though they go into captivity before their enemies, thence will I command the sword, and it shall slay them" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 4). Notice how all of this matches the horrendous threats leveled at the entire nation (not just the northern tribes) in Deuteronomy 28 and Leviticus 26. When God sets His eyes (vs. 4) on a nation, church, school, or man for ]{.class_s321}["EVIL, and not for good" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 4), they are in more trouble than they can handle even if they have health, wealth, family, friends, security, and a good job.]{.class_s321}

[The ]{.class_s321}["lower parts of the earth" ]{.class_s323}[(Psa. 63:9) where Hell is (Deut. 32:22) are under your feet (Psa. 71:20), and there are degrees of descent (see Psa. 86:13). What Conservative scholars think about a ]{.class_s321}[literal ]{.class_s322}[Hell with literal GATES and BARS (see Jonah 2:6) and KEYS (see Rev. 1:18) under your feet (Matt. 12:40) is no more consequence than Rome's teaching that the "GATES" of Hell (Matt. 16:18) are the "powers"---exactly as suggested in the ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[by the Lockman Foundation. David said if he went ]{.class_s321}[down ]{.class_s322}[to Hell, he still couldn't get away from God (Psa. 139:8). Israel may be ]{.class_s321}["hid in a field" ]{.class_s323}[(Matt. 13:44), but they will be ]{.class_s321}["sifted in a sieve"]{.class_s323}[ (va. 9) without "the least grain" falling on the ground. The "sifter" is Satan (see Luke 22:31).]{.class_s321}

"[The Lord God of hosts" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 5) is the One who will send hunters and fishers (Jer. 16:16) after Israel until they are reduced to such a pitiful remnant (Lam. 4:16--19) that the holocaust of Hitler and the inferno of Eichmann will be pleasant memories. Hitler only eliminated between five and six million Jews (and there are statistical tables available that would indicate a much lower number). However, when the Son of Perdition gets through killing them (and eating them---Isa. 6:13) there will probably not be left alive more than 5,000 out of a race of 15,000,000 people. ]{.class_s321}["All the sinners of my people shall die by the sword, which say, The evil shall not overtake nor prevent us" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 10). In describing the final judgments on Israel, in the Tribulation, the Lord repeats 8:8 and 9:5, which see.]{.class_s321}

"[It is he that buildeth his stories in the heaven" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 6). The three storied ]{.class_s321}[heaven ]{.class_s322}[(2 Cor. 12:1--4) is indicated by the three storied ]{.class_s321}[ark ]{.class_s322}[(Gen. 6:16) and the three storied ]{.class_s321}[temple ]{.class_s322}[(Ezek. 42:6). ]{.class_s321}["And hath founded his troop in the earth" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 6). The word has been altered to "vault" by Maurer, and this "front door boom jockey" is immediately followed by Motyer, Schultz, the ]{.class_s321}[ASV, NASV, RSV, NRSV, ]{.class_s322}[etc. The]{.class_s321}[ NEB ]{.class_s322}[has mistranslated ]{.class_s321}["troop" ]{.class_s323}[as "ceiling," while the corrupt LXX has made it to read "promises." (There is no confusion like the unmitigated Babel produced by men trying to make the Bible say what it does ]{.class_s321}[not ]{.class_s322}[say.)]{.class_s321}

"[HIS TROOP" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 6). The Hebrew word ]{.class_s321}[raguddah]{.class_s322}[)]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s322}[is kin to ]{.class_s321}[gad ]{.class_s322}[and ]{.class_s321}[gadud. ]{.class_s322}[Never does this word refer to any kind of a "band" (see any of the unqualified authorities) other than a group of living beings; ]{.class_s321}[not once does it ever refer to any kind of a band over the earth, arch over the heavens, vault over the earth, ]{.class_s322}[or any other cockeyed invention of some superstitious, sapheaded smart aleck. A solid "band" or "arch" is given in Hebrew as ]{.class_s321}[chebel, aboth, ]{.class_s322}[or ]{.class_s321}[esur ]{.class_s322}[(or ]{.class_s321}[elammim ]{.class_s322}[or ]{.class_s321}[ellamoth]{.class_s322}[). The "vault," invented by the campfire girls, came from the German rationalists (1700--1900) who taught that the superstitious man who wrote Genesis 1 didn't have any better sense than to adopt the ancient ]{.class_s321}[legend ]{.class_s322}[that the sky was a solid VAULT over the earth (see any text book of philosophy or science). We have covered this matter sufficiently under Genesis 1:1--6 to show who is really superstitious and who knew what they were talking about. But you see, the insertion of ]{.class_s321}["HIS TROOP" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 6) is such an abrupt change from the ]{.class_s321}["stories" ]{.class_s323}[which were built (vs. 6) that 100 percent of the Conservative scholars must insist the ]{.class_s321}[AV ]{.class_s322}[translators were "sincerely misled" because they did not have "access to the vast resources of modern claptrap" to help them out (see Prov. 30:6 and comments).]{.class_s321}

[But God's ]{.class_s321}["troop" ]{.class_s323}[is a reference to His "host" on this earth. Observe that God also has a host in ]{.class_s321}[Heaven ]{.class_s322}[(Dan. 4:35); one of God's favorite Old Testament titles is ]{.class_s321}["the God of Hosts"]{.class_s323}[ (Amos 3:13, 4:13, 5:14), ]{.class_s321}["the LORD of hosts" ]{.class_s323}[(Mal. 1:6,8,10--11,13; Zech. 8:1,3,6,9,11), and ]{.class_s321}["the Lord God of hosts" ]{.class_s323}[(Isa. 22:5,12). The host of Heaven (2 Chron. 33:5) mentioned []{#c1ZC.xhtml_page_340}in Acts 7:42 is one host and the other host is the ]{.class_s321}["troop in the earth" ]{.class_s323}[(text). This troop consists of God's ]{.class_s321}[creation; ]{.class_s322}[it not only includes the vast list of Psalm 147 and 148, but the literal ]{.class_s321}["TROOPS" ]{.class_s323}[of Joel 2 (see comments) who will be on earth in the Tribulation, plus the troops of heathen armies in the past which God mustered to attack JUDAH (see Isa. 10:6, 13:4, 19:2, 31:4, etc.). The kingdoms of this earth (see the following verses, Amos 9:7--9) are God's host ]{.class_s321}["in the earth" ]{.class_s323}[and are, therefore, for His use (see Jer. 5:15 and Jer. 1:10).]{.class_s321}

"[He that calleth for the waters of the sea" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 6) is the same as in Genesis 1:2, and ]{.class_s321}["poureth them out upon the face of the earth" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 6) is the same as in Genesis 6--7.]{.class_s321}

"[Are ye not as children of the Ethiopians unto me, O children of Israel?" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 7). Notice, again, the vicious bigotry of God Almighty as He reveals His "racist" tendencies. The Philistines, Egyptians, and Ethiopians were all of ]{.class_s321}[Negro origin ]{.class_s322}[(see Gen. 10 and comments in that Commentary), and even the Syrians (vs. 7) were from Southern Mesopotamia where Nimrod (Negroid) built the Tower of Babel. When God wants to liken His people to the lowest thing He can liken them to, He mentions three nations of half breed ]{.class_s321}[Hamitic ]{.class_s322}[stock. The idea is simple; God has done as much for ]{.class_s321}[Africans ]{.class_s322}[as He did for Israel, at least in respect to temporary release from their bondage to other nations. But just as the Syrians had to go back into captivity to Kir (see 2 Kings 16:9) and the Philistines had to go back into captivity (Ezek. 25:16), so the Jews will go back into captivity after getting out of Egypt (vs. 7). The God who is able to "liberate" is able to "enslave" (see Deut. 28:68). \[For ]{.class_s321}["Caphtor" ]{.class_s323}[see Gen. 10:14 and comments. Jeremiah 47:4 and Isaiah 14:29 are good comments on the passage.\]]{.class_s321}

[God will ]{.class_s321}["not utterly destroy the house of Jacob" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 8); so, a remnant returns under Ezra, Nehemiah, and Zerubbabel (Ezra 1--4), but verses 9--10 go far beyond the ]{.class_s321}[first captivity; ]{.class_s322}[they reach out to the great dispersions of A.D. 70--A.D. 1990 where England, Germany, Spain, France, Russia, China and (eventually) the USA unite to exterminate Israel[]{#c1ZC.xhtml_page_341}.]{.class_s321}

11 In that day will I raise up the tabernacle of David that is fallen, and close up the breaches thereof; and I will raise up his ruins, and I will build it as in the days of old:

12 That they may possess the remnant of Edom, and of all the heathen, which are called by my name, saith the LORD that doeth this.

13 Behold, the days come, saith the LORD, that the plowman shall overtake the reaper, and the treader of grapes him that soweth seed; and the mountains shall drop sweet wine, and all the hills shall melt.

14 And I will bring again the captivity of my people of Israel, and they shall build the waste cities, and inhabit them; and they shall plant vineyards, and drink the wine thereof; they shall also make gardens, and eat the fruit of them.

15 And I will plant them upon their land, and they shall no more be pulled up out of their land which I have given them, saith the LORD thy God.

[The passage is a source of untold grief for the Postmillennial and Amillennial expositors (Calvin, Berkhof, Clarke, Henry, Dummelow, Schaff, Machen, Warfield, Motyer, Harnack, Hitzig, Nowack, et al.), for it states (in third-grade English) that the ]{.class_s321}[literal, ]{.class_s322}[political nation of physical Israelites will someday return to Palestine on a permanent basis and rebuild its literal cities and dwell in them ]{.class_s321}[permanently]{.class_s322}[ (vs. 15).]{.class_s321}[ The Wycliffe Bible Commentary ]{.class_s322}[comes through with flying colors (this time!) and, of course, Williams and Bullinger (being Premillennialists) have no problem with the passage.]{.class_s321}

"[The tabernacle of David that is fallen" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 11) is mentioned by James in Acts 15:16--17 (see comments in that Commentary). INTRODUCTION TO

OBADIAH

[Obadiah is the shortest book in the Old Testament (3 John is the shortest in the New Testament). The word itself means "a servant of the Lord" or "worshiper of the Lord" or "a serving Jehovah." The ]{.class_s321}[Authorized Version]{.class_s322}[ places Obadiah in the ]{.class_s321}[fourth ]{.class_s322}[place among the minor prophets, since this is the order of the Hebrew canon. ]{.class_s321}[The corrupt LXX has Obadiah placed fifth, ]{.class_s322}[although the modern editors (any corrupter since 1880) have ]{.class_s321}[changed the order around in the Septuagint to match the King James' ]{.class_s322}[order: Typical. Jerome makes Obadiah a contemporary of Hosea, Joel, and Amos, but this was only done to bolster the theory that Jeremiah borrowed some of his prophecies (see Jer. 49:7--10) from a previous prophet. Obadiah, according to this Graf Wellhausen type of malarkey, had to prophesy verses 5--6, and 8 ]{.class_s321}[before ]{.class_s322}[Jeremiah quoted them. Following the antiquated guess work of Jaeger and Maurer, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown place Obadiah ]{.class_s321}[before ]{.class_s322}[Jeremiah and count the Edomite trouble (vss. 10--16) to be something that went on in 2 Chronicles 12 or 2 Chronicles 21 or 2 Chronicles 25 or 2 Kings 24. The idea here is that the Edomites took advantage of the Jews following persecution by the Egyptians, Philistines, and Arabians. Most of the scholars conjecture that the Edomites in Obadiah are taking vengeance on the Jews for the acts of 2 Chronicles 25:14.]{.class_s321}

[We shall place Obadiah immediately ]{.class_s321}[following ]{.class_s322}[the destruction of Jerusalem (around 587--580 B.C.), and we are going to be no more concerned about what "source" Jeremiah and Obadiah used when speaking the same thing than about the "Q" document of an Aramaic missing link which Deutero-Dumpty (see Gen. 16:7--11 and comments) used with Hezekiah's collection of Agur. The Bible is a supernatural book, and if God wishes to give two men the same message or let one quote another or let one ]{.class_s321}[freely ]{.class_s322}[quote another or let both quote ]{.class_s321}[one document ]{.class_s322}[or let both quote one ]{.class_s321}[oral message, ]{.class_s322}[it doesn't have []{#c21F.xhtml_page_348}any affect on prophetic truth one way or another.]{.class_s321}[ "All scripture is given by inspiration of God" ]{.class_s323}[(2 Tim. 3:16), not by any method invented by any higher critic who thought he was high enough to reshingle the top of an outhouse.]{.class_s321}

[If Obadiah and Jeremiah occasionally match, ]{.class_s321}[so do Matthew and Mark.]{.class_s322}

[If Isaiah and Amos occasionally match, so do ]{.class_s321}[Jude and Simon Peter.]{.class_s322}

However, all the balderdash about sources and historical dates are really beside the point since Obadiah is often talking about the Second Advent and the Millennium. Observe that verses 15, 17, 18, and 21 have no more to do with Ahaz, Amaziah, Joash, the Philistines, Nebuchadnezzar, and the Babylonian captivity (or restoration of Ezra and Nehemiah) than they have to do with Noah's ark. What Edom did to Judah when they went into captivity, they will pay for at the Second Advent because they have never paid for it since Judah went to Babylon. Psalm 137:7 and Lamentations 4:21--22 are definite types of the destruction that will take place in Revelation 11:14 and the future condition of Israel in Job's country---UZ (see Job 1:1 and comments in that Commentary).

[With this in mind, we will approach Obadiah as ]{.class_s321}[pre-written history ]{.class_s322}[as well as ]{.class_s321}[past recorded history, ]{.class_s322}[never forgetting the passage on Edom in Isaiah 34 was allegorized and "spiritualized" by every commentator since Origen. You will find the word "IMAGERY' pasted over every comment on Isaiah 34:11--14 by every Atheist, Agnostic, Fundamentalist, Catholic, Conservative, Liberal, and Deist, alike, in spite of the clear warning of Isaiah 34:16 that Isaiah 34:6--15 deals with the Millennial condition of Obadiah's charge---EDOM.]{.class_s321}

[]{#c21W.xhtml}

CHAPTER ONE

1:1 The vision of Obadiah. Thus saith the Lord GOD concerning Edom; We have heard a rumour from the LORD, and an ambassador is sent among the heathen, Arise ye, and let us rise up against her in battle.

2 Behold, I have made thee small among the heathen: thou art greatly despised.

3 The pride of thine heart hath deceived thee, thou that dwellest in the clefts of the rock, whose habitation is high; that saith in his heart, Who shall bring me down to the ground?

4 Though thou exalt thyself as the eagle, and though thou set thy nest among the stars, thence will I bring thee down, saith the LORD.

5 If thieves came to thee, if robbers by night, (how art thou cut off!) would they not have stolen till they had enough? if the grapegatherers came to thee, would they not leave some grapes?

6 How are the things of Esau searched out! how are his hidden things sought up!

7 All the men of thy confederacy have brought thee even to the border: the men that were at peace with thee have deceived thee, and prevailed against thee; they that eat thy bread have laid a wound under thee: there is none understanding in him.

8 Shall I not in that day, saith the LORD, even destroy the wise men out of Edom, and understanding out of the mount of Esau?

9 And thy mighty men, O Teman, shall be dismayed, to the end that every one of the mount of Esau may be cut off by slaughter[]{#c21W.xhtml_page_350}.

[Historically]{.class_s322}[, verse one remarks on the return of Nebuchadnezzar after the attack in 587 B.C., for Nebuchadnezzar returned, according to Jeremiah, and went through Edom on his way down into Egypt (Jer. 43:10--13) and captured it (Jer. 49:22). The ]{.class_s321}["rumor,"]{.class_s323}[ then,]{.class_s321}[ "from the LORD"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 1) comes from the mouth of Jeremiah in Jeremiah 49:7, and in those days "rumors were flying" (see Jer. 40:15).]{.class_s321}

"[Behold, I have made thee small among the heathen" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 2). So, Edom never becomes a third rate power during the history of Israel. From Esau's occupation of the land (after the Horites: see Gen. 36:20) to the time of Christ, the nation of Edorn was unable to conquer anyone: Judah, Israel, Egypt, Syria, Assyria, or the Philistines. In some way Edorn stands for ]{.class_s321}[Rome, ]{.class_s322}[although at one time Rome ruled the world. The Hebrew word for "Rome" is the same as the word for Edom, with the insertion of a ]{.class_s321}[vau ]{.class_s322}[after the ]{.class_s321}[daleth.]{.class_s322}

"[Esau" stands for all time as a male prostitute (see Heb. 12:16) who sold his birthright, exactly as the Catholic Church sold out Christianity to gain a name for herself in the political world (Rev. 17:18). And one must never forget that Rome, per se, went off the map before A.D. 500. The city only retained power after that through religious coercion, political chicanery, superstition, bigotry, compromise, and cowardice. Italy itself has never been a third rate power since the Reformation, and her future power will lie only in the ]{.class_s321}[religious realm ]{.class_s322}[(coming from ten acres of ground---the Vatican): not from her power as one of the heathen "nations" (vs. 2).]{.class_s321}

[Verse 3 is clear. Edom is likened to a smug, selfsatisfied (vs. 8), conceited man who has been deceived (vs. 7) by his own buddies and thinks he is safe (vs. 3) but is about to be knocked flat in the dirt (vs. 4). The rock city (Petra, 2 Kings 14:7) consisted of ]{.class_s321}["clefts"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 3). Throughout the discourse the country is personified as a man (Esau, vss. 6--7; Teman, vs. 9). Where real "imagery" occurs (vs. 4: the eagle with his nest among the stars), the reader should look a little closer, for the STARS are on the American flag and both Roman and []{#c21W.xhtml_page_351}American governments used the EAGLE as a national symbol.]{.class_s321}

It may be that the "high habitation" (vs. 3) is more than a reference to the hill country of Edom!

[Verse 5 implies that thieves and gleaners would not have taken everything, but when God deals with Edom they will lose everything including valuables that have been hidden (vs. 6). The tribes that Edom seeks to draw into an alliance betray him (vs. 7) because Edom betrayed his own brother (Num. 20:20--21). Those who shall sit and eat at the council table (see Prov. 23:6--8) will stab Edom in the back: ]{.class_s321}["they that eat thy bread have laid a wound under thee" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 7).]{.class_s321}

[Readers of the Book of Job may understand why ]{.class_s321}["wise men" ]{.class_s323}[and ]{.class_s321}["understanding" ]{.class_s323}[were associated with Edom (vs. 8), for it is here that the great drama of suffering, sin, Satan and salvation were portrayed by Elihu many years before Moses came up out of the land of Egypt. \[See ]{.class_s321}[The Bible Believer's Commentary on the Book of Job, ]{.class_s322}[1978.\] Verse 9 is self explanatory.]{.class_s321}

10 For thy violence against thy brother Jacob shame shall cover thee, and thou shalt be cut off for ever.

11 In the day that thou stoodest on the other side, in the day that the strangers carried away captive his forces, and foreigners entered into his gates, and cast lots upon Jerusalem, even thou wast as one of them.

12 But thou shouldest not have looked on the day of thy brother in the day that he became a stranger; neither shouldest thou have rejoiced over the children of Judah in the day of their destruction; neither shouldest thou have spoken proudly in the day of distress.

13 Thou shouldest not have entered into the gate of my people in the day of their calamity; yea, thou shouldest not have looked on their affliction in the day of their calamity, nor []{#c21W.xhtml_page_352}have laid hands on their substance in the day of their calamity;

14 Neither shouldest thou have stood in the crossway, to cut off those of his that did escape; neither shouldest thou have delivered up those of his that did remain in the day of distress.

15 For the day of the LORD is near upon all the heathen: as thou hast done, it shall be done unto thee: thy reward shall return upon thine own head.

16 For as ye have drunk upon my holy mountain, so shall all the heathen drink continually, yea, they shall drink, and they shall swallow down, and they shall be as though they had not been.

[What follows is God's eyewitness description (see Amos 8:7) of the things that took place in Jeremiah 52 which Jeremiah did not record. In that day (586 B.C.) the Edomites"stood with Nebuchadnezzar ]{.class_s321}["on the other side" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 11), and, like Paul (Acts 8:1), they consented to the murder and slavery of the inhabitants of Jerusalem: ]{.class_s321}["even THOU wast as one of them" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 11). Furthermore, the Edomites were rejoicing in Nebuchadnezzar's slaughter (vs. 12); they spoke ]{.class_s321}["proudly in the day of distress" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 12). (For a similar reaction one should read about the response of the Ukranians to the Germans' treatment of the Jews in the death camps.) The Edomites themselves entered Jerusalem after the siege was ended (vs. 13) and got pleasure from staring at staked prisoners, ravished women, and starving children (see Lam. 5:10--12): ]{.class_s321}["thou shouldest not have LOOKED ON THEIR AFFLICTION in the day of their calamity" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 13). If this were not enough, the Edomites then ]{.class_s321}["laid hands on their substance" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 13) and pillaged the destroyed city.]{.class_s321}

[But Edom's indignities to God's people did not end here. The Edomites blocked the Via Maris to Egypt, the passages that led from the Judean wilderness to the Arabah, the crossing at Jordan near Bethabarah, and the wilderness roads []{#c21W.xhtml_page_353}to Petra (vs. 14). At these places, they slaughtered Judean Jews or ]{.class_s321}["delivered up those of his that did remain in the day of distress" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 14).]{.class_s321}

[The harvest for this crop of malignity is]{.class_s321}[ "AS]{.class_s323}[ ]{.class_s321}[THOU HAST DONE, it shall be done UNTO THEE: thy reward shall return upon THINE OWN HEAD" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 15).]{.class_s321}

Jeremiah 49 speaks of this future destruction of Edom (and Moab) which will take place when the Lord Jesus Christ passes over Mt. Seir and Mt. Paran on the way to Mt. Olivet from Mt. Sinai (see Amos 1--2 and comments).

"[For THE DAY OF THE LORD is near upon all the heathen\...so shall all the heathen drink continually, yea, they shall drink\...and they shall be as though they had not been" ]{.class_s323}[(vss. 15--16). Every word of these verses tastes like the cup which Jeremiah was told to pass around the table (Jer. 25:16--26). This "cup of fury" (Psa. 75:8) will be poured out as a recompense for the heathen getting drunk on Mt. Zion (Obad. 16) following Nebuchadnezzar's carnage.]{.class_s321}

17 But upon mount Zion shall be deliverance, and there shall be holiness; and the house of Jacob shall possess their possessions.

18 And the house of Jacob shall be a fire, and the house of Joseph a flame, and the house of Esau for stubble, and they shall kindle in them, and devour them; and there shall not be any remaining of the house of Esau; for the LORD hath spoken it.

19 And they of the south shall possess the mount of Esau; and they of the plain the Philistines: and they shall possess the fields of Ephraim, and the fields of Samaria: and Benjamin shall possess Gilead.

20 And the captivity of this host of the children of Israel shall possess that of the Canaanites, even unto Zarephath; and the captivity of Jerusalem, which is in Sepharad, shall []{#c21W.xhtml_page_354}possess the cities of the south.

21 And saviours shall come up on mount Zion to judge the mount of Esau; and the kingdom shall be the LORD'S.

[All is evident and ]{.class_s321}[nothing ]{.class_s322}[in the passage is connected with any past history. Judean Jews in the south of Judah will possess Seir and Paran (see Hab. 3:3); Judean Jews who dwelt in the western portions of Judah (see Josh. 15:45--47) will possess Gaza, Ashdod, Ekron, Gath (Zeph. 2:7), and Ashkelon (the Philistine plain). [Jonah was from the city of Gath Hepher in Zebulon (Josh. 19:10--13), and he lived during the reign of Jeroboam II (2 Kings 14:25--27). He arose]{.class_s321}[ "OUT OF GALILEE" ]{.class_s323}[(John 7:52), showing how ignorant the leading scholars of Christ's day were in regard to knowledge of the Old Testament. Although these scholars were not as stupid as modern Christian scholars (see Matt. 27:47 and comments in that Commentary), they missed the new birth of Psalm 22:30 and the sign of the Messiah in Matthew 12:40 and John 2:19. The Prophet from Galilee (see Mark 6:1--6) likened Himself to a prophet from ]{.class_s321}[Galilee ]{.class_s322}[(Matt. 12:40), and Jonah is the old prophet that Christ likens Himself to specifically.]{.class_s321}

Jonah is called to preach to Gentiles, not to Jews. His autobiography is committed to writing by himself, and the use of the "third person" shouldn't throw anyone who has studied John 3:16, 17:3, 5:20, 23, 25. Moses had to write about himself (see Numbers 12:3, 7), and I am sure it was "most embarrassing" at times (see Exod. 4:25 and comments).

[1. It is a prophecy of the future work of the ]{.class_s321}[Jewish evangelists ]{.class_s322}[who will be called to preach the ]{.class_s321}[gospel of the Kingdom ]{.class_s322}[(Matt. 10) in the Tribulation []{#c23V.xhtml_page_362}(see Acts 9:15 and comments).]{.class_s321}

[The heathen fables, raised up by the god of this world to counterfeit the truthful narrative, have ]{.class_s321}[Andromeda ]{.class_s322}[being rescued from a sea monster by Perseus; ]{.class_s321}[Arion, ]{.class_s322}[the musician, carried to shore by a dolphin after being thrown into the water by sailors; and ]{.class_s321}[Hercules ]{.class_s322}[in the belly of a sea monster three days and three nights. [Six foot skeletons of men have been found in whale sharks (Rhinodin Typicus). Charles Bell Emerson (an authority on whales) tells us that a sulphur bottom whale can be 90 feet long, weighing 147 tons, with air spaces in its sinuses of 686 cubic feet. A sperm whale ejects the contents of its stomach (see Jonah 2:10) before it dies. In the ]{.class_s321}[Princeton Review ]{.class_s322}[of 1927 (Oct.) it was recorded that a man was found ALIVE, though unconscious, after being in the belly of a WHALE (not a "large fish," all you Bible rejecting Fundamentalists!) ]{.class_s321}[after two days. ]{.class_s322}[And on the 25th of August, 1881, Steersman James Bartlett, on a whaling vessel, cut open the stomach of a dead whale after taking the blubber off and found a man's body intact, although it had turned purple. In a stomach temperature of 104 degrees there were live fish swimming around the corpse, some of them over six feet in length.]{.class_s321}

[And yet this last item is still beside the real point, for Jonah DIED, if we are to believe Jesus Christ (Matt. 12:40). When the ]{.class_s321}[Century Bible ]{.class_s322}[says, "It is humiliating for a commentator to collect DOUBTFUL stories of sailors swallowed by sharks and vomited out alive," we are to enjoy the droll humor of []{#c23V.xhtml_page_363}the writer. [Of the 200 corrections made on the authoritative text by Kautzsch, Nowack, Marti, Orelli, Kimchi, Bauer, and Durnmelow, the reader may dispense with 200 of them. Durnmelow has Jonah written at the same time that the Greek myths are constructed (500--400 B.C.) so that an Atheist or Agnostic can pretend that Jonah ]{.class_s321}[borrowed from them: ]{.class_s322}[typical. Durnmelow's "authority" for moving the account 300--400 years out of place is the "fact" that Jonah 3:3 says, ]{.class_s321}["Nineveh was an exceeding great city." ]{.class_s323}[The excruciating logic behind this deduction is that the writer (in the Hebrew) is looking way back into history and is describing the city for a generation who didn't know that Nineveh was ]{.class_s321}["an exceeding great city"]{.class_s323}[ since it long ago had ceased to be one. Now this is the same type of "scientific research" and "qualified scholarship" which produced the ]{.class_s321}[ASV,]{.class_s322}[ ]{.class_s321}[NASV]{.class_s322}[, and ]{.class_s321}[RV ]{.class_s322}[of 1884 (England). It may best be described as higgledly-piggledy-derangement. By such "logic," Luke wrote the Book of Acts in A.D. 300 after people had forgotten that Philippi was a chief city of Macedonia (Acts 16:12), and the Book of Revelation was written in A.D. 400 after people forgot that Rome was ]{.class_s321}["that great city, which reigneth over the kings of the earth" ]{.class_s323}[(Rev. 17:18).]{.class_s321}

[What would possess a man to think that Jonah 3:3 was proof a book was written 400 years after the time of the man ]{.class_s321}[who went through the events described in the book? ]{.class_s322}[Did Dummelow really read the Hebrew? Did he notice that ]{.class_s321}[Heyatha ]{.class_s322}[was the same verb used in Genesis 1:2 for ]{.class_s321}["THE []{#c23V.xhtml_page_364}EARTH WAS WITHOUT FORM\...." ]{.class_s323}[Did he really check the scholars who translated that as "the earth BECAME a waste, etc."?]{.class_s321}

[Nineveh was an exceeding great city ]{.class_s321}[when Jonah was alive ]{.class_s322}[(2 Kings 14--16), and the purpose of Jonah in telling us this was to make sure we would know (as anyone else in ]{.class_s321}[any ]{.class_s322}[generation) that he was sent to a large city. Nothing near Jonah 3:3, in any direction, would indicate the date of the writing unless it would be the absolute proof that the book took place before A.D. 625. You see, Nineveh was not overthrown until ]{.class_s321}[long after ]{.class_s322}[Jonah preached (see the Preface to Nahum). What would Jonah be doing concluding his book with the mercy of God in sparing Nineveh if the city ]{.class_s321}[had already been overthrown when the book was written? ]{.class_s322}[The scholars give us 612 (Bullinger), 625 (Fausset), 607 (Dummelow), 610 (Young's Concordance), or 603 (Brown) as possible dates for the destruction of Nineveh, so there is about as much unanimity on the opinion as there is concerning the length of the sojourn of Israel in Egypt (see Exod. 1:8 and notes).]{.class_s321}

But no matter how you slice the pie or cut the cake, Jonah finishes his book with Nineveh standing under the mercy of God. Therefore, any attempt to slam the book forward past 630 B.C. is ridiculous. Since Jonah preached between 793 and 753 B.C., there is no reason to suppose the book was not committed to writing in his lifetime. One writer tells us that Nineveh was overthrown in 694 B.C. by Sardanapalus, during the seventh year of Uzziah; this is still sixty to seventy years after Jonah brings a revival to the city.

The book was written about 760 B.C., by one standard of chronologies, and about 790 B.C. by another. In no set of chronologies could the book have been written any later than 630 B.C.

Jonah has four chapters, 48 verses and 1,321 words. The word "Jonah" means "dove."

[In handling the sacred text we shall remember that it is a blood bought, blood stained text that came to us through the Waldenses, Albigenses, and Protestants, and the faith which the Baptist and Separatist martyrs produced.
