Robertson (in conjunction with the faculty members of Princeton, Yale, Harvard, Hyles Anderson, Chicago, Westminster, Dallas, Springfield, BJU, etc.), and we shall honor it as the infallible final authority in ALL matters of faith, doctrine, practice, and theology.]{.class_s321}

[Where 100 percent of the "godly, dedicated men" (whose "loyalty" to the word is "unquestioned") ]{.class_s321}[alter ]{.class_s322}[the text, we shall discard their life work and ministries as quickly as a menstruous cloth (see Isa. 30:22). "Godliness" was never any proper alibi for serving Satan in the role of a ]{.class_s321}[destructive critic]{.class_s322}[. Where "unquestioned ]{.class_s321}[loyalty ]{.class_s322}[to the Bible" amounts to five changes per verse or five changes per chapter, we shall not merely "question" the loyalty of the "dedicated servant of God," but we will pass judgment on his loyalty (2 Cor. 2:17) as quickly as Paul (1 Cor. 5:3) did on the Corinthians (see Rom. 16:18). In these Commentaries no one is going to be buffaloed out of believing God's word simply because some egotistical "dogooder" thinks his education equips him to tempt Eve (Gen. 3:1 3).]{.class_s321}

[]{#c24W.xhtml}

CHAPTER ONE

1:1 Now the word of the LORD came unto Jonah the son of Amittai, saying,

2 Arise, go to Nineveh, that great city, and cry against it; for their wickedness is come up before me.

3 But Jonah rose up to flee unto Tarshish from the presence of the LORD, and went down to Joppa; and he found a ship going to Tarshish: so he paid the fare thereof, and went down into it, to go with them unto Tarshish from the presence of the LORD.

[Jonah "the dove" is the son of ]{.class_s321}[Amittai ]{.class_s322}[("truth" or "truth telling") and perhaps this explains much of the hostility that scholars show towards the book. A dove is a type of the Holy Spirit (Matt. 3:16) so a "truth telling Holy Spirit" is not liable to concoct a myth, allegory, or legend, especially when he is dealing with the New Testament Gospel---the death, burial and resurrection (Matt. 12:39, 40) of the Lord Jesus Christ (1 Cor. 15:1--5). Jonah is from the town of ]{.class_s321}[Gath hepher ]{.class_s322}[in ]{.class_s321}[Zebulun ]{.class_s322}[(2 Kings 14:25, Josh. 19:10,13).]{.class_s321}

"[Arise, go to Nineveh" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 2).]{.class_s321}

[The commission is impossible from Jonah's standpoint. ]{.class_s321}[Nineveh ]{.class_s322}[was the capital of world power at that time, a power that was about to wipe out all of Jonah's people and nation. ]{.class_s321}[Assyria, ]{.class_s322}[with Nineveh as its capital, carries all of Israel into captivity in 2 Kings 17. It is during 2 Kings 14 that Jonah is called to go and preach to them. If they "repent" and get right with God then Jonah assures himself that they will clean up Israel on the spot. He knows Israel is not right. The reigning king in Israel was Jeroboam the son of Joash who ]{.class_s321}["did that which was EVIL IN THE SIGHT OF THE LORD" ]{.class_s323}[(2 Kings 14:24). Therefore, God is asking Jonah to sign his own death warrant and the death warrant of his own nation. How would you like to get ]{.class_s321}[that ]{.class_s322}[kind of a "call" to the ministry? It puts a lot of "light," doesn't it, on Jonah's actions at this time? We are prone to preach []{#c24W.xhtml_page_367}on Jonah as the great Bible picture of a backslidden preacher; perhaps his motive in ]{.class_s321}[refusing ]{.class_s322}[the call was better than the motive of some of the modern brethren for ]{.class_s321}[obeying ]{.class_s322}[it. Truth is stranger than fiction.]{.class_s321}

"[For their wickedness is come up before me" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 2) as the wickedness of the Amorites (Gen. 15:16), the Egyptians (Gen. 15:14), the Sodomites (Gen. 18:20, 21) and the Americans, Germans, Frenchmen, Japanese, Vietnamese, and Cambodians. ]{.class_s321}[No nation gets away with "permissive societies"; ]{.class_s322}[no nation gets away with idolatry (Acts 17:30), and no nation gets away with pretending "the Kingdom is coming" while they are making a liar out of God (Rom. 3:4).]{.class_s321}

"[But Jonah rose up to flee unto Tarshish from the presence of the LORD" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 3). Which is a very difficult job if we are to believe Psalm 139:1--9. Running away from God is like trying to outswim a porpoise. A little girl jumped in bed and covered up her head with a pillow and said, "Now God can't see me!" My little boy used to stand in the middle of the room and cover his eyes with his hands and say, "You can't see me now, daddy, I'm hiding." The biggest joke Satan ever pulled off on the high school students of America was when he instilled the religious dogma into them that God could not see through blue lights, darkened hallways, shower rooms, lavatories, smoke-filled lounges, and into the backseats of cars.]{.class_s321}[ "All things are NAKED AND OPENED unto the eyes of Him with whom we have to do" ]{.class_s323}[(Heb. 4:13). Jonah made the mistake of thinking that the Lord was only ]{.class_s321}["in His holy temple"]{.class_s323}[ (Hab. 2:20) and not guiding the schools of fish in the Mediterranean; he found out differently.]{.class_s321}

[Now observe the descending order of a backslidden preacher as he attempts to get out of the "calling." First of all he goes DOWN to Joppa (vs. 3), then he goes DOWN into the ship (vs. 3), and then he is found DOWN ]{.class_s321}["into the sides of the ship" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 5); and if that weren't enough, he has lain DOWN and is fast asleep!]{.class_s321}

4 But the LORD sent out a great wind into the sea, and there was a mighty tempest in the sea, so that the ship was like to be broken.

5 Then the mariners were afraid, and cried every man unto his god, and cast forth the wares that were in the ship into the sea, to lighten it of them. But Jonah was gone down into the sides of the ship; and he lay, and was fast asleep.

6 So the shipmaster came to him, and said unto him, What meanest thou, O sleeper? arise, call upon thy God, if so be that God will think upon us, that we perish not.

7 And they said every one to his fellow, Come, and let us cast lots, that we may know for whose cause this evil is upon us. So they cast lots, and the lot fell upon Jonah.

8 Then said they unto him, Tell us, we pray thee, for whose cause this evil is upon us; What is thine occupation? and whence comest thou? what is thy country? and of what people art thou?

9 And he said unto them, I am an Hebrew; and I fear the LORD, the God of heaven, which hath made the sea and the dry land.

10 Then were the men exceedingly afraid, and said unto him, Why hast thou done this? For the men knew that he fled from the presence of the LORD, because he had told them.

[We pay our own fare when we get out of the will of God (vs. 3). The wages of sin is death, and ]{.class_s321}[we ]{.class_s322}[pay the wages ]{.class_s321}["so HE paid the fare thereof" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 3). Jonah was in such a hurry to get out of God's will he didn't even wait for a regularly scheduled passenger ship; he simply ]{.class_s321}["found a ship going to Tarshish" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 3). It has no passengers on it but him---and ]{.class_s321}["mariners" ]{.class_s323}[(v. 5).]{.class_s321}

[This was a merchant marine ship (]{.class_s321}["cast forth the wares that were in the ship"]{.class_s323}[), and Jonah had no business on it. The boat sailed from []{#c24W.xhtml_page_369}Joppa (see Acts 9:43 and comments) to Spain, and ]{.class_s321}["the sea" ]{.class_s323}[of verse 4 is undoubtedly the Mediterranean. Jonah was called to go west to east; so, he promptly departs east to west. And from the very start, we sense the stubborn rebellion of the prophet as centering around one thought: "I don't want to preach to ]{.class_s321}[Gentiles, ]{.class_s322}[especially if there is a chance of them repenting." Jonah knew that God intended to use Assyria as an instrument of judgment against Israel (2 Kings 14:25); so, he figured logically (and correctly! see 4:11) that if Nineveh's sins (see Nahum 2:8--13) finished her off she wouldn't be around to attack Israel! Why take a chance on Assyria "getting right"? Vacate! So Jonah does.]{.class_s321}

"[But the LORD sent out a great wind into the sea, and there was a mighty tempest in the sea, so that the ship was like to be broken" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 4). \[Observe the Old English still current in the Southland ("like to be") as in Jer. 38:9.\] ]{.class_s321}[Disobedience ]{.class_s322}[always makes the waters rough; sin is a rough sea. The laws of nature and God are against human rebellion, and God's "seas" have swamped more than one boat that was going in the wrong direction. "One sinner destroyed much good" (Ecc. 9:18); so one backslider in the church can "rock the boat" till it nearly sinks. You'd better "rock the boat" with the ]{.class_s321}[truth ]{.class_s322}[than sink it with a pack of lies. One good preacher on board (see Acts 27:9--44) may be the salvation of the whole congregation.]{.class_s321}

"[Then the mariners were afraid" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 5), but not Jonah. Jonah is sleeping while they are praying (see Peter in Acts 12:6), and he is ]{.class_s321}["FAST asleep."]{.class_s323}[ As a matter of fact, he is so sound asleep that the Alexandrian scholars from Origen's hellhole have added "AND HE SNORED" to the text in order to explain why the shipmaster (vs. 6) came to him. Having committed this sin (see Prov. 30:6), the blundering faculty members of Alexandria make the shipmaster say, "Why SNOREST thou?" in verse 6. \[Nowack, in line with the Norman Vincent Peale Dale Carnegie rap with yourcommunity, has altered ]{.class_s321}["CRY AGAINST IT"]{.class_s323}[ to "preach to her." Typical: Typical modernistic slop.\]]{.class_s321}

"[And cried every man unto his god" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 5). There were merchant marines on board from Egypt, Phoenicia, Crete, Cyprus, Greece, Palestine, and Africa, and since all of the heathen are incurably religious (always []{#c24W.xhtml_page_370}have been and still are), each one has a final, absolute, infallible source of authority which he can appeal to in "all matters of faith and practise." Like the devotees of Baal (1 Kings 18:26--28) they ring loud and long but no one picks up the receiver. (It's kind of like trying to place a long distance call these days from a motel room; if you get the right number it's a miracle.) We have heard of a "Dial A Prayer Telephone" for Atheists; you dial the number and nobody answers.]{.class_s321}

[Old John Wesley Grant, a great colored preacher, put it this way: "De Lawd done looked down on dat backslidden preacher, prophet, teacher, educator and He say to Hiself 'Dat preacher ain't gonna stay wid dat ship no mo!' So de Lawd call for de north wind and he say, 'Nort wind, ah wants you to blow in a southerly direction!' Den He say, 'South wind, you blows north!' Den He say to de east wind, 'East wind, ah wants you to blow west, and de west wind is gwint to blow east,' ]{.class_s321}[and I wants you all to git together in a COOPERAT'IN WAY!" ]{.class_s322}[If that wouldn't create ]{.class_s321}["a mighty tempest in the sea"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 4), I don't know what would!]{.class_s321}

John Wesley Grant was a great expositor. He said, "De Lawd done picked Him out a great fish and said,'Hey man, there is a certain boat what I wants to have you swim in the whereabouts THEREOF'." And then, according to Brother Grant, the mariners picked up Jonah and said, "Preachuh, we so hates to do dis to you, but someone is rockin dis heah boat and you gottsta go!"

"[What meanest thou, O sleeper?" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 6). He is like Peter in the garden of Gethsemane; there is always someone that doesn't get the message. ]{.class_s321}["Arise, call upon THY God" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 6). "We've tried all the gods on the bargain counter of every shop in town and can't get one yet to control ]{.class_s321}['the prince of the powers of the air' ]{.class_s323}[(Eph. 2:2)." The bungling mistranslaters of the post Christian LXX (see above) couldn't understand why the shipmaster went to Jonah so they invented the ridiculous addition we noted. Why wouldn't a ship's captain be disturbed while dumping out his cargo (vs. 5) to see one man sound asleep in the middle of the cargo? The cargo is down in ]{.class_s321}["the sides of the ship." ]{.class_s323}[Jonah was sleeping on it.]{.class_s321}

[And if this hadn't been enough, one must remember that the seamen were []{#c24W.xhtml_page_371}already suspicious of Jonah because when he boarded the freighter, he had to explain to them why he had not taken a ]{.class_s321}[passenger ship. ]{.class_s322}[He had told them that ]{.class_s321}["he fled from the presence of the LORD" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 10). But when Jonah hitched his ride, he did not tell them that the LORD he was running from was the maker of ]{.class_s321}["THE SEA and the dry land" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 9). ]{.class_s321}[Observe how the backslider can get by as long as he doesn't identify Jesus Christ as ]{.class_s322}["God\...manifest in the flesh" ]{.class_s323}[(1 Tim. 3:16), and this may explain why the modern faculty members at Bob Jones University still tolerate the apostate versions which alter 1 Timothy 3:16 to match the Liberal "bibles" of the NCCC.]{.class_s321}

Jonah is about to get baptized.

"[Come, and let us cast lots, that we may know for whose cause this evil is upon us" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 7). So, they cast lots (dice, draw straws, flip a coin, etc.) and ]{.class_s321}["THE LOT FELL UPON JONAH" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 7). God is the final arbiter of "chance" (see Acts 1:26) because the lot cast into the lap is disposed of by the LORD (Prov. 16:33 and comments). Jonah's fugitive days are over. The hand of God has reached out from Gathhepher in Zebulun to a ship in the middle of the Mediterranean, and the finger of God has singled out one man from a crew of forty to fifty men and said, "Thou art the man"! And I have known backslidden Christians to forsake their profession, abandon their testimony, return to the juke joints and x rated movie theaters, and, then, in the middle of the most un Christian situations be ]{.class_s321}[identified ]{.class_s322}[as a believer.]{.class_s321}

I recall one young man who was one of the greatest soul winners I ever met. After ten years of resisting the call to the ministry (and three deliberate decisions which kept him in bad company in the Service), he finally went back to the beer, the barrooms, the beaches, the bottles, the babes, and the back rooms for relief. He got nowhere. The unsaved women avoided him like the plague and the unsaved men couldn't talk with him five minutes before they found themselves engaged in a discussion about Heaven, Hell, or God.

[The ]{.class_s321}["sleeper"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 6) suddenly becomes the center of attention (1 Cor. 15:34), and by virtue of providential circumstances (ordered by God), Jonah finds []{#c24W.xhtml_page_372}that he has to review the whole mess from verse 2 to the present.]{.class_s321}

1\. What is the reason why God is about to kill us (vs. 8)?

2\. What do you do to make a living (vs. 8)?

3\. Where did you come from (vs. 8)?

[4. Where is your ]{.class_s321}[real ]{.class_s322}[home (vs. 8)?]{.class_s321}

[5. What ]{.class_s321}[group of people ]{.class_s322}[are you connected with (vs. 8)?]{.class_s321}

It sounds almost like the maiden putting Simon on the spot (Matt. 26:71) or Nixon facing the news media!

And there is no way out. Once you are saved, your people are God's people (Ruth 1:16), your home is in Heaven, not on earth (Phil. 3:20), you came from the pits of Hell (Isa. 51:1), and your "living" now is Christ Himself (Gal. 2:20). You know the reason why God kills sinners (Rom. 6:23) and why God kills Christians (Rom. 8:13) and; there is no way on God's earth to lie around it; the heathen will find you out sooner or later. God will flush you like a bird dog flushing a covey of quail.

"[Why hast thou done this?" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 10). Up until now nobody questioned Jonah's evacuation plans. The seamen knew he was fleeing from the presence of his "god," but like any pagan, they figured it was because his "god" was mad at him and he had to skedaddle or ]{.class_s321}[he ]{.class_s322}[would have been killed. Any demon worshiper in Africa today follows the same pattern. You obey the "god" and meet its demands or keep it from getting mad at you and destroying your crops or killing your children. The ]{.class_s321}[RSV ]{.class_s322}[(any edition) omits the question mark in the verse although the Hebrew word ]{.class_s321}[Mah ]{.class_s322}[plainly states the question, ]{.class_s321}["WHY hast thou\...?"]{.class_s323}

11 Then said they unto him, What shall we do unto thee, that the sea may be calm unto us? for the sea wrought, and was tempestuous.

12 And he said unto them, Take me up, and cast me forth into the sea; so shall the sea be calm unto you: for I know that []{#c24W.xhtml_page_373}for my sake this great tempest is upon you.

13 Nevertheless the men rowed hard to bring it to the land; but they could not: for the sea wrought, and was tempestuous against them.

14 Wherefore they cried unto the LORD, and said, We beseech thee, O LORD, we beseech thee, let us not perish for this man's life, and lay not upon us innocent blood: for thou, O LORD, hast done as it pleased thee.

15 So they took up Jonah, and cast him forth into the sea: and the sea ceased from her raging.

16 Then the men feared the LORD exceedingly, and offered a sacrifice unto the LORD, and made vows.

[Woe be to the church that has a runaway prophet on board! If you had a dollar for every local church that is rocking because a ministerial defect is on board whom God called to be somewhere else, you could buy any church building in the United States. You can always spot them: Every church has one to five men in it who think they were called to "minister" to the congregation ]{.class_s321}[without anyone in the congregation knowing ]{.class_s322}[that they are the "minister." These chickenhearted, cowardly "church operators" and promotional executives are careful never to be found in the place where the shelling falls: ]{.class_s321}[the pulpit. ]{.class_s322}[They are always just ]{.class_s321}[behind ]{.class_s322}[the pulpit or the choir loft or hiding just behind the ]{.class_s321}[altar rail ]{.class_s322}[or leaning over the ]{.class_s321}[piano ]{.class_s322}[or peeping out of the ]{.class_s321}[baptistry; ]{.class_s322}[close enough to ]{.class_s321}[control ]{.class_s322}[the preacher and ]{.class_s321}[guide ]{.class_s322}[him into a disaster but never close enough to get slandered and abused ]{.class_s321}[themselves. ]{.class_s322}[They are the great "yellow bellies" of modern, Fundamental Christianity. No church can sail to port on an even keel with them aboard.]{.class_s321}

[I have often given this subject deep and prayerful thought. I have also said to myself, "What religious virus is it that infects these men to make them always want to ]{.class_s321}[run ]{.class_s322}[and ]{.class_s321}[control ]{.class_s322}[a minister without ever entering the ministry? What strange disease is it that makes a man satisfied to hide behind political []{#c24W.xhtml_page_374}connections, handshakes, backpats, and secret business meetings in order to control what is preached to the body of Christ?" And the longer you examine the problem and study it, the more you become certain that nearly every congregation in America contains about one man (per hundred) whom God called to preach and who has ]{.class_s321}[refused the call ]{.class_s322}[because he was afraid of giving up EARTHLY SECURITY.]{.class_s321}

[To "atone" for his disobedience, this man will encumber himself with so much "serving" in a local church that you would think he was a powerhouse for God set aflame by the Holy Spirit, but\...! But you just let any preacher in that pulpit come down hard on jealousy, laziness, fraud, insincerity, politicking, or griping and suddenly the "spirit filled powerhouse" becomes the emissary of Satan, whose real calling is obviously ]{.class_s321}[to get rid of the preacher. ]{.class_s322}[I believe this type of man---men who were originally called to be missionaries, preachers, teachers, or evangelists---is the greatest curse to the church of any curse known to man, outside of the ]{.class_s321}[Christian faculty members at Christian schools ]{.class_s322}[who destroy the faith of young men in the ]{.class_s321}[AV ]{.class_s322}[Bible of the Reformation.]{.class_s321}

[No church or ]{.class_s321}[school ]{.class_s322}[can chart a steady course with a Jonah on board.]{.class_s321}

"[What shall we do unto thee"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 11). The heathen have a great deal more sense about these matters than the National Education Association or the HEW (see Acts 28:4). The heathen know that God does get angry, that He does judge men, that He will punish men in this life, and that He will punish a group (see Josh. 7:1,25) for the sins of one man (see 2 Sam. 21:2--8). Now the college professors and "scientists" at Berkeley and the Russian Institute will allow that something like that could happen in the case of a ]{.class_s321}[Hitler ]{.class_s322}[or a ]{.class_s321}[Czar Nicholas, ]{.class_s322}[but it would never occur to the stupid fools that it could happen to a nation that followed ]{.class_s321}[Karl Marx ]{.class_s322}[or ]{.class_s321}[Charles Darwin; ]{.class_s322}[folks are funny.]{.class_s321}

"[Take me up, and cast me forth into the sea; so shall the Sea be calm unto you: for I know that for my sake this great tempest is upon you" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 12).]{.class_s321}

[Jonah's ultimate design is to save his own people from destruction by dying himself, and in this respect he qualifies as a ]{.class_s321}[type of Christ, ]{.class_s322}[at this point. []{#c24W.xhtml_page_375}However, the type here cannot be stretched ]{.class_s321}[very far, ]{.class_s322}[for Jonah is going into the drink for ]{.class_s321}[his own guilt ]{.class_s322}[as well, not the guilt of the ]{.class_s321}[sailors ]{.class_s322}[he is about to die for. As Wesley Grant says: "He wanted to commit suicide, but he wanted somebody else to do his killin' for him!" Notice the interpretation placed on the passage by the Holy Spirit in Jonah 2:5 and Matthew 12:40 when read with Job 41:1,31; Genesis 1:2--3; Jeremiah 51:34; and Psalm 148:4. Christ descends down through WATER when He comes to this earth, and He descends into the domain of a LEVIATHAN (see Job 41:1--31 and comments), of whom the WHALE (see Gen. 1:21) is a type. Consequently, the first men He calls into the ministry are fishermen.]{.class_s321}

[Notice that the only man aboard who knows what the trouble really is ]{.class_s321}[is the man who is causing it ]{.class_s322}[(see above on the "church operators"). We have a church here in town where an unordained, fake "Doctor" sits on the platform at every service and monitors the "pastor" to make sure he stays in line and doesn't drive away anyone ]{.class_s321}[who is giving money to the "Doctor's" school. ]{.class_s322}[After the service, the "Doctor" "fellowships" with the congregation, and if any of them were ruffled by the pastor's message, the "Doctor" shoots the bull with them till they are placated; then he "straightens out" the "pastor." Right now this professional executive is chalking down the marks on the pastor, and when the slate is full, the pastor will be asked to leave. Many people in that "church" wonder why (with a large building, good equipment, many young people, a Premillennial pastor, and a regenerated membership) the place is deader than a rusty hammer. I'll tell you one man who knows: The dirty money hog threatening the preacher, when the preacher was called to bear the brunt and reproach of preaching the truth. ]{.class_s321}[HE KNOWS!]{.class_s322}

[Now most pagan shipmen fear God (see Rev. 11:13), even if they keep their Christophers, Oscars, horseshoes, rabbits' feet, and four leaf clovers. So, ]{.class_s321}["the men rowed hard to bring it to the land; but they could not: for the sea wrought, and was tempestuous against them" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 13).]{.class_s321}

[Here is a picture of humanity trying desperately to get saved by works when what is needed is ]{.class_s321}[the DEATH of a substitute! ]{.class_s322}[Furthermore, the substitute is []{#c24W.xhtml_page_376}classified with ]{.class_s321}["INNOCENT blood" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 14), and the reader should observe how prudently the depraved Lockman Foundation has taken the article away from Christ's ]{.class_s321}["innocent blood" ]{.class_s323}[in Matthew 27:4 (]{.class_s321}[NASV]{.class_s322}[) so that it will match the bloodless ]{.class_s321}[RSV ]{.class_s322}[and ]{.class_s321}[NRSV]{.class_s322}[. The Lockman Foundation, who published ]{.class_s321}[The Amplified Version, ]{.class_s322}[has classified Christ's blood with ANY man's blood in the passage, ]{.class_s321}[exactly like it was done in the RSV or NRSV]{.class_s322}[ or any other Communist "bible." In spite of this gross infidelity and perverse disloyalty to the word of God, you will find their absurd word (]{.class_s321}[NASV]{.class_s322}[) recommended highly by ]{.class_s321}[Bob Jones University ]{.class_s322}[and ]{.class_s321}[Dallas Theological Seminary. ]{.class_s322}[\[Someone said, "There is no article in the Greek." Sure, kiddies, we teach Greek too. ]{.class_s321}[There is no article in 1 Corinthians 2:16 either, but the NASV and the NIV stuck one in. ]{.class_s322}[Who do you think you're dealing with, Laurel and Hardy?\]]{.class_s321}

In view of the information given them (vs. 9), the seamen are afraid to sacrifice Jonah; so, they work like mad to beach the boat, but the storm gets worse.

"[Wherefore they cried unto the LORD" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 14). That is, they finally accepted Jonah's God as the true God (see Dan. 4:34). ]{.class_s321}["We beseech thee, O LORD, we beseech thee, let us not perish for this man's life, and lay not upon us innocent blood" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 14). Most heathen have a great deal more sense about manslaughter and capital punishment (and adultery, Gen. 20:4--6) than SIECUS, the Supreme Court, or the federal school system in America (see Acts 28:4). \[Public schools till 1964; federal schools since.\]]{.class_s321}

"[So they took up Jonah, and cast him forth into the sea: and the sea ceased from her raging" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 15). You see, sometimes the wind and the rain and the ocean "get it in" for a man (Job 1:19). As far as the seamen are concerned, Jonah has gone home to Davey Jones Locker---permanently.]{.class_s321}

[The tempest quiets down, the ocean becomes placid the moment Jonah hits the water, and the seamen are as convinced of the Deity of Jonah's God as []{#c24W.xhtml_page_377}the fishermen were in Luke 5:8--9. \[Revival seems to follow Jonah wherever he goes, but it is kind of forced revival. Jonah never gets to enjoy the fruit of his revival labors because they are all produced by the overruling power of God (Jonah 2:5) working on other people ]{.class_s321}[in spite ]{.class_s322}[of Jonah's disobedience.\]]{.class_s321}

[Such a verse is not to be passed by with a nod. Getting pitched over the fantail of a ship in the open sea is no joke no matter who is doing it to whom. I have often thought (while traveling back and forth across the Pacific, 1945--1947) that if someone pushed you off the stern of one of those ships ]{.class_s321}[at night, ]{.class_s322}[that would be the end of you, sure enough. Nobody would hear you yell, and in less than an hour you would be so far behind the ship you couldn't reach it if it stopped and waited for you. No captain would alter his course during a troop convoy to pick you up, and the chances of some ship finding you the next morning, when the whole convoy could pass by you in six hours, is one out of 400,000. One colored G.I. out on the high seas (1944) said to his buddy: "Did you ebeah see so much watah in all yo life?" The other dark one replied, "Man, you ain't seen nothin' yet! Das jist de TOP of it!"]{.class_s321}

The crewmen's action gets the desired effect (vs. 15), but there is many a crew that went down with a ship (1700--1950) no matter what they did to keep the ship afloat. Back in 1880 when a ship was sinking and the lifeboats were being lowered and the passengers were rioting on board, some woman screamed, "Somebody do something religious, quick!!" Whereupon one alert soul picked up a plate and ran around shipboard taking up a collection. The pagans of Jonah's day were not that superstitious.

"[Then the men feared the LORD exceedingly, and offered a sacrifice unto the LORD, and made ]{.class_s323}[vows" (vs. 16). He had a real talent that he didn't appreciate!]{.class_s321}

CHAPTER TWO

2:1 Then Jonah prayed unto the LORD his God out of the fish's belly,

2 And said, I cried by reason of mine affliction unto the LORD, and he heard me; out of the belly of hell cried I, and thou heardest my voice.

3 For thou hadst cast me into the deep, in the midst of the seas; and the floods compassed me about: all thy billows and thy waves passed over me.

4 Then I said, I am cast out of thy sight; yet I will look again toward thy holy temple.

5 The waters compassed me about, even to the soul: the depth closed me round about, the weeds were wrapped about my head.

6 I went down to the bottoms of the mountains; the earth with her bars was about me for ever: yet hast thou brought up my life from corruption, O LORD my God.

7 When my soul fainted within me I remembered the LORD: and my prayer came in unto thee, into thine holy temple.

8 They that observe lying vanities forsake their own mercy.

9 But I will sacrifice unto thee with the voice of thanksgiving; I will pay that that I have vowed. Salvation is of the LORD.

10 And the LORD spake unto the fish, and it vomited out Jonah upon the dry land.

Since Jonah himself was just as disobedient to the word of God as the writers of ]{.class_s321}[The Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_s322}[were, God doesn't waste any time speaking to ]{.class_s321}[him.]{.class_s322}

Instead, we read:

"[And the LORD spake unto the fish, and it vomited out Jonah upon the dry land" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 10). Did the whale spit Jonah out near Gebal, Ugarit, Arvad, Seleucia, Antioch of Syria, or where? The whale did not swim back to Gathhepher in Zebulon, so Jonah did not start again from his original point of departure. If the three days journey of Jonah 3:3 is the distance Jonah had to walk, the whale would have had to swim up the Tigris River to Ashur. (We are not saying that he couldn't have, but perhaps the ]{.class_s321}["three days" ]{.class_s323}[of Jonah 3:3 could be a reference to the circumference of the city.)]{.class_s321}

So, up comes Jonah from whale college with a "degree" in vomit. With these credentials as a "qualified authority" and a "recognized Biblical scholar," he attempts to preach repentance to a Gentile city of over 800,000 people.

[]{#c292.xhtml}

CHAPTER THREE

3:1 And the word of the LORD came unto Jonah the second time, saying,

2 Arise, go unto Nineveh, that great city, and preach unto it the preaching that I bid thee.

3 So Jonah arose, and went unto Nineveh, according to the word of the LORD. Now Nineveh was an exceeding great city of three days' journey.

4 And Jonah began to enter into the city a day's journey, and he cried, and said, Yet forty days, and Nineveh shall be overthrown.

[The ]{.class_s321}[second time ]{.class_s322}[the word of the LORD comes to Jonah, "the original manuscript" has been altered by the Holy Spirit (as is sometimes the case---see Jer. 36:32). This time, instead of crying ]{.class_s321}[against ]{.class_s322}[the city (see 1:2), he is to ]{.class_s321}["preach unto it the preaching that I bid thee" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 2). This shows us two things: One, God has seen an attitude of sorrow or disgust for sin (or both) in the inhabitants of Nineveh while Jonah was out on "deputation"; two, all they deserved before was one good fair warning, now there is a need to enumerate the sins of the people specifically.]{.class_s321}

"[So Jonah arose" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 3). He has been sitting on the beach trying to rub the stink off of himself with sand. ]{.class_s321}["Now Nineveh was an exceeding great city of three days'journey" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 3). Robinson goes to work immediately (]{.class_s321}[The New Bible Commentary]{.class_s322}[) trying to whittle it down to the size of Topeka, Kansas; typical (see comments under Acts 27:37 in that Commentary). However, one of Nineveh's suburbs (Calah) had a population of 70,000, and there were four of these suburbs near Nineveh. "Greater Pensacola" runs about 80,000 people, while Pensacola runs about 50,000. If God said a city was ]{.class_s321}["exceeding great," ]{.class_s323}[it was certainly as big as Cincinnati or Detroit. The []{#c292.xhtml_page_390}circumference of Nineveh, including her suburbs, may have been ninety miles, three days journey of thirty miles a day (see Exod. 5:3). However, Wesley Grant, a colored preacher, probably has the best Biblical explanation (see vs. 4).]{.class_s321}

[Some of the commentators have changed the Hebrew idiom from the superlative (]{.class_s321}["an exceeding great city"]{.class_s323}[) to the literal "a great city to God" (]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[footnote) or "a great city before God," which means nothing in the English idiom. ]{.class_s321}["And Jonah began to enter into the city a days' journey"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 4). God puts Jonah "into overdrive," and he makes a three days journey in one day. He begins to enter the city limits after twenty four hours of "walking" at ten miles an hour! He cries, ]{.class_s321}["Yet forty days, and Nineveh shall be overthrown" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 4). His prophesy falls flat, and it doesn't come to pass for more than sixty years!]{.class_s321}

[And this is what bugs Jonah. It was bad enough that he should be called to preach to Israel's enemies, and it was even worse that they had a chance to get right; but what topped the humiliation was the Gentiles made a fool out of him and made his prophesy (vs. 4) look like that of a liar. A lying prophet was of the Devil and was ]{.class_s321}[to be put to death ]{.class_s322}[(Deut. 18:20). But what appeared to Abimilech as a liar (Gen. 20:7) was a prophet.]{.class_s321}

5 So the people of Nineveh believed God, and proclaimed a fast, and put on sackcloth, from the greatest of them even to the least of them.

6 For word came unto the king of Nineveh, and he arose from his throne, and he laid his robe from him, and covered him with sackcloth, and sat in ashes.

7 And he caused it to be proclaimed and published through Nineveh by the decree of the king and his nobles, saying, Let neither man nor beast, herd nor flock, taste any thing: let them not feed, nor drink water:

8 But let man and beast be covered with sackcloth, and cry mightily unto God: yea, let them turn every one from his []{#c292.xhtml_page_391}evil way, and from the violence that is in their hands.

9 Who can tell if God will turn and repent, and turn away from his fierce anger, that we perish not?

[Here follows the greatest one man revival found in the recorded history of mankind. Over 800,000 people get right; their ]{.class_s321}[leader ]{.class_s322}[gets right, his ]{.class_s321}[nobles ]{.class_s322}[get right (vs. 7), and to make doubly sure that nothing "quenches the spirit," the people make the ]{.class_s321}[animals ]{.class_s322}[get right (vs. 8).]{.class_s321}

[The key to this mighty awakening is found in verse 5: ]{.class_s321}["So the people of Nineveh BELIEVED GOD." ]{.class_s323}[Like the Thessalonicans (1 Thess. 2:13), they believed GOD---not the "majority of qualified jackasses." They believed what God said (see Exod. 7:20, 21 and comments), and they ]{.class_s321}["proclaimed a fast, and put on sackcloth, from the greatest of them even to the least of them" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 5). Now that's a revival, man. When those doctors, lawyers, bankers, and judges start wearing tailor mades of burlap along with the hippies, junkies, pimps, ditch diggers, street cleaners, and commercial fisherman, that's REVIVAL!]{.class_s321}

"[The king of Nineveh" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 6) gets off his throne and lays his robes aside and covers himself with sackcloth; and just like Job (see Job 1:20), he sits down ]{.class_s321}["in ashes"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 6). A proclamation goes out over CBS, NBC, Mutual, and ABC with: "This broadcast is interrupted to bring you this special bulletin: You've got forty days people, and after forty days, you are all dead men."]{.class_s321}

[The beasts go on a fast (vs. 7), since they have been involved in beastiality (see Lev. 20:15), and they are told to ]{.class_s321}["cry mightily unto God" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 8) along with the men. ]{.class_s321}["Let them turn EVERY ONE from his evil way, and from the violence that is in their hands" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 8). Ah, how that meeting would have blessed Sam Jones' heart! Every sermon that old circuit rider preached in his life was based on the simple text: "Cease to do evil; learn to do well" (Isa. 1:16--17). The Ninevites mean business; they repent. God takes one look at them and tells Michael to haul Jeremiah 18:7--8 out of the file and see []{#c292.xhtml_page_392}what it says. It says: ]{.class_s321}["If that nation, against whom I have pronounced, turn from their evil, I WILL REPENT OF THE EVIL that I thought to do unto them."]{.class_s323}

So, when God saw their works 

10 And God saw their works, that they turned from their evil way; and God repented of the evil, that he had said that he would do unto them; and he did it not.

And now the LORD has put the crowning insult on Jonah's "ministry." First He flushed him before a crew of pagans; then He pitched him over the rail of a boat and liked to have drowned him; then He let a whale eat him; then He ran him through Sheol; then He showed that not even a whale can stomach a backslider, and finally He makes a liar out of Jonah before 800,000 goyim. "Ach, mamma, vot a bummer, already." \[For the term "repented" see Exodus 32:14 and comments.\]

[]{#c29X.xhtml}

CHAPTER FOUR

4:1 But it displeased Jonah exceedingly, and he was very angry.

2 And he prayed unto the LORD, and said, I pray thee, O LORD, was not this my saying, when I was yet in my country? Therefore I fled before unto Tarshish: for I knew that thou art a gracious God, and merciful, slow to anger, and of great kindness, and repentest thee of the evil.

3 Therefore now, O LORD, take, I beseech thee, my life from me; for it is better for me to die than to live.

And now the final act in the drama comes before us. In chapter 1 Jonah was called and he rebelled; in chapter 2 he was chastened and repented; in chapter 3 he was recommissioned and obeyed, and now in chapter 4 he rebels and is rebuked.

[You see, Jonah cannot go back home to Gathhepher if anyone ever finds out what he preached (Deut. 18:20). So, ]{.class_s321}["It displeased Jonah exceedingly, and he was very angry" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 1). But "the wrath of man worketh not the righteousness of God" (see James 1:20) so it isn't going to do Jonah any good to sulk and pout.]{.class_s321}

[The pagan shipmen were so anxious to save one life (see 1:13) that they took a chance on being swamped, but Jonah is so put out with God for sparing 120,000 children (plus adults) that he would rather die alone in the suburbs of the city than go downtown and do personal work. Jonah is ]{.class_s321}[orthodox, ]{.class_s322}[but he is more interested in God judging heretics than he is in people getting ]{.class_s321}[saved ]{.class_s322}[(see 1 Thess. 1:9; Acts 20:20). He should have stayed downtown with his Bible, teaching the Ninevites about the coming Messiah; instead, he goes ]{.class_s321}[outside ]{.class_s322}[the city (vs. 5) and slumps down in a heap, grumps through his beard, and takes his sixth trip ]{.class_s321}[down---]{.class_s322}[this time he goes down into the "dumps." Elijah, James, and []{#c29X.xhtml_page_394}John (Matt. 17) all had this streak in them, and even Moses was not exempt (Num. 20:10) at times. And even our blessed Lord, with tears of compassion raining down His face (Luke 19:41) for the Hebrew "Nineveh" of His times (Matt. 12:41), would occasionally snap out with "Leave'em alone; they're blind as a bat. If the blind lead the blind, the whole bunch will wind up in the pit" (Matt. 23). But Christ obeyed the admonition to ]{.class_s321}["BE YE ANGRY AND SIN NOT" ]{.class_s323}[(Eph. 4:26), while Jonah is sinning in the ]{.class_s321}[midst ]{.class_s322}[of his anger.]{.class_s321}

"[And he prayed unto the LORD" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 2). Sam Jones said he tried for six weeks to get two brethren to make up with each other, and when that didn't work, he tried for six weeks to get them to fight it out. When they finally got to hugging each other again, Sam asked one of them if he had done any praying during the twelve weeks---in view of Matthew 5:23. The man said, "Nossir, Sam, I may be a sinner, but I ain't no fool. I didn't pray one time in that twelve weeks because I knew itda made a fool outta nobody but me."]{.class_s321}

"[O LORD, was not this my saying, when I was yet in my country?"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 2). Then Jonah had already anticipated the ]{.class_s321}[results ]{.class_s322}[of his preaching; he had told the Lord, "Those people are going to get right, and then they will get stronger and they'll attack us, and\...etc." ]{.class_s321}["Therefore I fled before unto Tarshish: for I knew THAT THOU ART A GRACIOUS GOD, and merciful, slow to anger"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 2). You see, Jonah had read Exodus 34:7 and Numbers 14:18, and he knew what was going to happen. But consider this wild scene. If you were called to preach to Russia or Red China and you knew revival would follow, would you worry about a nation that got ]{.class_s321}[right ]{.class_s322}[with God wiping out a nation that ]{.class_s321}[wasn't]{.class_s322}[ right with God? When did any citizen of Heaven ever have more respect for a nation that was living like the Devil than for that one that was trying to obey God? And if God gave you 800,000 converts after one message, wouldn't you be so happy that you would trust God to spare your country? More than 800,000 people saved in one meeting?! Why, Moody, Torrey, Finney, Whitefield, Wesley, Sunday, Graham, and Spurgeon never had any results like that. ]{.class_s321}[Man, that's preaching.]{.class_s322}[ So, when Christ says, ]{.class_s321}["a GREATER than Jonah is here"]{.class_s323}[ (Matt. 12:41), He]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s323}[can only be referring to []{#c29X.xhtml_page_395}the fact that every soul led to Him in the Church Age was led through His own Spirit. When it came to ]{.class_s321}[results, ]{.class_s322}[Peter, at Pentecost, never got close enough to Jonah's revival to enlist a "prospect" in Training Union.]{.class_s321}

"[Therefore now, 0 LORD, take, I beseech thee, my life from me; for it is better for me to die than to live" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 3).]{.class_s321}

[And that's how Moses felt about it in Numbers 11:15. And that's how Elijah felt about it in 1 Kings 19:4. And that's how Paul laid it out in Philippians 1:23 and 2 Corinthians 5:8. Men may vary in their ]{.class_s321}[motives. ]{.class_s322}[Moses is so loaded with ]{.class_s321}[responsibilities ]{.class_s322}[that he wants to quit; Elijah is ]{.class_s321}[so ashamed ]{.class_s322}[of himself that he wants to quit; Paul is so ]{.class_s321}[hungry for heaven ]{.class_s322}[that he wants to quit. But Jonah has been ]{.class_s321}[so abased ]{.class_s322}[by the One who called him that he isn't interested in going on. Well, what if Elijah had quit? He would have missed the rapture. What if Moses had quit? He would have missed a seat at the right hand of the throne (Zech. 4:14 and comments). What if Peter and Paul had quit? Where would you be?]{.class_s321}

On the wall of my home I have a poster of a sixteen year old boy sitting on a bench by the football field. His helmet is off, his head is down. He is not going to make the team. Two words are printed above the boy's head: "I QUIT." Below him in the corner of the poster is a miniature picture of Calvary. Two words are printed beneath it: "I DIDN'T."

4 Then said the LORD, Doest thou well to be angry?

5 So Jonah went out of the city, and sat on the east side of the city, and there made him a booth, and sat under it in the shadow, till he might see what would become of the city.

6 And the LORD God prepared a gourd, and made it to come up over Jonah, that it might be a shadow over his head, to deliver him from his grief. So Jonah was exceeding glad of the gourd.

7 But God prepared a worm when the morning rose the next day, and it smote the gourd that it withered.

8 And it came to pass, when the sun did arise, that God prepared a vehement east wind; and the sun beat upon the head of Jonah, that he fainted, and wished in himself to die, and said, It is better for me to die than to live.

9 And God said to Jonah, Doest thou well to be angry for the gourd? And he said, I do well to be angry, even unto death.

10 Then said the LORD, Thou hast had pity on the gourd, for the which thou hast not laboured, neither madest it grow; which came up in a night, and perished in a night:

11 And should not I spare Nineveh, that great city, wherein are more than sixscore thousand persons that cannot discern between their right hand and their left hand; and also much cattle?

Now God begins to reason with his disgruntled servant (see Acts 10:15).

[The grossly corrupt LXX has Jonah merely "grieved" in verse 4, but the new "bibles" pick up this ancient clinker, proving once again that "choice of texts" is ]{.class_s321}[subjective ]{.class_s322}[and ]{.class_s321}[arbitrary ]{.class_s322}[in ANY of the new "translations." They merely ]{.class_s321}[pretend ]{.class_s322}[to be going by some set of objective "scientific" rules; their rules are no more "scientific" than the foundation teachings of the French and Bolshevist revolutions.]{.class_s321}

[Jonah doesn't answer God's question (vs. 4). He flounces out of the city as soon as the first penitents begin to cry out to God and sits ]{.class_s321}["on the east side of the city, and there made him a booth, and sat under it in the shadow, till he might see what would become of the city" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 5).]{.class_s321}

[This takes place before the forty days (3:4) are up; so, Jonah hopes to the end (Heb. 3:6) that God will yet do something to the city to make it pay for its sins. God's question (vs. 4) is so well timed that a time bomb going off on the second couldn't beat it; you see God ]{.class_s321}[was ]{.class_s322}[angry with Nineveh (1:2). But, then again, God was angry with ]{.class_s321}[Israel ]{.class_s322}[(Exod. 32:10). The LORD has "lost his temper" over many []{#c29X.xhtml_page_397}an individual (Prov. 1:26) and many a nation (Ezek. 32:22--32), and after saying, ]{.class_s321}["The wrath of man worketh not the righteousness of God" ]{.class_s323}[(James 1:20) and ]{.class_s321}["Be not hasty in thy spirit to be angry" ]{.class_s323}[(Ecc. 7:9) and ]{.class_s321}["Anger resteth in the bosom of FOOLS" ]{.class_s323}[(Ecc. 7:9), the Lord says to Jonah, "I was put out with them just as much as you are---or worse---and I can forgive and forget, how about YOU!?" \[See Eph. 4:32 and comments in that Commentary.\]]{.class_s321}

[A man said that he could always tell the ]{.class_s321}[winning side ]{.class_s322}[in a debate before it ended. Someone asked him how he could tell, and he said, "The man who is going to lose the ]{.class_s321}[argument ]{.class_s322}[always loses his ]{.class_s321}[temper first]{.class_s322}[." An American saw two Chinamen screaming at each other in the streets of Shanghai. After twenty minutes of it, they departed without a blow. He asked one of them why it was that neither of them had struck each other after the argument had gotten so vehement. The man bowed and replied "He who uses violence ]{.class_s321}[first ]{.class_s322}[has lost the argument."]{.class_s321}

[Now this mundane epigram is one of the truest gems of wisdom spoken by the human race. If you will consider it, you will see that it explains nearly every problem in ]{.class_s321}[governmental operations ]{.class_s322}[that ever took place on this earth. You see, ALL man made "utopias" and "kingdom comes" and "golden ages" and "thousand year reichs" and "just economic systems" and "liberation of the oppressed movements" use violence, coercion, slander, plunder, torture, arrest, or imprisonment to bring about PEACE and SECURITY. ]{.class_s321}[There isn't one exception in 6,000 years of history. ]{.class_s322}[The surest proof that ]{.class_s321}[every form of government devised by man is Satanic ]{.class_s322}[(see Luke 4:6) is the fact that everyone of them must resort to FORCE to overthrow the written witness of the Bible or the verbal witness of the born again Christian.]{.class_s321}

[Whether this Satanic force be the ]{.class_s321}[Democratic force ]{.class_s322}[of Socialism---mixing races indiscriminately to the tune of 7,000 hospitalized children a year; or whether this Satanic force be the ]{.class_s321}[Fascist force of Nazism]{.class_s322}[---murdering 6,000,000 civilians in "camps"; or whether this Satanic force be the ]{.class_s321}[Communist force of Marxism]{.class_s322}[---murdering 7,000,000 Chinese []{#c29X.xhtml_page_398}in order to bring in "land reform"; or whether this force be the ]{.class_s321}[Roman Catholic forces of Papism]{.class_s322}[---murdering 20,000,000 people through the political machinations that started World War I, the Hundred Years War, the Thirty Years War, the Crusades, and the attacks on the Waldensen and Albigensen villages, it is basically the ]{.class_s321}[same system ]{.class_s322}[(2 Cor. 4:4).]{.class_s321}

[No Communist, Catholic, Capitalist, or Fascist has ever proved a political argument without ]{.class_s321}[violence, ]{.class_s322}[for their position on government is FALSE. No Communist or Catholic was ever caught once since A.D. 500 in an open, ]{.class_s321}[public ]{.class_s322}[debate on Evolution, Marxism, the Assumption of Mary, the public school system, the Immaculate Conception, integration, the "infallible, pope, the Irish question, or Hegers Dialectic with a ]{.class_s321}[Bible believing, ]{.class_s322}[college-educated Christian, unless that Communist or Catholic was, at the same time, plotting the arrest or murder of the opponent, or using every political means available (]{.class_s321}[especially through the news media]{.class_s322}[) to distort the position of the opponent.]{.class_s321}

[No man living a lie and supporting a liar can support his position with reason and facts ONLY. He must resort to ]{.class_s321}[violence ]{.class_s322}[or ]{.class_s321}[slander ]{.class_s322}[before he gets through. There isn't a case on record in the history of the New Testament where any New Testament believer had to resort to demonstrations, civil rights marches, litigations, lobbyists in Congress, street riots, obscene placards, "civil disobedience," or "militant community involvement" to get across the eternal truths of his beliefs that concern mankind. Everything listed above is the result of a LIAR who is ]{.class_s321}[losing ]{.class_s322}[the argument because he is trusting a ]{.class_s321}[lie. ]{.class_s322}[Christ was murdered (Acts 7:52; that's the right word you backslidden reprobate!) because He couldn't be ANSWERED (Matt. 22:46). The only way that a real liar can handle the real truth is by ]{.class_s321}[murder ]{.class_s322}[(John 11:51).]{.class_s321}

"[DOEST THOU WELL TO BE ANGR?" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 4).]{.class_s321}

[Jonah is sitting in the shadow of a hastily constructed "brush arbor," and it affords about as much "shade" as a trellis without a vine. The LORD takes one look at it and says to Himself: "Well, you can't ever teach'em nothing, can you? They always think what they build is better than what I do. I guess I'll have to prove it to him." So, ]{.class_s321}["The LORD God prepared a gourd" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 6). This []{#c29X.xhtml_page_399}gourd plant has leaves on it about a foot square. (It is probably a bottle gourd which was made to grow up over a trellis.) Most commentators identify the plant as the Egyptian ]{.class_s321}[ricinus ]{.class_s322}[or castor oil plant---my, how suggestive! This plant grows rapidly but fades rapidly when injured. In the next two weeks (two to five weeks), the gourd plant entwines the booth and overgrows it, and Jonah has a solid shade as good as a regularly pruned Mimosa tree. "So ]{.class_s321}[Jonah was exceeding GLAD" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 6), whereas he had been ]{.class_s321}["exceedingly"]{.class_s323}[ MAD (vs. 1).]{.class_s321}

"[But God prepared a worm" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 7). Notice the four things that God prepared for Jonah's curriculum in whale college and "sull" seminary.]{.class_s321}

1\. He prepared a GREAT fish (1:17).

2\. He prepared a GOURD (4:6).

3\. He prepared a WORM (4:7).

4\. He prepared an EAST WIND (4:8).

Now what could be more "despised" and "foolish" (1 Cor. 1:27) than those items?

[Here is a GREAT fish which no recognized scholar in the world believes to ]{.class_s321}[be ]{.class_s322}[what it ]{.class_s321}[was. ]{.class_s322}[Here is a GOURD which can't stay alive more than two months. Here is one WORM, by himself, who causes a God called prophet to argue with God. And here is an EAST WIND that makes a man say, ]{.class_s321}["It is better for me to die than to live" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 8).]{.class_s321}

God never has to exert Himself when He wants to correct a man.

[I read of an infidel back in the 1800s who spent a lot of his time cursing God and ridiculing the Bible. One day, during a thunderstorm, he demonstrated his "bold, uncompromising, scientific, objective grasp of the inductive scientific facts" by going out in front of his house and daring God to strike him with a lightning bolt. His neighbors, in terror, locked their doors and peered out the windows. The infidel had a time of it for about fifteen minutes during a pouring rain, and then a bolt struck. It did not strike ]{.class_s321}[him. ]{.class_s322}[It struck the side of his house, and the charge went through an open shutter and killed his ]{.class_s321}[six-year-old daughter. ]{.class_s322}[An hour after the storm ceased that man went out in his back yard []{#c29X.xhtml_page_400}(still cursing God) and prepared his daughter for burial. While he split kindling to make a fire and boil water, he was haranguing God as a "bully," a "coward," etc., for "picking on a child," etc. A spider in the woodpile bit him while he was picking up faggots; it was a black widow spider. In less than twenty four hours he was dead.]{.class_s321}

[Why use 20,000 volts when an ]{.class_s321}[insect ]{.class_s322}[can get the job done just as well?]{.class_s321}

"[God prepared a WORM when the morning rose the next day" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 7)---meaning the day following one of Jonah's "alleluia" sessions where he was praising God for the gourd. ]{.class_s321}["And it smote the gourd that it withered" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 7). Ecclesiastes 7:14 is an excellent comment on the passage.]{.class_s321}

[God is trying to teach his servant something (Acts 10:1--18). God is trying to show Jonah that he "nourishes and cherishes his OWN FLESH" (Eph. 5:29), but he doesn't really care about anyone else. If Jonah's real desire was to die (vs. 9), he could have died and stayed dead when he went into the briny deep. All he had to do was keep his mouth shut and NOT pray. There is a gap, then, between theory and practice when it comes to ]{.class_s321}[dying, ]{.class_s322}[and this "credibility gap" is evident in the lives of some of the greatest men who everlived.]{.class_s321}

[Here is Moses saying,]{.class_s321}[ "Kill me"]{.class_s323}[ (Num. 11:15) and then, "Let me live" (Deut. 3:24--25). Here is Elijah saying, "Kill me" (1 Kings 19:4), after running from a woman who was going to do it (1 Kings 19:1). Here is Jonah bless his heart! saying, "Kill me" (Jonah 1:12), and then, ]{.class_s321}["When my soul fainted within me I remembered the LORD" ]{.class_s323}[(2:7); now he is back to his normal game---"KILL ME." Not only has Jonah's shade departed (vs. 7) but the LORD adds the hot, dry, withering Sirocco (see Exod. 10:13). Jonah suffers from sunstroke and he faints again (see 2:8). Half dead and half alive (this time!), he says, ]{.class_s321}["It is better for me to die than to live" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 8).]{.class_s321}

"[And God said to Jonah, Doest thou well to be angry for the GOURD?" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 9).]{.class_s321}

"[Yeah, and if you don't agree, gowan and kill me!"]{.class_s321}

[Oh yes! That is what the text says "in the original." "[If you got that upset about something that had no animal life in it---something that was destroyed by its creator---how come you don't get upset about me destroying my animal creation over in that town? The book of Jonah shows us that: a man cannot hide from God; a nation that doesn't repent will get wiped out; God will chasten a disobedient servant; God will use the heathen to straighten out a Christian; God is merciful and long suffering; the foolishness of God is wiser than the wisdom of man; God can save from death and Hell; a man is a fool to contend with God about His judgments; and God is just as interested in worms, fish, gourds, and cattle as He is in galaxies, constellations, star clusters, and cosmic gases.

Any commentary, school, teacher, preacher, philosophy, ideological system, scientist, educator, or church that fails to find THOSE lessons in the book of Jonah is not worth the time it would take to consult them.

CHAPTER ONE

1:1 The word of the LORD that came to Micah the Morasthite in the days of Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah, kings of Judah, which he saw concerning Samaria and Jerusalem.

2 Hear, all ye people; hearken, O earth, and all that therein is: and let the Lord GOD be witness against you, the Lord from his holy temple.

3 For, behold, the LORD cometh forth out of his place, and will come down, and tread upon the high places of the earth.

4 And the mountains shall be molten under him, and the valleys shall be cleft, as wax before the fire, and as the waters that are poured down a steep place.

5 For the transgression of Jacob is all this, and for the sins of the house of Israel. What is the transgression of Jacob? is it not Samaria? and what are the high places of Judah? are they not Jerusalem?

6 Therefore I will make Samaria as an heap of the field, and as plantings of a vineyard: and I will pour down the stones thereof into the valley, and I will discover the foundations thereof.

7 And all the graven images thereof shall be beaten to pieces, and all the hires thereof shall be burned with the fire, and all the idols thereof will I lay desolate: for she gathered it of the hire of an harlot, and they shall return to the hire of an harlot.

8 Therefore I will wail and howl, I will go stripped and naked: I will make a wailing like the dragons, and mourning as the owls.

9 For her wound is incurable; for it is come unto Judah; he is come unto the gate of my people, even to Jerusalem[]{#c2CA.xhtml_page_408}.

10 Declare ye it not at Gath, weep ye not at all: in the house of Aphrah roll thyself in the dust.

11 Pass ye away, thou inhabitant of Saphir, having thy shame naked: the inhabitant of Zaanan came not forth in the mourning of Bethezel; he shall receive of you his standing.

12 For the inhabitant of Maroth waited carefully for good: but evil came down from the LORD unto the gate of Jerusalem.

13 O thou inhabitant of Lachish, bind the chariot to the swift beast: she is the beginning of the sin to the daughter of Zion: for the transgressions of Israel were found in thee.

14 Therefore shalt thou give presents to Moreshethgath: the houses of Achzib shall be a lie to the kings of Israel.

15 Yet will I bring an heir unto thee, O inhabitant of Mareshah: he shall come unto Adullam the glory of Israel.

16 Make thee bald, and poll thee for thy delicate children; enlarge thy baldness as the eagle; for they are gone into captivity from thee.

Herewith follows a list of cities: Gath, Aphrah, Saphir, Zaanan, Beth ezel, Maroth, Jerusalem, Lachish, Moresheth gath, Achzib, Mareshah, and Adullam.

Before expounding the passage let us look briefly (please!) at the work of the Conservative, Liberal, Fundamentalist, and Catholic critics who hold membership in the Alexandrian Cult:

[1. The ]{.class_s321}[ASV ]{.class_s322}[of 1901 (recommended by A. T. Robertson and J. G. Machen) has altered ]{.class_s321}["APHRAH" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 10) to ]{.class_s321}[Bethaphrah ]{.class_s322}[by simply refusing []{#c2CA.xhtml_page_414}to translate the word ]{.class_s321}[Beth. ]{.class_s322}[(Observe how they ]{.class_s321}[have ]{.class_s322}[translated the word in vs. 14.) Since the ]{.class_s321}[AV ]{.class_s322}[translated the word in verse 10 but did not in verse 11 (]{.class_s321}["Beth ezel"]{.class_s323}[),]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s323}[the ]{.class_s321}[ASV ]{.class_s322}[or ]{.class_s321}[NASV]{.class_s322}[ took the liberty to do the same thing. However, one can see at a glance that the English construction reads much better with ]{.class_s321}["THE HOUSE of Aphrah" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 10), ]{.class_s321}["THOU INHABITANT of Saphir"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 11),]{.class_s321}[ "the INHABITANT of Maroth" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 12), ]{.class_s321}["THOU INHABITANT of Lachish" ]{.class_s323}[(v. 13), ]{.class_s321}["O INHABITANT of Mareshah" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 15), etc.]{.class_s321}

2\. The Dead Bible (Kenneth Taylor) has added "weep ye men of Bakah" to verse 10, which is in no Hebrew manuscript of any Hebrew Bible.

The ]{.class_s321}[RSV ]{.class_s322}[(any edition) has written"shall take away from you its standing place" (vs. 11), while the ridiculous bible of the Jehovah's Witnesses (]{.class_s321}[NWT]{.class_s322}[) has written "inhabitress" (for ]{.class_s321}["inhabitant"]{.class_s323}[) in verses 11 and 12.]{.class_s321}

"[Declare ye it not at Gath, weep ye not at air, ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 10). Now since some of the cities listed match the statements made after them (]{.class_s321}["Aphrah"]{.class_s323}[ for dust---]{.class_s321}["roll thyself in the dust"]{.class_s323}[), the scholars have decided to make GATH mean "weep town" (so it will match ]{.class_s321}["weep ye not at all"]{.class_s323}[). The writer of the ]{.class_s321}[NEB ]{.class_s322}[(C. H. Dodd) got so hung up here that he translated []{#c2CA.xhtml_page_415}verse 10 as "weep your eyes out\..." to match what he and others call "WEEP TOWN" (Gath). But ]{.class_s321}["Gath"]{.class_s323}[ never meant "weep town" in the eyes of anyone but someone who was trying to prove a lie. ]{.class_s321}["GATH"]{.class_s323}[ means a winepress or "good fortune."]{.class_s321}

[But the ]{.class_s321}[Hebrew language ]{.class_s322}[never stood in the way of a Hebrew scholar when he set out to attack the ]{.class_s321}[King James Version]{.class_s322}[. So, for a moment, we shall translate the list with the "scholars" and see what comesout.]{.class_s321}

1\. "Weep not in weep town, in the house of Dust roll yourself in the dust" (vs. 10).

2\. "Beauty town has its shame naked" (vs. 11).

3\. "Aside town came not forth" (vs. 11).

4\. "Neighbor town shall, etc."

[This would be the equivalent of saying, "Is there quarreling in CONCORD?" (Massachusetts). "Is their race trouble in PHILADELPHIA?" (Pennsylvania). Or "Is there war in SALEM?" or "Are there fallen angels in LOS ANGELES?" The pun in these expressions is quite apparent, but when the scholars attempt to make a ]{.class_s321}[whole list of puns ]{.class_s322}[in Micah 1 (in keeping with the dictum that God must write according to the preconceived standards of sinners), nothing comes out right. ]{.class_s321}["Moresheth gath" ]{.class_s323}[has nothing to do with ]{.class_s321}["presents"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 14). ]{.class_s321}["Achzib"]{.class_s323}[ will match the lie of the same verse (vs. 14), but ]{.class_s321}["Lachish" ]{.class_s323}[means "height" and has nothing to do with any "chariot" of any ]{.class_s321}["swift beast"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 13). ]{.class_s321}["Zaanan" ]{.class_s323}[means "rich in flocks" and has nothing to do with ]{.class_s321}["the mourning of Beth ezel" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 11). Making Lachish to mean "horsetown" so that it will match the chariots (vs. 13) and making Zaanan mean "march town" (vs. 11) and making Gath "telltown" (vs. 10) is about as scholarly and "accurate" as making New York mean "ghetto town," Chicago mean "choo choo town," and Kansas City to mean "kitty cat town." Whatever occasional pun may be found in the passage (Aphrah, Saphir, Maroth, and Achzib) does not put God under the obligation of playing scrabble with the Alexandrian Cult.]{.class_s321}

[The passage describes the approaching slaughter by Sennacherib which is later augmented by Nebuchadnezzar. The towns in and near Judah are given []{#c2CA.xhtml_page_416}warning. Second Samuel 1:20 matches the first clause in verse 10; the idea is: "Don't let our enemies the Philistines know about our loss!" Rolling in the dust (vs. 10) indicates great penance and sorrow (see Psa. 7:5, 22:15; Isa. 47:1, etc.). The inhabitant of Saphir passes away, in that he goes into captivity naked (see Isa. 47:3). The folks in Zaanan did mourn during the destruction of Beth ezel when they were attacked (vs. 11), and so the people in Zaanan take their ]{.class_s321}["standing" ]{.class_s323}[in God's eyes from Beth ezel by so doing (vs. 11). ]{.class_s321}["Evil came down from the LORD" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 12) when the people in Maroth ]{.class_s321}["waited carefully for good" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 12). For a similar case, see England and Chamberlain waiting ]{.class_s321}["carefully for good" ]{.class_s323}[after Munich (1939) and receiving for their "positive outlook" the invasion of Poland and the bombing of London. Or try it this way: The people in South Vietnam ]{.class_s321}["waiting carefully for good" ]{.class_s323}[(1965--1973) before the North Vietnamese proved that the "Paris Peace Talks" were about as helpful and as rational as a panic in a snake pit. Or try it this way: The people of America (1920--1970) waiting ]{.class_s321}["carefully for good" ]{.class_s323}[(after teaching two generations that "good" and "evil" are RELATIVE TERMS and should be replaced with "values"!) and then receiving inflation, famine, racial wars, and bankruptcy. There is nothing like "the power of positive thinking" to help you cheerfully accept death, disease, disaster, and degeneration!]{.class_s321}

"[The chariot" ]{.class_s323}[has to be called out to meet the Assyrian attack but ]{.class_s321}["safety is of the LORD" ]{.class_s323}[(Prov. 21:31) and ]{.class_s321}["the swift beast" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 13) can no more deliver a wicked city or wicked ruler (see Exod. 15:19) than he delivered Ahab (1 Kings 22:34) or Joram (2 Kings 9:23--24). ]{.class_s321}["She is the beginning of the SIN to the daughter of Zion\...in THEE" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 13).]{.class_s321}

[Lachish ]{.class_s322}[was on the Philistine border (Josh. 15:39) and was certainly NOT lying near the "border of the northern kingdom" (Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown commentary, p. 813). Since the city was near Philistine territory, the chances are better than one hundred to one that Judah first became infested with idolatry from this area. In 1 Kings 11:1 we read that Pharaoh's daughter (see also 1 Kings 11:1--8) brought her Egyptian paganism with her into Jerusalem, []{#c2CA.xhtml_page_417}and the sin, ]{.class_s321}["THE TRANSGRESSIONS OF ISRAEL"]{.class_s323}[ (see Hos. 10:9 and 1 Kings 12:26--30), began in Lachish as far as ]{.class_s321}["THE DAUGHTER OF ZION" ]{.class_s323}[was concerned. Now Moabites, Ammonites, and Zidonians contributed to Jerusalem's idolatry AFTER the time of Solomon (960 B.C.), but the sins of Jerusalem had to begin with 2 Samuel 5--6 (1040 B.C.) when David first captures "Zion" (2 Sam. 5:7). Observe that the Philistines attack immediately (2 Sam. 5:18). The intimation is that the Philistine teachings were first accepted by ]{.class_s321}[Judean Jews in Lachish on the border, ]{.class_s322}[these religious teachings include the golden images of animals (1 Sam. 6:4), the worship of the fish god (1 Sam. 5:3), hauling religious objects on carts (1 Sam. 6:10; 2 Sam. 6:3), and intermarriage with other races (Gen. 20:14), etc.]{.class_s321}

"[Therefore shalt thou give presents" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 14). Lachish will send bribes to ]{.class_s321}[Moresheth gath ]{.class_s322}[for help and will get none. And help promised by Achzib would turn out to be false: ]{.class_s321}["shall be a lie to the kings of Israel" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 14). At the time that Sennacherib comes, the kings of Judah are the ]{.class_s321}["kings of Israel" ]{.class_s323}[to all practical purposes, for the last king of the northern tribes went into captivity in 722--720 (2 Kings 17).]{.class_s321}

"[Yet will I bring an heir unto thee\...he shall come unto Adullam the glory of Israel" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 15). Although the immediate historical reference is to the new heir---Sennacherib---one should be very careful in claiming full knowledge of the prophecy. Readers of Daniel and Revelation can see the Second Advent of Christ "lurking" behind verses 11, 15, and 16. Historically speaking, the ]{.class_s321}["GLORY of Israel" ]{.class_s323}[may have been in the fortified city of ]{.class_s321}[Adulam ]{.class_s322}[which was nearly impregnable because of its cave system (see 1 Sam. 22:1). Another possible reading is that the ]{.class_s321}["glory of Israel" ]{.class_s323}[is the ]{.class_s321}["inhabitant of Mareshah" ]{.class_s323}[(same verse), and he will have to flee to the caves of David (1 Sam. 22) to hide. But an HEIR who is ]{.class_s321}["THE GLORY OF ISRAEL"]{.class_s323}[ is also shifting back and forth before our eyes in the passage.]{.class_s321}

"[Make thee bald\...they are gone into captivity" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 16), indicating a terrible mourning for loss of children (see Ezra 9:3 and Job 1:20).]{.class_s321}

CHAPTER TWO

2:1 Woe to them that devise iniquity, and work evil upon their beds! when the morning is light, they practise it, because it is in the power of their hand.

2 And they covet fields, and take them by violence; and houses, and take them away: so they oppress a man and his house, even a man and his heritage.

3 Therefore thus saith the LORD; Behold, against this family do I devise an evil, from which ye shall not remove your necks; neither shall ye go haughtily: for this time is evil.

4 In that day shall one take up a parable against you, and lament with a doleful lamentation, and say, We be utterly spoiled: he hath changed the portion of my people: how hath he removed it from me! turning away he hath divided our fields.

5 Therefore thou shalt have none that shall cast a cord by lot in the congregation of the LORD.

6 Prophesy ye not, say they to them that prophesy: they shall not prophesy to them, that they shall not take shame.

Now Micah gets down to specific sins. The reader should notice that the false prophets are found in both sections (1--6 and 7--13), and Micah is speaking of these things long before Jeremiah's great tirade against the "dreamers" (Jer. 23:28) of the NCCC and "Southern Christian" Leadership Conference (Jer. 23:21).

[Verses 1 and 2 are self explanatory. Those who have the power to do evil do it. If they have power to repossess houses and land after sharing 10 percent interest (compounded "semiannually" etc.), they do it. ]{.class_s321}["They covet fields, and take them by VIOLENCE" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 2), and whether this violence be the []{#c2ED.xhtml_page_419}Comintern confiscating farmland at the point of guns mounted on tanks (1930--1960) or whether it be property confiscated for "the people" by murdering the landowner (Red China, 1948--1968) or whether it be "environmentalists" and "civil rights workers" stealing schools and yards that don't belong to them by "militant activism" and "ecological legislation" (1970--1980), it is all the same crew---covetous, lazy, murdering ]{.class_s321}[thieves.]{.class_s322}[ "So]{.class_s323}[ ]{.class_s322}[they oppress a man and his house, even a man and his heritage" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 2). The great Old Testament illustration is the robbery of Naboth's vineyard (1 Kings 21), and the great New Testament illustration is the robbery of God's vineyard (Matt. 21). The great contemporary case in the twentieth century is the Catholic and Communist plans for world domination.]{.class_s321}

[Since God ]{.class_s321}["HATETH"]{.class_s323}[ all the workers of iniquity (Psa. 5:5), He says, ]{.class_s321}["Against this family do I devise an EVIL\...for this time is evil" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 3). The family referred to is the family of Judah (see vs. 7). The people who transgress the law (Lev. 25:23) to obtain real estate (Lev. 6:4, 19:13) will have someone ]{.class_s321}["devise"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 3) against them exactly the way they "devised" (vs. 1) against their tenants. The punishment usually "fits the crime" (Heb. 2:2), so the reward of the covetous for stealing land is to lose ]{.class_s321}[their ]{.class_s322}[land (vs. 4). ]{.class_s321}["Ye shall not remove your necks" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 3) from a yoke of iron (Jer. 28:10), even though God did take the yoke off of them once (Hos. 11:4); they are going to wear this yoke whether they like it or not (see Hos. 10:11 and comments).]{.class_s321}

[The ]{.class_s321}["parable" ]{.class_s323}[of verse 4 following ]{.class_s321}["this time is evil" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 3) shows us plainly that the events will be repeated in the Great Tribulation. ]{.class_s321}["Therefore thou shalt have none that shall cast a cord by lot" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 5). The people who persecute the poor (see Tribulation contexts in Matt. 25:31--46; Job 24:1--12; and James 5:1--7) will have no allotment in the Millennium (see Ezek. 48), for the next time the land is measured out---it was measured out in Joshua 20--24 the first time---the rich oppressors will have no land at all. The]{.class_s321}[ "cord"]{.class_s323}[ is the equivalent of the modern surveyor's "chain," and the ]{.class_s321}["lot"]{.class_s323}[ was cast to determine who got what parcel of land when it was measured out.]{.class_s321}

"[Prophesy ye not\...that they shall not take shame" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 6). []{#c2ED.xhtml_page_420}Notice how similar the verse is to Hosea 9:7 and Amos 5:13, which see. Rice) as soon as possible. Before looking at the sadistic mutilations of the text by the "good men" of 1800--1980, we shall stop long enough to get some edification and exhortation.]{.class_s321}

[The verse says in effect: "To those prophets who are prophesying, tell them to quit! I am going to replace them with ]{.class_s321}[false prophets ]{.class_s322}[who say that 'modern drinking' is all right (vs. 11). I am going to replace the real prophets (Hos. 12:10) with false prophets (vs. 11), and the real prophets ]{.class_s321}['SHALL NOT PROPHESY' ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 6) to my people, because if they did, THEY (the real prophets) would take shame." (Observe how this matches Prov. 26:4 and 2 Chron. 25:16.)]{.class_s321}

Now, with something this plain, what do you suppose the faculty members at Dallas, Fort Worth, Columbia, Hyles Anderson, Lynchburg, Bob Jones University, Arlington, and Springfield are going to do with the passage---if they believe in "reliable translations" at all?

7 O thou that art named the house of Jacob, is the spirit of the LORD straitened? are these his doings? do not my []{#c2ED.xhtml_page_422}words do good to him that walketh uprightly?

8 Even of late my people is risen up as an enemy: ye pull off the robe with the garment from them that pass by securely as men averse from war.

9 The women of my people have ye cast out from their pleasant houses; from their children have ye taken away my glory for ever.

10 Arise ye, and depart; for this is not your rest: because it is polluted, it shall destroy you, even with a sore destruction.

11 If a man walking in the spirit and falsehood do lie, saying, I will prophesy unto thee of wine and of strong drink; he shall even be the prophet of this people.

12 I will surely assemble, O Jacob, all of thee; I will surely gather the remnant of Israel; I will put them together as the sheep of Bozrah, as the flock in the midst of their fold: they shall make great noise by reason of the multitude of men.

13 The breaker is come up before them: they have broken up, and have passed through the gate, and are gone out by it: and their king shall pass before them, and the LORD on the head of them.

[False prophets ]{.class_s322}[are preaching to Judah (vs. 11), and God ]{.class_s321}[is allowing ]{.class_s322}[them to preach (vs. 11); and the ]{.class_s321}["fruits" ]{.class_s322}[that come from ]{.class_s321}[believing ]{.class_s322}[this preaching (see Ezek. 14:1--10) are enmity with God (vs. 8), stealing (vs. 8), robbery (vs. 9), and pollution (vs. 10). "[Do not my words DO GOOD" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 7). He didn't say, "Are not my words GOOD?" There is nothing here about "Are not my predictions sweet and congenial to all you cheerful folks?" CHAPTER FOUR

4:1 But in the last days it shall come to pass, that the mountain of the house of the LORD shall be established in the top of the mountains, and it shall be exalted above the hills; and people shall flow unto it.

2 And many nations shall come, and say, Come, and let us go up to the mountain of the LORD, and to the house of the God of Jacob; and he will teach us of his ways, and we will walk in his paths: for the law shall go forth of Zion, and the word of the LORD from Jerusalem.

3 And he shall judge among many people, and rebuke strong nations afar off; and they shall beat their swords into plowshares, and their spears into pruninghooks: nation shall not lift up a sword against nation, neither shall they learn war any more.

4 But they shall sit every man under his vine and under his fig tree; and none shall make them afraid: for the mouth of the LORD of hosts hath spoken it.

5 For all people will walk every one in the name of his god, and we will walk in the name of the LORD our God for ever and ever.

6 In that day, saith the LORD, will I assemble her that halteth, and I will gather her that is driven out, and her that I have afflicted;

7 And I will make her that halted a remnant, and her that was cast far off a strong nation: and the LORD shall reign over them in mount Zion from henceforth, even for ever.

5:1 Now gather thyself in troops, O daughter of troops: he hath laid siege against us: they shall smite the judge of Israel with a rod upon the cheek.
