[The reference then in Micah 5:1 to Jesus Christ is one of those ]{.class_s321}[double references ]{.class_s322}[(see 1 Pet. 1:11) which occur again and again in the Old []{#c2KG.xhtml_page_448}Testament to confound not only the Jew who rejects the New Testament, but the Gentile who rejects the Old Testament. ]{.class_s321}["THE JUDGE OF ISRAEL" ]{.class_s323}[in the passage is the first advent mentioned in Isaiah 50:6 and Matthew 26:67, but the ]{.class_s321}["RULER IN ISRAEL"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 2) is the Second Advent of Christ (Isa. 11:1--3), with his BIRTH (first advent) and His RULERSHIP (second advent) occurring in the same verse. "[O DAUGHTER OF TROOPS" ]{.class_s323}[will have to match the virgin ]{.class_s321}["DAUGHTER OF ZION"]{.class_s323}[ found in 2 Kings 19:21, and it is aimed at the gentleman of Revelation 13:3--7. "[But thou, Bethlehem, Ephratah\...out of thee shall he come forth unto me that is to be RULER IN ISRAEL" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 2).]{.class_s321}

[This is the great Messianic prophecy concerning the birthplace of Christ: Bethlehem in ]{.class_s321}[Judah---not Zebulun ]{.class_s322}[(Gen. 35:19)---for the scepter shall not ]{.class_s321}["depart from JUDAH" ]{.class_s323}[(Gen. 49:10) until ]{.class_s321}[Shiloh ]{.class_s322}[come. [Now there are ]{.class_s321}[forty eight prophecies ]{.class_s322}[on the ]{.class_s321}[first coming of Christ, ]{.class_s322}[which are all found in books written 400--1500 years before He was born. These mathematical odds are rooted and grounded in the scientific laws of mathematics where they deal with statistical probability. They are as firm as the first three laws of thermodynamics. That is, the chances of ANYONE making forty eight prophecies about a man ]{.class_s321}[before ]{.class_s322}[he was born and then those prophecies coming ]{.class_s321}[true, ]{.class_s322}[when those prophecies involved such details as birthplace, place of death, time of death, manner of death, time and place of triumphal entry, manner of treatment before death, quotations yet unspoken, etc., are greater chances than the chances of organic []{#c2KG.xhtml_page_450}life springing from inorganic matter.]{.class_s321}

"[Then the remnant of his brethren shall return" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 3). The brethren are the ones to whom the Judge of Israel came (John 1:11), and although they received Him not (Zech. 13:6), they will at His Second Coming (see Gen. 45:1--4 and comments).]{.class_s321}

4 And he shall stand and feed in the strength of the LORD, in the majesty of the name of the LORD his God; and they shall abide: for now shall he be great unto the ends of the earth.

5 And this man shall be the peace, when the Assyrian shall come into our land: and when he shall tread in our palaces, then shall we raise against him seven shepherds, and eight principal men.

6 And they shall waste the land of Assyria with the sword, and the land of Nimrod in the entrances thereof: thus shall he deliver us from the Assyrian, when he cometh into our land, and when he treadeth within our borders.

7 And the remnant of Jacob shall be in the midst of many people as a dew from the LORD, as the showers upon the grass, that tarrieth not for man, nor waiteth for the sons of men.

[Much of the passage is enigmatic. Any of the commentators can apply the context to the passage (Christ in vs. 2), but this ignores the paragraph mark in verse 4, and it ignores the fact that Christ is NOT ]{.class_s321}["the peace" ]{.class_s323}[when the Assyrians invade the land, unless it is at the END OF THE MILLENNIUM (see Rev. 20:7--8 and Ezek. 38:9). Furthermore, the ]{.class_s321}["seven shepherds"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 5) and ]{.class_s321}["eight principal men" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 5) have nothing to do with any known []{#c2KG.xhtml_page_452}prophecy in regard to the last half of Daniel's Seventieth Week, unless it has something to do with Revelation 17:10--11. Williams, the Premillennialist, comes as close to an honest treatment of the text as anyone, but, of course, he does not give us any more light than that the principals and shepherds "WILL be recognized" in the Tribulation. Gleason Archer Jr., (]{.class_s321}[The New Bible Commentary]{.class_s322}[) ]{.class_s321}[spiritualizes ]{.class_s322}[verse 4 to make it fit into the First Advent of verse 2 and tells us that "as the church carries out the great commission," Christ's "saving power" is extended ]{.class_s321}["unto the ends of the earth" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 4). Of course, that is exactly what the verse did NOT say and did NOT mean. Bullinger realizes the context is the Second Advent, not the First, so he says (about the ]{.class_s321}["shepherds" ]{.class_s323}[and ]{.class_s321}["principal men" ]{.class_s323}[of vs. 5): "When that time comes, the meaning of this will be seen."]{.class_s321}

[Now taking it one word at a time, in the infallible ]{.class_s321}[Authorized ]{.class_s322}[text, we notice that:]{.class_s321}

[1. Verse 4 could refer to Christ, although the ]{.class_s321}["THEY SHALL ABIDE" ]{.class_s323}[shows that the context is the Millennial Advent of Christ, not His ]{.class_s321}[first coming ]{.class_s322}[to die as a suffering servant. The ]{.class_s321}["He"]{.class_s323}[ is the ]{.class_s321}["Shepherd of Israel" ]{.class_s323}[(see Psa. 80:1; Gen. 49:24; and Jer. 31:10).]{.class_s321}

[2. However, the ]{.class_s321}["THIS MAN"]{.class_s323}[ ]{.class_s321}[of verse 5 may be an ]{.class_s322}["anointed" ]{.class_s323}[(see Lam. 4:20) of a different breed. Observe that Israel's travail (vs. 3) brings forth a ]{.class_s321}["MAN CHILD" ]{.class_s323}[(Rev. 12:13) who may, or may NOT, be Jesus Christ (see context of Rev. 12:13 and compare with Lam. 4:20). The insistence that the "man" of verse 2 is the man of ]{.class_s321}[verses 4 and 5 ]{.class_s322}[is a typical attempt by expositors to force God to keep His revelation within one context without switching; but the Lord God is quite a "switcher" if you have observed Him through a pilgrimage of sixty six books (Cf. Prov. 30; Joel 2; Num. 20; Nah. 2).]{.class_s321}

[3. ]{.class_s321}["THE ASSYRIAN"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 5) is plainly the ]{.class_s321}[Antichrist ]{.class_s322}[(see comments under Hosea 5:13 and 10:6, plus Isa. 10:10--16 and Exod. 1:8). He will ]{.class_s321}["come into OUR land" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 5) in Daniel 11:21. The reader will note that every attempt by the commentators to make the prophecy refer (historically) to 2 Kings 19 is a wipeout. No Jew wasted ]{.class_s321}["the land of Assyria with the sword" ]{.class_s323}[]{#c2KG.xhtml_page_453}[(vs. 6) ]{.class_s321}[BEFORE or AFTER the time of Hezekiah. ]{.class_s322}[The reader will also observe the ]{.class_s321}["OUR"]{.class_s323}[ showing up here (for Israel), whereas until here, Israel with its Messiah and its cities (vss. 1--4) have been spoken of in the third person singular and plural. Suddenly we find ]{.class_s321}["OUR land"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 5), ]{.class_s321}["our palaces" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 5) and ]{.class_s321}["WE shall raise" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 5), and ]{.class_s321}["deliver ]{.class_s323}[US" (vs. 6). The way the commentators have handled this is to pretend that the WE is the ]{.class_s321}[Trinity; ]{.class_s322}[this was done so as to keep Christ as the ]{.class_s321}["man"]{.class_s323}[ of verse 4 and verse 5. But when we find ]{.class_s321}["our borders" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 6), it is a little too much to swallow. God speaks of Israel as "His" land (or "MY land") but ]{.class_s321}["our land\...our palaces\...and\...our borders" ]{.class_s323}[is to big a quid to chew on. Micah is speaking for ISRAEL in verses 5--6.]{.class_s321}

[4. When we read ]{.class_s321}["THUS SHALL HE DELIVER US" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 6), the reference was to a man in Israel (vs. 5) who had brought about peace (vs. 5), until the Assyrian entered Palestine (vs. 5). All indications point to the man child of Lamentations 4:20 who secures a temporary peace with the Antichrist (Dan. 11:21) until the middle of the Tribulation (Dan. 9:27).]{.class_s321}

"[As a dew from the LORD, as the showers upon the grass" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 7). All of the commentators who believe the text are able to make an allegorical application: "The blessed refreshing influence of the etc.," "the invigorating, life giving power of the etc.," "the liberating truths of Christ's gospel, etc."; but somehow they conveniently forget that the September-October Advent, before the Millennium, is marked by a ]{.class_s321}[literal shower of rain ]{.class_s322}[(see comments under Joel 2:23).]{.class_s321}

[The corrupt Greek Uncial manuscripts recommended by Bob Jones University and the Westcott and Hort Cult read, "And as lambs on the grass" (vs. 7) in the passage, although the grossly corrupt ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[(]{.class_s321}[from these manuscripts]{.class_s322}[) did not dare translate the passage. ]{.class_s321}[The Pulpit Commentary ]{.class_s322}[tells us that the Jew is only "given up" (vs. 3) until the ]{.class_s321}[birth of Christ]{.class_s322}[ (!) and that the ]{.class_s321}["she"]{.class_s323}[ of verse 3 (]{.class_s321}["SHE which travaileth"]{.class_s323}[) is MARY! The]{.class_s321}[ "ASSYRIAN"]{.class_s323}[ of verse 5 is merely "the world powers arrayed against God's people" (]{.class_s321}[The Pulpit Commentary,]{.class_s322}[ p. 68), while the]{.class_s321}[ "showers"]{.class_s323}[ of verse 7 []{#c2KG.xhtml_page_454}are "God's free unmerited gift and will come upon the nations in his good time and way." Matthew Henry tells us that the ]{.class_s321}["remnant of Jacob" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 7) is actually the Church, "dispersed like gold in the ore, or wheat in the heap." Since none of these comments have anything to do with the Biblical truth, we would do well to dispense with them.]{.class_s321}

8 And the remnant of Jacob shall be among the Gentiles in the midst of many people as a lion among the beasts of the forest, as a young lion among the flocks of sheep: who, if he go through, both treadeth down, and teareth in pieces, and none can deliver.

9 Thine hand shall be lifted up upon thine adversaries, and all thine enemies shall be cut off.

10 And it shall come to pass in that day, saith the LORD, that I will cut off thy horses out of the midst of thee, and I will destroy thy chariots:

11 And I will cut off the cities of thy land, and throw down all thy strong holds:

12 And I will cut off witchcrafts out of thine hand; and thou shalt have no more soothsayers:

13 Thy graven images also will I cut off, and thy standing images out of the midst of thee; and thou shalt no more worship the work of thine hands.

14 And I will pluck up thy groves out of the midst of thee: so will I destroy thy cities.

15 And I will execute vengeance in anger and fury upon the heathen, such as they have not heard.

[Verse ]{.class_s321}[8 ]{.class_s322}[tells any anti Semitic British Israelite that the final position of the descendants of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob will be chief of the nations (Num. []{#c2KG.xhtml_page_455}23:24) and "king of the mountain" (Deut. 28:1,10). Isaiah 2:2, in the Bible, is not idle fancy of daydreamers, even though the one world daydreamers of Soviet America have plastered Isaiah 2:4 on their ecumenical hellhole in downtown New York (stink city). ]{.class_s321}[Jacob ]{.class_s322}[comes out on top in the Millennium; with the Lion of the Tribe of ]{.class_s321}[Judah ]{.class_s322}[as his ruler (see Matt. 27:37), he is ]{.class_s321}["as a LION among the beasts of the forests" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 8). In the Millennium, the leading world power ]{.class_s321}[is Israel ]{.class_s322}[in the land of Palestine, and, beside it, the United States, Germany, Japan, England, and Russia are only "satellites." ]{.class_s321}["Thine hand shall be lifted up upon thine adversaries, and ALL THINE ENEMIES SHALL BE CUT OFF"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 9). How literally this will be fulfilled is indicated in Jeremiah 30:11. Matthew Henry's comment on the]{.class_s321}[ "Thine"]{.class_s323}[ of verse 9 is very typical of the agonizing exegesis that must be resorted to at times by the modern "Christian" who refuses "sound doctrine." Henry states that "the CHURCH will tear down and tread" her enemies in the sense of "overcoming spiritual enemies." (Sounds almost like the Spanish Inquisition, doesn't it?) The Wycliffe commentator (E. L. Carlson) takes the middle ground ]{.class_s321}[between ]{.class_s322}[Matthew Henry and the truth and states that it is the "SPIRITUAL Israel" (]{.class_s321}[Wycliffe Bible Commentary, ]{.class_s322}[p. 858) which accomplishes verse 8, spiritually in a spiritual kingdom. \[It will be about as "spiritual" (see Luke 19:12; Rev. 5:10) as your left foot.\]]{.class_s321}

Before God restores Israel (Millennium), she will go through the fire (Isa. 43:2; Mal. 3:14).

[1. ]{.class_s321}["I will cut off thy horses\...and I will destroy thy chariots" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 10). God will reduce Israel to a helpless remnant (Lam. 5) with no army to get them out of their calamities (2 Kings 7:13). \[Cf. Deut. 17:16; Psa. 20:7; and 1 Kings 10:26, 28.\]]{.class_s321}

[2. ]{.class_s321}["I will cut off the cities\...throw down all thy strong holds" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 11). Salvation is of the Lord---not of high towers and fenced cities (2 Kings 18:13; Ecc. 9:14; Prov. 18:10--11).]{.class_s321}

[3. ]{.class_s321}["And I will cut off witchcrafts\...no more soothsayers" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 12). Israel is going to be purged of idolatry and Satanism before the Lord does []{#c2KG.xhtml_page_456}anything for her militarily or politically. (See 2 Kings 9:22; Isa. 47:9; Nah. 3:4; and Deut. 18:10).]{.class_s321}

[4. ]{.class_s321}["Thy graven images also will I cut off\...thou shalt no more worship the work of thine hands" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 13). All is obvious; Isaiah 44:9--20 is a good comment on the passage. (See also Isa. 46:5--8 and 47:6--15).]{.class_s321}

[5. ]{.class_s321}["And I will pluck up thy GROVES\...so will I destroy thy cities" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 14). Matthew 22:7 gives us the first fulfillment of these matters, and they have been described at length in the histories written by Josephus. However, Matthew 22 is plainly a ]{.class_s321}[Tribulation context, ]{.class_s322}[so a future destruction of Israel's cities is in the offing in the next twenty years.]{.class_s321}

[6. ]{.class_s321}["And I will execute VENGEANCE in anger and fury upon the HEATHEN" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 15). God repays the Assyrian and the "Babylonian" (Jer. 50:17) in double coin (Isa. 10:16; Jer. 51). God uses the heathen to punish Israel (Jer. 52), and then punishes the heathen for persecuting His people (Jer. 50:35). This has been going on for over 3,300 years (see Judg. 1--10)---although the secular historians have never once noticed the phenomenon enough to even remark on it.]{.class_s321}

Before looking at our next chapter, let us examine the majority of Conservative blunders and their flummoxed expositions which still clutter up the shelves of the theological libraries.

[1. ]{.class_s321}[The "Living" ]{.class_s322}[(pray brother, pray!) ]{.class_s321}[Bible ]{.class_s322}[has capitalized PEACE (vs. 5) so that it will have to refer to ]{.class_s321}[Christ ]{.class_s322}[instead of the ]{.class_s321}["anointed" ]{.class_s323}[of Lamentations 4:20. And it has shyly inserted "idol temples" for ]{.class_s321}["groves"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 14) to line up with the ]{.class_s321}[RSV, ASV, NASV,]{.class_s322}[ etc., who all covered up for the standard twentieth-century Roman Catholic violation of Deuteronomy 16:21.]{.class_s321}

Rice, ]{.class_s321}[The Sword of the Lord, ]{.class_s322}[1975) has added "borne a CHILD" to verse 3 so that you will think it is ]{.class_s321}[Mary ]{.class_s322}[giving birth to Christ (cf. Isa. 9:6), and then it has capitalized the word ]{.class_s321}["this"]{.class_s323}[ to be "this One" in verse 5 so that you will not find ]{.class_s321}[any reference to the anointed man child of Lamentations 4:20. ]{.class_s322}[And the []{#c2KG.xhtml_page_457}word ]{.class_s321}["heard" ]{.class_s323}[has been changed to "obeyed" (vs. 15) to match the Communist Bible of 1952 (the ]{.class_s321}[RSV ]{.class_s322}[of the NCCC).]{.class_s321}

[3. The No Heller Bible (]{.class_s321}[NWT]{.class_s322}[)]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s322}[has transferred the word ]{.class_s321}["everlasting" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 2) into "the days of time INDEFINITE," which, of course, it had to do since neither Judge Rutherford nor Pastor Russell thought Jesus Christ was the FIRST God: He was the SECOND one, and still is in the Watchtower Literature and in the ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[justified by Bob Jones III, (see John 1:18 in the ]{.class_s321}[NASV]{.class_s322}[). ]{.class_s321}["Groves"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 14) has been altered to "sacred poles," in keeping with the contemporary bull shooting of the "scholars" that the GROVES were really not "groves"they were really "images."]{.class_s321}

[Since all of the Bibles resort to this last alteration of text, it might be a blessing for the believing student of the Scripture to observe how the Holy Spirit has corrected the stupid blundering of these recognized scholars by giving us the material of Deuteronomy 16:21, ]{.class_s321}["PLANT\...a grove of any TREES"]{.class_s323}[;]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s323}[2 Chronicles 31:1, ]{.class_s321}["Brake the IMAGES in pieces, AND cut down the groves"]{.class_s323}[;]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s323}[2 Chronicles 33:19, ]{.class_s321}["And set up groves AND graven images"]{.class_s323}[;]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s323}[2 Chonicles 34:3, ]{.class_s321}["And the groves, AND the carved images, AND the molten images"]{.class_s323}[; and 2 Kings 23:14 ]{.class_s321}["And he brake in pieces the IMAGES, and cut down the GROVES."]{.class_s323}

[Don't you find it rather interesting to notice that bones are buried in a PLACE where a grove was? And isn't it enlightening to notice that ]{.class_s321}["NEITHER"]{.class_s323}[ (Deut. 16:22) has been set in opposition to ]{.class_s321}["A grove of any trees" ]{.class_s323}[(Deut. 16:21), and the ]{.class_s321}["neither" ]{.class_s323}[is a reference to ]{.class_s321}["ANY IMAGE"]{.class_s323}[?]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s323}[Interesting, isn't it? An ]{.class_s321}[asherim ]{.class_s322}[(see any comment by any Hebrew scholar) ]{.class_s321}[can be ]{.class_s322}[an image cut out of wood that came from a GROVE, but a GROVE is a GROVE, exactly as you find it growing around statues of Mary on the campus of every Catholic school in America.]{.class_s321}

[Finally, may we notice that the altering of the last word in the chapter to "obeyed" (]{.class_s321}[ASV, NASV, RSV, NRSV, The Living Bible]{.class_s322}[) has been accomplished by absolutely refusing to translate ANY Hebrew text.]{.class_s321}

[After all the depraved stink about the ]{.class_s321}[AV]{.class_s322}['s failure to "translate the gender" []{#c2KG.xhtml_page_458}of the verb in Ruth 3:15---talk with any egotist at Dallas Theological Seminary---]{.class_s321}[The New International Version ]{.class_s322}[and ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[translators (with the ]{.class_s321}[RSV ]{.class_s322}[and ]{.class_s321}[NRSV]{.class_s322}[ translators) have refused to translate the verb (Heb.---]{.class_s321}[shama'u) ]{.class_s322}[in any manner. The verb (from ]{.class_s321}[shama]{.class_s322}[) means to HEAR or to LISTEN. By changing this to "which hearkened not" (]{.class_s321}[RV]{.class_s322}[, recommended by Dummelow) or "refuse to obey me" (]{.class_s321}[Living Bible]{.class_s322}[), the entire sense of the verse was altered. The sense was that God would pour out wrath and fury on the HEATHEN---not "nations" as in the corrupt ]{.class_s321}[New Scofield Reference Bible---]{.class_s322}["SUCH AS THEY HAVE NOT ]{.class_s334}[HEARD"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 15). Notice how this is the Biblical way the expression is used: Deuteronomy 4:32; Joel 1:2; Exodus 9:18; and Habakkuk 3:2. The meaning here is not that the nations would not LISTEN; the meaning is that they are going to go through a "hell on earth" (see Matt. 24:21) unlike anything they ever heard of in the past.]{.class_s321}

CHAPTER SIX

6:1 Hear ye now what the LORD saith; Arise, contend thou before the mountains, and let the hills hear thy voice.

2 Hear ye, O mountains, the LORD'S controversy, and ye strong foundations of the earth: for the LORD hath a controversy with his people, and he will plead with Israel.

3 O my people, what have I done unto thee? and wherein have I wearied thee? testify against me.

4 For I brought thee up out of the land of Egypt, and redeemed thee out of the house of servants; and I sent before thee Moses, Aaron, and Miriam.

5 O my people, remember now what Balak king of Moab consulted, and what Balaam the son of Beor answered him from Shittim unto Gilgal; that ye may know the righteousness of the LORD.

[The tirade is addressed to the ]{.class_s321}["hills" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 1) and the ]{.class_s321}["strong foundations of the earth" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 2). For a similar treatment, notice the prophecy on the Virgin Birth addressed to the ]{.class_s321}["EARTH" ]{.class_s323}[(Jer. 22:29). The Lord takes it for granted that sound waves can be stored up in ROCK (see Josh. 24:27); so, when Satan tells Jesus to ]{.class_s321}["command that these STONES" ]{.class_s323}[(Matt. 4:3), he is thinking in a literal context (see Jer. 2:27; Gen. 35:14; Josh. 4:3,5--9,21; Psa. 141:6; Luke 3:8; and 1 Pet. 2:4) about something which scientists and physicists are ignorant. (Science and physics, it must be remembered, ALWAYS lag behind the ]{.class_s321}[King James]{.class_s322}[ revelation from 50 to 400 years.)]{.class_s321}

In the passage, God's people (Israel) are called to take the witness stand and testify against Him (vs. 3), exactly as some of them (the Pharisees) did on another occasion (Matt. 26:59). Before giving the negative evidence, the Lord []{#c2NJ.xhtml_page_460}appeals to the conscience of the nation on four counts:

1\. The fact that He got them out of Africa (vs. 4).

2\. The fact that He set them free from slavery (vs. 4).

3\. The fact that He used their own kinfolk to deliver them (vs. 4).

4\. The fact that when their enemies gathered together to slaughter them (Num. 22--23), the Lord would not let them do it, and He even turned a false prophet into a true prophet (temporarily) in order to "read the handwriting on the wall" (vs. 5). The last verse (vs. 5) is of such tremendous consequence that we shall comment on it more fully, later.

[The scene is a courtroom where the Lord is calling in the hills and the mountains and the foundations of the earth as a jury to decide a lawsuit between Him and Israel. He tells Israel to ]{.class_s321}["Arise, contend\...let the hills hear thy voice" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 1). The "controversy" of verse 2 is a legal case which must be decided in court (1 King 22:19).]{.class_s321}

"[What have I done unto thee? ]{.class_s323}[and wherein have I wearied thee?" (vs. 3). They were not "worn out" going through the wilderness because the Lord fed and clothed them for forty years. Moses was so virile and healthy after forty years of it that he didn't need glasses, and his "natural force was not abated" (Deut. 34:7). It was ]{.class_s321}[god ]{.class_s322}[who got "worn out" and wearied (Isa. 1:14) from carrying a load of idolatrous, stubborn, stiff-necked, self righteous, griping, rebellious backsliders (Jer. 15:6; Acts 7:51; Heb. 3:10). \[See the extensive comments in ]{.class_s321}[The Bible Believer's Commentary on Exodus, ]{.class_s322}[1976.\]]{.class_s321}

[Now verse 5 is what bombs the "scholars" out---Conservative, ]{.class_s321}[Fundamental, ]{.class_s322}[Liberal, Catholic, Communist, or Neo orthodox; the statement is that any Israelite of Micah's day can know ]{.class_s321}["THE RIGHTEOUSNESS OF THE LORD" ]{.class_s323}[(]{.class_s321}[AV ]{.class_s322}[text). Now isn't that profound revelation in view of Romans 10:1--6 and Romans 3:21? How do you suppose any "good, godly, dedicated Fundamentalist" could fail to see that connection?]{.class_s321}

[You see, Balaam's answer (vs. 5), recorded in Numbers, ]{.class_s321}[does NOT reveal the righteous "ACTS" ]{.class_s322}[of ]{.class_s321}[the Lord at all! ]{.class_s322}[To the contrary, from the human standpoint, it reveals ]{.class_s321}[the UNRIGHTEOUS ACTS]{.class_s322}[---although it reveals the RIGHTEOUSNESS of the Lord.]{.class_s321}

[Now study Balaam's answer (Num. 23:21). Was there ]{.class_s321}[iniquity ]{.class_s322}[in Jacob? Look at it again (Num. 23:21). Was there ]{.class_s321}[perverseness ]{.class_s322}[in Israel? [The truth of the matter is that Israel had not been forsaken by God, ]{.class_s321}["though their land was filled WITH SIN against the Holy One of Israel" ]{.class_s323}[(Jer. 51:5), and that is not a description of a RIGHTEOUS ACT of the Lord; that is a manifestation of HIS RIGHTEOUSNESS. 6 Wherewith shall I come before the LORD, and bow myself before the high God? shall I come before him with burnt offerings, with calves of a year old?

[The passage has been printed in the International Sunday School lessons for twenty five years. Here is the "catch" in the discourse.

[In the first place, the sinner was told to WALK with God, but he was just told (Amos 3:3) that two cannot ]{.class_s321}["walk together, except they be agreed"]{.class_s323}[! His name is ]{.class_s321}["Jesus"]{.class_s323}[ (Matt. 1:25), and He is the one who meets God's requirements (vs. 8) and ]{.class_s321}[does ]{.class_s322}[]{#c2NJ.xhtml_page_466}[offer His body (vs. 7), as the Firstborn (vs. 7), for the sins of the soul (vs. 7), according to the doctrinal statements of the Holy Spirit in Isaiah 53:10.]{.class_s321}

[The ]{.class_s321}["rod"]{.class_s323}[ is Satan, in the person of the ]{.class_s321}[Antichrist ]{.class_s322}[(see Jer. 48:17 and Ezek. 19:11--14), who is typified by THE ASSYRIAN (see Isa. 10:12 and Jer. 50:17). 10 Are there yet the treasures of wickedness in the house of the wicked, and the scant measure that is abominable?

11 Shall I count them pure with the wicked balances, and with the bag of deceitful weights?

12 For the rich men thereof are full of violence, and the inhabitants thereof have spoken lies, and their tongue is deceitful in their mouth.

13 Therefore also will I make thee sick in smiting thee, in making thee desolate because of thy sins.

14 Thou shalt eat, but not be satisfied; and thy casting down shall be in the midst of thee; and thou shalt take hold, but shalt not deliver; and that which thou deliverest will I give up to the sword.

15 Thou shalt sow, but thou shalt not reap; thou shalt []{#c2NJ.xhtml_page_468}tread the olives, but thou shalt not anoint thee with oil; and sweet wine, but shalt not drink wine.

All is apparent in the passage. God enumerates the sins of His people and then tells them what He is going to do about it.

"[The scant measure" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 10) is the "rigged" measuring scale of Proverbs 11:1 and Amos 8:5. The ]{.class_s321}["treasures of wickedness" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 10) got into the house of the wicked because they gypped their customers out of their money.]{.class_s321}

"[Wicked balances, and with the bag of deceitful weights" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 11) describes the operation (Hos. 12:7) because the man parceling out the goods is carrying a different set of leads in his "bag." Sometimes the "pound" is sixteen ounces and sometimes it is seventeen and sometimes it is thirteen and a half.]{.class_s321}

[Will God count such businessmen as]{.class_s321}[ "pure"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 11) along with their "gaffed" paraphernalia? No, He won't. If the standard of truth one time is the Living Bible, and another time it is the ]{.class_s321}[NASV]{.class_s322}[, and then it is switched to the ]{.class_s321}[AV ]{.class_s322}[on occasion and then back to ]{.class_s321}[The International Version, ]{.class_s322}[until the ]{.class_s321}[NRSV ]{.class_s322}[supplies the needed heresy, shall God count such Christian scholars as ]{.class_s321}["PURE"]{.class_s323}[? If ]{.class_s321}["a little leaven leaveneth the whole lump" ]{.class_s323}[and an ]{.class_s321}[adulterous whore ]{.class_s322}[(Rev. 17) is known by the ]{.class_s321}[leaven ]{.class_s322}[she implants into the pure meal (Matt. 13) of the word of God (Luke 4:4), do you think that God will make the mistake that John R. Rice made all of his life: Calling men "godly" in the same context where he mentions their ]{.class_s321}[corrections on the word of ]{.class_s322}[God? If R. A. Torry was "godly," there was nothing "godly" about his espousal of the ]{.class_s321}[RV ]{.class_s322}[(1884) when he wanted to show off his German scholarship. \[Torrey studied in Germany---as did every educated Liberal in America from 1850--1920---under Delitzsch.)]{.class_s321}

[Verses 12 and 13 need no exposition: Anyone can understand them in the ]{.class_s321}[AV ]{.class_s322}[of 1611. Before examining the fatuities of Fundamental and Conservative []{#c2NJ.xhtml_page_470}scholarship (where they leaned on the famous "bruised reeds" to save face before the Modernists of 1900---Machen, Warfield, Robertson, Wilson, and Schaff), we reproduce the last verse of the chapter.

16 For the statutes of Omri are kept, and all the works of the house of Ahab, and ye walk in their counsels; that I should make thee a desolation, and the inhabitants thereof an hissing: therefore ye shall bear the reproach of my people.

[The ]{.class_s321}["statutes of Omri\...and all the works of the house of Ahab" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 16) are described in 1 Kings 16:31, and we have commented on them abundantly under Revelation 2:20 and Genesis 49:16. CHAPTER SEVEN

7:1 Woe is me! for I am as when they have gathered the summer fruits, as the grapegleanings of the vintage: there is no cluster to eat: my soul desired the firstripe fruit.

2 The good man is perished out of the earth: and there is none upright among men: they all lie in wait for blood; they hunt every man his brother with a net.

3 That they may do evil with both hands earnestly, the prince asketh, and the judge asketh for a reward; and the great man, he uttereth his mischievous desire: so they wrap it up.

4 The best of them is as a brier: the most upright is sharper than a thorn hedge: the day of thy watchmen and thy visitation cometh; now shall be their perplexity.

5 Trust ye not in a friend, put ye not confidence in a guide: keep the doors of thy mouth from her that lieth in thy bosom.

6 For the son dishonoureth the father, the daughter riseth up against her mother, the daughter in law against her mother in law; a man's enemies are the men of his own house.

7 Therefore I will look unto the LORD; I will wait for the God of my salvation: my God will hear me.

[We have printed three sections (1--2, 3--4, and 5--7) because all three deal primarily with the Great Tribulation or ]{.class_s321}["the time of Jacob's Trouble" ]{.class_s323}[(Jer. 30:7). Paul quotes Micah 7:2, along with Psalm 14 and 53, when he describes the general condition of fallen mankind; but there is an exact generation referred to (see Prov. 30:11--14 and comments in that Commentary), for the date is given as ]{.class_s321}["WHEN THEY HAVE GATHERED THE []{#c2S2.xhtml_page_473}SUMMER FRUITS" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 1), clearly locating us at September October (Feast of Tabernacles) at the close of Daniel's Seventieth Week (see Exod. 23:14 and comments in that Commentary). Jeremiah refers to this when he wails, ]{.class_s321}["The harvest is past, the SUMMER IS ENDED, and we are not saved" ]{.class_s323}[(Jer. 8:20).]{.class_s321}

[Once the post Tribulation rapture has taken place (Rev. 14:18), the only harvest left is for the ]{.class_s321}["VINE OF THE EARTH" ]{.class_s323}[(see Rev. 14:18 and Deut. 32:14).]{.class_s321}

[1. ]{.class_s321}["The good man is perished out of the earth" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 2). What is left is ]{.class_s321}["grapes of gall" ]{.class_s323}[(Deut. 32:32) and rotten figs (Jer. 24:8).]{.class_s321}

[2. ]{.class_s321}["They all lie in wait for BLOOD" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 2). ]{.class_s321}[Literally, ]{.class_s322}[in the Tribulation, for there will be human sacrifices then (see Rev. 6:9 and comments) with ]{.class_s321}["drink offerings of blood" ]{.class_s323}[(Psa. 16:4) as the "sons of God" (see Gen. 6:1 and comments) try to reproduce by taking blood ORALLY (see Isa. 6:13 and Rev. 16:6).]{.class_s321}

[3. ]{.class_s321}["The prince\...the judge\...and the great man" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 3) are just as deceived and led astray as the most immoral junkie or the dumbest moron who ever lived. They sign up with the Antichrist (Dan. 11:38) to keep their "social image" (1 Cor. 2:8), and they mobilize against God and His Christ (Rev. 6:15) to "wrap things up" (vs. 3).]{.class_s321}

[4. ]{.class_s321}["The best of them is as a brier: the most upright is sharper than a thorn hedge" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 4). What a commentary on Darwin's plan of salvation! What a notation on Norman Vincent Peale and the suckers who believed him. The ]{.class_s321}["best" ]{.class_s323}[is only fit for burning (look at 2 Sam. 23:6 and Heb. 6:8 for the "brier and the thorns"). So much for 64scientific progress" in the twentieth century.]{.class_s321}

[5. ]{.class_s321}["Trust ye not in a friend, put ye not confidence in a guide' ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 5). In the coming jungle society of dog eat dog, you are to trust ]{.class_s321}[no one, ]{.class_s322}[including your wife (vs. 5: ]{.class_s321}["from her that lieth in thy bosom"), ]{.class_s323}[and if 500 preachers, 500 psychiatrists, 500 psychologists, 500 politicians, and the United Nations told you to do ]{.class_s321}[otherwise, ]{.class_s322}[it would be of no more significance than a dog baying at the moon. Jeremiah concurred with Micah in Jeremiah 9:2--6; Jesus Christ supported him in Matthew 10:35 and Luke 12:53; and Paul backs all of them up in 2 Timothy 3:1--10. [Jeremiah 17:5,7 tells why Micah should do this instead of looking to men: ]{.class_s321}["Cursed be the man that trusteth in MAN\...and whose heart departeth from the LORD\...Blessed is the man that trusteth in the LORD, and whose hope the LORD is." ]{.class_s323}[For the man who trusteth in the "things of man" (Matt. 16:23), there is nothing in store but punishment, disappointment, and eventual death (Luke 16:19--23). 8 Rejoice not against me, O mine enemy: when I fall, I shall arise; when I sit in darkness, the LORD shall be a light unto me.

9 I will bear the indignation of the LORD, because I have sinned against him, until he plead my cause, and execute judgment for me: he will bring me forth to the light, and I shall behold his righteousness.

10 Then she that is mine enemy shall see it, and shame shall cover her which said unto me, Where is the LORD thy God? mine eyes shall behold her: now shall she be trodden down as the mire of the streets.

11 In the day that thy walls are to be built, in that day shall the decree be far removed.

12 In that day also he shall come even to thee from Assyria, and from the fortified cities, and from the fortress even to the river, and from sea to sea, and from mountain to mountain.

13 Notwithstanding the land shall be desolate because of them that dwell therein, for the fruit of their doings.

[Since Assyria is likened to a ]{.class_s321}[female whore ]{.class_s322}[(see Nah. 3:4), most of the commentators assume that this is the female referred to here (vs. 10: ]{.class_s321}["SHE that is mine enemy"]{.class_s323}[). However, this overlooks the great prophetic truth that the King of Assyria and the King of Babylon are matchmeets for the Antichrist (see Jer. 50:17--18); therefore, Nineveh and Babylon are both personifications of the great female demon of Revelation 17 and Zechariah 5:7. When Micah says, ]{.class_s321}["Shall SHE be trodden down as the mire of the streets" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 10), he is plainly connecting Israel's]{.class_s321}[ "enemy"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 10) with Jezebel (see 2 Kings 9:33). \[Cf. also Psa. 68:21--23.\] The reference then goes beyond Assyria as a ]{.class_s321}[]{#c2S2.xhtml_page_476}[world power, ]{.class_s322}[and it goes beyond the historical past (722--600 B.C.) where Sennacherib and Nebuchadnezzar sacked Israel and Judah. Notice how closely Micah's words in verse 9---]{.class_s321}["He will bring me forth to the light, and I shall behold his righteousness"---]{.class_s323}[match the astounding faith of Job: ]{.class_s321}["But he knoweth the way that I take: when he hath tried me, I shall come forth as gold" ]{.class_s323}[(Job 23:10).]{.class_s321}

Micah's passage, then, points to the end of the Church Age, and if there were any doubt about it, all doubt is dispelled at once by the literal prophesy of Israel's "feeding" in the wilderness (Rev. 12:6) occurring in verses 14 and 15.

[Since 100 percent of the scholars (saved or lost) ]{.class_s321}[missed ]{.class_s322}[that reference, it is quite natural that they would fail to see verses 8--13 as a reference to the Second Advent and the works of Satan's Bride (Rev. 17:1--18).]{.class_s321}

"[When I fall, I shall arise" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 8) because a just man ]{.class_s321}["falleth seven times" ]{.class_s323}[(Prov. 24:16) and rises again, for ]{.class_s321}["he shall not be utterly cast down" ]{.class_s323}[(Psa. 37:24). "[I will bear the indignation of the LORD, because I have []{#c2S2.xhtml_page_477}sinned against him" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 9). c\. God will plead his cause before the Devil when he confesses and repents (vs. 9).

d\. God will judge those that have caused him to stumble (vss. 8--9).

f\. He will wind up like Jesus Christ, no matter what (vs. 9)!

[When we sit ]{.class_s321}["in darkness"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 8), we have the Father (vs. 7, ]{.class_s321}["THE LORD"]{.class_s323}[), the]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s323}[Son (vs. 7, ]{.class_s321}["THE GOD"]{.class_s323}[) and the Holy Spirit (vs. 7, "MY GOD"), whether that darkness be the gloomy hour of temptation, the trying hour of difficulty, or the midnight of bereavement. Jeremiah sinks into the "slough of despond" (Jer. 20:14), and the inky cloud of unbelief nearly absorbs him (Jer. 15:18). Moses is tempted almost beyond his powers (Num. 11:15), and the darkness of discouragement nearly wipes him out (Exod. 32:32). Naomi is so stripped of her loved ones that she despairs of ever seeing an heir in her family (Ruth 1:11--13), but when the darkness of death and Hell descended on Calvary, the Son could still say, ]{.class_s321}["He will bring me forth to the light" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 9)!]{.class_s321}

[As an old time saint once said, "Lord, let me have ]{.class_s321}[anything ]{.class_s322}[with THY smile but nothing with Thy ]{.class_s321}[frown." ]{.class_s322}[Some of the survivors of the Frankfort, Illinois, mine disaster (1951) had a light 500 feet below ground, in a caved-in tunnel, that Pope Paul and Jack Kennedy never saw a day in their life; and it wasn't a miners lamp or a flashlight. The reporters asked one of the survivors what they did down there in the dark, slowly dying, without fresh air or water, and he said: "We sang the greatest song ever written." I once was lost but now []{#c2S2.xhtml_page_478}I'm found, t'was blind BUT NOW I SEE!!"]{.class_s321}

And that's more than you can say for 90 percent of the congressmen in America.

[2. A ]{.class_s321}[decree to pour out the accumulated wrath ]{.class_s322}[of ]{.class_s321}[God ]{.class_s322}[on Judah, Israel, and the nations who persecute her (Jer. 51:44--50; Zeph. 2:2, 3:8).]{.class_s321}

Discarding the accumulated scholarship of nineteen centuries (Conservative or Infidel: same crowd when it comes to hating the authority of God Almighty), we shall see that the decree is NOT:

But! When the walls are rebuilt the second time (Ezek. 40--48), the decree to rebuild them (Dan. 9:25) is again removed by 2,400 years into the past, for the Church Age intervenes between the DECREE and the fulfillment of the decree.

The text is exact. It presents dual prophecies with dual fulfillment, separated by 2,300--2,400 years, and there is nothing as "obscure" about the text as the muddled logic of the blind idiots who changed it to suit their own depraved fancies.

When in doubt\...you know what.

"[In that day also he shall come" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 12). [What about this: ]{.class_s321}["HE THAT BELIEVETH AND IS BAPTIZED" ]{.class_s323}[means "God lets the righteous suffer to make them sympathetic"? INTRODUCTION

TO NAHUM

[The city called "Capernaum" in the New Testament was called "the village of Nahum" after some man of that name. The chances are that it was named after the prophet referred to here, although this cannot be proved. The Jewish rabbis taught that Nahum was carried away into captivity around 722 B.C. and that he wrote and prophesied from ]{.class_s321}[Elkosh, ]{.class_s322}[west of the Tigris River and north of Mosul of Assyria. ]{.class_s321}["Nahum the Elkoshite" ]{.class_s323}[would refer to a man from that town or else a man from that family. The origination of either is uncertain as there is also a town called "Elkosh" (]{.class_s321}[Elkesi]{.class_s322}[)]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s322}[in Galilee in the land of Palestine.]{.class_s321}

[Nahum prophesies around the time of Hezekiah and Isaiah, so we may roughly locate his lifetime as somewhere around 740 B.C. to 700 B.C. However, the ]{.class_s321}[New Scofield Reference Bible ]{.class_s322}[puts the prophecies of Nahum AFTER the coming of Sennecherib to Jerusalem (]{.class_s321}[NSRB]{.class_s322}[, p. 950) so that he makes no real prophecies concerning Sennacherib at all. \[Note how Liberals and Atheists did this with Daniel's prophecies on Rome, Greece, and Persia: ]{.class_s321}[The Coming Prince, ]{.class_s322}[Sir Robert Anderson. By having Nahum prophesy AFTER 700 B.C. (see margin in ]{.class_s321}[NSRB]{.class_s322}[) he can make no reference to the events of 2 Kings 18--19.\]]{.class_s321}

[Bullinger is way out in left field with a date of 603 B.C. for the invasion of Sennacherib, so he has Micah living between 640 and 600 B.C., give or take a few years. \[For Bullinger's variant of one hundred years in chronology see Appendix 86, ]{.class_s321}[Companion Bible]{.class_s322}[.\]]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s322}[Whatever dates are chosen, it is apparent that at the time of the writing the ten tribes have already gone into captivity, Assyria has invaded Egypt (1:9--13, 2:13), and Sermacherib had probably already started his invasion of Judah on his way to Jerusalem.]{.class_s321}

[Williams alone (of the major commentators) is able to grasp the truth that the book still waits a future, final fulfillment. Charles Feinberg (]{.class_s321}[Wycliffe Bible Commentary]{.class_s322}[) treats the entire book as dead history, fulfilled in the ]{.class_s321}[past. ]{.class_s322}[Although it is true that ]{.class_s321}[Cyaxares ]{.class_s322}[and ]{.class_s321}[Nabupolassar ]{.class_s322}[overthrew Nineveh back []{#c2VN.xhtml_page_489}in 625 (or 603) B.C., it is perfectly apparent from Nahum 1:1,3--5, 2:3--4, and 3:4,19 that more is intended by the Holy Spirit than the destruction of an ancient city that has been dust and ashes for sixteen centuries. Nineveh, connected with the Assyrian, is the matchmeet of Babylon, connected with the Antichrist (see Hosea and Exodus with comments).]{.class_s321}

[Much of Nahum's language matches the style of Isaiah (see Isa. 8:8, 10:23, 21:3, 24:1, and 52:7). Nahum's prophecy is "negatory" from start to finish with only a breath or two of positive thinking. There is not one trace of "faith in your fellow man," not one iota of "bringing out the best in people," not one atom of "winning people to Christ," nor one shred of "making the world a better place to live in." When Nahum opens his mouth, it is unremitting destruction, unsheeted hell, death, and damnation from start to finish (see 1:6, 10, 14; 2:1, 10, 13; 13:1, 4, 6, 12, 19). The only relief from this blast are three verses in chapter 1 (vss. 7, 13, 15) which deal with Israel (vs. 15)---not mankind, humanity, brotherhood, the UN, etc. And neither of these verses is positive from the standpoint of an unsaved man or any nation but Israel only. When Nahum says ]{.class_s321}["he knoweth them that trust in Him" ]{.class_s323}[(1:7), he is not referring to a nation of sex crazy, money mad egotists who print "In God We Trust" on their phony play money.]{.class_s321}

[The peculiar "twist" of Amillennial and Postmillennial theology that makes verse 15 (]{.class_s321}["Behold upon the mountains"]{.class_s323}[) refer to Romans 10:15 and Romans 10:15 ONLY is quite typical of the screwed up exegesis that some scholars come out with. Both verses have been appropriated to illustrate the spiritual aspects of the First Advent (Matt. 4:16); but without an understanding of Acts 7 (see comments in that commentary) such a manipulation of prophetic application is FOOLISHNESS. [Nahum has 3 chapters, 47 verses and 1,285 words. The word "Nahum" means "comforter" and "consolation," although another possible meaning []{#c2VN.xhtml_page_491}derived from the same root means "vengeance." Edwards, Renke, Breteneicher, Vance Smith, G. G. Strauss, Justin, Holemann, O. Strauss, Kalinsky, Agrek, Bodin, Fruhn, Hufenreffer, Tarnvovius, Ursin, Crocius, Greve, Grimm, Svangorg, and Rev. W. J. Deane (]{.class_s321}[The Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s322}[). CHAPTER ONE

1:1 The burden of Nineveh. The book of the vision of Nahum the Elkoshite.

2 God is jealous, and the LORD revengeth; the LORD revengeth, and is furious; the LORD will take vengeance on his adversaries, and he reserveth wrath for his enemies.

3 The LORD is slow to anger, and great in power, and will not at all acquit the wicked: the LORD hath his way in the whirlwind and in the storm, and the clouds are the dust of his feet.

4 He rebuketh the sea, and maketh it dry, and drieth up all the rivers: Bashan languisheth, and Carmel, and the flower of Lebanon languisheth.

5 The mountains quake at him, and the hills melt, and the earth is burned at his presence, yea, the world, and all that dwell therein.

6 Who can stand before his indignation? and who can abide in the fierceness of his anger? his fury is poured out like fire, and the rocks are thrown down by him.

7 The LORD is good, a strong hold in the day of trouble; and he knoweth them that trust in him.

8 But with an overrunning flood he will make an utter end of the place thereof, and darkness shall pursue his enemies.

Everything in the section points to the Second Advent. While 1,500 scholars (see preface) play "footsie" with the dead history of the past, Nahum proceeds into descriptions which could only match the events of Revelation 19; 2 Thessalonians 2; and Joel 1.

Observe:

Now (briefly, let us hope!), we shall go into the nursery and watch the kiddies spilling their milk, throwing their rattles, and sticking themselves with diaper pins.

[Historically speaking, ]{.class_s322}[God did judge Nineveh, although He prolonged their "grace period" for nearly 200 years. The Assyrian who came down like "a wolf on the fold" (Byron) returns with 185,000 casualties (2 Kings 19:36), and although the midnight slaughter by the angel of the Lord (2 Kings 19:35) is accompanied by none of the phenomena of verses 4--8, the future slaughter (Rev. 14, 19) will be accompanied by the things listed.]{.class_s321}

9 What do ye imagine against the LORD? he will make an utter end: affliction shall not rise up the second time.

10 For while they be folden together as thorns, and while they are drunken as drunkards, they shall be devoured as stubble fully dry.

11 There is one come out of thee, that imagineth evil against the LORD, a wicked counsellor.

12 Thus saith the LORD; Though they be quiet, and []{#c2WB.xhtml_page_497}likewise many, yet thus shall they be cut down, when he shall pass through. Though I have afflicted thee, I will afflict thee no more.

13 For now will I break his yoke from off thee, and will burst thy bonds in sunder.

14 And the LORD hath given a commandment concerning thee, that no more of thy name be sown: out of the house of thy gods will I cut off the graven image and the molten image: I will make thy grave; for thou art vile.

15 Behold upon the mountains the feet of him that bringeth good tidings, that publisheth peace! O Judah, keep thy solemn feasts, perform thy vows: for the wicked shall no more pass through thee; he is utterly cut off.

[Having stated that the final destruction of Nineveh and Assyria (vs. 9) will not allow ]{.class_s321}["affliction" ]{.class_s323}[to rise up the ]{.class_s321}["second time,"]{.class_s323}[ Nahum proceeds.]{.class_s321}

"[For while they be folden together as thorns, and while they are drunken as drunkards" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 10).]{.class_s321}

[History tells us that a drunken feast was going on the night the Babylonians took over the city. Diodorus Siculus (]{.class_s321}[The Historical Library, ]{.class_s322}[p. 226) records one account of a drunken revel going on when the enemy came in. The note is not unusual (see 1 Kings 20:16 and Dan. 5). Drugs and liquor have accompanied many an attack, being present in the victims and the conqueror before, and after, the battle. It is FIRE, however, that is the ultimate weapon for the destruction of the Ninevites (vs. 10); so, they are likened to the thorns of Hebrews 6 and 2 Samuel 23:6--7---both are Tribulation passages.]{.class_s321}

"[There is ONE\...Ahat imagineth evil against the LORD" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 11). Sennacherib is only the type (2 Kings 19:22) referred to in Ezekiel 38.]{.class_s321}

"[Though they be quiet, and likewise many, yet thus shall they be cut down" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 12). Most of the commentators assume that the reading is: "Thus the Assyrian host---though it be at rest and be numerous---shall []{#c2WB.xhtml_page_498}be cut down by the angel of the Lord" (2 Kings 19) when Sennacherib shall "pass AWAY home" (2 Kings 19:35--36). CHAPTER TWO

2:1 He that dasheth in pieces is come up before thy face: keep the munition, watch the way, make thy loins strong, fortify thy power mightily.

2 For the LORD hath turned away the excellency of Jacob, as the excellency of Israel: for the emptiers have emptied them out, and marred their vine branches.

3 The shield of his mighty men is made red, the valiant men are in scarlet: the chariots shall be with flaming torches in the day of his preparation, and the fir trees shall be terribly shaken.

4 The chariots shall rage in the streets, they shall justle one against another in the broad ways: they shall seem like torches, they shall run like the lightnings.

5 He shall recount his worthies: they shall stumble in their walk; they shall make haste to the wall thereof, and the defence shall be prepared.

[Since we have seen that ]{.class_s321}[dual application ]{.class_s322}[follows the prophets almost anytime they speak, we should not be surprised to find a wholesale slaughter of the King's English when we get to Nahum 2:3--4. [All of this is "very interesting" (to quote ]{.class_s321}[LaughIn]{.class_s322}[),]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s322}[but since we had so much trouble trying to get these unbelieving Conservatives to believe Joel 2 (see comments), wouldn't you suspect that we are about to recross the same Interstate on another highway? [3. ]{.class_s321}["He shall recount his worthies" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 5).]{.class_s321}

[4. ]{.class_s321}["Behold, I AM AGAINST THEE, saith the LORD of hosts" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 13).]{.class_s321}

The trouble is that if this interpretation is taken, then ]{.class_s321}["THE DAY OF HIS PREPARATION" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 3) has nothing to do with the Assyrian king, Cyaraxes, Nabopolossar, Nebopoleser, Niblickolacker, or Nabbitpolecat; it has to do with the Antichrist getting ready to take over the earth, and that very work is used in the Hebrew text]{.class_s321}[---hekino.]{.class_s322}

[But if this is so, then the ]{.class_s321}["chariots" ]{.class_s323}[of verses 3--4 have to refer to modern mechanized ]{.class_s321}[motor vehicles! ]{.class_s322}[With the Hebrew reading "FIRE IN STEEL" (vs. 3) and the chariots moving by ]{.class_s321}["FUEL OF FIRE"]{.class_s323}[ (Isa. 9:5), there is the utmost necessity that every major Conservative and Evangelical scholar in Europe and America attack the text with everything at his command.
