[3. The verse should be interpreted according to 1 Maccabees 6:39: "Now when the sun shone upon the shields of gold and brass, the mountains glistened therewith and shined LIKE lamps of fire!" (]{.class_s321}[The Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s322}[).]{.class_s321}

[4. The ]{.class_s321}[NRSV ]{.class_s322}[(and ]{.class_s321}[RSV]{.class_s322}[): "The chariots flash like flame\...the chargers prance" (vs. 3). These two communist Bibles have simply erased the last eight []{#c2YK.xhtml_page_504}words in the verse.]{.class_s321}

5\. "The mighty men sport with fire" in the erroneous Septuagint (LXX), and you couldn't find a fir tree (vs. 3) in the LXX with a radar scope.

[6. So, the ]{.class_s321}["FIR TREES" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 3) quietly disappear from the ]{.class_s321}[RV ]{.class_s322}[(1884), ]{.class_s321}[ASV ]{.class_s322}[(1901), ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[(1960), ]{.class_s321}[RSV ]{.class_s322}[(1943, 1952), ]{.class_s321}[NRSV ]{.class_s322}[(1974), ]{.class_s321}[Living Bible, ]{.class_s322}[Amplified Claptrap and the New English Blow it-out-your-nose. Cypress spears, cypress forests, horsemen thrown into confusion, and brandished fir tree "lances" are the depraved paraphernalia drug out of religious mythology to undo the damage done by ]{.class_s321}["THE DAY OF HIS PREPARATION" ]{.class_s323}[(]{.class_s321}[AV]{.class_s322}[).]{.class_s321}

[7. ]{.class_s321}[Williams, ]{.class_s322}[the premillennial Fundamentalist, simply gets rid of both verses by saying, "These two verses should read as the ]{.class_s321}[Revised Version."]{.class_s322}

[Since neither verse should be read as ]{.class_s321}[any ]{.class_s322}["revised version," and since not one man from Origen to E. S. English had any more idea what he was talking about (when he corrected the God given text) than the worst infidel who ever lived, we shall abandon with due haste all of the Conservatives and Fundamentalists, along with the Liberals and Atheists, and begin a study of the words of Divine Authority as they have been preserved by the Holy Ghost.]{.class_s321}

[1. The one who ]{.class_s321}["dasheth in pieces" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 1) is the ]{.class_s321}[ax ]{.class_s322}[of Isaiah 10:15 and the ]{.class_s321}[hammer ]{.class_s322}[of Jeremiah 23:29. He is called the ]{.class_s321}["King of Assyria"]{.class_s323}[ in Isaiah 10, but he is called the King of Babylon in Jeremiah 50:17--18. It is with this "dasher" that the Lord will ]{.class_s321}["break in pieces the horse and his rider\...and\...the chariot and his rider man and woman\...old and young\...the shepherd and his flock\...the husbandman and his yoke of oxen; and\...captains and rulers" ]{.class_s323}[(Jer. 51:20--23).]{.class_s321}

[2. He is not allowed to "dash in pieces" (vs. 1) until ]{.class_s321}["the LORD bath turned away the excellency of Jacob, as the excellency of Israel" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 2). All points to the last half of Daniel's Seventieth Week, and if this is true, then verses 3 and 4 are no reference to any chariot that ever rolled on this earth up to 1900, unless it was the "chariot of the gods" (Gen. 6)!]{.class_s321}

"[When the ]{.class_s321}["horses"]{.class_s323}[ come up ]{.class_s321}["as the ROUGH CATERPILLERS" ]{.class_s323}[(Jer. 51:27), you can run to Delitzsch, back to Gesenius, over to []{#c2YK.xhtml_page_505}Keil, down to Kittel and then ransack the LXX, the Coptic, the Syriac, the Aramaic and the Pig Latin, and you cannot avoid seeing HORSE POWER in TANKS. The context of Jeremiah 51:35, 49 is Revelation 17--18 and the]{.class_s321}[ "vengeance" ]{.class_s323}[of Jeremiah 51:6 and 11 is no more a reference to Darius and Cyrus than a reference to "Sonny and Cher."]{.class_s321}

[3. The FIR TREES shake till their pine needles come off them when the "whirlwind" passes over (see 1:3), and the ridiculous transition of]{.class_s321}[ "FIR TREES"]{.class_s323}[ to ]{.class_s321}[cypress spears is ]{.class_s322}[too pitiful to deserve comment. Tell us something: if people are flying like birds to their windows (Isa. 60:8) on eagle's wings (Rev. 12:14), don't you suppose the FIR trees are ]{.class_s321}["terribly shaken"]{.class_s323}[ when the jets go over them? Would you convince any of us who have ]{.class_s321}[SEEN ]{.class_s322}[IT that it is otherwise?]{.class_s321}

[4. The chariots RAGE in the streets (vs. 4) of Los Angeles, Washington, Detroit, Cleveland, Atlanta, and ]{.class_s321}["justle one against another in the broad ways"]{.class_s323}[ and run ]{.class_s321}["like the lightnings" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 4), which is perfectly evident from a study of any "timed exposures" made on a highway at night. And they do ]{.class_s321}["seem like torches"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 4), for the English "TORCH" is your flashlight; a flashlight runs on a battery. Get it? BATTERY. \[And how do men ]{.class_s321}["RIDE OVER"]{.class_s323}[ your heads (Psa. 66:12) with ]{.class_s321}["fuel of fire"]{.class_s323}[ (Isa. 9:5) as birds (Isa. 60:8) unless the reference is to 1970 plus?\]]{.class_s321}

[The entire passage is the Antichrist preparing for battle ]{.class_s322}[(see Rev. 19:19). Whatever past historical fulfillment may be found in it---and this is granted---the context still points beyond the church age into the events of the Tribulation.]{.class_s321}

[Historically ]{.class_s322}[the picture is "The Assyrian"---(Nebuchadnezzar)---]{.class_s321}["BEFORE THY FACE"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 1), while the ]{.class_s321}["thy"]{.class_s323}[ was JUDAH in the previous verse (1:15). Destruction follows (vs. 2) as it will in Revelation 11:1 3. After Judah is ]{.class_s321}["emptied"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 2) and ]{.class_s321}["marred" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 2), the Lord attacks! (See 2:13 and comments.) The preparation (vs. 3) the Antichrist makes is with mechanized units (vs. 4) and armed men (vs. 3) who stumble (vs. 5) when they mount the walls to defend their cities (vs. 5). That the references here are to ]{.class_s321}[Babylon ]{.class_s322}[]{#c2YK.xhtml_page_506}[(598--536 B.C.), and not to Assyria, is apparent by verse 8, which begins]{.class_s321}[ "BUT Nineveh"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 8). The close association of the king of Assyria with the king of Babylon (see previous remarks) explains the reading of Nahum 2:1--5, and it explains the expositor's consternation on reading the word ]{.class_s321}["HUZZAB"]{.class_s323}[ in verse 7.]{.class_s321}

Nahum 2:1--5 pictures Nebuchadnezzar attacking Jerusalem and then Babylon defending itself against Persia. Future fulfillment will be the Antichrist attacking Jerusalem (Rev. 11:1 3) and then defending his kingdom against the Lord Jesus Christ (Rev. 17:14).

6 The gates of the rivers shall be opened, and the palace shall be dissolved.

7 And Huzzab shall be led away captive, she shall be brought up, and her maids shall lead her as with the voice of doves, tabering upon their breasts.

8 But Nineveh is of old like a pool of water: yet they shall flee away. Stand, stand, shall they cry; but none shall look back.

9 Take ye the spoil of silver, take the spoil of gold: for there is none end of the store and glory out of all the pleasant furniture.

10 She is empty, and void, and waste: and the heart melteth, and the knees smite together, and much pain is in all loins, and the faces of them all gather blackness.

11 Where is the dwelling of the lions, and the feedingplace of the young lions, where the lion, even the old lion, walked, and the lion's whelp, and none made them afraid?

12 The lion did tear in pieces enough for his whelps, and strangled for his lionesses, and filled his holes with prey, and his dens with ravin.

13 Behold, I am against thee, saith the LORD of hosts, []{#c2YK.xhtml_page_507}and I will burn her chariots in the smoke, and the sword shall devour thy young lions: and I will cut off thy prey from the earth, and the voice of thy messengers shall no more be heard.

[Verse 6 should have stopped the Scholars' Union from its madness. The reference was to the "dasher" of Babylon---not the King of Assyria. ]{.class_s321}["THE GATES OF THE RIVER SHALL BE OPENED"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 6) is a reference to so well known an historical fact that no historian could have missed it: Cyrus' seige and entry into Babylon---Herodotus (Book 1, sec. 191) describes the entry of the Persians into Babylon and every commentator on the market applies Isaiah 45:1 to that operation. Therefore, Nahum 2:6 ]{.class_s321}[is NOT a reference to Nineveh, ]{.class_s322}[for it is contrasted with Nineveh---]{.class_s321}["BUT NINEVEH"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 8). \[You would think if a man thought himself proficient enough to correct the word of God 30,000 times (every version since 1880 corrects it that many times or more) that he would be proficient enough to understand a disjunctive conjunction ]{.class_s321}[in his own tongue]{.class_s322}[.\]]{.class_s321}

"[And Huzzab shall be led away captive\...tabering upon their breasts" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 7). Kenneth Taylor ignores all of the Hebrew texts and says that Huzzab is the ]{.class_s321}[Queen of Nineveh. ]{.class_s322}[The LXX mistranslates "and the fountain has been exposed." The ]{.class_s321}[RSV ]{.class_s322}[tells us the "meaning is uncertain," so it hazards "its mistress" while the ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[simply says, "IT IS FIXED" (whatever in Hades THAT means!). After having made the ]{.class_s321}["gates of the rivers"]{.class_s323}[ METAPHORICAL (]{.class_s321}[The Pulpit Commentary]{.class_s322}[),]{.class_s321}[ ]{.class_s322}["Huzzab"]{.class_s323}[ is dumped altogether. Not to be outdone (by anyone), the Lockman Foundation includes both readings ("it is decreed"---as the ]{.class_s321}[ASV ]{.class_s322}[of 1901 and "its mistress"---as the ]{.class_s321}[RSV ]{.class_s322}[of the National Council of Communist Churches). Nice work.]{.class_s321}

[Maurer was responsible for the ]{.class_s321}[ASV ]{.class_s322}[and ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[reading, while Vatablus is responsible for the Kenneth Taylor distortion of the text. Both of these archaic conjectures were in vogue in 1900, putting the ]{.class_s321}[NASV ]{.class_s322}[and ]{.class_s321}[The Living Bible ]{.class_s322}[]{#c2YK.xhtml_page_508}[fifty to seventy years out of date. ]{.class_s321}[Young's Concordance ]{.class_s322}[calls the place "the Zab country" east of the Tigris River, thus tying it again to ]{.class_s321}[Assyria; ]{.class_s322}[however, a city (Nineveh) is compared with Huzzab, indicating that Huzzab would more likely be a reference to a city than an area.]{.class_s321}

[However, the root word is from the Hebrew ]{.class_s321}[ver naisch ]{.class_s322}[meaning pure, innocent, or faithfully. It also means to lead in music as a "chorister." It can also mean (Niphal) to be "entire" or "perfect." In Chaldean, the word means "to surpass."]{.class_s321}

[Now the "serious student" (I believe that is the doggerel used to deceive a modern ministerial student) has observed that Babylon goes by more than one name; for example, notice the cryptical ]{.class_s321}["SHESHACH"]{.class_s323}[ of Jeremiah 51:41, which is left unlocated in the Hebrew lexicon published by Samuel Bagster and edited by Samuel Davidson. ]{.class_s321}["Huzzab"]{.class_s323}[ has some interesting overtones:]{.class_s321}

[1. A ]{.class_s321}[choir director ]{.class_s322}[who leads a chorus in singing (Ezek. 28:13; Job 38:7).]{.class_s321}

[2. Something PERFECT in its ]{.class_s321}[original state ]{.class_s322}[(Ezek. 28:15).]{.class_s321}

3\. Something beautiful and glorious (Ezek. 28:13,15).

4\. Someone who can conquer (Luke 4:6).

[Since the word is feminine, ]{.class_s321}["She shall be brought up" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 7), the conclusion is inescapable. This is not simply the well favored mistress of Assyria (Nineveh, see 3:4,7); ]{.class_s321}[this is the well favored Whore of Revelation ]{.class_s322}[17 (Babylon: see Jer. 50--51). She is ]{.class_s321}[Satan's Bride, ]{.class_s322}[and this accounts for the references found in the word ]{.class_s321}["HUZZAB" ]{.class_s323}[as given above.]{.class_s321}

"[BUT NINEVEH is of old like a pool of water" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 8). After the destruction of Babylon (vs. 7), Nineveh comes in for her share (vss. 9--11). Her defenders cry ]{.class_s321}["stand, stand!" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 8), but they can't even look over their shoulder running, let alone "stand"! The rape of Nineveh is fulfilled by the Babylonian kings who precede Nebuchadnezzar. Her spoils (see vs. 9) amounted to over \$25,000,000 in gold, not counting the silver (Gonom Bonomi, ]{.class_s321}[Nineveh and Its Discoveries, ]{.class_s322}[pp. 334--346). Nineveh is certainly]{.class_s321}[ "of old"]{.class_s323}[ for she appears in Genesis 10:11 as the seat of idolatry (see Isa. 10:10). Like a ]{.class_s321}["pool of water" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 8) she has been a stagnant mud puddle for over two []{#c2YK.xhtml_page_509}millenniums. ]{.class_s321}["She is empty, and void, and waste" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 10) like the original creation became (see Gen. 1:2 and comments). The]{.class_s321}[ "blackness" ]{.class_s323}[of the faces (vs. 10) indicates that people are burning (see Joel 2:6), exactly as prophesied in 1:6,10, although John Calvin does his best to tone this terrible truth down into a bland diet of mush: "withdraw their glow." Yes, I suppose if your face has turned from scorched skin in a furnace, it has sort of "lost its glow." \[Note Ezek. 20:47 for the literal burning of literal faces, and observe that "serious" students like John Calvin reacted the same way in that day as John did in his: UNBELIEF (Ezek. 20:49).\]]{.class_s321}

"[Where is the dwelling of the lions\...young lions\...the lion\...the old lion\...the lion's whelp" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 11). \[Cf. comments under Job 4:10--11.\] Anyone can spiritualize the passage, but ]{.class_s321}[locating the lions is ]{.class_s322}[something else. G. T. Armstrong locates Assyria as GERMANY. Most commentators locate the ]{.class_s321}[young ]{.class_s322}[lions as the USA, Canada and Australia and the old lion as Great Britain. The point is not yet clear nor have the conjectures yet been proved; "later," as we said in our prefatory comments. Winged lions are quite common on the Assyrian monuments and tombs, but the winged lion of Daniel 7 is not Assyrian---nor is it Babylonian (see comments in the Commentary on Revelation, Rev. 13:13). The LION (vs. 11) has some mates (vs. 12) and some whelps (vs. 12), and he has ]{.class_s321}["filled his holes with prey, and his dens with ravin" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 12). We can say that Nineveh is "personified" as a lion or that Sennacherib is ]{.class_s321}[typified ]{.class_s322}[by a lion, but no one who reads the rest of the Bible would stop there; there is more gold in the passage than the last 1,500 scholars have mined yet. The Antichrist is typified by three lions (PHARAOH, SENNACHERIB, and NEBUCHADNEZZAR: see Ezek. 32:2 and Jer. 50:17).]{.class_s321}

[Now from verse 8 onward we are certainly dealing with the city of Nineveh, and yet even here we should never forget the calculated design of the Holy Spirit in equating ]{.class_s321}[the king of Babylon ]{.class_s322}[with the king of Assyria (Ezek. 31:3) and equating them ]{.class_s321}[both ]{.class_s322}[(Isa. 10:5) with the Antichrist (Jer. 50:17). This means there is something peculiar about Nineveh which is related to the end time, and what we are now reading has a double application: once to the literal city of Nahum's []{#c2YK.xhtml_page_510}time and again to the great ]{.class_s321}["bloody city" ]{.class_s323}[(3:1) who is a ]{.class_s321}["mistress of witchcrafts" ]{.class_s323}[and ]{.class_s321}["whoredoms" ]{.class_s323}[(3:4)a perfect twin sister of Babylon, the ]{.class_s321}["mother of harlots"]{.class_s323}[ (Rev. 17:5),]{.class_s321}[ "abominations and filthiness" ]{.class_s323}[(Rev. 17:4).]{.class_s321}

[Never forget the doctrinal prophetic implications of the prophets, and never make the mistake that William Biederwolf made (]{.class_s321}[The Second Coming Bible, ]{.class_s322}[Baker Book House, 1972) when he excluded Nahum from having a single reference dealing with the future. ]{.class_s321}[The Second Coming Bible, ]{.class_s322}[advertised by Baker Book House (Grand Rapids, Mich.), claims that it is "THE COMPLETE TEXT OF EVERY SCRIPTURE passage concerned with the Second Coming of Christ That is the most arrogant, blatant type of Christian lying that ever came off of the press. ]{.class_s321}[There is not one reference given on the Second Coming ]{.class_s322}[in ]{.class_s321}[Nahum ]{.class_s322}[in ]{.class_s321}[that publication, ]{.class_s322}[and yet anyone can find ten without consulting any Hebrew or Greek text (or scholar) available.]{.class_s321}

"[Behold, I am against thee, saith the Lord of hosts" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 13).]{.class_s321}

[Nothing in the verse is obscure. God intends to cut off Nineveh, and when He does, she will no longer be able to send ambassadors, premiers, and officials up and down the country.]{.class_s321}[ "Thy prey"]{.class_s323}[ would be the people and nations whom Nineveh had preyed upon.]{.class_s321}

[]{#c30E.xhtml}

CHAPTER THREE

3:1 Woe to the bloody city! it is all full of lies and robbery; the prey departeth not;

2 The noise of a whip, and the noise of the rattling of the wheels, and of the pransing horses, and of the jumping chariots.

3 The horseman lifteth up both the bright sword and the glittering spear: and there is a multitude of slain, and a great number of carcases; and there is none end of their corpses; they stumble upon their corpses:

4 Because of the multitude of the whoredoms of the wellfavoured harlot, the mistress of witchcrafts, that selleth nations through her whoredoms, and families through her witchcrafts.

5 Behold, I am against thee, saith the LORD of hosts; and I will discover thy skirts upon thy face, and I will shew the nations thy nakedness, and the kingdoms thy shame.

6 And I will cast abominable filth upon thee, and make thee vile, and will set thee as a gazingstock.

7 And it shall come to pass, that all they that look upon thee shall flee from thee, and say, Nineveh is laid waste: who will bemoan her? whence shall I seek comforters for thee?

[The prey (vs. 1) is the unlawful booty that Nineveh has gathered by pillaging her subjects. Hermann Goering's "art collection" (1942--1945) would come under this category as would the treasures at the Vatican. The "prey" will depart (vs. 1) when it is ]{.class_s321}["cut off" ]{.class_s323}[(2:13), but God will attend to that. ]{.class_s321}["The bloody city" ]{.class_s323}[was capitol of the bloodiest of the ancient nations; they were noted for their cruelty to prisoners and inhuman treatment of conquered countries. Pictures of []{#c30E.xhtml_page_512}Assyrians cutting out the tongues of prisoners, flaying them alive, carrying them around in cages not big enough to stand up in and impaling them on stakes and strangling them with cords are quite common in Assyriology; their successors---the Roman Catholic popes, inquisitors, priests, and International Soviet Socialists---still continue in the tradition.]{.class_s321}

[Verses 2 and 3 give an eyewitness account of the coming battle with the Medo-Babylonian conquerors; the scene is contemporary; men have always stumbled ]{.class_s321}["upon the corpses"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 3). Feinberg's naive comment athat "in ]{.class_s321}[this]{.class_s322}[ siege" there is no time for decent burial is rather lame. "Decent burial" was not given to Germans at Stalingrad because the ground was frozen eight feet deep. "Decent burial" was not granted to U.S. troops in the Pacific who were often bulldozed into mass graves on the beach. "Decent burial" was so uncommon in World War I many a corpse was "resurrected" by artillery fire twenty-four hours after the burial detail put him to bed with a shovel. "Decent burial" is not characteristic of ]{.class_s321}[ANY type of large military engagement,]{.class_s322}[ siege or not.]{.class_s321}

[Verse 4 points directly to the Harlot of Revelation 17. Observe the expressions: ]{.class_s321}["whoredoms\...harlot...her witchcrafts\...abominable filth\...vile\...gazingstock\...who will bemone her?"]{.class_s323}[ (vss. 4--7). Compare these words with Revelation 17--18. In some way, which is not clear to the commentators (and never will be as long as they reject the ]{.class_s321}[Authorized]{.class_s322}[ text), Nineveh is a twin matchmate for Babylon, at least prophetically, where the cities are involved at the Second Advent. The LORD states that Nineveh will be disgraced publicly as a woman who has her clothes torn off her body and manure slopped on her (vs. 5). \[Cf. Jer. 13:22; Ezek. 16:37--44; and Isa. 47:2--3.\] The ]{.class_s321}["gazingstock"]{.class_s323}[ would be like a woman in the "stocks" (hands and feet locked into wooden clamps) set out in the town square for people to stare at.]{.class_s321}

8 Art thou better than populous No, that was situate among the rivers, that had the waters round about it, whose []{#c30E.xhtml_page_513}rampart was the sea, and her wall was from the sea?

9 Ethiopia and Egypt were her strength, and it was infinite; Put and Lubim were thy helpers.

10 Yet was she carried away, she went into captivity: her young children also were dashed in pieces at the top of all the streets: and they cast lots for her honourable men, and all her great men were bound in chains.

11 Thou also shalt be drunken: thou shalt be hid, thou also shalt seek strength because of the enemy.

12 All thy strong holds shall be like fig trees with the firstripe figs: if they be shaken, they shall even fall into the mouth of the eater.

13 Behold, thy people in the midst of thee are women: the gates of thy land shall be set wide open unto thine enemies: the fire shall devour thy bars.

14 Draw thee waters for the siege, fortify thy strong holds: go into clay, and tread the morter, make strong the brickkiln.

15 There shall the fire devour thee; the sword shall cut thee off, it shall eat thee up like the cankerworm: make thyself many as the cankerworm, make thyself many as the locusts.

16 Thou hast multiplied thy merchants above the stars of heaven: the cankerworm spoileth, and flieth away.

17 Thy crowned are as the locusts, and thy captains as the great grasshoppers, which camp in the hedges in the cold day, but when the sun ariseth they flee away, and their place is not known where they are.

18 Thy shepherds slumber, O king of Assyria: thy nobles shall dwell in the dust: thy people is scattered upon the mountains, and no man gathereth them.

19 There is no healing of thy bruise; thy wound is grievous: all that hear the bruit of thee shall clap the hands over thee: for upon whom hath not thy wickedness passed []{#c30E.xhtml_page_514}continually?

[The judgments of God continue without abatement: Nineveh is reminded of the fact that ]{.class_s321}["No"]{.class_s323}[ (modern "Thebes"---see Jer. 46:25 and Ezek. 30:14--16) was conquered by Sargon (Isa. 18, 20) even though her allies who came to help her were Ethiopia (Cush: upper Egypt), Egypt (lower Egypt), Put (Fhut: Gen. 10:6, west of Egypt), and Lubim (modern Libya). All those listed are descendants of Ham (see comments under Gen. 10 in that Commentary). Observe that the word ]{.class_s321}["infinite" ]{.class_s323}[used here is not according to strict doctrinal definition. Often the Lord uses words exactly as we use them, and this is for the purpose of trapping the self righteous (Ezek. 14) and causing them to pervert the truth (2 Thess. 2). \[Notice, for example, how John 9:2--3 could be used to prove that the blind man had a previous existence and was now at work "getting rid of his 'karma'!"\] The ]{.class_s321}["everlasting hills" ]{.class_s323}[(Gen. 49:26) are not like the "everlasting life" of John 3:16, and the ]{.class_s321}["infinite" ]{.class_s323}[strength of Egypt and Ethiopia is obviously (see Ezek. 29:2) not to be compared with the ]{.class_s321}["infinite" ]{.class_s323}[wisdom of God (Job 22:5).]{.class_s321}

"[Yet was she carried away, she went into captivity" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 10). What happened to No ]{.class_s321}["whose rampart was the sea"]{.class_s323}[ (cf. Isa. 18:2 and 19:5) happens to Judah (Lam. 1--4) and will happen to Assyria (cf. Isa. 51:17; Jer. 25:15; Isa. 28:4; Rev. 6:13; and Isa. 20:3,41. No Ammon was called "the City of God" by the superstitious Greek scholars because the Egyptian counterpart of JUPITER was worshipped there (see comments under Acts 14:12--13 in that Commentary). It was located on both sides of the Nile (in upper Egypt) and was said to have had one hundred gates (Homer). Nevertheless, No Ammon fell, and so Nineveh will fall.]{.class_s321}

[The inhabitants of Nineveh will head for the mountains, caves and bomb shelters when the enemy comes in (Cf. vs. 11 and 1 Kings 22:25). They will try to form military alliances to get help against Babylon (vs. 11), but their efforts will be futile (vs. 12) and their "Maginot Line" will fall down like over-ripe figs falling off a tree (vs. 12). ]{.class_s321}["Thy people in the midst of thee are WOMEN" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 13) []{#c30E.xhtml_page_515}is a reference to the courage and morale of the troops. The modern International Socialist would fail to see the humor in it, as he (and all "humanitarians") would have already assumed that men and women are "equal" and that therefore women could defend a city as well as men. Well, they can't.]{.class_s321}

It is true that you can regiment women (USSR) and brutalize them till they are half male, but no army led and trained by women (see the modern nonsense going on at West Point and Annapolis) is going to stand up against any army led and trained by men. If you could train 100,000 women as Green Berets, night fighters, Seabees, guerrilla scouts, and hand to hand combatants, it wouldn't prove anything except you had to train women to think, eat, sleep, walk, fight, and ACT like MEN (see Jer. 51:30). We will grant that if a woman is robbed of her dress, her makeup, her gentleness, her femininity, her motherly instincts, her passiveness, and her poise that she can compete with a man, but what does this prove? What man wants to marry a competitor? Is that good business?

"[Thy bars"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 13) is a reference to the barred bolt on the city gate (Judg. 16:3).]{.class_s321}

[Verse 14 is ordering preparations for the siege: an additional supply of water (which is always the first necessity), reinforcement of garrisons, repair of the breaches, and erection of additional fortifications out of brick. However, ]{.class_s321}["the fire, ]{.class_s323}[shall devour them ]{.class_s321}["THERE"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 15)---where they make every effort to strengthen the defenses. The sword "eating people" (vs. 15) is the common military vernacular: "cannon fodder"---food for the cannons. ]{.class_s321}["Make thyself many as the cankerworm" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 15); that is, try to multiply your forces till you are as many as those who are "eating you up" (vs. 15). "Thou hast ]{.class_s321}[multiplied thy merchants" ]{.class_s323}[(vs. 16), so why can't you multiply your troops? Seven times the first person possessive is used:]{.class_s321}

1\. Thy merchants.

2\. Thy crowned.

3\. Thy captains.

4\. Thy shepherds.

5\. Thy nobles.

6\. Thy people.

7\. Thy wound.

[The invading troops will spoil the merchants like a cankerworm (vs. 16) and go right on past them. The ]{.class_s321}["crowned"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 17) are princes (Rev. 9:7) who swarm like locusts, and the ]{.class_s321}["captains"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 17) with them are about to run for their lives (vs. 17) when the ]{.class_s321}["sun ariseth"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 17). Notice that we have more here than an illustration about the habits of grasshoppers; the reference to the Second Advent is quite apparent when compared with James 1:11 and Psalm 19:4--6. The leaders of the flock (v. 18) are like the dumb dogs of Isaiah 56:10. They are sound asleep and ob livious of impending danger---sort of like any Liberal in the National Council of churches (1900--1990). The ]{.class_s321}["nobles"]{.class_s323}[ (vs. 18) are going to be slain (Psa. 83:11, 149:8) and will ]{.class_s321}["dwell in the dust" ]{.class_s323}[in the sense of Job 4:19, 10:9. The old English ]{.class_s321}["bruit" ]{.class_s323}[in verse 19 is for "noise" or to make a "noise" (see Jer. 10:22), and in this case it is the noise broadcast by those who hear of Nineveh's downfall.]{.class_s321}

Nahum's prophecies are concise and clear. He has dealt with the immediate destruction of Nineveh by Babylon, and at the same time he has forecast the destruction of the Antichrist and his forces at the end of the Tribulation.
